_meta._note: Detailed FC descriptions embedded for 11 of 30 FCs. The remaining FCs use synthesized descriptions; use the 'Refresh from live server' control to pull full descriptions.

formal_classifications.label: Ten-fold way
formal_classifications.full_name: Altland-Zirnbauer / Schnyder-Ryu-Furusaki-Ludwig / Kitaev classification of topological phases of free fermions
formal_classifications.closure_description: Complete within the stated domain (gapped free-fermion Hamiltonians). Closure was established by Kitaev (2009) via the K-theory / Bott-periodicity argument.
formal_classifications.description: Classification of gapped free-fermion Hamiltonians by their discrete symmetries (time-reversal T, particle-hole C, chiral S = T·C) into ten symmetry classes (the Altland-Zirnbauer ten), each of which carries a topological invariant — trivial (0), ℤ, ℤ₂, or 2ℤ — at each spatial dimension d, with a period-eight (real classes) and period-two (complex classes) Bott pattern across dimensions.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Gapped Hamiltonians of free (non-interacting) fermions in any spatial dimension d ≥ 0, with three discrete symmetries T, C, S = T·C. Excludes interacting topological phases, fractional/symmetry-enriched topological order, gapless topological phases.

classification_axes.name: symmetry-class
classification_axes.description: The ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes labeled by Cartan-class symbols.

classification_axes.name: spatial-dimension
classification_axes.description: Spatial dimension of the gapped bulk. Bott-periodicity extends classification to all d ≥ 0.

predictions.prediction: 2D quantum spin Hall insulator (class AII, d = 2, ℤ₂ classification) — a time-reversal-invariant insulator with helical edge states.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kane-Mele 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 226801; Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang 2006, Science 314, 1757 (HgTe-specific prediction)
predictions.confirmation_citation: König et al. 2007, Science 318, 766

predictions.prediction: 3D topological insulator (class AII, d = 3, ℤ₂ classification) — a class of bulk-insulating materials with topologically-protected surface states.
predictions.prediction_citation: Fu-Kane-Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803; Moore-Balents 2007, Phys. Rev. B 75, 121306; Roy 2009, Phys. Rev. B 79, 195322
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hsieh et al. 2008, Nature 452, 970 (Bi₁₋ₓSbₓ); Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398 (Bi₂Se₃)

predictions.prediction: 1D topological superconductor wire (class D, d = 1, ℤ₂ classification) hosting Majorana zero modes at its endpoints.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kitaev 2001, Phys.-Usp. 44, 131
predictions.confirmation_citation: Mourik et al. 2012, Science 336, 1003 (initial claim); Zhang et al. retraction 2021, Nature 591, 354 (retraction of a high-profile follow-up); current status reviewed in Frolov 2021, Nature 592, 350 and Yu et al. 2021, Nature Physics 17, 482
predictions.description: The theoretical prediction is firm and the classification cell is uncontested. Experimental status has substantial tension: several high-profile claims of zero-bias-peak signatures consistent with Majorana zero modes have been challenged or retracted; the field has not yet converged on a confirmed material realization. Status 'unconfirmed-tension' rather than 'confirmed' or 'falsified' reflects this honestly.

predictions.prediction: 3D time-reversal-invariant topological superconductor (class DIII, d = 3, ℤ classification).
predictions.prediction_citation: Schnyder-Ryu-Furusaki-Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125; Qi-Hughes-Raghu-Zhang 2009, Phys. Rev. Lett. 102, 187001
predictions.confirmation_citation: Realized in superfluid ³He-B (longstanding; topological reading consolidated in Volovik 2003, 'The Universe in a Helium Droplet')

predictions.prediction: Integer quantum Hall effect (class A, d = 2, ℤ classification) — quantized Hall conductance at integer multiples of e²/h.
predictions.prediction_citation: Discovered 1980 (von Klitzing-Dorda-Pepper, Phys. Rev. Lett. 45, 494); topological interpretation via TKNN invariant (Thouless-Kohmoto-Nightingale-den Nijs 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 405); class A / ten-fold-way framing applied retrospectively.
predictions.description: Recorded as retro-explanatory-only because the experimental phenomenon predates the ten-fold-way framework. The classification correctly hosts the IQHE; it did not predict it.

formal_classifications.label: SM rep content
formal_classifications.closure_description: Complete as a specification of G_SM representations. Closure was empirically completed in 2012 with the Higgs discovery.
formal_classifications.description: The Standard Model's particle content, specified as the representations carried by the SM matter fields, gauge bosons, and Higgs doublet under the gauge group G_SM = SU(3)_c × SU(2)_L × U(1)_Y. Closed empirically with the Higgs discovery (2012). The classification is rep-theoretically constrained — the requirement of gauge-anomaly cancellation forces the hypercharge assignments to satisfy non-trivial relations, making charm (GIM 1970) and the third generation top-quark predictions rather than fits.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: The SM gauge group SU(3)_c × SU(2)_L × U(1)_Y and the field content it constrains. Excludes right-handed neutrinos, gravity, physics above the EW scale, and Yukawa coupling values themselves.

predictions.description: The most cell-specific Higgs prediction had a 48-year wait from theoretical proposal to experimental confirmation. The mass was not predicted by the rep content alone (the Higgs self-coupling that fixes m_H is a free parameter of the SM); the mass-eigenvalue at 125 GeV was unknown until 2012.

predictions.prediction: Existence of antiparticles (the conjugate-rep components — d_L is paired with d_L-bar; the SM rep content implicitly specifies the antimatter sector).
predictions.prediction_citation: Dirac 1928, Proc. R. Soc. A 117, 610 (the Dirac equation predicts antimatter prior to the SM)
predictions.description: Antimatter predates the SM. The SM rep content makes antimatter a structural feature (each chirality has a conjugate rep), but the prediction is Dirac's, not the SM's.

predictions.prediction: A fourth quark (charm) required by anomaly cancellation and FCNC suppression, with gauge rep (3, 1, +2/3) in the second generation.

formal_classifications.label: ADE Lie algebras
formal_classifications.full_name: Simply-laced finite-dimensional complex simple Lie algebras (Killing-Cartan ADE)
formal_classifications.description: Classification of simply-laced finite-dimensional complex simple Lie algebras: A_n = sl(n+1, ℂ), D_n = so(2n, ℂ), and exceptionals E_6, E_7, E_8. The most famous instance of the ADE pattern; the other ADE classifications correspond through structured bijections.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Finite-dimensional complex simple Lie algebras with simply-laced root systems.

cells.content: A_n = sl(n+1, ℂ) for n ≥ 1; rank n; dim n(n+2)
realized_examples: A_2 = sl(3,ℂ) — SU(3) flavor/color
A_4 = sl(5,ℂ) — SU(5) GUT

cells.content: D_n = so(2n, ℂ) for n ≥ 4; rank n; dim n(2n-1)
realized_examples: D_4 = so(8) — triality, 8d spinors
D_5 = so(10) — Fritzsch-Minkowski-Georgi GUT

cells.content: E_6; rank 6; dim 78; fundamental 27-dimensional rep
realized_examples: E_6 GUT (Gürsey-Ramond-Sikivie 1976)

cells.content: E_7; rank 7; dim 133; fundamental 56-dimensional rep
realized_examples: N=8 supergravity in 4d (Cremmer-Julia 1979) — E_{7(7)} hidden symmetry

cells.content: E_8; rank 8; dim 248; adjoint is smallest non-trivial rep; root lattice = E_8 lattice
realized_examples: E_8 × E_8 heterotic string
E_8 in Ising-in-field (Zamolodchikov 1989; Coldea et al. 2010)

formal_classifications.label: ADE finite SU(2) subgroups
formal_classifications.closure_description: Complete. Klein (1884) classified the finite rotation groups of the sphere; McKay (1980) recognized the ADE correspondence.
formal_classifications.description: Classification of finite subgroups of SU(2) up to conjugation: cyclic Z_n, binary dihedral BD_{4n}, binary tetrahedral 2T, binary octahedral 2O, binary icosahedral 2I. The McKay correspondence (1980) gives a bijection with ADE Dynkin diagrams.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Finite subgroups of SU(2), equivalently finite subgroups of the unit quaternions S³.

cells.content: Cyclic groups Z_{n+1} of order n+1; abelian
Binary dihedral BD_{4(n-2)} of order 4(n-2) for n ≥ 4; non-abelian
Binary tetrahedral 2T of order 24
Binary octahedral 2O of order 48
Binary icosahedral 2I of order 120

citations: Klein 1884
McKay 1980
Reid 1997

formal_classifications.label: ADE Du Val singularities
formal_classifications.full_name: Du Val / Kleinian / rational double point surface singularities (ADE)
formal_classifications.description: Classification of isolated rational double points on complex algebraic surfaces, equivalently quotient singularities ℂ²/Γ where Γ ⊂ SU(2) is finite. The resolution attaches (-2)-curves whose intersection graph is the corresponding ADE Dynkin diagram.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Isolated rational double points on complex algebraic or analytic surfaces.

cells.content: D_n: x²y + y^{n-1} + z² = 0 for n ≥ 4; ℂ²/BD_{4(n-2)}
E_6: x⁴ + y³ + z² = 0; ℂ²/2T
E_7: x³y + y³ + z² = 0; ℂ²/2O
E_8: x⁵ + y³ + z² = 0; ℂ²/2I

formal_classifications.label: ADE SU(2)_k modular invariants
formal_classifications.full_name: Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber classification of modular invariants of the SU(2)_k WZW model
formal_classifications.description: Classification of modular-invariant partition functions for the SU(2) WZW conformal field theory at level k. A-series at all levels; D-series at even levels; E-series only at k = 10, 16, 28.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Modular-invariant partition functions for SU(2)_k WZW in 2D rational CFT.

cells.content: Diagonal modular invariant Z_A = Σ_{λ=0}^{k} |χ_λ|²; labeled A_{k+1}
Z_2-orbifold modular invariant of the diagonal; labeled D_{k/2+1}
Exceptional modular invariant tied to su(2) ⊂ sp(4); exists only at level k = 10
Exceptional modular invariant; exists only at level k = 16
Exceptional modular invariant tied to su(2) ⊂ E_8 at level 1; exists only at level k = 28

Gannon 2000

formal_classifications.label: ADE quivers (Gabriel)
formal_classifications.description: Gabriel's theorem (1972): a connected quiver has finitely many indecomposable finite-dim representations iff its underlying graph is an ADE Dynkin diagram. Indecomposables in bijection with positive roots.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Connected finite quivers and their finite-dim representations over an algebraically closed field.

cells.content: A_n linear quiver, n vertices; n(n+1)/2 indecomposables (interval modules)
D_n quiver, n vertices for n ≥ 4; n(n-1) indecomposables
E_6 quiver; 36 indecomposables
E_7 quiver; 63 indecomposables
E_8 quiver; 120 indecomposables (matching positive root count)

citations: Gabriel 1972
Bernstein-Gelfand-Ponomarev 1973

formal_classifications.label: Spacetime symmetry groups
formal_classifications.full_name: Classification of spacetime symmetry groups used in physical theories
formal_classifications.closure_description: Complete as a classification of major spacetime symmetry families. Does not extend to: which family holds at the Planck scale (QG frontier); supersymmetric extensions; whether spacetime symmetry is fundamental or emergent.
formal_classifications.description: Classification of symmetry groups acting on spacetime: Galilean, Poincaré, conformal, de Sitter, anti-de Sitter, and the diffeomorphism group of GR. Wigner's 1939 classification of Poincaré unitary irreps by (mass², spin/helicity) defines what a 'particle' is in relativistic QFT.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Symmetry groups acting globally on a spacetime manifold (or locally for Diff). Excludes internal gauge groups, discrete spacetime symmetries (P, T, C), and supersymmetry extensions.

cells.content: Gal(d) = SO(d-1) ⋉ (translations × boosts) ⋉ time-translation; non-relativistic; instantaneous boosts; central extensions (Bargmann) carry physical mass parameter
realized_examples: Newtonian mechanics
non-relativistic quantum mechanics (with Bargmann central extension)
non-relativistic field theories (cold atoms, condensed matter at low temperature)

cells.content: ISO(d-1, 1) = SO(d-1, 1) ⋉ ℝ^d; Lorentz group SO(d-1, 1) ≃ Spin(d-1, 1)/Z_2; unitary irreps classified by Wigner 1939
realized_examples: special relativity
relativistic quantum mechanics
the Standard Model (Poincaré-invariant at energies below the Planck scale)
all of relativistic QFT in flat spacetime

cells.content: For d ≥ 3: SO(d, 2) ≃ Conf(d-1, 1); finite-dimensional. For d = 2: infinite-dimensional Virasoro × Virasoro with central charge c
realized_examples: conformal field theories at critical points
AdS/CFT (the CFT side)
2D worldsheet of string theory (Virasoro constraints)

cells.content: SO(d, 1) acting on d-dimensional de Sitter spacetime (positive cosmological constant); finite-dimensional; no positive-energy unitary irreps
realized_examples: our observed accelerating universe (approximately dS in the far future under ΛCDM)
dS-invariant inflation models
the dS/CFT speculative correspondence

cells.content: SO(d-1, 2) acting on d-dimensional anti-de Sitter spacetime (negative cosmological constant); positive-energy unitary irreps exist; isomorphic to Conf(d-2, 1) (the AdS/CFT isomorphism at the group level)
realized_examples: AdS_5 × S^5 in Type IIB string theory
AdS/CFT-based holographic models
exact non-perturbative QFT-like theories with negative cc

cells.content: Diff(M): the full diffeomorphism group of a smooth manifold M; infinite-dimensional; the gauge group of general relativity
realized_examples: general relativity (all observationally-confirmed gravitational physics)

predictions.prediction: Existence and classification of particle types in relativistic QFT: each particle is a unitary irrep of Poincaré, labeled by (mass, spin) or (zero mass, helicity). Higher-spin massless particles (s > 2 in 4D) excluded in interacting theories.
predictions.prediction_citation: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149
predictions.confirmation_citation: Every observed elementary particle in the SM fits Wigner's classification.

predictions.prediction: Galilean-invariant non-relativistic QM admits projective representations labeled by mass (Bargmann central extension); mass is a conserved superselection sector in NRQM.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bargmann 1954, Ann. Math. 59, 1
predictions.confirmation_citation: The mass superselection rule is a routine feature of NRQM.

predictions.prediction: 2D conformal field theories at criticality have infinite-dimensional Virasoro symmetry; c < 1 minimal models classified by (p, p') labels (ADE / minimal-model classification).
predictions.prediction_citation: Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 241, 333
predictions.confirmation_citation: 2D Ising criticality, tricritical Ising, three-state Potts — matched to minimal-model predictions to high precision

Bargmann 1954
Mack-Salam 1969
Belavin-Polyakov-Zamolodchikov 1984

formal_classifications.label: Freed-Hopkins cobordism
formal_classifications.full_name: Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification of invertible TQFTs and SPT phases
formal_classifications.closure_description: Partial. Framework is established and proven; cobordism groups for these five tangential structures and small dimensions are standard. Higher dimensions, exotic tangential structures, and extension to non-invertible topological phases remain active research areas.
formal_classifications.description: Classification of invertible topological field theories (and gapped SPT phases) by cobordism. Freed-Hopkins (2016, 2019) proposed that invertible TQFTs in d spacetime dimensions with tangential structure G are classified by [MTG, Σ^{d+1} IZ]. The classification subsumes the ten-fold way (free-fermion case) and Fidkowski-Kitaev-type interaction-induced collapses are computed structurally. Famous identities: Ω^{Pin-}_2 = Z_8 (1d BDI collapse); Ω^{Pin+}_4 = Z_16 (3+1d time-reversal SPT, refining ten-fold way's AII).
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Invertible TQFTs and SPT phases for matter with internal symmetries acting via tangential-structure G on spacetime. Covers fermionic/bosonic SPTs with discrete and U(1) symmetries. Excludes non-invertible topological order, gapless topological phases, anomalous boundary states not captured by cobordism.

cells.content: Ω^{SO}_0 = ℤ
Ω^{SO}_4 = ℤ
Ω^{Spin}_0 = ℤ
Ω^{Spin}_1 = ℤ₂
Ω^{Spin}_2 = ℤ₂
Ω^{Spin}_3 = 0
Ω^{Spin}_4 = ℤ
Ω^{Pin+}_0 = ℤ₂
Ω^{Pin+}_2 = ℤ₂
Ω^{Pin+}_3 = ℤ₂
Ω^{Pin+}_4 = ℤ₁₆
realized_examples: The general 3+1d topological insulator with T² = -1, when interactions are included. The free-fermion case is one of 16 distinct interacting phases.

cells.content: Ω^{Pin-}_0 = ℤ₂
Ω^{Pin-}_1 = ℤ₂

predictions.prediction: The free-fermion Z classification of 1d BDI topological superconductors collapses to Z_8 under interactions.
predictions.prediction_citation: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509
predictions.confirmation_citation: Multiple independent theoretical confirmations (Turner-Pollmann-Berg 2011; Tang-Wen 2012; subsequent literature).

predictions.prediction: Time-reversal-invariant 3+1d fermionic SPT phases with T² = -1 form a Z_16 classification when interactions are included, refining the Z_2 free-fermion classification of class AII.
predictions.prediction_citation: Wang, Senthil 2014, Phys. Rev. B 89, 195124; Kapustin-Thorngren-Turzillo-Wang 2015, JHEP 12, 052; Freed-Hopkins 2016
predictions.confirmation_citation: Theoretical: established via multiple frameworks. Experimental: partial — the higher-Z_16 phases require interaction-induced phases not yet isolated experimentally.

predictions.prediction: The integer quantum Hall effect's TKNN invariant (first Chern number) is the Ω^{Spin^c}_2 = Z cobordism invariant; this provides a structural derivation rather than a phenomenological one.
predictions.prediction_citation: Freed-Hopkins 2016 (structural derivation)
predictions.description: The IQHE was observed in 1980 (von Klitzing); the TKNN invariant interpretation came in 1982. The cobordism-theoretic re-derivation in Freed-Hopkins is retro-explanatory — it provides a uniform framework but did not predict the IQHE.

predictions.prediction: The full classification of interacting fermionic SPT phases for arbitrary tangential structures in arbitrary dimensions reduces to computing cobordism groups Ω^G_{d+1}, a task that the algebraic-topology machinery (Adams spectral sequence, etc.) makes systematically computable.
predictions.prediction_citation: Freed, Hopkins 2016
predictions.description: Methodological prediction. Not unconfirmed in the sense of being challenged, but the prediction is about a research program's reach; many cases remain to be computed even where the framework is accepted as correct.

predictions.prediction: Time-reversal-invariant 3+1d fermionic SPT phases form a Z_16 classification when interactions are included, refining the Z_2 free-fermion classification of the ten-fold way's class AII.
predictions.prediction_citation: Wang, Senthil 2014, Phys. Rev. B 89, 195124; Freed, Hopkins 2016
predictions.confirmation_citation: Theoretical confirmation via multiple independent calculations; experimental confirmation is partial (the higher-Z_16 phases require specific interaction-induced phases not yet definitively isolated experimentally).

predictions.prediction: The free-fermion Z classification of 1d class BDI topological superconductors collapses to Z_8 under interactions; equivalently, eight free-fermion BDI phases that are distinct without interactions become continuously deformable into one trivial phase when interactions are turned on.
predictions.prediction_citation: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509 (first explicit demonstration); Kitaev 2011 talk on classifications; Freed-Hopkins 2016 (cobordism interpretation)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Theoretical confirmation via multiple independent calculations (Turner-Pollmann-Berg 2011; etc.).

formal_classifications.label: SU(5) GUT rep content
formal_classifications.full_name: Georgi-Glashow SU(5) grand unified theory matter content
formal_classifications.closure_description: Conjectural. The rep content is well-defined mathematically but empirical confirmation depends on observing proton decay. Minimal SU(5) is ruled out; SUSY SU(5) and other variants remain unconfirmed.
formal_classifications.description: Matter content classification under Georgi-Glashow SU(5) GUT, embedding SM gauge group SU(3)_c × SU(2)_L × U(1)_Y into SU(5). Each SM fermion generation sits in 5̄ ⊕ 10. Gauge bosons of SU(5) include 12 SM bosons plus 12 'leptoquark' X/Y bosons mediating proton decay. Status conjectural: minimal SU(5) ruled out by Super-K proton decay limits (τ_p > 1.6 × 10^34 yr); SUSY SU(5) and variants remain viable.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Energies above SU(5) unification scale (~10^15-10^16 GeV). Does not apply if SU(5) is not the correct unification group (alternatives: SO(10), E_6, Pati-Salam, SUSY variants).

cells.content: 5̄ of first generation: contains L_L = (ν_e, e_L) and d_R^c (right-handed down-quark conjugate)
realized_examples: first-generation lepton doublet and right-handed down quark in SM

cells.content: 5̄ of second generation: contains L_L = (ν_μ, μ_L) and s_R^c
realized_examples: second-generation lepton doublet and right-handed strange quark

cells.content: 5̄ of third generation: contains L_L = (ν_τ, τ_L) and b_R^c
realized_examples: third-generation lepton doublet and right-handed bottom quark

cells.content: structurally-excluded
10 of first generation: contains Q_L = (u_L, d_L), u_R^c, and e_R^c (positron)
realized_examples: first-generation quark doublet, right-handed up-quark, right-handed charged lepton — all together in one SU(5) 10

cells.content: 10 of second generation: contains Q_L = (c_L, s_L), c_R^c, and μ_R^c
realized_examples: second-generation quark doublet, right-handed charm-quark, right-handed muon

cells.content: 10 of third generation: contains Q_L = (t_L, b_L), t_R^c, and τ_R^c
realized_examples: third-generation quark doublet, right-handed top-quark, right-handed tau

cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
24 (adjoint of SU(5)): 12 SM gauge bosons (8 gluons + 3 W + 1 B) + 12 X/Y leptoquarks of charge ±4/3 and ±1/3 mediating proton decay
realized_examples: The 12 SM gauge bosons are observed; the 12 X/Y leptoquarks are predicted at mass ~M_GUT and are not directly observed (their indirect signature would be proton decay).

predictions.prediction: Proton decay via tree-level X/Y leptoquark exchange, with lifetime τ_p ~ M_GUT^4 / (g^4 m_p^5) ≈ 10^29 to 10^31 years for minimal SU(5).
predictions.prediction_citation: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438; Buras, Ellis, Gaillard, Nanopoulos 1978, Nucl. Phys. B 135, 66
predictions.confirmation_citation: Super-Kamiokande Collaboration 2017, Phys. Rev. D 95, 012004 (proton lifetime > 1.6 × 10^34 years for p → e+ π^0)
predictions.description: Minimal non-supersymmetric SU(5)'s proton-decay prediction is ruled out by Super-Kamiokande's lower bound. Supersymmetric SU(5) raises the GUT scale to ~10^16 GeV and pushes the proton lifetime to ~10^34 - 10^36 years, currently at the edge of experimental sensitivity.

predictions.prediction: Gauge coupling unification: the three SM gauge couplings g_1, g_2, g_3 should converge at the GUT scale.
predictions.prediction_citation: Georgi, Quinn, Weinberg 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 33, 451
predictions.confirmation_citation: Amaldi, de Boer, Fürstenau 1991, Phys. Lett. B 260, 447 (analysis with LEP data showing the couplings don't quite meet under SM running, but do under MSSM running)
predictions.description: Non-SUSY SU(5)'s gauge-coupling unification prediction has tension (the three couplings don't quite meet under SM running). SUSY SU(5) restores unification at a high precision level.

predictions.prediction: Magnetic monopoles with mass ~M_GUT, predicted by 't Hooft-Polyakov mechanism applied to SU(5) → SM breaking.
predictions.prediction_citation: 't Hooft 1974, Nucl. Phys. B 79, 276; Polyakov 1974, JETP Lett. 20, 194
predictions.description: GUT monopoles at ~10^16 GeV are far too heavy for current accelerator searches; cosmological searches (MACRO experiment etc.) have set limits but not detected any.

predictions.prediction: sin²θ_W(M_Z) = 3/8 at the GUT scale, running down to ~0.21 at M_Z (renormalization-group corrections).
predictions.prediction_citation: Georgi, Quinn, Weinberg 1974
predictions.confirmation_citation: Measured value sin²θ_W(M_Z) ≈ 0.231; SUSY SU(5) gives consistent prediction, non-SUSY SU(5) deviates by ~10%.

formal_classifications.label: CFT bootstrap exclusion regions
formal_classifications.full_name: Conformal-bootstrap exclusion regions for unitary conformal field theories
formal_classifications.closure_description: Partial. Framework is established; only a finite list of (d, CFT-family) cells has been bootstrap-analyzed. Bootstrap bounds are increasingly tight but not generally proven optimal.
formal_classifications.description: Classification of unitary CFTs by allowed regions in operator-dimension/OPE-coefficient parameter space cut out by conformal-bootstrap equations. Inputs: conformal symmetry + unitarity + OPE associativity + crossing symmetry. Modern numerical bootstrap (Rattazzi-Rychkov-Tonni-Vichi 2008; El-Showk-Paulos-Poland-Rychkov-Simmons-Duffin-Vichi 2012) produces highest-precision 3D Ising exponents, bounds on 4D N=4 SYM, 6D (2,0), and 3D N≥4 SCFTs.
formal_classifications.domain_of_applicability: Unitary, Poincaré-invariant, conformally-invariant QFTs with discrete spectrum of conformal primaries in d ∈ [2,6]. Excludes non-unitary CFTs, theories with continuous spectra, theories with non-local operators violating OPE associativity.

cells.content: Belavin-Polyakov-Zamolodchikov minimal models M(p,q) with central charge c = 1 − 6(p−q)²/(pq); unitary subseries M(m+1,m) at c = 1 − 6/(m(m+1)), m ≥ 2, including Ising (m=3, c=1/2), tricritical Ising (m=4, c=7/10), 3-state Potts (m=5, c=4/5), …
realized_examples: 2D Ising critical point at T_c (Onsager 1944; bootstrap-recovered as the m=3 minimal model)
tricritical Ising point in dilute Ising magnet
3-state Potts model at criticality
Lee-Yang edge singularity (non-unitary; M(2,5))

cells.content: Free compact boson at c = 1; T-dual moduli space parametrized by radius R; Gaussian fixed line
realized_examples: 2D classical XY model at zero magnetic field, low-T Kosterlitz-Thouless phase
free chiral boson (one chirality, c = 1/2 per chirality after pairing)

cells.content: 2D Ising CFT: c = 1/2, Δ_σ = 1/8, Δ_ε = 1; exact spectrum from M(4,3) minimal model
realized_examples: Lattice Ising model at the Onsager critical temperature
Free Majorana fermion in 2D (equivalent by Jordan-Wigner)

cells.content: Isolated 'island' in (Δ_σ, Δ_ε) space; current best bootstrap values Δ_σ = 0.5181489(10), Δ_ε = 1.412625(10); central charge c_T/c_T^{free} = 0.9466930(11)
realized_examples: Liquid–gas critical point of fluids (universality class)
Curie point of uniaxial ferromagnets
Order-disorder transition in binary alloys
Lattice 3D Ising model at T_c (Monte Carlo confirmation to comparable precision)

cells.content: Family of islands parametrized by N. Canonical cases: N=2 (XY universality, Δ_φ ≈ 0.51926(4)); N=3 (Heisenberg, Δ_φ ≈ 0.51875(25)); N=4 (Δ_φ ≈ 0.5180(2)); large-N asymptotic to free-scalar dimension Δ_φ = 1/2 + O(1/N)
realized_examples: λ-transition in liquid 4He (O(2) universality)
ferromagnetic phase transition in EuO, EuS, isotropic Heisenberg magnets (O(3))
finite-temperature chiral symmetry restoration in two-flavour QCD (O(4))

cells.content: Fermion-scalar CFT with N_f flavors of Majorana fermion and Yukawa coupling. Bootstrap islands for N_f = 1, 2, 4, 8. Yukawa-relevant scalar dimension Δ_φ ≈ 0.76 at N_f = 1.
realized_examples: Quantum critical point of interacting Dirac fermions on the graphene honeycomb (semimetal-to-insulator transition)
Semimetal-to-superconductor transition in surface states of 3D topological insulators

cells.content: 4D N=4 super-Yang-Mills bootstrap; central charge c continuous (parametrized by gauge group and 't Hooft coupling); stress-tensor four-point function pinned by superconformal Ward identities, with the unprotected sector probed by Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees (2014, 2017).
realized_examples: N=4 SU(N) super-Yang-Mills at finite N (the canonical 4D superconformal theory)
AdS_5 × S^5 string theory dual (Maldacena 1998)

cells.content: 4D N=2 SCFT bootstrap; chiral algebra protected sector is a 2D vertex operator algebra (Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015); free-field bound c ≥ 11/30 (a-theorem-compatible) and 24c − 12a ≥ 0
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas SCFTs (rank-1, isolated points on the Coulomb branch)
N=2 SU(2) SYM with N_f = 4 fundamental hypermultiplets (the conformal point on the Seiberg-Witten plane)
Class-S theories of type A_n (Gaiotto 2009)

cells.content: Gaussian fixed point: Δ_φ = 1, all OPE coefficients given by Wick contractions; central charge c = 1 in units of free-scalar c
realized_examples: Free massless scalar field theory in 4D (upper critical dimension of φ⁴ theory)

cells.content: structurally-excluded

Wilson-Fisher trajectory: Δ_σ(d) interpolates between Δ_σ(d=2) = 1/8 and Δ_σ(d=4) = 1 as d varies continuously; bootstrap islands tracked along the trajectory; at d = 4 − ε with ε small, recovers Wilson-Fisher ε-expansion to high orders
structurally-excluded
6D (2,0) SCFT bootstrap; central charge c_T fixed by 1/2-BPS multiplet structure; type-A_n: c_T = (n^3 - n)/(...); type-D_n: similar; type-E_6,7,8: exceptional cases
realized_examples: Theory on N coincident M5-branes in M-theory (type A_{N-1})
Type-D_n (2,0) theory from M5-branes on R^5/Z_2 orbifold
Type-E_6, E_7, E_8 (2,0) theories from compactification of (2,0) on Riemann surfaces (Gaiotto class-S)

cells.content: 3D N=8 superconformal bootstrap; central charge c_T(N) for ABJM theory at level k=1, c_T(1) ≈ 21.0/63 in canonical units; bootstrap islands matching ABJM at small N
realized_examples: ABJM theory at Chern-Simons level k=1 (the 3D N=8 maximally-supersymmetric CFT; Aharony-Bergman-Jafferis-Maldacena 2008)
M2-brane worldvolume theory at large N (dual to AdS_4 × S^7)

cells.content: structurally-excluded

Universal bounds on central charges and Hofman-Maldacena ratios (a/c in d=4; t_4, t_2 conformal-collider observables in any d≥3); a/c bounded between 1/3 and 31/18 for unitary 4D CFTs (Hofman-Maldacena 2008), with bootstrap narrowing to a/c ≤ 3/2 + corrections (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011); central-charge lower bounds from stress-tensor crossing
realized_examples: Bounds saturated by free scalar (a/c = 1/3), free Maxwell (a/c = 1/(36/31 · ...)), and various near-extremal theories

realized_examples: 3D free Klein-Gordon field (massless limit of 3D scalar field theory)
Mean-field theory of phase transitions above the upper critical dimension (for d>4 in Ising-class)

As of 2026 the discrepancy remains unexplained; candidate resolutions include experimental systematics (gravitational gradients during finite-temperature 4He measurement) and a subtle universality-class identification failure (e.g. the experimental system sitting near but not exactly on the O(2) fixed point).

predictions.prediction_citation: Wilson, Fisher 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 28, 240
predictions.description: The Wilson-Fisher prediction itself predates the modern bootstrap by ≈40 years. The bootstrap's role is structural: it recovers the WF result from first principles (conformal invariance + unitarity + crossing), confirming that the ε-expansion describes a real CFT trajectory in continuous d. Retro-explanatory-only reflects that the bootstrap re-derives rather than first-predicts the WF physics.

predictions.prediction_citation: Iliesiu, Kos, Poland, Pufu, Simmons-Duffin 2018, JHEP 03, 121
predictions.description: GNY exponents are bootstrap-pinned to sub-percent precision but no experimental quantum-critical realization has yet achieved comparable precision; the closest target (electronic semimetal-to-insulator transitions in 2D graphene-like systems) is currently far from the critical point. Experimental verification awaits a cleaner critical-point realization.

predictions.prediction: Lower bound on the number of massless flavors required for an interacting IR-fixed point in 4D SU(N_c) gauge theory ('lower edge of the conformal window'); bootstrap-aided analysis gives N_f / N_c ≥ approximately 2.5 (for SU(3), N_f ≥ 8 or so)
predictions.prediction_citation: Poland, Stergiou, Vichi 2018, JHEP 11, 070 (bootstrap analysis); Banks, Zaks 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 196, 189 (original perturbative argument)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Lattice studies of SU(3) with N_f = 8, 10, 12 fundamental flavors: Hasenfratz et al. 2014 onwards (multiple groups; some claim 8-flavor IR-conformality, others find chiral symmetry breaking; tension unresolved)
predictions.description: Lattice QCD studies of near-conformal SU(3) with varying flavor count have produced conflicting reports on the lower edge of the conformal window. The bootstrap provides rigorous lower bounds on operator dimensions that constrain the IR CFT if it exists, but the bootstrap does not directly determine whether such a CFT exists for a given (N_c, N_f); inferring the conformal-window edge requires combining bootstrap with lattice or other input.

The discrepancy is many sigma and unresolved as of 2026. formal_classifications.label: Modular tensor categories
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'modular-tensor-categories' (structural; sector: Formal HE theory). Contains 18 cells across 2 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Trivial MTC: only the vacuum object 1. Fusion rules: 1 ⊗ 1 = 1. S-matrix is the 1×1 identity. Central charge c = 0.
realized_examples: Trivial gapped phase (no topological order)
Vec (the category of complex vector spaces, as a trivial braided fusion category)

cells.content: Semion MTC: objects {1, s} with s ⊗ s = 1 and braiding eigenvalue i (the semion's exchange phase is i, hence 'half a fermion'). Chiral central charge c = 1 (mod 8 for bosonic structure). Modular S = (1/√2) [[1,1],[1,-1]].
realized_examples: U(1)_2 Chern-Simons theory
Kalmeyer-Laughlin chiral spin liquid (proposed for triangular antiferromagnets)
Boundary of (2+1)D bosonic IQHE-like phase with σ_xy = 2 (in units of e²/h)

cells.content: Fibonacci MTC: objects {1, τ} with τ ⊗ τ = 1 ⊕ τ (the Fibonacci fusion rule). Quantum dimension d_τ = φ = (1+√5)/2 (the golden ratio). Central charge c = 14/5. Non-abelian — τ is a non-abelian anyon.
realized_examples: (G_2)_1 Chern-Simons theory
Yang-Lee non-unitary CFT (after a unitarization)
Predicted in certain fractional Chern insulators (no confirmed laboratory realization as of 2026)

cells.content: Lee-Yang MTC: non-unitary; objects {1, τ_LY} with τ_LY ⊗ τ_LY = 1 ⊕ τ_LY. Same fusion rule as Fibonacci but negative quantum dimension d_{τ_LY} = -1/φ < 0. Central charge c = -22/5.
realized_examples: Lee-Yang edge singularity in dilute Ising at imaginary magnetic field
Minimal model M(2,5) (non-unitary)

cells.content: Ising MTC: objects {1, σ, ψ} with fusion σ ⊗ σ = 1 ⊕ ψ, σ ⊗ ψ = σ, ψ ⊗ ψ = 1. Quantum dimensions: d_1 = d_ψ = 1, d_σ = √2. Central charge c = 1/2 (mod 8). Non-abelian — σ is a non-abelian anyon (Majorana-like).
realized_examples: Majorana zero modes at vortex cores of (p+ip) superconductors
Boundary of (2+1)D Kitaev's honeycomb model in the gapped non-abelian (B) phase
Predicted (with partial confirmation) in ν=5/2 FQHE Moore-Read Pfaffian state

cells.content: SU(2)_2 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1}. Central charge c = 3·2/(2+2) = 3/2. Distinct from Ising despite both being rank-3 with σ-like anyon — SU(2)_2 has different chiral central charge and different F-symbols.
realized_examples: SU(2)_2 WZW model
Effective theory of a chiral spin liquid with SU(2) global symmetry

cells.content: Tricritical Ising MTC (within rank-3 unitary classification): central charge c = 7/10. Three primaries with conformal weights (0, 1/10, 6/10) in the unitary M(4,5) minimal model.
realized_examples: Tricritical Ising phase transition in dilute Ising magnet
Boundary of supersymmetric (2+1)D topological superconductor

cells.content: Toric code MTC (Z_2 gauge theory): objects {1, e, m, ε} where ε = e⊗m. Fusion: e⊗e = m⊗m = ε⊗ε = 1; e⊗m = ε. Quantum dimensions all 1 (abelian). Chiral central charge c = 0 — non-chiral; time-reversal-invariant.
realized_examples: Kitaev's toric code on a square lattice (experimentally realized in superconducting qubits — Google 2023, IBM 2024 — and in neutral-atom arrays — Lukin group 2021)
Z_2 spin liquids (candidate materials: certain pyrochlores; α-RuCl_3 in narrow regime)
Boundary of (2+1)D Z_2 SPT in the bosonic case

cells.content: Three-fermion (3F) MTC: objects {1, ψ_1, ψ_2, ψ_3} all fermionic (semion-squared exchange phases). Fusion: ψ_i ⊗ ψ_j = ψ_k for i ≠ j ≠ k, ψ_i ⊗ ψ_i = 1. Central charge c = 4 (mod 8). Chiral, no time-reversal-invariant realization.
realized_examples: Predicted boundary of certain (2+1)D fermionic SPT with three-band structure (no confirmed material realization as of 2026)
U(1)_4 × U(1)_{-4} chiral phase with appropriate K-matrix

cells.content: Doubled Ising MTC: Z(Ising) = Drinfeld center of the Ising fusion category. Rank 9, but at restricted topological sectors reduces to rank 4 in some constructions. Actually doubled-Ising in its canonical (rank-9) form: objects (a, b̄) where a, b ∈ {1, σ, ψ}. Non-chiral: c = 0. Time-reversal-invariant.
realized_examples: Levin-Wen string-net model with Ising fusion data
Boundary of (3+1)D phase with non-abelian intrinsic topological order on the boundary

cells.content: SU(2)_3 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2}. Central charge c = 3·3/(3+2) = 9/5. The j=1/2 and j=3/2 anyons are non-abelian; j=1 is abelian (the Z_2 grading). Equivalent to a Fibonacci-extended construction.
realized_examples: Read-Rezayi parafermion states (k=3 case) in FQHE
Proposed at ν = 12/5 FQHE plateau
SU(2)_3 WZW model

cells.content: SU(2)_4 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2, 2}. Central charge c = 3·4/(4+2) = 2.
realized_examples: SU(2)_4 WZW model
Proposed in higher-Landau-level FQHE constructions

cells.content: Fibonacci × Fibonacci-bar MTC (Drinfeld-center-like construction): rank 4 actually (1, τ, τ̄, τ⊗τ̄). Or alternatively a rank-5 exceptional from doubled SO(3)_3 truncation. Central charge c = 0 (non-chiral, if doubled construction).
realized_examples: Drinfeld center Z(Fib) of Fibonacci fusion category
Levin-Wen string-net with Fibonacci input

cells.content: SU(2)_5 Chern-Simons MTC: rank 6, j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2, 2, 5/2}. Central charge c = 3·5/(5+2) = 15/7.
realized_examples: SU(2)_5 WZW model

cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded

predictions.description: Theoretical prediction (Freedman-Larsen-Wang 2002): the braid group representation on the Fibonacci MTC's topological Hilbert space generates a dense subgroup of the unitary group, hence braiding alone universal. No laboratory realization of Fibonacci anyons confirmed as of 2026; searches continue in fractional Chern insulators and certain FQHE states (notably ν=12/5).
predictions.prediction_citation: Freedman, Larsen, Wang 2002, Commun. Math. Phys. 227, 605
predictions.prediction: Fibonacci anyons support universal topological quantum computation via braiding alone.

predictions.description: Multiple platforms (superconducting qubits — Google Quantum AI 2021; neutral atoms — Harvard/MIT 2021; trapped ions — Quantinuum 2023) have prepared toric-code ground states and demonstrated anyonic excitations consistent with MTC predictions. The most stringent test — explicit braiding of e and m anyons to confirm mutual statistics — was achieved by Satzinger et al. (Google Quantum AI 2021) and refined in subsequent work.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kitaev 2003, Ann. Phys. 303, 2
predictions.confirmation_citation: Satzinger et al. (Google Quantum AI) 2021, Science 374, 1237; Bluvstein et al. (Harvard) 2022, Nature 604, 451; Iqbal et al. (Quantinuum) 2023, Nature 626, 505
predictions.prediction: Toric code Z_2 anyons (e, m, ε) realized in digital quantum simulation with measurable braiding signatures.

predictions.description: Moore-Read 1991 prediction: the ν=5/2 FQHE plateau hosts Ising-MTC anyons (Pfaffian or anti-Pfaffian state). Experimental status 2026: Willett interferometry experiments (2009-2013) and Banerjee thermal-Hall measurements (2018) provide partial evidence consistent with non-abelian Ising statistics. The PH-Pfaffian state (Son 2015) gives opposite chiral central charge and is favored by some thermal-conductance experiments. As of 2026 the correct topological state remains contested; the question is open whether Pfaffian, anti-Pfaffian, or PH-Pfaffian is realized.
predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Read 1991, Nucl. Phys. B 360, 362
predictions.prediction: Non-abelian Ising anyons realized in ν=5/2 FQHE with Pfaffian topological order.
predictions.confirmation_citation: Willett, Pfeiffer, West 2010, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. 107, 8853 (interferometry evidence); Banerjee et al. 2018, Nature 559, 205 (thermal Hall conductance, half-quantized — partial confirmation, contested by PH-Pfaffian interpretation)

predictions.description: Banerjee et al. (2017) reported half-quantized thermal Hall conductance in α-RuCl_3 under in-plane magnetic field, interpreted as evidence for Kitaev-honeycomb-model-like non-abelian spin liquid. Subsequent re-measurements (e.g., Czajka et al. 2021) showed the half-quantization is not universal across samples and may be phonon-thermal-Hall not topological. As of 2026 the spin-liquid identification in α-RuCl_3 is contested.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2
predictions.prediction: Z_2 spin liquid topological order (toric-code MTC at low temperatures) in α-RuCl_3.
predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2017, Nat. Mater. 17, 4 (initial evidence); Czajka et al. 2021, Nat. Phys. 17, 915 (counter-evidence: non-universality, phonon contribution) — observations contested

predictions.description: Read-Rezayi 1999 prediction: the ν=12/5 FQHE plateau hosts SU(2)_3-like MTC. Observed plateau (Xia et al. 2004) but no detailed topological characterization. Universal topological quantum computation accessible via braiding if the prediction holds.
predictions.prediction_citation: Read, Rezayi 1999, Phys. Rev. B 59, 8084
predictions.prediction: Read-Rezayi parafermion state at ν=12/5 FQHE.

predictions.description: Moore-Seiberg 1989 established the categorical-equivalence between rational 2D CFTs and MTCs. This is structurally a classification theorem about a pre-existing object (rational CFT, established since the 1970s minimal-model era) rather than a prediction of new phenomena. The MTC framework reorganizes and clarifies rational CFT structure but does not predict new rational CFTs to be observed. Retro-explanatory.
predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177
predictions.prediction: Moore-Seiberg classification: every rational 2D CFT has the algebraic data of an MTC.

predictions.description: Reshetikhin-Turaev 1991 construction produces a 3D TQFT from any MTC, yielding quantum invariants of 3-manifolds and links (the Jones polynomial generalizes to all MTCs). This is a mathematical confirmation rather than a physical experiment, but it's rigorously established — every MTC produces well-defined 3-manifold and link invariants, computable in polynomial time.
predictions.prediction_citation: Reshetikhin, Turaev 1991, Invent. Math. 103, 547
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bakalov, Kirillov 2001 (textbook proof)
predictions.prediction: Reshetikhin-Turaev TQFTs as 3-manifold invariants from MTC data.

formal_classifications.label: Generalized symmetries / SymTFT
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'generalized-symmetries' (structural; sector: Formal HE theory). Contains 17 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: 2D 0-form discrete symmetry. Canonical examples: Z_2 of Ising CFT, S_3 of 3-state Potts, Z_n of Z_n parafermions. Anomaly structure: H^3(G, U(1)) ≃ Ω_3^G classification.
realized_examples: Ising CFT Z_2 spin-flip symmetry (anomaly-free)
Tricritical Ising Z_2 × Z_2 (with mixed anomaly)
3-state Potts S_3 symmetry

cells.content: 2D 0-form non-invertible Tambara-Yamagami symmetry. Kramers-Wannier-duality-like fusion: D × D = 1 + Z + ... (D = duality defect, Z = group elements). The fusion is non-invertible: D² is NOT 1.
realized_examples: Kramers-Wannier duality of 2D Ising (D acts as the self-duality)
Z_n × Z_n duality category for the rank-2 toric-code-like phase
Verlinde-line dualities in c=1 free-boson CFT

cells.content: 2D 0-form general fusion-category symmetry (Verlinde lines and beyond). Topological defect lines (TDLs) in 2D CFT carry fusion-category structure C; non-invertible TDLs occur whenever C is not pointed.
realized_examples: Verlinde lines of 2D rational CFTs (Petkova-Zuber 2001)
Topological defect lines in tricritical Ising minimal model (Frolich-Fuchs-Runkel-Schweigert 2007)
Non-invertible defects in c=1 free-boson at irrational radius

cells.content: 3D 0-form discrete symmetry. Examples: Z_2 of 3D Ising CFT at criticality, time-reversal Z_2^T of (2+1)D topological insulators in the AII class.
realized_examples: 3D Ising critical point Z_2 (above the Wilson-Fisher fixed point)
Z_2 time-reversal of topological insulators (AII symmetry class)
Z_4 chiral symmetry of certain (2+1)D Dirac fermions

cells.content: 3D 1-form discrete symmetry acting on line operators. Canonical case: Z_N 1-form symmetry of U(1)_N Chern-Simons theory (acts on Wilson lines).
realized_examples: Z_2 × Z_2 1-form of 3D Z_2 toric code (the e-line and m-line are 1-form symmetry charges)
Z_N 1-form of U(1)_N Chern-Simons theory
Z_N 1-form of Z_N gauge theory in (2+1)D

cells.content: 4D 0-form continuous Lie-group symmetry. Examples: SU(N_f) chiral flavor symmetry of N_f-flavor QCD; U(1)_B baryon number; U(1)_em electromagnetic gauge (when treated as global at the boundary).
realized_examples: SU(3)_L × SU(3)_R chiral flavor of 3-flavor QCD
SU(N) flavor symmetry of large-N QCD
U(1)_B baryon number in SM
SU(2) custodial symmetry of SM electroweak sector

cells.content: 4D 1-form center symmetry of pure SU(N) Yang-Mills. The Z_N center acts on Wilson lines, classifying them by N-ality. Spontaneously broken in deconfined (high-T) phase; unbroken in confined (low-T) phase. Order parameter: Polyakov loop.
realized_examples: Z_N 1-form of pure SU(N) YM in 4D (canonical Komargodski-Sulejmanpasic-Unsal example)
Z_2 magnetic 1-form of free 4D Maxwell theory ('t Hooft line)
Z_n 1-form of 4D Z_n gauge theory

cells.content: 4D 0-form non-invertible symmetry from ABJ anomaly. The chiral U(1)_A of QED is anomalous and naively breaks; recast as a NON-INVERTIBLE discrete Z-valued (or rational) symmetry by gauging instanton number. Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022; Cordova-Ohmori 2022.
realized_examples: U(1)_A of massless QED — recast as a fractional / non-invertible symmetry
Chiral symmetry of massless QCD — partial non-invertible structure
Theta-shifts in 4D N=4 SYM viewed as non-invertible symmetry

cells.content: 4D 2-group structure mixing 0-form and 1-form symmetries via Postnikov class. Canonical example: axion-photon system where the axion shift symmetry (0-form U(1)) mixes with the photon 1-form Z by anomaly inflow.
realized_examples: Axion-photon system (the canonical 4D 2-group)
Theta-term-shift mixing with electromagnetic 1-form in 4D
Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2-group of 4D N=2 theories

cells.content: 4D 2-form discrete symmetry acting on 2D defects. Generators are codimension-3 in 4D = 1D in bulk SymTFT. Examples: 2-form of free 2-form gauge theory in 4D; emergent 2-form in certain (3+1)D phases.
realized_examples: Free 2-form gauge theory in 4D (Bantay-Vafa-like models)
Emergent 2-form symmetry in (3+1)D Z_2 topological order

cells.content: 6D 2-form center symmetry of the (2,0) theory. The 6D N=(2,0) SCFT has 2-form symmetry corresponding to the center of the gauge group (A_n, D_n, E_6,7,8 types). Acts on M2-brane-like 2D surface operators. Canonical SymTFT example linking 6D dynamics to 7D Chern-Simons-like invertible TFT.
realized_examples: 6D N=(2,0) theory of type A_N (= N M5-branes) has Z_N 2-form symmetry
6D (2,0) of type D_n has Z_2 2-form symmetry
6D (2,0) of type E_n has trivial 2-form symmetry

cells.content: 3D 1-form non-invertible symmetry. Acts on line operators with fusion-category structure (no group inverse). Canonical: certain Chern-Simons-matter theories where dualities yield non-invertible 1-form structure.
realized_examples: Certain 3D N=4 quiver gauge theories with non-trivial duality structure
3D ABJM at level k with non-invertible defect symmetries

cells.content: 5D 0-form discrete symmetry. 5D theories (e.g., 5D N=1 SCFTs from M-theory on Calabi-Yau singularities) carry intricate global symmetry structure including discrete 0-form factors classified by Ω_5^{SO × BG}.
realized_examples: 5D N=1 E_n SCFTs from M-theory on del Pezzo surfaces
5D Seiberg theories with various flavor symmetries

cells.content: 2D 1-form symmetry: acts on 1-dim defects (= boundaries in 2D bulk). For 2D theories with boundaries, 1-form symmetry classifies boundary state structure; for closed 2D theories, the 1-form acts trivially on the empty surface — the symmetry is structurally vacuous in the closed-spacetime sector.
realized_examples: Boundary classification of 2D Ising under Z_2 1-form (boundary states organized by Z_2)

cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded

predictions.prediction: 't Hooft anomaly matching for 4D pure SU(N) YM Z_N 1-form symmetry rules out trivially-gapped intermediate phases.
predictions.description: Komargodski-Sulejmanpasic-Unsal 2017 used the mixed 't Hooft anomaly between 1-form center symmetry and theta-angle shifts to rule out trivially-gapped phases at theta=pi in 4D YM. Confirmed via lattice gauge theory studies (Kitano-Suyama-Yamazaki 2020 and later refinements) and analytical arguments.
predictions.prediction_citation: Komargodski, Sulejmanpasic, Unsal 2017, Phys. Rev. B 97, 054418
predictions.confirmation_citation: Kitano, Suyama, Yamazaki 2020, JHEP 09, 197 (lattice confirmation of theta=pi structure)

predictions.prediction: Non-invertible discrete symmetry replaces ABJ-anomalous U(1)_A in massless QED.
predictions.description: Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022 and Cordova-Ohmori 2022 established that the would-be U(1)_A symmetry of massless QED, lost to the ABJ anomaly under ordinary symmetry analysis, can be reorganized as a non-invertible Z-valued (or rational) symmetry after gauging instanton number. This is a 'recast' rather than a new physical prediction — the ABJ anomaly itself is decades-old confirmed physics — but the structural reinterpretation has been independently verified by multiple groups and is now standard.
predictions.prediction_citation: Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 161601
predictions.confirmation_citation: Cordova, Ohmori 2022, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011034 (independent verification with refined framework)

predictions.prediction: 6D (2,0) theory's 2-form center symmetry has explicit cobordism-classified anomaly.
predictions.description: Cordova-Lam 2020 derived the 2-form anomaly of 6D N=(2,0) theory via cobordism, showing the anomaly is fully captured by a 7D invertible TFT — the canonical SymTFT example for a non-Lagrangian QFT. Subsequently extended by Apruzzi et al. (2023) to derive the 6D (2,0) SymTFT directly from string theory M-theory geometry.
predictions.prediction_citation: Cordova, Lam 2020, arXiv:2003.04579
predictions.confirmation_citation: Apruzzi, Bonetti, Garcia, Saxena, Heckman, Schafer-Nameki 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895

predictions.prediction: Verlinde topological defect lines in 2D rational CFT have categorical symmetry structure.
predictions.description: Petkova-Zuber 2001 introduced the Verlinde lines; Frohlich-Fuchs-Runkel-Schweigert 2007 established their full categorical structure. Lattice realization (Aasen-Mong-Fendley 2016) confirms the categorical-symmetry action experimentally accessible in 2D classical-statistical lattice models.
predictions.prediction_citation: Petkova, Zuber 2001, Phys. Lett. B 504, 157
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aasen, Mong, Fendley 2016, J. Phys. A 49, 354001 (lattice realization)

predictions.prediction: Categorical / non-invertible symmetries exist in real (3+1)D quantum materials.
predictions.description: The SymTFT framework predicts that categorical / non-invertible symmetry structures should be detectable in (2+1)D and (3+1)D quantum materials with appropriate topological order. As of 2026, no laboratory realization has cleanly identified a non-invertible symmetry beyond the (well-established) toric-code and Kitaev-honeycomb realizations of MTC-style data. Active experimental search direction.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bhardwaj et al. 2023, Nucl. Phys. B 990, 116167 (review predicting material realizations)

predictions.prediction: Higher-form symmetry breaking patterns predict new universality classes for (2+1)D quantum phase transitions.
predictions.description: Lake-Hermele 2018 and subsequent work predict that 1-form symmetry breaking in (2+1)D leads to universality classes distinct from ordinary Landau-Ginzburg patterns. The Higgs / confinement transition of (2+1)D Z_2 gauge theory is the simplest case. Experimental confirmation in quantum spin liquid candidates is ongoing.
predictions.prediction_citation: Lake, Hermele 2018, Phys. Rev. B 98, 134418

predictions.prediction: Standard Model has hidden non-invertible symmetries from anomalous discrete subgroups.
predictions.description: Several authors (Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022; subsequent extensions) have argued that the SM may carry non-invertible symmetry structure from anomalous discrete subgroups of its continuous chiral symmetries. No phenomenological consequence has been derived that is testable beyond existing SM physics; this remains a structural prediction.
predictions.prediction_citation: Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 161601 (closing section on SM application)

formal_classifications.label: Higgs/Coulomb branches of N=2 SCFTs
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'higgs-coulomb-branches' (structural; sector: Formal HE theory). Contains 18 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 flavors at the conformal point. Coulomb branch is 1D, parameterized by u = ⟨Tr(φ²)⟩. The Seiberg-Witten curve is the canonical example: y² = (x − u)(x² − Λ²) deformed by N_f=4 flavor masses, which at the conformal point is the original Seiberg-Witten SU(2) Nf=4 curve.
realized_examples: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 fundamental flavors (Seiberg-Witten 1994)
Tested via Lagrangian construction; canonical worked example

cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 flavors: Higgs branch is the symmetric product Sym²(C²)/Z_2, of complex dim 4 / 2 = 2 in some parameterization; the hyperkähler quaternionic dimension equals 3 for the full SO(8) flavor structure. Equivalently, the Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of so(8) at this conformal point.
realized_examples: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 (the same theory as the Coulomb-branch cell — SCFTs have both branches)
Realized as a Nakajima quiver variety for the affine D_4 Dynkin diagram

cells.content: A_1 (or H_0) Argyres-Douglas theory: rank-1 Coulomb branch with singular point at the origin where the SU(2) SQCD theory with N_f=1 develops mutually non-local light states (an electric monopole and a magnetic monopole simultaneously massless). Non-Lagrangian SCFT.
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas H_0 = simplest AD theory
Singular point on the SU(2) N_f=1 Coulomb branch
Coulomb-branch operator with non-integer scaling dimension Δ = 6/5

cells.content: A_1 Argyres-Douglas H_0: no Higgs branch (rank 0). The theory has no flavor symmetry that could parameterize a hyperkähler moduli space. This is a generic feature of the simplest AD theories: they have non-trivial Coulomb branches but trivial Higgs branches.
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas H_0 has trivial flavor sector

cells.content: E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: rank-1 Coulomb branch with single Coulomb-branch operator of scaling dimension Δ = 3. The Seiberg-Witten curve is a genus-1 elliptic fibration over the Coulomb branch with E_6 monodromy structure.
realized_examples: E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996 (the canonical rank-1 SCFT with exceptional flavor symmetry)
Realized as the theory living on a single M5-brane probe of an E_6 singularity

cells.content: E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_6, of complex dimension 22 (quaternionic dimension 11). Hyperkähler manifold with E_6 isometry; one of the canonical 'symplectic singularities' in the Beauville-Bogomolov classification.
realized_examples: Minimal nilpotent orbit O_min(e_6), the closure of the highest-root orbit
Realized as a hyperkähler cone with E_6 holomorphic isometry

cells.content: E_7 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_7, of complex dimension 34 (quaternionic dim 17).
realized_examples: Minimal nilpotent orbit O_min(e_7)

cells.content: E_8 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_8, of complex dim 58 (quaternionic dim 29).
realized_examples: Minimal nilpotent orbit O_min(e_8) — the largest exceptional example

cells.content: T_3 theory (= class-S type A_2 on 3-punctured sphere): rank-2 Coulomb branch with 2 Coulomb-branch operators of dimensions (2, 3). The simplest non-Lagrangian class-S theory; equivalent to E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky in its rank-1 limit but here treated as the parent rank-2 class-S construction.
realized_examples: T_3 = T[A_2] theory on 3-punctured sphere
Realized via M5-brane construction on the 3-punctured sphere with maximal regular punctures

cells.content: T_3 = T[A_2] theory: Higgs branch is a known hyperkähler manifold of complex dim 10 (quaternionic dim 5), realized as a moment map / quiver variety.
realized_examples: T_3 Higgs branch as moment map level set for SU(3)³ flavor symmetry

cells.content: N=4 SYM with SU(2) gauge group: Coulomb branch is 1D, parameterized by u = ⟨Tr(φ²)⟩. The Seiberg-Witten curve is trivial (Λ → 0 in the SU(2) Nf=4 family); the prepotential is exactly classical. The full N=4 SCFT has a U-duality structure (SL(2,Z)) acting on coupling τ.
realized_examples: N=4 SU(2) SYM in the gauge-conformal phase
Mass-deformation to SU(2) Nf=4 recovers Seiberg-Witten

cells.content: N=4 SU(2) SYM: Higgs branch is the symmetric product Sym²(C²)/Z_2, which equals the minimal nilpotent orbit of sl(2) (closed up to closure). Hyperkähler quotient of C² × C² / Z_2 by the diagonal SU(2). Quaternionic dim = 3.
realized_examples: N=4 SU(2) Higgs branch as 2-instanton moduli space
Mirror to the Coulomb branch in the 3D N=4 reduction

cells.content: N=2* mass-deformation of N=4 SU(N) SYM: mixed branch structure where the mass parameter m interpolates between N=4 (m=0) and pure N=2 SYM (m→∞). Both Higgs and Coulomb branches are non-trivial; their intersection captures the mass-deformation dynamics.
realized_examples: N=2* SU(N) SYM with adjoint hypermultiplet mass m
Continuum interpolation between N=4 and pure-N=2

cells.content: Free N=2 vector multiplet (rank 1): Coulomb branch is the complex plane C, parameterized by the vev of the free scalar φ. No interactions; Seiberg-Witten prepotential is classical kinetic term only.
realized_examples: Single free N=2 vector multiplet
The U(1) case of N=2 abelian gauge theory

cells.content: Free N=2 hypermultiplet (rank 1): Higgs branch is the quaternionic plane H = C², parameterized by the two complex scalars of the hyper. No interactions.
realized_examples: Single free N=2 hypermultiplet

cells.content: Class-S theory of D-type, e.g., T[D_3] = SO(6) on a 3-punctured sphere: rank-3 Coulomb branch with 3 Coulomb-branch operators of specific dimensions.
realized_examples: T[D_3] on 3-punctured sphere
Realized via M5-brane construction with Z_2 orbifold action

cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded

predictions.prediction: Schur sector of any 4D N=2 SCFT is a 2D vertex operator algebra (SCFT/VOA correspondence).
predictions.description: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli 2015 established that every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra (VOA) capturing its Schur sector. This is a categorical equivalence at the level of the Schur sector. Extensively confirmed for specific theory families (N=4 SYM, class-S, Argyres-Douglas) by independent computations of the VOAs.
predictions.prediction_citation: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359
predictions.confirmation_citation: Lemos 2024, arXiv:2401.xxx (review of 4D-2D dictionary, summarizing extensive subsequent computations)

predictions.prediction: Argyres-Douglas theories arise at singular points of Coulomb branches where mutually non-local BPS states become simultaneously massless.
predictions.description: Argyres-Douglas 1995 predicted and constructed the H_n series of non-Lagrangian SCFTs as IR theories at specific Coulomb-branch singular points. Subsequent extensions (Argyres-Plesser-Seiberg-Witten 1996; Argyres-Lotito-Lu-Martone 2018) completed the rank-1 and rank-2 classifications. Confirmed via independent calculations of operator dimensions matching the predicted fractional values.
predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Douglas 1995, Nucl. Phys. B 448, 93
predictions.confirmation_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lu, Martone 2018, JHEP 02, 002 (complete rank-1 classification)

predictions.prediction: Higgs branches of rank-1 Minahan-Nemeschansky theories are minimal nilpotent orbits of e_{6,7,8}.
predictions.description: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996, 1997 predicted that the Higgs branches of the rank-1 E_{6,7,8} SCFTs are exactly the minimal nilpotent orbits of the exceptional Lie algebras, of complex dimensions 22, 34, 58 respectively. Confirmed by direct moment-map computation and by the matching of flavor-current OPE coefficients to the orbit-geometry data.
predictions.prediction_citation: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, Nucl. Phys. B 482, 142; 1997, Nucl. Phys. B 489, 24
predictions.confirmation_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lu, Martone 2018, JHEP 02, 002 (independent rank-1 classification confirming the M-N predictions); Beem, Rastelli 2018, JHEP 01, 026 (chiral algebra confirmation of E_{6,7,8} Higgs branches)

predictions.prediction: 3D N=4 mirror symmetry exchanges Higgs and Coulomb branches.
predictions.description: Intriligator-Seiberg 1996 predicted that 3D N=4 SCFTs (obtained by reducing 4D N=2 SCFTs on a circle) come in mirror pairs where the Higgs and Coulomb branches swap. Confirmed extensively for quiver gauge theories (Hanany-Witten brane realization), abelian gauge theories, and class-S reductions. Provides quantitative checks at the level of moment-map and Coulomb-branch formula matching.
predictions.prediction_citation: Intriligator, Seiberg 1996, Phys. Lett. B 387, 513
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bullimore, Dimofte, Gaiotto 2017, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 21, 1003 (rigorous Coulomb-branch formula)

predictions.prediction: Coulomb branches of 3D N=4 theories are quantized symplectic singularities with explicit operator algebras.
predictions.description: Bullimore-Dimofte-Gaiotto 2017 provided the rigorous Coulomb-branch formula for 3D N=4 gauge theories, expressing the Coulomb branch as a quantized symplectic singularity with explicit generators and relations. Confirmed by independent mathematical-physics computations (Braverman-Finkelberg-Nakajima) and by matching to Hilbert series of known examples.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bullimore, Dimofte, Gaiotto 2017, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 21, 1003
predictions.confirmation_citation: Braverman, Finkelberg, Nakajima 2019, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 23, 75 (mathematical confirmation)

predictions.prediction: Complete classification of rank-1 4D N=2 SCFTs.
predictions.description: Argyres-Lotito-Lu-Martone 2018 completed the classification of all rank-1 4D N=2 SCFTs by systematic Coulomb-branch geometry analysis. The result: 16 distinct theories (Lagrangian SU(2) Nf series, AD H_n series, Minahan-Nemeschansky E_{6,7,8}, plus several others). Confirmed by independent checks via Higgs-branch and SCFT/VOA computations.
predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lu, Martone 2018, JHEP 02, 002
predictions.confirmation_citation: Caorsi, Cecotti 2018, JHEP 11, 052 (independent classification of rank-1 N=2 SCFTs via different methods); Argyres, Martone 2020, JHEP 06, 044 (rank-2 extension corroborating rank-1 completeness)

predictions.prediction: Higher-rank class-S theories admit complete Higgs-branch classifications via affine Grassmannian / nilpotent orbit data.
predictions.description: Predicted that class-S theories of type ADE at arbitrary rank have Higgs branches classifiable via affine Grassmannian slices and nilpotent orbits (Moore-Tachikawa 2012; Cremonesi-Hanany-Mekareeya-Zaffaroni 2014). For low rank this is established; for general higher rank the classification is partial and active research.
predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Tachikawa 2012, Surveys in Differential Geometry 18, 129

formal_classifications.label: Hadronic states
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'hadronic-states' (phenomenon; sector: Hadronic). Contains 20 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Light pseudoscalar mesons (J^P^C = 0-+): the SU(3) flavor octet π, K, η plus singlet η'.
realized_examples: K+ (494 MeV), K0 (498 MeV), K- (494 MeV), K0-bar (498 MeV)
η (548 MeV), η' (958 MeV)

cells.content: Light vector mesons (J^P^C = 1--): ρ, ω, K*, φ — the SU(3) flavor nonet.
realized_examples: ω (783 MeV)
φ (1020 MeV)

cells.content: Light tensor mesons (J^P^C = 2++): a_2, f_2, K_2*, f_2'.
Lowest-lying baryon octet (J^P = 1/2+): N (nucleon doublet), Λ, Σ, Ξ — the SU(3) flavor octet of spin-1/2 baryons.
realized_examples: proton p (938.3 MeV), neutron n (939.6 MeV)
Λ (1116 MeV)

cells.content: Lowest-lying baryon decuplet (J^P = 3/2+): Δ, Σ*, Ξ*, Ω — the SU(3) flavor decuplet.
J/ψ and other charmonium 1-- states: c-cbar bound states with J^P^C = 1--.
realized_examples: J/ψ (3097 MeV) — the canonical charmonium discovery (1974)
ψ(4040), ψ(4160), ψ(4415) — higher charmonium states

cells.content: Υ and other bottomonium 1-- states: b-bbar bound states with J^P^C = 1--.
realized_examples: Υ(1S) (9460 MeV) — bottomonium ground state, discovered 1977

cells.content: Charm-baryon 1/2+ states: Λ_c, Σ_c, Ξ_c, Ω_c.
Bottom-baryon 1/2+ states: Λ_b, Σ_b, Ξ_b, Ω_b.
Doubly-charm baryon: Ξ_cc++ (3621 MeV), confirmed by LHCb 2017. The first doubly-heavy baryon. Doubly-bottom baryons Ξ_bb, Ω_bb predicted at ~10 GeV but not yet observed.
realized_examples: Ξ_cc++ (3621 MeV) — observed LHCb 2017
Ξ_bc, Ξ_bb, Ω_bb, B_c+ family — predicted but unobserved at various masses

cells.content: X(3872): the first confirmed exotic tetraquark candidate. Discovered Belle 2003. Mass 3872 MeV, J^P^C = 1++, narrow width.
realized_examples: X(3872) — Belle 2003, confirmed by BaBar, CDF, D0, LHCb, CMS

cells.content: Hidden-charm pentaquarks: P_c(4380), P_c(4312), P_c(4440), P_c(4457). Discovered LHCb 2015 and 2019 in Λ_b decays.
realized_examples: P_c(4312), P_c(4440), P_c(4457) — LHCb 2019 (refined spectroscopy)
P_cs(4459) — strange pentaquark, LHCb 2020

cells.content: Charged charmonium / bottomonium-like tetraquark states: Z_c(3900), Z_c(4020), Z_b(10610), Z_b(10650). Definitively non-standard (charged charmonium implies tetraquark content).
realized_examples: Z_c(3900) — BESIII / Belle 2013
Z_c(4020) — BESIII 2013

cells.content: Lowest scalar glueball: lattice QCD predicts mass ≈ 1500-1700 MeV with J^P^C = 0++. Mendeleev-style empty cell — predicted but not unambiguously identified. Candidate states f_0(1500) and f_0(1710) have significant glueball admixture but mix with conventional q-qbar states.
realized_examples: f_0(1500) — leading glueball candidate (disputed)
f_0(1710) — alternative glueball candidate (disputed)
Lattice prediction: m ≈ 1730 MeV (Morningstar-Peardon 1999), 1500 MeV (recent SU(3) lattice)

cells.content: Light 1-+ hybrid meson: predicted by lattice QCD at ≈ 1.8-2.0 GeV. The 1-+ J^P^C is exotic (forbidden for pure q-qbar mesons), so observation of any 1-+ light meson is direct evidence of hybrid content. Candidates π_1(1400) and π_1(1600) exist with disputed interpretations; η_1(1855) discovered by BESIII 2022 is the cleanest candidate to date.
realized_examples: π_1(1400) — early candidate, contested
π_1(1600) — better-established candidate, hybrid interpretation favored but not universal
η_1(1855) — BESIII 2022 discovery, isoscalar hybrid candidate

cells.content: d*(2380) dibaryon candidate: a 6-quark state with quantum numbers I(J^P) = 0(3+), mass ≈ 2380 MeV, observed by WASA-at-COSY 2014. The first dibaryon resonance with credible experimental support. Status: partial — observed by one collaboration with consistent signal across multiple decay modes; awaits independent confirmation at higher statistics.
realized_examples: d*(2380) — WASA-at-COSY 2014, mass 2380 MeV, width 70 MeV

cells.content: T_cc+(3875): doubly-charm tetraquark with quark content ccūd̄, J^P=1+, observed by LHCb 2021. The first confirmed doubly-heavy tetraquark and an unambiguously exotic state.
realized_examples: T_cc+(3875) — LHCb 2021/2022, mass 3875 MeV, very narrow width

cells.content: Predicted doubly-bottom tetraquark T_bb (bbū-d̄, J^P=1+) at mass ≈ 10.4 GeV. Predicted to be stable against strong decay (Karliner-Rosner 2017; Eichten-Quigg 2017). Mendeleev empty cell — strong theoretical prediction, no experimental observation as of 2026.
realized_examples: T_bb (bbū-d̄) — predicted at m ≈ 10.4 GeV, J^P = 1+
T_bbb-bar — predicted higher-mass extension

cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded

predictions.prediction: Ω- baryon (sss, J^P=3/2+) predicted at mass ≈ 1685 MeV by Gell-Mann's SU(3) eightfold-way classification.
predictions.description: Gell-Mann 1962 predicted the Ω- baryon as the missing member of the baryon decuplet, with specific mass ≈ 1685 MeV and quantum numbers I(J^P)=0(3/2+). Discovered at Brookhaven in 1964 at mass 1672 MeV — the cleanest Mendeleev-style hadronic prediction confirmation. The 1% mass-prediction accuracy established SU(3) flavor symmetry as a real organizing principle of hadrons.
predictions.prediction_citation: Gell-Mann 1962, Phys. Rev. 125, 1067
predictions.confirmation_citation: Barnes et al. 1964, Phys. Rev. Lett. 12, 204

predictions.prediction: Charmonium spectrum (J/ψ, ψ(2S), χ_c states) at GeV-scale masses predicted by GIM mechanism + quark-model arguments.
predictions.description: Glashow-Iliopoulos-Maiani 1970 introduced the charm quark to suppress flavor-changing neutral currents; subsequent quark-model calculations predicted a 1-- c-cbar state at ~3 GeV. J/ψ discovery in November 1974 confirmed this and triggered the 'November Revolution'. The full charmonium spectrum was subsequently mapped out, matching Cornell-potential predictions.
predictions.prediction_citation: Glashow, Iliopoulos, Maiani 1970, Phys. Rev. D 2, 1285 (GIM mechanism predicting charm)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aubert et al. 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 33, 1404 (J/psi discovery)

predictions.prediction: Pentaquark states with hidden-charm quark content at ~4.3-4.5 GeV.
predictions.description: Various pentaquark predictions emerged from 2003 onwards; the diquark model (Maiani-Polosa-Riquer 2015) predicted hidden-charm pentaquark states in the 4.3-4.5 GeV range. LHCb discovery in 2015 (P_c(4380), P_c(4450)) and refined spectrum in 2019 (P_c(4312), P_c(4440), P_c(4457)) confirmed the hidden-charm pentaquark sector after decades of failed searches for light pentaquarks (notably the false Θ+ claim of 2003).
predictions.prediction_citation: Maiani, Polosa, Riquer 2015, Phys. Lett. B 749, 289
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aaij et al. (LHCb) 2015, Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 072001

predictions.prediction: Doubly-charm tetraquark T_cc+ at mass ≈ 3875 MeV, stable or near-stable.
predictions.description: Karliner-Rosner 2017 and Eichten-Quigg 2017 (independent calculations) predicted the doubly-charm tetraquark T_cc+ (cc-ūd̄) just below the DD* threshold with J^P=1+. LHCb 2021/2022 confirmed T_cc+(3875) with mass and width matching predictions, establishing the doubly-heavy-tetraquark family.
predictions.prediction_citation: Karliner, Rosner 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 202001
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aaij et al. (LHCb) 2022, Nat. Commun. 13, 3351

predictions.prediction: Lowest scalar glueball at mass ≈ 1500-1700 MeV with J^P^C=0++.
predictions.description: Lattice QCD predicts a scalar glueball at this mass range (Morningstar-Peardon 1999 quenched-lattice; more recent unquenched lattice studies). Candidates f_0(1500) and f_0(1710) have significant glueball-q-qbar mixing; no consensus has emerged on which is dominantly the glueball, and some authors argue the glueball is split across both. Active experimental search (GlueX, LHCb) and theoretical refinement (unquenched lattice) ongoing. Tension: prediction is sharp; identification is disputed.
predictions.prediction_citation: Morningstar, Peardon 1999, Phys. Rev. D 60, 034509
predictions.confirmation_citation: Klempt, Zaitsev 2007, Phys. Rep. 454, 1 (review of disputed identification)

predictions.prediction: Doubly-bottom tetraquark T_bb (bb-ūd̄, J^P=1+) at mass ≈ 10.4 GeV, stable against strong decay (decays only weakly).
predictions.description: Karliner-Rosner 2017 and Eichten-Quigg 2017 predicted T_bb to lie below the BB* threshold and thus be STABLE against strong decay. Would decay only weakly with macroscopic lifetime — observation would be one of the cleanest exotic-hadron confirmations possible. No experimental detection as of 2026 despite searches at LHCb and Belle II. Mass and structure predictions are sharp; null result thus far is significant but not yet decisive.
predictions.prediction_citation: Karliner, Rosner 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 202001

predictions.prediction: H-dibaryon (uuddss, J^P=0+) bound state with mass below 2 m(Λ).
predictions.description: Jaffe 1977 predicted an H-dibaryon stable against strong decay with mass below 2 m(Λ) ≈ 2230 MeV. Extensively searched in nuclear-physics experiments through the 1980s-2000s; no clear signal. Modern lattice-QCD calculations (with realistic quark masses) find the H-dibaryon is unbound or only weakly bound above 2 m(Λ). Status: falsified for the original prediction (deeply bound H below threshold); refined predictions of a near-threshold state remain open.
predictions.prediction_citation: Jaffe 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 38, 195
predictions.confirmation_citation: Inoue et al. (HAL QCD) 2012, Nucl. Phys. A 881, 28 (lattice study finding H near or above threshold)

predictions.prediction: Light hybrid meson with exotic J^P^C=1-+ at mass ≈ 1.8-2.0 GeV.
predictions.description: Lattice QCD predicts light 1-+ hybrid mesons at this mass. Candidates π_1(1400), π_1(1600), and most recently η_1(1855) (BESIII 2022) have been identified but hybrid interpretation remains contested. The exotic quantum numbers 1-+ rule out pure q-qbar interpretation; whether the candidates are hybrids, tetraquarks, or molecular states remains debated. Active analysis at GlueX (JLab) and BESIII.
predictions.prediction_citation: Lacock, Michael, Boyle, Rowland (UKQCD) 1996, Phys. Lett. B 401, 308
predictions.confirmation_citation: Ablikim et al. (BESIII) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 192002 (eta1(1855) discovery — interpretation contested)

predictions.prediction: Doubly-bottom baryon Ξ_bb at mass ≈ 10 GeV.
predictions.description: Quark-model and lattice-QCD calculations predict doubly-bottom baryons at mass ~10 GeV (Ξ_bb at ~10.2 GeV, Ω_bb at ~10.3 GeV). Ξ_cc++ has been confirmed (LHCb 2017); the bb analogs remain unobserved despite searches. Production cross-section at LHC is small but within reach of HL-LHC luminosities. Expected to be observed within the next decade.
predictions.prediction_citation: Karliner, Rosner 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 094007

formal_classifications.label: Fractional quantum Hall states
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'fractional-quantum-hall-states' (phenomenon; sector: Condensed matter). Contains 19 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: ν=1/3 Laughlin state. Hall conductance σ_xy = (1/3)e²/h. Quasiparticles have fractional charge e/3 with abelian fractional statistics (exchange phase 2π/3). Ground-state degeneracy 3 on torus. The foundational FQH state — Tsui-Stormer-Gossard 1982 discovery; Laughlin 1983 theory.
realized_examples: ν=1/3 in GaAs heterostructures — original 1982 discovery (Tsui, Stormer, Gossard)
ν=1/3 in graphene (Du et al. 2009; Bolotin et al. 2009)
ν=1/3 in ZnO/MgZnO 2DEG

cells.content: ν=1/5 Laughlin state. Quasiparticle charge e/5; abelian statistics with phase 2π/5. Ground-state degeneracy 5 on torus.
realized_examples: ν=1/5 in high-mobility GaAs (Pan et al. 2002)

cells.content: ν=2/5 Jain composite-fermion state. The first 'non-Laughlin' Jain state. Quasiparticle charge e/5. Abelian statistics.
realized_examples: ν=2/5 in GaAs (Willett et al. 1987)

cells.content: ν=3/7 Jain state. CF filling 3.
realized_examples: ν=3/7 in GaAs (Pan et al. 2003)

cells.content: ν=4/9 Jain state. CF filling 4. Approaching the half-filled Landau level limit.
realized_examples: ν=4/9 in GaAs (Pan et al. 2003)

cells.content: ν=2/3 particle-hole conjugate of ν=1/3. Same anyon content as 1/3 by PH symmetry within the LLL.
realized_examples: ν=2/3 in GaAs (early FQH experiments)

cells.content: ν=5/2 Moore-Read Pfaffian candidate. Half-filled second Landau level with predicted non-abelian Ising anyon quasiparticles. Pfaffian wavefunction Pf(1/(z_i - z_j)) times Laughlin factor. Universal TQC NOT supported by Ising anyons alone (requires supplementation).
realized_examples: ν=5/2 plateau in highest-mobility GaAs heterostructures (Willett et al. 1987)
Specific Pfaffian-state identification: contested (Pfaffian vs anti-Pfaffian vs PH-Pfaffian)
