cells.content: ν=5/2 anti-Pfaffian alternative. Particle-hole conjugate of Moore-Read Pfaffian within the second Landau level. Different anyonic content (anti-Ising statistics) from original Pfaffian. One of three competing candidates for the 5/2 state.
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction (Levin-Halperin-Rosenow 2007; Lee-Ryu-Nayak-Fisher 2007)
Not unambiguously identified experimentally; competes with Pfaffian and PH-Pfaffian

cells.content: ν=5/2 PH-Pfaffian candidate. Particle-hole-symmetric pairing pattern proposed by Son 2015 in the composite-Dirac-fermion framework. Distinct anyon content from Pfaffian and anti-Pfaffian. Thermal Hall measurements (Banerjee 2018) initially appeared consistent with PH-Pfaffian; later analyses dispute this.
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction (Son 2015)
Possibly favored by thermal Hall data (Banerjee et al. 2018) but disputed

cells.content: ν=12/5 Read-Rezayi state with Fibonacci anyons. Would support UNIVERSAL topological quantum computation by braiding alone. The only proposed FQH state with Fibonacci anyons; observation would be a landmark.
realized_examples: ν=12/5 plateau observed in highest-mobility GaAs (Xia et al. 2004; Choi et al. 2008)
Identification as Read-Rezayi state: partial evidence (numerical match), not unambiguously confirmed
Particle-hole conjugate at ν=13/5 also observed

cells.content: ν=7/2 Moore-Read Pfaffian analog. Half-filled third Landau level. Less studied than 5/2; the analog non-abelian Ising-anyon state in the third LL.
realized_examples: ν=7/2 plateau observed (less mobile samples)

cells.content: ν=8/3 CF state. In the second Landau level. Particle-hole conjugate of 7/3 (also a Jain state).
realized_examples: ν=8/3 in GaAs second LL (Pan et al. 1999)

cells.content: ν=4/11 unconventional state. Predicted via second-generation Jain hierarchy (composite-fermion-of-composite-fermion); observed at higher mobility. Partial identification.
realized_examples: ν=4/11 plateau observed (Pan et al. 2003) — robust but identification debated

cells.content: ν=7/5 Read-Rezayi Z_4 parafermion candidate. Particle-hole conjugate of 12/5 within the LLL. PREDICTED Fibonacci-like or Z_4-parafermion anyons. NOT confirmed.
realized_examples: ν=7/5 plateau weakly observed (less robust than 12/5)
Theoretical Read-Rezayi prediction not confirmed by experiment

cells.content: ν=3/8 bosonic hierarchy state. Less robust; observed in highest-mobility samples.
realized_examples: ν=3/8 weak plateau (Pan et al.)

cells.content: Predicted Fibonacci-anyon state at unconventional ν. Mendeleev empty cell. Various theoretical proposals for FQH states supporting Fibonacci anyons beyond 12/5; observation would enable universal TQC.
realized_examples: No experimental observation of Fibonacci anyons at any ν as of 2026
Predicted at multiple specific ν values in various theoretical proposals

cells.content: ν=1/9 Laughlin state. Very fragile (extremely small gap); observed only in highest-mobility specimens.
realized_examples: ν=1/9 weakly observed in cleanest samples

cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded

predictions.prediction: Quasiparticle fractional charge e/m at Laughlin ν=1/m states.
predictions.description: Laughlin 1983 predicted that quasiparticles at ν=1/m carry fractional charge e/m. Confirmed by shot-noise measurements at ν=1/3 (Saminadayar et al. 1997, e/3) and ν=1/5 (Reznikov et al. 1999, e/5). One of the most direct confirmations of FQH theory.
predictions.prediction_citation: Laughlin 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1395
predictions.confirmation_citation: Saminadayar, Glattli, Jin, Etienne 1997, Phys. Rev. Lett. 79, 2526

predictions.prediction: Composite-fermion construction explains the Jain sequence ν=p/(2p±1).
predictions.description: Jain 1989 predicted that flux attachment maps FQH at ν=p/(2p+1) to integer QH of composite fermions at filling p. Confirmed by observation of the full series ν=2/5, 3/7, 4/9, 5/11, ... and by detection of composite fermions via Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations near ν=1/2 (Du, Stormer 1993; Kang, Stormer 1993).
predictions.prediction_citation: Jain 1989, Phys. Rev. Lett. 63, 199
predictions.confirmation_citation: Kang, Stormer, Pfeiffer, Baldwin, West 1993, Phys. Rev. Lett. 71, 3850 (composite-fermion direct detection)

predictions.prediction: Anyonic exchange statistics directly measurable via interferometry.
predictions.description: Predicted that braiding quasiparticles around each other yields fractional exchange phases (Laughlin 1983, theoretical). Direct interferometric measurement: Bartolomei et al. 2020 (Nature) at ν=1/3 measured fractional statistics phase 2π/3; Nakamura et al. 2020 (Nature Physics) independently confirmed. Established that FQH anyons are real, not just an effective description.
predictions.prediction_citation: Laughlin 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1395
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bartolomei et al. 2020, Science 368, 173; Nakamura et al. 2020, Nature Physics 16, 931

predictions.prediction: Non-abelian Ising anyons exist at ν=5/2.
predictions.description: Moore-Read 1991 predicted Ising-type non-abelian anyons at ν=5/2 (the Pfaffian state). The 5/2 plateau is firmly observed; whether its topological order is Moore-Read Pfaffian, anti-Pfaffian, or PH-Pfaffian remains contested. Thermal Hall measurement (Banerjee 2018) gave κ_xy ≈ 2.5(πk_B²T/6ℏ) — consistent with PH-Pfaffian — but interpretation is disputed (Simon 2020; Yan 2022). Status: tension.
predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Read 1991, Nucl. Phys. B 360, 362
predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2018, Nature 559, 205 (thermal Hall consistent with PH-Pfaffian; interpretation disputed)

predictions.prediction: Read-Rezayi Z_3 parafermion state with Fibonacci anyons at ν=12/5.
predictions.description: Read-Rezayi 1999 predicted the ν=12/5 state to be a Z_3 parafermion phase supporting Fibonacci anyons (which enable universal TQC). The 12/5 plateau is observed (Xia et al. 2004); numerical exact-diagonalization studies favor RR identification (Bonderson-Slingerland 2008); experimental confirmation via Fibonacci-anyon-specific signatures has NOT been achieved. Status: tension — strong theoretical support, no decisive experiment yet.
predictions.prediction_citation: Read, Rezayi 1999, Phys. Rev. B 59, 8084
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bonderson, Slingerland 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 125323 (numerical RR identification favored, not decisive)

predictions.prediction: Universal topological quantum computation by braiding Fibonacci anyons alone.
predictions.description: Predicted that Fibonacci anyons (if realized) support universal TQC by braiding alone (Nayak et al. 2008 review). The prediction is mathematically rigorous; experimental realization requires (i) detecting Fibonacci anyons in any FQH platform — not yet achieved; (ii) demonstrating braiding-based gate operations. Most-likely platform: ν=12/5 Read-Rezayi if confirmed. Status: not-yet-tested — both prerequisites pending.
predictions.prediction_citation: Nayak, Simon, Stern, Freedman, Das Sarma 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1083

predictions.prediction: Thermal Hall conductance quantization κ_xy = (c/2)(πk_B²T/6ℏ) where c is central charge of edge theory.
predictions.description: Kane-Fisher 1997 predicted quantized thermal Hall conductance proportional to edge central charge. Confirmed at integer plateaus by Jezouin et al. 2013; at FQH plateaus including 1/3 by subsequent experiments (Banerjee 2017, 2018). At 5/2 the measured value tests which non-abelian candidate is realized.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kane, Fisher 1997, Phys. Rev. B 55, 15832
predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2017, Nature 545, 75 (thermal Hall at integer and 1/3)

predictions.prediction: Particle-hole symmetry constrains the 5/2 topological order — specifically forbidding pure Pfaffian or pure anti-Pfaffian if PH symmetry is exact.
predictions.description: Son 2015 predicted that exact particle-hole symmetry within the LLL forbids the original Pfaffian and anti-Pfaffian, selecting PH-Pfaffian as the unique PH-symmetric candidate at 5/2. Experimental tests: thermal Hall favored PH-Pfaffian initially (Banerjee 2018), but interpretation disputed. Open question whether real samples have exact PH symmetry — Landau-level mixing breaks it weakly.
predictions.prediction_citation: Son 2015, Phys. Rev. X 5, 031027
predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2018, Nature 559, 205 (interpretation tension)

formal_classifications.label: Compact astrophysical objects
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'compact-astrophysical-objects' (phenomenon; sector: Astrophysics). Contains 17 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Canonical white dwarfs: M ≈ 0.6 M_sun, R ≈ R_Earth (~6000 km), supported by electron degeneracy pressure. Cooling timescales ~ Gyr. Mass-radius relation is well-measured for individuals including Sirius B (M ≈ 1.02 M_sun, R ≈ 0.0084 R_sun) and 40 Eridani B.
realized_examples: Sirius B — paradigmatic WD, M ≈ 1.02 M_sun, R ≈ 0.0084 R_sun (Bond et al. 2017 HST astrometric mass)
40 Eridani B — closest WD-triple system to Earth
Procyon B — high-mass WD in nearby binary
Van Maanen 2 — first isolated WD discovered (1917)

cells.content: White dwarfs near the Chandrasekhar limit M_Ch ≈ 1.4 M_sun (for nonrotating, nonmagnetic, μ_e = 2 composition). Approach to M_Ch leads to instability (electron capture, pyconuclear reactions) and is the precursor to type-Ia supernovae or accretion-induced collapse to neutron stars.
realized_examples: RX J0648.0-4418 — accreting massive WD
ZTF J153932.16+502738.8 — eclipsing ultramassive double WD inspiral
WD 1856+534 — high-mass WD with transiting giant planet

cells.content: Ultramassive WDs with M ≳ 1.1 M_sun, formed in mergers of WD pairs or massive progenitors. Crystallizing interiors (Coulomb crystallization at low T) and possible neon-capture-driven collapse to NSs are predicted; the latter constitutes the accretion-induced-collapse formation channel for low-mass NSs.
realized_examples: ZTF J1901+1458 — M ≈ 1.35 M_sun ultramassive WD with strong B field (Caiazzo et al. 2021)
WD J0551+4135 — ultramassive WD with composition suggesting merger origin (Hollands et al. 2020)

cells.content: Canonical neutron stars: M ≈ 1.4 M_sun (the median observed NS mass), R ≈ 11-13 km. Surface gravity GM/(Rc²) ≈ 0.2 — general-relativistic regime. Crust + core structure: outer crust (Coulomb lattice of nuclei in electron gas), inner crust (neutron-drip + neutron superfluid), outer core (npe + possibly μ matter), inner core (composition uncertain — hyperons, kaon condensate, quark matter all proposed).
realized_examples: PSR J0030+0451 — NICER mass-radius measurement M = 1.34 (+0.15/-0.16) M_sun, R = 12.71 (+1.14/-1.19) km (Miller et al. 2019, Riley et al. 2019)
PSR B1913+16 (Hulse-Taylor) — first binary pulsar, M ≈ 1.44 M_sun
PSR J0737-3039A/B — double pulsar system
PSR J0437-4715 — NICER mass-radius (Choudhury et al. 2024)

cells.content: Massive neutron stars with M ≳ 2 M_sun, constraining the TOV maximum mass from below. These observations rule out soft hadronic-only EOS and disfavor first-order quark-matter phase transitions at densities below ~3-4 n_0.
realized_examples: PSR J0740+6620 — M = 2.08 ± 0.07 M_sun (Cromartie et al. 2020; Fonseca et al. 2021); NICER + radio mass-radius: M = 2.072 M_sun, R = 12.39 km (Miller et al. 2021)
PSR J0348+0432 — M = 2.01 ± 0.04 M_sun (Antoniadis et al. 2013)
PSR J1614-2230 — M ≈ 1.93 M_sun (Demorest et al. 2010; revised in Arzoumanian et al. 2018)
PSR J0952-0607 — claimed M ≈ 2.35 M_sun, the most-massive known NS as of 2024 (Romani et al. 2022; mass updated; observational systematics under continued review)

cells.content: Magnetars: neutron stars with extreme magnetic fields B ~ 10^14-10^15 G (~10^4 × canonical NS). Phenomenology includes soft-gamma-repeater (SGR) bursts, anomalous X-ray pulsar (AXP) emission, giant flares (10^46 erg in seconds), and occasional Fast-Radio-Burst (FRB) associations.
realized_examples: SGR 1806-20 — 2004 giant flare, brightest extrasolar event ever recorded
SGR J1935+2154 — magnetar associated with FRB 200428, the first galactic FRB
1E 1841-045 — paradigmatic AXP
PSR J1119-6127 — magnetar-pulsar transition object

cells.content: Hypermassive neutron-star remnants: short-lived (~ms-1s) products of binary NS coalescence with total mass exceeding M_TOV. Differential rotation supports against immediate collapse; viscous evolution drives collapse to BH on ms-s timescales.
realized_examples: GW170817 post-merger — total mass ≈ 2.74 M_sun (above M_TOV); remnant identity (immediate BH, short-lived hypermassive NS, or transient supramassive NS) constrained by EM observations and kilonova ejecta mass; consensus disfavors prompt collapse, favors short-lived hypermassive NS → BH (Margalit, Metzger 2017; Bauswein et al. 2017)

cells.content: Stellar-mass black holes in X-ray binaries: M ~ 5-30 M_sun, identified by accretion-disk X-ray emission and dynamical mass measurements of the companion. The first BH identifications were in this cell (Cyg X-1).
realized_examples: Cyg X-1 — M ≈ 21.2 M_sun (Miller-Jones et al. 2021), the first BH discovered
GRS 1915+105 — M ≈ 10.1 M_sun, microquasar with relativistic jets
V404 Cygni — M ≈ 9 M_sun, transient BH X-ray binary
LMC X-1 — M ≈ 10.9 M_sun, persistent BH HMXB
GX 339-4 — transient BH X-ray binary, M ≈ 5-7 M_sun

cells.content: Stellar-mass black holes in merging compact binaries, catalogued by LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA gravitational-wave observations. As of the O4 run (2024-26), GWTC-4 contains ~150+ confident BBH mergers spanning component masses ~5-80 M_sun. Mass distribution shows distinctive features: a low-mass-gap edge near ~3-5 M_sun, a pile-up at ~10 M_sun, and a partially-suppressed pair-instability gap at ~50-100 M_sun.
realized_examples: GW150914 — first GW detection, M_1 ≈ 36 M_sun, M_2 ≈ 29 M_sun, BBH merger at z ≈ 0.09
GW190521 — total mass ≈ 150 M_sun; final BH ≈ 142 M_sun straddles intermediate-mass BH regime
GW200115 — first confirmed NS-BH merger, with BH M ≈ 5.7 M_sun, NS M ≈ 1.5 M_sun
GW190412 — first confidently asymmetric-mass-ratio BBH merger (q ≈ 0.28)

cells.content: The lower-mass-gap region: 3-5 M_sun. X-ray-binary observations of the 1990s-2010s found very few BHs below ~5 M_sun, suggesting a gap between the maximum NS mass (~2.3 M_sun) and the minimum BH mass (~5 M_sun). GW observations are beginning to populate this gap (GW200115 with BH M ≈ 5.7 M_sun, GW190425, GW230529 — see below).
realized_examples: GW230529_181500 — compact-object merger with primary mass 2.5-4.5 M_sun (LVK Collaboration 2024); occupies the lower mass-gap region
GW200115 — NS-BH merger with BH at lower edge of gap

cells.content: Intermediate-mass black holes, 10^2-10^5 M_sun. The largest empty zone in the BH mass distribution. Astrophysically expected as: (i) seeds of SMBHs (Volonteri-style seed scenarios); (ii) end-products of runaway collisions in dense stellar clusters; (iii) accreted descendants of Pop-III stellar BHs.
realized_examples: GW190521 final BH at ≈ 142 M_sun — the strongest single IMBH candidate from GW astronomy
HLX-1 (in ESO 243-49) — hyperluminous X-ray source, M ≈ 10^4 M_sun candidate (debated)
NGC 6166 ULX-1 candidates — multiple IMBH-mass-range candidates
M82 X-1 — possible IMBH (debated)

cells.content: Supermassive black holes at galactic centers. Stellar-dynamical mass measurements (S-star orbits at the Galactic Center), maser-dynamics measurements (NGC 4258), and event-horizon-scale imaging (EHT for Sgr A* and M87*) provide the cleanest individual mass constraints. SMBHs at galactic centers correlate with host galaxy properties (M-σ relation).
realized_examples: Sgr A* — M ≈ 4.297 ± 0.012 × 10^6 M_sun (GRAVITY Collaboration 2022); EHT imaging (EHT Collaboration 2022) confirms shadow size consistent with Kerr metric prediction
M87* — M ≈ 6.5 × 10^9 M_sun (EHT Collaboration 2019); first EHT image of a BH shadow
NGC 4258 — M ≈ 4 × 10^7 M_sun, gold-standard SMBH mass via H2O megamaser
Andromeda M31 nucleus — M ≈ 1.4 × 10^8 M_sun

cells.content: Supermassive black holes powering active galactic nuclei (AGN) and quasars: M ~ 10^7-10^10 M_sun. Mass measurements via reverberation mapping (variability time-lag of broad-line region), single-epoch virial estimators, or stellar/gas dynamics for nearby AGN. The most massive known SMBHs reach ~ 10^10 M_sun (ultramassive cell may host some).
realized_examples: TON 618 — M ≈ 6.6 × 10^10 M_sun, one of the most-massive known SMBHs (mass via H-beta line FWHM, single-epoch)
3C 273 — first quasar identified (1963), M ≈ 9 × 10^8 M_sun
NGC 1275 — Perseus cluster central AGN, M ≈ 8 × 10^8 M_sun
Pop-III-descendant quasars at z > 6 (J0313-1806 at z ≈ 7.6, M ≈ 1.6 × 10^9 M_sun — challenging early-Universe seed scenarios)

cells.content: Quark stars (strange quark stars): hypothetical compact objects composed of stable strange quark matter (SQM), predicted if Bodmer-Witten conjecture (SQM as true QCD ground state at zero pressure) holds. Mass-radius relation differs from NS: R increases with M for low-mass SQM stars, opposite to NS trend.

Primordial black holes in the asteroid-mass window: M ~ 10^17-10^22 g (~ 10^-15 to 10^-11 M_sun). This window remains open for PBHs being a substantial DM fraction; constraints from femto/picolensing, Hawking-evaporation gamma rays, and neutron-star capture rates only partially close it.

Primordial black holes in the LIGO mass window: M ~ 1-100 M_sun. Once proposed as a substantial DM fraction (especially after GW150914), the window is now strongly excluded as ≳ 10% DM by combined microlensing (OGLE, EROS, MACHO), GW merger-rate statistics, CMB-accretion limits, and dwarf-galaxy survival constraints.

Exotic compact objects: boson stars, gravastars, fuzzballs, dark stars, anisotropic stars, wormholes — proposed BH-mimickers that match BH external metric beyond the horizon scale but differ at horizon scale (no horizon, soft surface, etc.). Each model predicts a distinct GW-ringdown 'echo' signature or distinct shadow morphology potentially detectable at next-generation precision.

predictions.prediction: Maximum neutron-star mass M_TOV ≳ 2.0 M_sun (lower bound from observation of massive pulsars)
predictions.description: TOV-equation integration with any reasonable hadronic EOS predicts a maximum NS mass; the empirical lower bound from PSR J0740+6620 (2.08 M_sun, Cromartie et al. 2020; Fonseca et al. 2021) and PSR J0348+0432 (2.01 M_sun, Antoniadis et al. 2013) confirms M_TOV ≥ 2.0 M_sun, ruling out the softest hadronic EOS that fail to support such masses.
predictions.prediction_citation: Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV equation)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Cromartie et al. 2020, Nature Astron. 4, 72 (PSR J0740+6620 mass)

predictions.prediction: Black-hole no-hair theorem: stationary axisymmetric BHs are described by the Kerr-Newman family with parameters (M, J, Q) only
predictions.description: Israel-Carter-Hawking-Robinson uniqueness theorems predict that astrophysical BHs (Q ≈ 0 in practice) are fully described by (M, J). EHT shadow imaging of Sgr A* and M87* is consistent with Kerr-metric predictions at current angular-resolution precision (~10% on shadow diameter). BBH-ringdown spectroscopy (Isi et al. 2019, GW150914 first-overtone analysis; updated in O3+O4 data) probes consistency at the percent level; results are consistent with Kerr. Status `confirmed` AT CURRENT PRECISION; future-precision tests (LISA, next-generation EHT) will probe nearby modifications at the per-mille level. Caveats not falsifying the prediction but limiting its scope: the theorem assumes vacuum (matter near horizon could in principle add hair); scalar-tensor and dark-environmental modifications cannot yet be excluded at next-precision levels.
predictions.prediction_citation: Israel 1967, Phys. Rev. 164, 1776; Carter 1971, Phys. Rev. Lett. 26, 331 (uniqueness theorems)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102 (BBH-ringdown no-hair test); EHT Collaboration 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 930, L12 (Sgr A* shadow consistent with Kerr)

predictions.prediction: Sgr A* shadow diameter ≈ 51 ± 2 μas (Kerr-metric prediction given M and D)
predictions.description: Given the GRAVITY-Collaboration mass M ≈ 4.297 × 10^6 M_sun and distance D ≈ 8.277 kpc, the Kerr-metric shadow has predicted angular diameter ≈ 51 μas. EHT Collaboration 2022 measured shadow diameter 51.8 ± 2.3 μas, in excellent agreement.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bardeen 1973 (Kerr shadow formulation); Falcke, Melia, Agol 2000, Astrophys. J. Lett. 528, L13
predictions.confirmation_citation: EHT Collaboration 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 930, L12 (Sgr A* shadow)

predictions.prediction: Binary neutron-star mergers produce kilonovae and r-process nucleosynthesis
predictions.description: Lattimer-Schramm 1974 and Eichler-Livio-Piran-Schramm 1989 predicted that NS-NS mergers eject neutron-rich material that undergoes r-process nucleosynthesis and powers a kilonova transient. GW170817 + multi-messenger EM follow-up confirmed: kilonova spectrum (AT 2017gfo), r-process line identifications (strontium, Watson et al. 2019), and short-GRB association (GRB 170817A).
predictions.prediction_citation: Lattimer, Schramm 1974, Astrophys. J. 192, L145; Eichler, Livio, Piran, Schramm 1989, Nature 340, 126
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12 (GW170817 multi-messenger); Watson et al. 2019, Nature 574, 497 (strontium r-process)

predictions.prediction: Magnetars produce extragalactic Fast Radio Bursts
predictions.description: Magnetar-flare models for FRBs (Popov-Postnov 2013; Lyubarsky 2014) predicted that magnetars can produce coherent radio bursts of the FRB type. SGR J1935+2154 produced FRB 200428, the first galactic FRB, with luminosity bridging the SGR-burst and extragalactic-FRB populations — confirming a magnetar source for at least a subpopulation of FRBs.
predictions.prediction_citation: Popov, Postnov 2013, arXiv:1307.4924; Lyubarsky 2014, MNRAS 442, L9
predictions.confirmation_citation: CHIME/FRB Collaboration 2020, Nature 587, 54 (FRB 200428); Bochenek et al. 2020, Nature 587, 59

predictions.prediction: Hawking radiation from astrophysical black holes
predictions.description: Hawking 1974 predicted that BHs emit thermal radiation at T_H = ℏc³/(8πGMk_B). For stellar-mass BHs T_H ~ 10^-8 K — far below the CMB temperature, making the emission unobservable against the CMB background. For Sgr A* (M ≈ 4×10^6 M_sun) T_H ~ 10^-14 K. Astrophysical Hawking radiation is retro-explanatory: it features in the theoretical framework but no direct measurement is feasible with current or near-future technology. (Possible exception: PBH evaporation in the asteroid-mass window may produce gamma-ray emission detectable by GECCO/AMEGO-X — that would move PBH-related Hawking radiation toward confirmable, but no detection as of 2026.)
predictions.prediction_citation: Hawking 1974, Nature 248, 30; 1975, Commun. Math. Phys. 43, 199

predictions.prediction: Primordial black holes constitute 100% of dark matter in the LIGO mass window (1-100 M_sun)
predictions.description: Bird et al. 2016 proposed that the GW150914 BBH could be the first signal of a PBH-DM population in the LIGO mass range. Subsequent analyses combining microlensing (OGLE, EROS), GW merger-rate statistics, CMB-spectral-distortion constraints from accretion, and dwarf-galaxy dynamical-friction limits exclude PBHs as 100% DM in 1-100 M_sun at f_PBH ≲ 10^-3 at peak sensitivity. PBHs may still contribute as a small subdominant component of the BBH merger population.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bird, Cholis, Muñoz, Ali-Haïmoud, Kamionkowski, Kovetz, Raccanelli, Riess 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 201301
predictions.confirmation_citation: Brandt 2016, Astrophys. J. Lett. 824, L31 (Eridanus II); Hütsi et al. 2021, JCAP 03, 068 (BBH-rate constraints); Carr, Kühnel 2020 review (combined)

predictions.prediction: Intermediate-mass black hole population in 10^2-10^5 M_sun range
predictions.description: Multiple formation channels predict IMBHs: Pop-III stellar collapse, runaway stellar collisions in dense clusters, direct-collapse seeds for SMBHs. As of 2024-26 the IMBH population is sparsely sampled: GW190521 final BH (~142 M_sun) is a confirmed individual at the lower end; ULX candidates (HLX-1, M82 X-1) remain disputed. Confirmation of a continuous IMBH population, with empirical mass function, awaits LISA observations (2030s+) of IMBH inspirals and improved EHT/ULX surveys.
predictions.prediction_citation: Madau, Rees 2001, Astrophys. J. Lett. 551, L27 (Pop-III seeds); Portegies Zwart, McMillan 2002, Astrophys. J. 576, 899 (runaway-collision IMBH formation)

predictions.prediction: Quark-star / strange-quark-matter compact-object population at NS masses
predictions.description: If Bodmer-Witten SQM-stability conjecture holds, strange quark stars (or hybrid hadron-quark stars with a strange quark matter core) should exist as a parallel or replacement compact-object population. No confirmed individual identification. Candidates have been proposed (4U 1820-30, RX J1856.5-3754) based on inferred small radii but remain consistent with stiff-hadronic-EOS interpretations. NICER + GW170817 data are starting to discriminate among hadron-only, hybrid, and quark-only EOS classes but do not yet require a quark-matter component.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bodmer 1971, Phys. Rev. D 4, 1601; Witten 1984, Phys. Rev. D 30, 272; Alcock, Farhi, Olinto 1986, Astrophys. J. 310, 261

predictions.prediction: Black-hole-merger ringdown frequencies match Kerr quasi-normal-mode spectrum
predictions.description: Chandrasekhar-Detweiler 1975 derived the quasi-normal-mode spectrum of Kerr BHs. BBH-merger ringdowns observed by LIGO-Virgo (starting with GW150914) match the predicted (l, m, n) mode frequencies for a Kerr final BH of inferred (M_f, χ_f). Recent overtone-resolved analyses (Isi et al. 2019; Capano et al. 2023) extract the n=1 overtone in addition to the dominant n=0 mode, providing two-mode no-hair tests consistent with Kerr at the few-percent level.
predictions.prediction_citation: Chandrasekhar, Detweiler 1975, Proc. R. Soc. Lond. A 344, 441 (Kerr QNM spectrum)
predictions.confirmation_citation: LIGO/Virgo Collaboration 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 221101 (GW150914 ringdown); Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102 (overtone-resolved no-hair)

formal_classifications.label: Topological insulator and superconductor materials
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials' (phenomenon; sector: Condensed matter). Contains 18 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: HgTe/CdTe quantum well above critical thickness ~6.3 nm
realized_examples: Molecular-beam-epitaxy-grown HgTe quantum wells (Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang theoretical prediction 2006; König et al. transport observation 2007). The quantum-spin-Hall phase with longitudinal conductance σ_xx = 2e²/h plateau is observed.

cells.content: Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ — the canonical 3D TI family
realized_examples: Bi₂Se₃ — single Dirac cone at Γ on the surface confirmed by ARPES (Xia, Hsieh 2009). Bi₂Te₃ and Sb₂Te₃ — analogous family members with larger bulk gap and stronger surface Dirac cones. STM imaging confirms surface state robustness to non-magnetic disorder.

cells.content: Monolayer 1T'-WTe₂
realized_examples: Monolayer 1T'-WTe₂ exfoliated from bulk crystals. Quantum spin Hall edge conductance observed up to ~100 K — substantially higher than HgTe. STM verifies edge state density.

cells.content: Bismuthene — monolayer Bi(111) on SiC substrate
realized_examples: Bismuthene grown epitaxially on SiC(0001). Spin-orbit-induced gap of ~0.8 eV — the largest known 2D-TI gap. Edge states verified by ARPES and STM.

cells.content: SmB₆ — topological Kondo insulator candidate
realized_examples: SmB₆ single crystals. Bulk Kondo insulating behavior confirmed; surface state interpretation contested — some experiments support a topological surface state (ARPES of in-gap states, surface conduction at low T), others interpret the in-gap features as non-topological.

cells.content: SnTe and Pb₁₋ₓSnₓTe — topological crystalline insulators
realized_examples: SnTe single crystals (Tanaka et al. 2012 ARPES). Pb₁₋ₓSnₓTe alloy with x > 0.32 shows the band-inverted phase. Surface Dirac cones at L points of the bulk BZ confirmed; symmetry-broken splitting under uniaxial strain confirmed (Okada et al. 2013 STM).

cells.content: FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ — superconducting iron chalcogenide with claimed Majorana surface states
realized_examples: Single crystals of FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅. Wang et al. 2018 reported zero-bias conductance peaks at vortex cores via STM, interpreted as Majorana zero modes. Subsequent experiments report inconsistent vortex-to-vortex peak behavior; alternative non-topological interpretations (Caroli-de Gennes-Matricon states, Yu-Shiba-Rusinov bound states from impurities) remain viable.

cells.content: Sr₂RuO₄ — long-debated chiral p-wave superconductor candidate
realized_examples: Sr₂RuO₄ single crystals; long thought to be a p_x + ip_y triplet superconductor (Mackenzie-Maeno 2003). 2019 NMR Knight-shift measurements (Pustogow et al. 2019) showed the spin susceptibility drops in the superconducting state — inconsistent with the equal-spin-pairing triplet scenario. The pairing symmetry is now actively re-debated; the original chiral-p-wave claim is substantially weakened though not formally retracted.

cells.content: InSb/Al nanowire — the retracted 2018 Majorana zero-mode claim
realized_examples: Zhang et al. 2018 Nature paper claimed quantized 2e²/h conductance peaks attributable to Majorana zero modes in an InSb/Al hybrid nanowire. The paper was retracted in 2021 after re-analysis of the original data revealed that signatures consistent with MZMs were instead disorder-induced Andreev bound states, with the published quantization claim not supported by the underlying data.

cells.content: TaAs, NbAs, TaP, NbP — the canonical Weyl semimetal family
realized_examples: TaAs single crystals (Lv et al. 2015 ARPES; Xu et al. 2015 surface Fermi arcs). Surface Fermi-arc spectra confirmed; bulk Weyl-point band crossings confirmed via soft-X-ray ARPES at varying photon energies.

cells.content: Co₃Sn₂S₂ — magnetic Weyl semimetal
realized_examples: Co₃Sn₂S₂ single crystals. Below T_C ~ 175 K, ferromagnetic ordering breaks time-reversal. Anomalous Hall effect with large intrinsic contribution, surface Fermi arcs imaged by STM (Morali et al. 2019), are consistent with bulk magnetic Weyl points.

cells.content: Cd₃As₂ — 3D Dirac semimetal
realized_examples: Cd₃As₂ single crystals. ARPES (Neupane et al. 2014) confirms two bulk 3D Dirac points along the k_z axis protected by C₄ rotational symmetry. Magnetoresistance studies show the predicted negative longitudinal magnetoresistance from the chiral anomaly.

cells.content: MnBi₂Te₄ and (MnBi₂Te₄)(Bi₂Te₃)ₙ family — intrinsic magnetic TI / axion insulator candidate
realized_examples: MnBi₂Te₄ single crystals — antiferromagnetic ordering below T_N ~ 25 K. Quantum anomalous Hall effect observed in thin flakes (Deng et al. 2020 Science). Axion-insulator response (θ = π) signatures reported but contested in terms of cleanliness of the bulk vs surface contributions.

cells.content: Bismuth (rhombohedral Bi) — first confirmed higher-order topological insulator
realized_examples: Rhombohedral bismuth (Bi) — STM imaging of 1D conducting hinge states between (111) and (110) surface facets (Schindler et al. 2018 Nature Phys.). Bulk classification: a HOTI with 1D hinge modes at intersections of surface facets where the second-order topological invariant changes.

cells.content: Fractional topological insulator (3D analog of FQHE) — predicted, no confirmed material
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Theoretical proposals from Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang (2010) and subsequent works construct 3D class AII insulators with fractional axion angle θ = π/k for integer k > 1, protected by time-reversal and a global U(1) charge symmetry.

cells.content: Intrinsic bulk axion insulator with cleanly realized θ = π (no surface complication)
realized_examples: No material. Theoretical proposals require a 3D antiferromagnetic insulator whose surface gap can be opened cleanly and whose axion response can be measured via topological magnetoelectric effect (image-monopole signature near surface). MnBi₂Te₄ partly realizes this but with experimental complications.

cells.content: Class CI 3D topological superconductor in a real material — no clear candidate
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Class CI requires both T² = +1 (time-reversal) and C² = +1 (particle-hole) — a combination rare in superconductors with strong spin-orbit coupling but predicted to support nontrivial 3D topology with ℤ invariant.

cells.content: Higher-order topological superconductor with helical Majorana hinge modes — predicted
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Theoretical proposals (Khalaf 2018; Geier et al. 2018) construct higher-order DIII TSCs with helical Majorana hinge modes at codimension-2 boundaries. Candidate platforms (iron-based superconductors with crystalline symmetry) have been suggested but not confirmed.

predictions.prediction: 2D class AII TI helical edge states carry quantized longitudinal conductance σ_xx = 2e²/h
predictions.description: Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang 2006 predicted the QSH phase in HgTe quantum wells above critical thickness. König et al. 2007 measured σ_xx = 2e²/h plateau, confirming quantization. Subsequent confirmations in 1T'-WTe₂, bismuthene.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bernevig, Hughes, Zhang 2006, Science 314, 1757
predictions.confirmation_citation: König et al. 2007, Science 318, 766; Fei et al. 2017, Nature Phys. 13, 677 (1T'-WTe₂)

predictions.prediction: 3D class AII TIs host a single Dirac cone on the (111) (or analogous) surface
predictions.description: Zhang et al. 2009 predicted Bi₂Se₃ as a single-Dirac-cone 3D TI. Xia et al. 2009 and Hsieh et al. 2009 confirmed via ARPES, with Hsieh et al. additionally measuring helical spin texture.
predictions.prediction_citation: Zhang, Liu, Qi, Dai, Fang, Zhang 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 438
predictions.confirmation_citation: Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398; Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101

predictions.prediction: Higher-order topological insulator (Benalcazar-Bernevig-Hughes 2017) has 1D hinge modes at codimension-2 boundaries
predictions.description: Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017 introduced HOTIs with quadrupole topology. Schindler et al. 2018 confirmed in rhombohedral bismuth by STM observation of helical 1D hinge modes between surface facets, consistent with the bulk HOTI invariant.
predictions.prediction_citation: Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017, Science 357, 61
predictions.confirmation_citation: Schindler et al. 2018, Nature Phys. 14, 918

predictions.prediction: Weyl semimetals have bulk Weyl points connected by surface Fermi arcs
predictions.description: Wan et al. 2011 first developed the Weyl-semimetal phenomenology theoretically. Huang et al. 2015 and Weng et al. 2015 predicted TaAs as a candidate. Lv et al. 2015 confirmed bulk Weyl points via soft-X-ray ARPES; Xu et al. 2015 observed surface Fermi arcs.
predictions.prediction_citation: Wan, Turner, Vishwanath, Savrasov 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 205101; Huang et al. 2015, Nature Commun. 6, 7373
predictions.confirmation_citation: Lv et al. 2015, Phys. Rev. X 5, 031013; Xu et al. 2015, Science 349, 613

predictions.prediction: Topological crystalline insulator SnTe has mirror-symmetry-protected surface Dirac cones at L points
predictions.description: Fu 2011 proposed TCIs; Hsieh, Lin, Liu, Duan, Bansil, Fu 2012 predicted SnTe as a candidate. Tanaka et al. 2012 confirmed via ARPES; Okada et al. 2013 STM revealed strain-induced gap opening consistent with mirror-symmetry breaking.
predictions.prediction_citation: Fu 2011, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 106802; Hsieh et al. 2012, Nature Commun. 3, 982
predictions.confirmation_citation: Tanaka et al. 2012, Nature Phys. 8, 800; Okada et al. 2013, Science 341, 1496

predictions.prediction: Majorana zero modes in InSb/Al hybrid nanowires give quantized 2e²/h zero-bias conductance
predictions.description: Zhang et al. 2018 Nature 556, 74 reported quantized 2e²/h conductance peaks attributed to MZMs. Subsequent re-analysis of the data showed the apparent quantization was disorder-induced rather than topological; the paper was retracted in 2021 (Nature 591, 354). This is the most prominent retracted topological-matter claim and is recorded here as a falsified specific prediction. Note that MZM-platform searches in InAs/Al, InSb/Pb, and related systems continue with more careful protocols — the falsification applies to this specific 2018 quantization claim, not to MZM platforms as a whole.
predictions.prediction_citation: Zhang et al. 2018, Nature 556, 74
predictions.confirmation_citation: Zhang et al. 2021, Nature 591, 354 (retraction); Frolov 2021, Nature 592, 17 (commentary)

predictions.prediction: Sr₂RuO₄ has chiral p_x + ip_y triplet pairing, making it an intrinsic 2D topological superconductor with half-quantum vortices
predictions.description: Mackenzie-Maeno 2003 reviewed evidence for chiral p-wave pairing — strong before 2019. Pustogow et al. 2019 NMR Knight shift measurements showed the spin susceptibility drops on entering the superconducting state, inconsistent with equal-spin triplet pairing. Subsequent ultrasound (Ghosh et al. 2020) further constrained the pairing symmetry. The chiral p-wave + topological-superconductor scenario is substantially weakened; alternative pairings remain debated. Listed as `tension` because the pairing is still unconventional and might yet be topological under different scenarios, but the specific chiral-p-wave claim has lost most of its support.
predictions.prediction_citation: Mackenzie, Maeno 2003, Rev. Mod. Phys. 75, 657
predictions.confirmation_citation: Pustogow et al. 2019, Nature 574, 72 (NMR contradicts); Ghosh et al. 2020, Nature Phys. 17, 199

predictions.prediction: Topological magnetoelectric effect: a TI surface in a magnetic field has an image-monopole-like response (axion electrodynamics)
predictions.description: Qi, Hughes, Zhang 2008 predicted that an electrically charged source near a TI surface in a magnetic field generates an image-monopole-like field configuration, with the axion angle θ = π fixing the response. As of 2026, indirect signatures (Kerr/Faraday rotation in TI films, magnetic-field-induced gap opening of surface Dirac cones) are confirmed; direct image-monopole-style measurement remains a target. The closely-related axion-insulator response in MnBi₂Te₄ thin films shows partial signatures but is contested as a clean bulk realization.
predictions.prediction_citation: Qi, Hughes, Zhang 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195424

predictions.prediction: Majorana zero modes at vortex cores of FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ are non-Abelian anyons (Ising MTC) supporting topologically-protected braiding
predictions.description: Wang et al. 2018 reported zero-bias conductance peaks at vortex cores in FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅, consistent with MZMs of a 2D class D effective surface TSC. The braiding-protected non-Abelian statistics has not been demonstrated. Subsequent experiments report vortex-to-vortex inconsistency in the zero-bias peak presence; impurity-induced Yu-Shiba-Rusinov bound states remain a viable alternative explanation. Listed as `tension` because the signatures are striking but alternative interpretations are not ruled out.
predictions.prediction_citation: Hosur, Ghaemi, Mong, Vishwanath 2011, Phys. Rev. Lett. 107, 097001 (general theory of MZMs in vortex cores of SC + TI surfaces); Hu et al. 2018 (FeTe/Se prediction)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Wang et al. 2018, Science 362, 333 (vortex zero-bias); Machida et al. 2019, Nature Mater. 18, 811 (vortex-dependence concerns)

predictions.prediction: Fractional topological insulator (3D analog of FQHE) exists in some strongly-correlated 3D material with time-reversal symmetry
predictions.description: Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang 2010 and subsequent works constructed fractional 3D TIs with fractional axion angle θ = π/k for integer k > 1, protected by time-reversal and global U(1). The phenomenon is theoretically clean — parton constructions confirm consistency, and the topological-magnetoelectric response is fractionalized. No material realization exists. This is the longest-standing open Mendeleev cell of 3D-topological-matter physics.
predictions.prediction_citation: Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 105, 246809; Swingle, Barkeshli, McGreevy, Senthil 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 195139

formal_classifications.label: Neutrino-sector phenomenology
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'neutrino-sector-phenomenology' (phenomenon; sector: Neutrino). Contains 18 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Solar mixing angle θ_12 ≈ 33.45°
realized_examples: KamLAND reactor + SNO solar combined analyses give sin²(θ_12) ≈ 0.307 ± 0.013; the precision is dominated by KamLAND long-baseline reactor measurements of Δm²_21 and θ_12 together.

cells.content: Atmospheric mixing angle θ_23 ≈ 49° (NH) with octant ambiguity
realized_examples: Super-Kamiokande atmospheric-ν fit, MINOS, T2K, NOvA disappearance measurements. sin²(θ_23) ≈ 0.55 (NH best-fit, NuFIT 2024); the octant (θ_23 below or above 45°) is not yet definitively resolved.

cells.content: Reactor mixing angle θ_13 ≈ 8.6°
realized_examples: Daya Bay reactor short-baseline experiment in China measured sin²(2θ_13) = 0.0856 ± 0.0029 (2022); RENO and Double Chooz are consistent.

cells.content: Leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP
realized_examples: T2K Collaboration 2020 Nature report best-fit δ_CP ≈ -π/2 in NH (CP-violating); NOvA 2024 results report best-fit closer to δ_CP ≈ 0 in NH. The two experiments are in slight tension on δ_CP at the ~1.5σ level.

cells.content: Solar mass-squared difference Δm²_21 ≈ 7.4 × 10⁻⁵ eV²
realized_examples: KamLAND + solar global fit: Δm²_21 = (7.42 ± 0.21) × 10⁻⁵ eV² (NuFIT 2024).

cells.content: Atmospheric mass-squared difference |Δm²_31| ≈ 2.5 × 10⁻³ eV² (sign open)
realized_examples: T2K, NOvA, MINOS, IceCube atmospheric fits all give |Δm²_31| ≈ 2.51 × 10⁻³ eV². The sign distinguishes normal hierarchy (Δm²_31 > 0) from inverted hierarchy (Δm²_31 < 0).

cells.content: Mass hierarchy: normal (NH, m1<m2<m3) vs inverted (IH, m3<m1<m2)
realized_examples: Global fits including NOvA, T2K, atmospheric data, and cosmological mass bounds slightly prefer NH over IH at ~2-3σ. JUNO, DUNE, and IceCube-Upgrade are designed to resolve this at >5σ within the next decade.

cells.content: Absolute electron-neutrino mass bound from tritium-endpoint
realized_examples: KATRIN Collaboration 2022 Nature: m(ν_e) < 0.8 eV at 90% CL (combined first three measurement campaigns). Final KATRIN target: 0.2 eV sensitivity.

cells.content: Cosmological sum-of-masses bound Σm_ν < 0.12 eV
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing: Σm_ν < 0.12 eV (95% CL, ΛCDM). Tighter bounds from CMB-S4 are anticipated in the late 2020s — the experiment should detect non-zero Σm_ν if the value is ≳ 0.04 eV (the lower bound implied by NH).
ACT + DESI 2024 combined results give Σm_ν < 0.08 eV (95% CL) under ΛCDM, in mild tension with the NH lower bound.

cells.content: Dirac vs Majorana character of neutrino mass
realized_examples: No direct experimental test resolves Dirac vs Majorana definitively. Majorana character would manifest in observable lepton-number-violating 0νββ rate. Current 0νββ searches bound m_ββ ≲ 150 meV (KamLAND-Zen 2024). Next-generation experiments target m_ββ ≲ 10-20 meV (LEGEND-1000, nEXO).

cells.content: Neutrinoless double-beta decay search programs
realized_examples: KamLAND-Zen (Xe-136) 2024: T_1/2^{0νββ} > 2.3 × 10^{26} yr, corresponding to m_ββ < 36-156 meV (matrix-element dependent). LEGEND-200 (Ge-76) currently taking data. CUPID (Mo-100), nEXO design at next-generation sensitivity m_ββ ≲ 10 meV.

cells.content: Heidelberg-Moscow 76Ge 0νββ claim (Klapdor-Kleingrothaus 2001/2006) — FALSIFIED
realized_examples: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001 / 2006 claimed a 0νββ signal in Ge-76 corresponding to m_ββ ≈ 0.32 eV. GERDA Phase II (Agostini et al. 2020) and Majorana Demonstrator (Alvis et al. 2019) decisively excluded the claimed signal: GERDA reached T_1/2 > 1.8 × 10^{26} yr, ruling out the Heidelberg-Moscow claim by orders of magnitude. The 2001/2006 claim is one of the cleanest experimental falsifications in neutrino physics.

cells.content: LSND 1996 νe-appearance interpreted as eV-scale sterile neutrino — FALSIFIED in original form
realized_examples: LSND Collaboration 1996, 2001 reported νe appearance in a ν_μ beam at L/E suggesting Δm² ~ 1 eV² oscillation; interpretation: a fourth sterile neutrino at eV mass scale. Subsequent dedicated short-baseline searches: MiniBooNE saw partial confirmation but with energy-spectrum complications; IceCube-DeepCore and MINOS+ excluded the LSND-interpretation 3+1-sterile parameter space at high CL. MicroBooNE 2022 saw no excess in the expected LSND parameter space.

Reactor antineutrino anomaly and Gallium anomaly briefly reignited eV-scale sterile interest but recent reactor flux re-evaluations (HKSS / SM2018) and BEST 2022 results have not converged on a single sterile parameter space consistent across experiments.

cells.content: Remaining eV-scale sterile-ν parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies
realized_examples: BEST 2022 Gallium-anomaly results show ~4σ ν_e disappearance consistent with a sterile-ν oscillation at Δm² ~ 1 eV². STEREO and PROSPECT reactor experiments DID NOT confirm reactor anomaly; recent reactor-flux re-evaluations (HKSS, SM2018) reduced the predicted antineutrino flux, partly explaining the original anomaly without sterile neutrinos.

cells.content: keV-scale sterile-ν as warm-dark-matter candidate
realized_examples: X-ray line searches (XMM-Newton, NuSTAR, Suzaku) have placed bounds on the sterile-ν → ν + γ decay X-ray line in galaxy clusters. The 2014 ~3.5 keV unidentified line in Perseus and other clusters was tentatively interpreted as sterile-ν decay (m_s ~ 7 keV); subsequent observations (deeper Chandra, XMM, and 2020 Hitomi data) significantly weakened but did not definitively eliminate this interpretation. Structure-formation bounds (Lyman-α, dwarf-galaxy abundance) limit warm-DM masses to m_s ≳ a few keV.

cells.content: Type-I see-saw mechanism for neutrino mass generation
realized_examples: No direct experimental test. The see-saw type-I mechanism postulates right-handed neutrinos N_R with large Majorana masses M_R ≫ electroweak scale (typically GeV-TeV-scale for low-scale see-saw; up to ~10^14 GeV for GUT-scale see-saw); the light neutrino mass is generated as m_ν ≈ y_ν² v² / M_R, naturally explaining the smallness of m_ν.

cells.content: Leptogenesis as origin of cosmological matter-antimatter asymmetry
realized_examples: No direct experimental test possible at terrestrial experiments. Connections: requires Majorana neutrinos (cf. cell-nu-Dirac-vs-Majorana); requires CP violation in the lepton sector (cf. cell-nu-deltaCP) — though low-energy δ_CP need not match the high-energy CP phases relevant to leptogenesis without additional symmetry assumptions.

cells.content: Supernova 1987A neutrino burst detection
realized_examples: SN 1987A burst neutrinos detected at Kamiokande-II (11 events), IMB (8 events), Baksan (5 events). Total energy released ~3 × 10^{53} erg, consistent with core-collapse neutrino emission models. Sub-bound on neutrino mass from time-of-flight spread: m_ν ≲ ~10 eV (loose).
Future galactic SN burst would yield O(10^4) events in Super-K, IceCube, JUNO, and DUNE — a major motivation for the SuperNova Early Warning System (SNEWS) network.

predictions.prediction: Solar neutrinos undergo flavor-changing oscillation (large-mixing-angle MSW solution); θ_12 large
predictions.description: Mikheyev-Smirnov-Wolfenstein matter-effect predicted resonant flavor conversion in the solar interior. SNO 2002 directly confirmed the missing solar electron-neutrino flux was converted to ν_μ + ν_τ. KamLAND reactor confirmed the LMA-MSW solution with sin²(θ_12) ≈ 0.31. Final precision-coincidence of solar + reactor measurements.
predictions.prediction_citation: Wolfenstein 1978, Phys. Rev. D 17, 2369; Mikheyev, Smirnov 1985, Yad. Fiz. 42, 1441
predictions.confirmation_citation: Ahmad et al. (SNO) 2002, Phys. Rev. Lett. 89, 011301; Eguchi et al. (KamLAND) 2003, Phys. Rev. Lett. 90, 021802

predictions.prediction: Atmospheric neutrinos undergo flavor oscillation with Δm² ~ 10⁻³ eV² and near-maximal θ_23
predictions.description: Super-Kamiokande 1998 reported zenith-angle dependence of atmospheric ν_μ flux indicating ν_μ → ν_τ oscillation with Δm² ≈ 2 × 10⁻³ eV² and large mixing. MINOS, T2K, NOvA, and IceCube subsequently confirmed and precisely measured |Δm²_31| and sin²(θ_23).
predictions.prediction_citation: Pontecorvo 1957/1968 (oscillation framework); Wolfenstein 1978 (matter effects)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Fukuda et al. (Super-K) 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 1562; Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004

predictions.prediction: Reactor mixing angle θ_13 is non-zero
predictions.description: Pre-2012 upper bounds (CHOOZ) on θ_13 were below the level required for resolving leptonic CP violation; some theoretical frameworks (tribimaximal mixing) predicted θ_13 = 0. The 2012 measurements by Daya Bay (and RENO, Double Chooz) established sin²(2θ_13) ≈ 0.085, opening the CP-violation measurement program.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bilenky, Pontecorvo 1978, Phys. Rep. 41, 225 (3-flavor framework); Daya Bay design proposal 2007
predictions.confirmation_citation: An et al. (Daya Bay) 2012, Phys. Rev. Lett. 108, 171803

predictions.prediction: Cosmological probes can bound the sum of neutrino masses below 1 eV
predictions.description: Hu-Eisenstein-Tegmark 1998 framework for neutrino mass effects on structure formation. Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing established Σm_ν < 0.12 eV at 95% CL. ACT + DESI 2024 tightened to ~0.08 eV.
predictions.prediction_citation: Hu, Eisenstein, Tegmark 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 80, 5255
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

predictions.prediction: Mass hierarchy is normal (m_1 < m_2 < m_3)
predictions.description: Current global fits show NH preferred over IH at ~2-3σ but not at 5σ. The slight tension is between (a) atmospheric data + NOvA which slightly prefer NH and (b) the absolute mass-bound tension between cosmology and the IH lower bound (IH requires Σm_ν ≳ 0.1 eV, which is in mild tension with ACT+DESI 2024). DUNE and JUNO are designed to definitively resolve at >5σ.
predictions.prediction_citation: No specific prediction; the preference for NH emerged from global fits over ~2020-2024
predictions.confirmation_citation: Capozzi, Lisi, Marrone, Palazzo 2021, Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 102, 48 (global fits showing NH preference)

predictions.prediction: Leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP ≈ -π/2
predictions.description: T2K 2020 best-fit δ_CP ≈ -π/2 with hint of CP violation. NOvA 2022 best-fit closer to 0 in NH (or non-trivial in IH). The two experiments are in slight tension at the ~1.5σ level. DUNE is designed to measure δ_CP at 10° precision over 10 years.
predictions.prediction_citation: Hyper-K and DUNE design documents 2017-2020 (forecasts for CP-violation discovery)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (T2K) 2020, Nature 580, 339; Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004

predictions.prediction: Heidelberg-Moscow claim: 76Ge 0νββ signal at T_1/2 ~ 1.2 × 10^{25} yr, m_ββ ≈ 0.3 eV
predictions.description: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001/2006 claimed a 0νββ signal in 76Ge. GERDA Phase II 2020 reached T_1/2 > 1.8 × 10^{26} yr, ruling out the claimed signal by more than an order of magnitude. This is the canonical falsified specific claim in 0νββ history. Distinguished from a 'Majorana hypothesis falsification' — the Majorana hypothesis remains open; only the specific Heidelberg-Moscow claim of positive detection is falsified.
predictions.prediction_citation: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001, Mod. Phys. Lett. A 16, 2409
predictions.confirmation_citation: Agostini et al. (GERDA) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 252502 (decisive exclusion)

predictions.prediction: LSND νe-appearance is explained by a sterile neutrino with Δm² ~ 1 eV² and sin²(2θ_μe) ~ 0.003 (3+1 model)
predictions.description: LSND 2001 reported an excess interpretable as a 3+1 sterile neutrino oscillation. IceCube-DeepCore 2020 search for atmospheric ν_μ disappearance excluded the 3+1 parameter space at high CL. MicroBooNE 2022 (and the broader SBN program) excluded the LSND-implied parameter space for the original 3+1 interpretation. Listed `falsified` for the specific LSND-derived 3+1 interpretation; a different eV-scale sterile parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies (cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension) remains in tension rather than falsified.
predictions.prediction_citation: Aguilar et al. (LSND) 2001, Phys. Rev. D 64, 112007
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aartsen et al. (IceCube) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141801; Acero et al. (MicroBooNE) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 241801

predictions.prediction: Neutrinos are Majorana particles, with m_ββ ≈ 10-50 meV detectable by next-generation 0νββ experiments
predictions.description: If neutrinos are Majorana (favored on grounds of seesaw + leptogenesis), m_ββ should be detectable by LEGEND-1000, nEXO, and other next-gen 0νββ experiments. Current sensitivity (KamLAND-Zen 2024) bounds m_ββ < ~150 meV, an order of magnitude above the favored Majorana-NH prediction range. Listed `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the prediction is well-defined but the experimental sensitivity is not yet there.
predictions.prediction_citation: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980; Avignone, Elliott, Engel 2008 (review of m_ββ expectations)

predictions.prediction: Galactic core-collapse supernova produces detectable neutrino burst
predictions.description: Core-collapse SN models (Bethe-Wilson 1985 onwards) predict ~10^{53} erg neutrino emission over ~10 s. SN 1987A burst confirmed at Kamiokande-II, IMB, and Baksan; the energy, time profile, and event count match canonical core-collapse models.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bethe, Wilson 1985, Astrophys. J. 295, 14; Burrows, Lattimer 1986
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hirata et al. 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1490; Bionta et al. 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1494

formal_classifications.label: Real critical systems
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'real-critical-systems' (phenomenon; sector: Condensed matter). Contains 18 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Liquid-gas critical points in pure fluids — H₂O (T_c = 647 K), CO₂ (T_c = 304 K), Xe (T_c = 290 K)
realized_examples: High-pressure light-scattering and density measurements near the critical point of CO₂, Xe, H₂O, methane, ethane. Critical exponents (ν, η, γ) measured to few-percent precision and consistent with 3D-Ising bootstrap predictions: ν = 0.630, η = 0.036, γ = 1.24.

cells.content: Uniaxial antiferromagnets FeF₂ (T_N = 78 K), MnF₂ (T_N = 67 K)
realized_examples: Neutron scattering measurements of order-parameter exponent β at the Néel transition. FeF₂ and MnF₂ are textbook uniaxial antiferromagnets — their order parameter is a 1-component spin staggered magnetization, giving Z_2 symmetry → 3D-Ising universality.

cells.content: Binary-fluid critical demixing — 3-methylpentane + nitroethane and similar mixtures
realized_examples: Light-scattering and refractive-index measurements at the upper consolute critical point of binary miscible-fluid systems near the demixing transition. Order parameter is the concentration difference; symmetry is Z_2 (concentration-swap) → 3D Ising.

cells.content: 4He superfluid λ-transition at T_λ = 2.17 K — THE 4He λ-puzzle cell
realized_examples: Lipa et al. 1996, 2003 Phys. Rev. B microgravity experiments aboard space shuttle (and ground-based heat-capacity measurements at sub-μK precision near T_λ): measured ν = 0.6709 ± 0.0001 and α = -0.0127 ± 0.0003. Chester et al. 2020 Phys. Rev. D bootstrap for 3D O(2): ν = 0.6719(7), α = -0.0157(20). The two values differ by ~5σ — the current 'λ-puzzle' in critical phenomena.

cells.content: Planar (easy-plane) magnetic Curie / Néel transitions — gadolinium intermetallics, BaCo(PO₃)₂
realized_examples: Neutron scattering of planar (XY-symmetry) ferromagnets / antiferromagnets near their Curie / Néel temperature. Exponents consistent with 3D O(2) universality at the few-percent level — less precise than 4He λ but qualitatively confirming universality.

cells.content: Isotropic 3D Heisenberg ferromagnets / antiferromagnets — Ni (T_C = 627 K), EuO (T_C = 69 K), RbMnF₃ (T_N = 83 K)
realized_examples: Neutron scattering, magnetization, and specific-heat measurements of isotropic 3D ferromagnets and antiferromagnets give exponents consistent with 3D O(3) bootstrap: ν ≈ 0.7117, η ≈ 0.038, γ ≈ 1.396.

cells.content: BKT transition in 2D thin 4He superfluid films at T_BKT ≈ 1-2 K (film-dependent)
realized_examples: Bishop, Reppy 1978 Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 1727 — torsional-oscillator measurements of thin 4He films revealing the universal superfluid-density jump ρ_s(T_BKT⁻)/(k_B T_BKT) = 2 m²/(π ℏ²), confirming Kosterlitz-Thouless prediction.

cells.content: 3He-4He mixture tricritical point at T_t ≈ 0.87 K, x_t ≈ 0.67
realized_examples: Specific-heat and concentration measurements of 3He-4He liquid mixtures near the tricritical point where the λ-transition line meets the first-order phase-separation line. Exponents show mean-field-like behavior with logarithmic corrections, as predicted by Riedel-Wegner theory (tricritical d_c = 3).

cells.content: Self-avoiding random walks for polymers in dilute good-solvent solution — R_g ~ N^ν with ν ≈ 0.588
realized_examples: Light-scattering measurements of radius of gyration vs degree of polymerization for polystyrene, PMMA, and other polymers in good solvent. ν ≈ 0.588, matching the bootstrap-equivalent 3D-SAW prediction (which corresponds to the 3D O(N→0) limit of the Wilson-Fisher series).

cells.content: 3D percolation universality class — cluster-mass scaling exponent τ ≈ 2.189
realized_examples: Site / bond percolation on 3D lattices (cubic, BCC, FCC) — Monte Carlo simulations match analytic predictions for cluster-size exponents. Experimental realizations in sol-gel transitions, microemulsion conductivity, and some glass-formers show qualitative agreement with percolation exponents.

cells.content: Lifshitz points in modulated magnetic systems — MnP, NaV₂O₅ candidate observations
realized_examples: MnP phase diagram exhibits a candidate Lifshitz point where ferromagnetic, helical, and paramagnetic phases meet. Neutron-scattering measurements partly confirm Lifshitz-class exponents but the realization is less clean than Wilson-Fisher classes.

cells.content: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD — 3D O(4) universality (theoretical only)
realized_examples: Pisarski-Wilczek 1984 prediction: in the massless 2-flavor limit, the chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R / SU(2)_V transition is in the 3D O(4) universality class (4-component order parameter = chiral condensate). Lattice QCD simulations at very small but nonzero quark mass show evidence consistent with O(4) scaling but interpretation requires extrapolation.

cells.content: 2D Ising model — exactly solved (Onsager 1944), ν = 1, η = 1/4
realized_examples: Onsager 1944 exact solution gives exact critical exponents. Experimental realizations in thin magnetic films (limited cleanliness) and in lattice-model simulations match perfectly. The 2D Ising CFT has central charge c = 1/2 and is the chiral Ising MTC.

cells.content: 3D O(N≥5) universality classes — no clean material realization
realized_examples: Theoretical bootstrap-precision exponents exist for 3D O(N) for any N (Chester et al. 2021, 2022). No clean material realization of N ≥ 5. Some candidates (deconfined-criticality multi-component order parameters) have been proposed but the universality identification is debated.

cells.content: Ordinary surface critical exponents in bulk-critical magnets — neutron-reflectometry on Ni, EuO surfaces
realized_examples: Neutron reflectometry of magnetic films at the bulk Curie point reveals modified critical exponents at the surface — the 'ordinary' surface universality class (Diehl 1986).

cells.content: Surface-extraordinary universality class — predicted, no clean material realization
realized_examples: Diehl 1986 predicted the 'extraordinary' surface universality where surface ordering exceeds bulk ordering at the bulk-critical point. No clean experimental realization confirmed; some recent quantum-Hall-edge realizations have been proposed.

cells.content: Rare-earth Heisenberg ferromagnet EuS (T_C = 16.5 K), other Eu chalcogenides
realized_examples: Neutron-scattering and specific-heat measurements of EuS at its Curie point give ν and γ consistent with 3D-Heisenberg universality at the few-percent level. EuS is canonical because it has nearly-isotropic Heisenberg exchange and minimal lattice anisotropy.

cells.content: Isobutyric acid + water binary-fluid critical demixing
realized_examples: Light scattering and refractive-index measurements of isobutyric-acid + water mixtures near the upper consolute critical point. Exponents fit 3D-Ising precisely.

predictions.prediction: 3D Ising critical exponents are universal across liquid-gas critical points, uniaxial magnets, and binary-fluid mixtures
predictions.description: Universality predicted by Kadanoff (1966), Wilson, Fisher (1972) RG theory. Independent precision measurements in CO₂, Xe, H₂O liquid-gas critical points (Sengers reviews), FeF₂ and MnF₂ uniaxial antiferromagnets (Hutchings 1972, Belanger 1987), and binary-fluid mixtures (Beysens 1982) all give consistent exponents ν ≈ 0.630, η ≈ 0.04. Bootstrap-precision values (Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin 2014, El-Showk 2014) are consistent with measurements at the few-percent level.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kadanoff 1966, Physics 2, 263; Wilson, Fisher 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 28, 240
predictions.confirmation_citation: Sengers, Levelt Sengers 1986, Annu. Rev. Phys. Chem. 37, 189; Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin 2014, JHEP 11, 109

predictions.prediction: 3D O(2)/XY ν ≈ 0.6719 from bootstrap (Chester et al. 2020) — matches 4He λ-transition exponent
predictions.description: The Chester et al. 2020 bootstrap result ν = 0.6719(7) is in ~5σ tension with the Lipa et al. 2003 satellite-microgravity 4He λ-transition measurement ν = 0.6709(1). This is the most prominent quantitative theory-experiment tension currently in critical phenomena — the '4He λ-puzzle.' Possible resolutions are open: bootstrap systematic underestimate, residual gravitational rounding in the satellite experiment, or higher-order corrections to scaling on either side. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because the underlying universality assignment (4He → 3D O(2)) is confirmed but the bootstrap-precision exponent value is in tension.
predictions.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 084017
predictions.confirmation_citation: Lipa, Nissen, Stricker, Swanson, Chui 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68, 174518 (4He λ measurement)

predictions.prediction: BKT transition in 2D 4He films has universal superfluid-density jump ρ_s(T_BKT⁻)/(k_B T_BKT) = 2 m² / (π ℏ²)
predictions.description: Kosterlitz, Thouless 1973 predicted that the 2D XY model has a topological phase transition driven by vortex unbinding, with a universal jump in superfluid density at the transition. Bishop and Reppy 1978 measured the jump in thin 4He films using torsional oscillators and confirmed the predicted universal value — one of the cleanest universal-jump observations in critical phenomena.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kosterlitz, Thouless 1973, J. Phys. C 6, 1181
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bishop, Reppy 1978, Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 1727

predictions.prediction: 3D O(3)/Heisenberg ν ≈ 0.7117 — matches Ni / EuO / EuS Curie-point measurements
predictions.description: Bootstrap result (Chester et al. 2021): 3D O(3) ν = 0.7117(5). Measurements in Ni, EuO, EuS at their Curie points give ν ≈ 0.71 at the few-percent level (limited by measurement precision rather than discrepancy). Consistent within current precision.
predictions.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hohenemser, Rosov, Kleinhammes 1989, Hyperfine Interact. 49, 267 (Ni)

predictions.prediction: Polymer scaling exponent ν ≈ 0.588 in 3D dilute polymer solutions in good solvent (SAW exponent)
predictions.description: de Gennes 1972 mapped the SAW problem onto the n → 0 limit of the O(N) Wilson-Fisher fixed point, predicting ν ≈ 0.588 in 3D. Light-scattering measurements of polystyrene, PMMA, and other polymers in good solvent (Schäfer-Kappeler 1985 and subsequent) confirm at the few-percent level.
predictions.prediction_citation: de Gennes 1972, Phys. Lett. A 38, 339
predictions.confirmation_citation: Schäfer, Kappeler 1985, J. Phys. Paris 46, 1853; Belohorec, Nickel 1997 Monte Carlo

predictions.prediction: Tricritical exponents in 3He-4He mixtures are mean-field with logarithmic corrections (d_c = 3 for tricritical)
predictions.description: Riedel-Wegner 1972 predicted that 3D is the upper critical dimension for tricritical phase transitions, giving mean-field-like exponents with logarithmic corrections. Goellner-Behringer-Meyer 1973 specific-heat measurements of 3He-4He mixtures at the tricritical point confirm.
predictions.prediction_citation: Riedel, Wegner 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 29, 349
predictions.confirmation_citation: Goellner, Behringer, Meyer 1973, J. Low Temp. Phys. 13, 113

predictions.prediction: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD is in 3D O(4) universality class
predictions.description: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984 predicted O(4) universality in the chiral limit of 2-flavor QCD. Real QCD has nonzero u, d quark masses, which break the exact chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R symmetry — the predicted phase transition becomes a smooth crossover at physical quark masses. Lattice QCD at small but nonzero quark masses is consistent with O(4) scaling in the extrapolation, but cannot directly test the prediction at zero quark mass. Listed `retro-explanatory-only` — the prediction is consistent with what data exists but is not falsifiable at physical conditions.
predictions.prediction_citation: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338

predictions.prediction: 3D O(5), O(6), and higher-N Wilson-Fisher fixed points exist as theoretical CFTs with calculable exponents — no clean material realization yet
predictions.description: Bootstrap (Chester et al. 2022 and earlier) gives precision exponents for any 3D O(N). Some proposed deconfined-quantum-criticality scenarios may realize high-N Wilson-Fisher fixed points but the universality assignments remain contested. No clean experimental realization confirmed.
predictions.prediction_citation: Chester et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (O(N) bootstrap)

predictions.prediction: Surface-extraordinary critical universality class is realizable in materials with enhanced surface ordering
predictions.description: Diehl 1986 predicted the 'extraordinary' surface universality where the surface orders more strongly than the bulk near the bulk-critical point. Bootstrap-precision analysis (Padayasi et al. 2022) gives the surface exponents but no clean experimental realization is confirmed. Some quantum-Hall-edge realizations have been proposed.
predictions.prediction_citation: Diehl 1986, Phase Transitions and Critical Phenomena vol. 10

predictions.prediction: Universality holds across all microscopic realizations of a given symmetry / dimension combination
predictions.description: The cornerstone of critical phenomena. Predicted by Kadanoff (1966), formalized via RG by Wilson (1971-72), and confirmed empirically across dozens of independent systems sharing the same universality class (3D Ising: liquid-gas + uniaxial magnets + binary fluids; 3D O(3) Heisenberg: Ni + EuO + EuS + RbMnF₃; etc.). This is the structural fact that makes the Periodic-Table-of-Universality entry meaningful.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kadanoff 1966, Physics 2, 263; Wilson 1971, Phys. Rev. B 4, 3174
predictions.confirmation_citation: Multiple independent measurements across cells — the universality prediction has been tested by every system-class transition tabulated in this FC

formal_classifications.label: Dark matter candidates
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'dark-matter-candidates' (phenomenon; sector: Dark matter). Contains 18 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Canonical WIMP miracle — thermal-relic GeV-TeV particle with weak-scale annihilation cross-section
realized_examples: LZ Collaboration 2024 Phys. Rev. Lett. result: spin-independent cross-section σ_SI < 2.2 × 10⁻⁴⁸ cm² at m_χ = 36 GeV (90% CL); XENONnT 2023 Phys. Rev. Lett. equivalent bounds. Canonical SUSY-neutralino-LSP parameter space at ~weak-scale-cross-section is essentially excluded.
Heavy-WIMP candidates (TeV-PeV scale, with non-standard freeze-out / non-thermal-relic histories) remain viable; the exclusion applies to the canonical thermal-relic WIMP-miracle prediction.

cells.content: QCD axion in DFSZ / KSVZ models — sub-eV mass linked to Peccei-Quinn scale
realized_examples: ADMX (Axion Dark Matter Experiment) at U. Washington — most-stringent search for sub-eV QCD axion via axion-photon coupling g_aγγ in a high-Q microwave cavity in strong B-field. ADMX-G2 (2018-2024) exclusion of m_a in 2.66-4.2 μeV range at DFSZ sensitivity. HAYSTAC (Yale) and ABRACADABRA (MIT) extending to higher masses. Other haloscope programs (ORGAN, QUAX, CAPP) ongoing.

cells.content: Axion-like particles (ALPs) — generic pseudoscalar bosons in wider mass-coupling space
realized_examples: ALPS-II at DESY (light-shining-through-walls), IAXO (axion helioscope), MADMAX (high-mass haloscope), ADMX-Sidecar — multi-pronged ALP search programs. ALPs decoupled from the strong-CP-solving requirement allow wider g_aγγ - m_a parameter space.

cells.content: Sterile neutrino at keV scale as warm-DM candidate (shared with neutrino-phen cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM)
realized_examples: X-ray line searches (XMM-Newton, NuSTAR) bound sterile-ν → γ + ν decay rate; 2014 Bulbul et al. and Boyarsky et al. tentatively detected ~3.5 keV X-ray line in galaxy clusters, suggesting sterile-ν mass ~7 keV. Subsequent observations (Dessert-Rodd-Safdi 2020 Science 367, 1465) significantly weakened the 3.5 keV claim. Structure-formation bounds from Lyman-α and Milky Way satellite-galaxy counts bound m_s ≳ few keV.

Dodelson-Widrow oscillation-production gives a relic abundance dependent on sterile-active mixing; Shi-Fuller resonant production gives alternative cosmological histories. Parameter space partially excluded but not eliminated.

cells.content: eV-scale sterile neutrino as primary DM — shared with neutrino-phen cells cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension
realized_examples: eV-scale sterile-ν as primary DM is largely excluded — Σm_ν cosmological bound (< 0.12 eV from Planck) and active-sterile mixing constraints leave a narrow window where eV-sterile could be a fraction of DM but not the bulk. Some remaining parameter space from BEST gallium-anomaly results.

cells.content: Asteroid-mass primordial black holes (10¹⁷-10²² g) as viable DM — shared with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-asteroid-mass
realized_examples: Microlensing surveys (Subaru HSC for short-duration events, OGLE-IV) bound PBH fraction at most masses but leave the asteroid-mass-to-sublunar window (M ≈ 10¹⁷-10²² g) less constrained. PBH-asteroid-mass remains viable as the bulk of DM in this narrow window.

cells.content: PBHs in LIGO-mass window (1-100 M_sun) as 100% DM — excluded (shared with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded)
realized_examples: LIGO-Virgo binary-merger rate in the 10-100 M_sun window is incompatible with PBHs constituting 100% of DM — the implied merger rate exceeds the observed LVK rate by orders of magnitude. PBH fraction in this mass window is bounded at f_PBH ≲ 10⁻³.

cells.content: Fuzzy / ultralight dark matter — bosonic field at m ~ 10⁻²² eV (de Broglie wavelength ~ kpc)
realized_examples: Pulsar timing array bounds on m_φ ≳ 10⁻²³ eV; Lyman-α forest bounds (Iršič et al. 2017) on m_φ ≳ 2 × 10⁻²¹ eV. Galaxy rotation-curve and dwarf-galaxy-core analyses partially favor or exclude depending on mass. Subhalo / cosmic-web observations partially constrain.
Active galactic-nuclei light-curve searches (superradiance) constrain ultralight bosons coupled to BH spins — Arvanitaki et al. 2015.

cells.content: Self-interacting dark matter — σ/m ~ 0.1-10 cm²/g, motivated by small-scale-structure problems
realized_examples: Bullet Cluster (Markevitch et al. 2004) bounds σ/m < 0.7 cm²/g for hot DM-DM collisions; cluster offset measurements give similar bounds. Galaxy-scale observations (cored profiles, dwarf-galaxy diversity, fewer-than-expected satellites) suggest σ/m ~ 0.1-1 cm²/g at galactic-velocity scales — implying velocity-dependent cross-sections (Yukawa-like).
Specific particle realizations: Yukawa-coupled hidden-sector mediators with sub-GeV mass; dark photon coupling; ELDER dark matter.

cells.content: Asymmetric dark matter — relic abundance set by a separate dark asymmetry, paralleling baryogenesis
realized_examples: Specific UV completions: hidden-sector baryogenesis with dark-baryon-number; pangenesis (Hall-Marsano-Cui 2010); aidnogenesis (Davoudiasl-Kim-Murayama). Models predict m_DM ~ few GeV (set by ratio Ω_DM/Ω_b ≈ 5 and dark-asymmetry size ~ baryon asymmetry).

cells.content: Mirror dark matter — parallel sector of mirror baryons with mirror-baryogenesis
realized_examples: Limited direct testability — mirror-baryon-baryon kinetic mixing with photon (via U(1)_em - U(1)'_em) is constrained but limited. Mirror-DM scenarios remain theoretically motivated by particle-physics symmetry but lack distinctive predictions distinguishable from other hidden-sector DM.

cells.content: Dark photon as portal between SM and dark sector — eV-GeV mass with kinetic mixing ε ~ 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹
realized_examples: Heavy Photon Search (HPS) at JLab, NA64 at CERN, beam-dump constraints (E137, NuCal, U70), DM search experiments interpreted in dark-photon framework. Parameter-space exclusion plots in (m_A', ε) plane have multi-decade reach across mass and kinetic-mixing scales.
Distinct from QCD-axion / ALP — dark photon is a vector (spin-1) mediator, not pseudoscalar.

cells.content: Gravitino LSP in SUSY scenarios — TeV-PeV mass, produced from heavy-NLSP late decays
realized_examples: Big-bang-nucleosynthesis (BBN) bounds constrain late gravitino-producing decays — abundant gravitinos produced by NLSP decays during BBN would disrupt light-element abundances. Gravitino-LSP scenarios are restricted to specific NLSP-mass / lifetime windows.
LHC searches for gravitino-LSP signatures (mono-photon + missing energy, displaced vertices) provide complementary bounds in the high-scale-SUSY-mediation parameter space.

cells.content: MACHOs as primary halo DM — brown dwarfs, faint stars, planetary-mass compact objects — falsified by microlensing surveys
realized_examples: EROS-2 (2007) and OGLE-IV microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 (8% of halo DM) across the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound that total baryonic mass is far less than Ω_DM, MACHOs cannot constitute the bulk of dark matter.

Distinguishes MACHO-baryonic-compact from primordial-black-hole-compact: PBHs are non-baryonic (formed from gravitational collapse of cosmological density fluctuations) and not subject to the BBN constraint on baryonic matter.

cells.content: DAMA/LIBRA annual modulation interpretation as WIMP signal — FALSIFIED by XENON/LZ/PandaX null results
realized_examples: DAMA/NaI (1996-2002), DAMA/LIBRA (2003-2024) reported ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) scintillator detector, consistent with expected WIMP-Earth-velocity-modulation signal. The interpreted parameter space (m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm²) was excluded by XENON1T, XENONnT, LZ at multiple orders of magnitude below the implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100 results using NaI(Tl) target have not reproduced the modulation signal.

cells.content: CDMS-II silicon detector 2010 3-event excess — FALSIFIED by subsequent SuperCDMS exclusions
realized_examples: CDMS-II 2010 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 expected background in silicon detectors, interpreted as a hint of low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. Subsequent CDMS-Lite (2014) and SuperCDMS (2018+) silicon-detector exclusions ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint.

cells.content: String-axiverse — multitude of ALPs predicted by string compactifications across decades of mass scales
realized_examples: No specific axiverse-ALP has been detected. Theoretical motivation: string compactifications give large numbers of axion-like pseudoscalars with masses log-distributed across many orders of magnitude (Arvanitaki et al. 2010). Some specific candidates: heavy ALPs from Calabi-Yau compactifications, ultra-light axions from string moduli, fuzzy-DM-like axions.

cells.content: Supermassive PBHs as seeds for galactic-center SMBHs (cross-reference to compact-astro SMBH cells)
realized_examples: High-redshift SMBH formation (z > 6 quasars with M_BH > 10⁹ M_sun observed by JWST 2023) is challenging for stellar-collapse seed scenarios. PBH-seeded SMBH growth from massive primordial seeds (M ~ 10⁵-10⁶ M_sun) is one of several proposed solutions.

predictions.prediction: Canonical WIMP miracle: thermal-relic GeV-TeV particle with weak-scale annihilation cross-section gives Ω_DM ≈ 0.27 naturally and is detectable by direct-detection experiments
predictions.description: Lee-Weinberg 1977 established the WIMP-miracle relic-abundance calculation. SUSY-LSP and analogous candidates motivated three decades of WIMP-search programs. LZ 2024 (Aalbers et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002) and XENONnT 2023 placed σ_SI bounds orders of magnitude below typical WIMP-miracle predictions. Canonical SUSY-neutralino-LSP parameter space is essentially excluded; non-canonical heavy-WIMP variants remain viable.
