Listed `unconfirmed-tension` rather than falsified because (a) heavy-WIMP / non-thermal-WIMP scenarios remain alive, (b) the community uses 'constrained' or 'largely excluded' rather than falsified, (c) the WIMP-miracle as a class prediction is weakened but specific WIMP subclasses survive.
predictions.prediction_citation: Lee, Weinberg 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 39, 165
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aalbers et al. (LZ) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002 (most stringent constraint)

predictions.prediction: DAMA/LIBRA NaI(Tl) annual modulation is a WIMP signal
predictions.description: Bernabei et al. 1996-2024 observe ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) detector at DAMA/LNGS. Interpreted as WIMP-Earth-velocity modulation with m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm². XENON1T (2018), XENONnT (2023), and LZ (2024) all excluded the parameter space orders of magnitude below the DAMA-implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100, using NaI(Tl) targets similar to DAMA, have not reproduced the modulation in WIMP-compatible parameter space. The WIMP interpretation is falsified; the modulation itself remains an open puzzle (possible systematics: cosmic-ray seasonal variation, muon contamination, environmental).
predictions.prediction_citation: Bernabei et al. (DAMA/NaI / LIBRA) 1996-present
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aprile et al. (XENON1T) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 111302; Adhikari et al. (ANAIS-112) 2024, arXiv:2404.17348 (NaI-target null result)

predictions.prediction: MACHOs (faint baryonic compact objects) constitute the bulk of galactic-halo dark matter
predictions.description: Pre-1990s MACHO hypothesis. EROS-2 (Tisserand et al. 2007) and OGLE-IV (Wyrzykowski et al. 2011) microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 of halo DM in the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound on baryonic matter, MACHOs cannot be the bulk of DM. Type-A falsified prediction (a specific BSM-or-near-BSM hypothesis ruled out).
predictions.prediction_citation: Paczynski 1986, Astrophys. J. 304, 1 (MACHO proposal)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Tisserand et al. (EROS-2) 2007, Astron. Astrophys. 469, 387

predictions.prediction: QCD axion in DFSZ / KSVZ band with m_a ~ μeV-meV and g_aγγ ~ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ is the dark matter
predictions.description: Theoretical prediction from Peccei-Quinn 1977 + Weinberg 1978 + Wilczek 1978 plus misalignment cosmology gives axion-DM with mass linked to PQ-breaking scale. ADMX-G2 has excluded m_a in 2.66-4.2 μeV at DFSZ sensitivity (Bartram et al. 2021); the full cosmologically-allowed window (m_a from ~ neV to ~ meV) requires years more of haloscope scanning. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the prediction is well-defined and the experiment is in progress.
predictions.prediction_citation: Preskill, Wise, Wilczek 1983, Phys. Lett. B 120, 127 (misalignment cosmology); Abbott, Sikivie 1983

predictions.prediction: Sterile neutrino at m_s ~ 7 keV decays to γ + ν producing a detectable X-ray line in galaxy clusters
predictions.description: Bulbul et al. 2014 and Boyarsky et al. 2014 reported a ~3.5 keV unidentified X-ray line in stacked observations of galaxy clusters, interpretable as decaying 7 keV sterile-ν dark matter. Subsequent observations (Dessert-Rodd-Safdi 2020 Science 367, 1465; Foster et al. 2020) significantly weakened the line claim using deeper observations and improved background modeling. The sterile-ν-DM interpretation is not falsified but the specific 3.5 keV line claim is substantially weakened.
predictions.prediction_citation: Dodelson, Widrow 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 17 (sterile-ν DM); Bulbul et al. 2014 (3.5 keV line)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Dessert, Rodd, Safdi 2020, Science 367, 1465 (weakening of 3.5 keV line)

predictions.prediction: PBHs in the LIGO mass window (10-100 M_sun) constitute the bulk of dark matter
predictions.description: v21 falsified prediction (cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded). LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA binary-merger rates incompatible with PBH-as-100%-DM in this mass window — implied PBH merger rates exceed observed rates by orders of magnitude. Hütsi-Raidal-Vaskonen 2021 derived f_PBH ≲ 10⁻³ in LIGO window. Cross-reference: same falsified prediction recorded in compact-astrophysical-objects v21; here it appears as falsified prediction for `excluded-as-major-fraction` cell.
predictions.prediction_citation: Carr, Kühnel, Sandstad 2016, Phys. Rev. D 94, 083504 (PBH-DM in LIGO window)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hütsi, Raidal, Vaskonen 2021, Phys. Rev. D 103, 063510

predictions.prediction: CDMS-II silicon-detector 2010 3-event excess is a WIMP signal at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV
predictions.description: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 background in silicon detectors, hinting at low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. SuperCDMS subsequent exclusions (Agnese et al. 2018 Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002) ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint at high significance. Type-B falsified-specific-claim — low-statistics hint excluded by increased exposure.
predictions.prediction_citation: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013, Phys. Rev. Lett. 111, 251301
predictions.confirmation_citation: Agnese et al. (SuperCDMS) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002

predictions.prediction: Fuzzy / ultralight dark matter at m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV explains galaxy-scale small-scale-structure anomalies (core-cusp, missing satellites)
predictions.description: Hu-Barkana-Gruzinov 2000 proposed fuzzy DM. Iršič et al. 2017 Lyman-α bounds m_φ ≳ 2 × 10⁻²¹ eV, in tension with the original 10⁻²² eV motivation. Galaxy rotation-curve analyses partially favor m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV but Lyman-α and Milky Way satellite counts disfavor. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` — different observations point in different directions.
predictions.prediction_citation: Hu, Barkana, Gruzinov 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 85, 1158
predictions.confirmation_citation: Iršič et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 031302 (Lyman-α tension)

predictions.prediction: Self-interacting dark matter with velocity-dependent σ/m solves galactic core-cusp + dwarf-galaxy-diversity problems
predictions.description: Spergel-Steinhardt 2000 motivated SIDM. Observational evidence for SIDM-style halo structure is suggestive but not conclusive; specific particle-physics realizations (Yukawa-mediated dark sector) remain to be confirmed. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the framework is internally consistent but requires identifying specific particle realization.
predictions.prediction_citation: Spergel, Steinhardt 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 3760

predictions.prediction: Asymmetric dark matter explains the Ω_DM / Ω_b ≈ 5 coincidence via shared asymmetry-generation
predictions.description: Nussinov 1985 + subsequent work. Connects baryogenesis to DM-genesis. Predicts m_DM ~ 1-15 GeV. Sub-GeV-DM direct-detection programs (SuperCDMS, SENSEI, DAMIC) target the predicted mass range; no signal so far.
predictions.prediction_citation: Nussinov 1985, Phys. Lett. B 165, 55; Petraki, Volkas 2013 review

formal_classifications.label: Cosmological observations
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'cosmological-observations' (phenomenon; sector: Cosmology). Contains 18 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: CMB temperature anisotropy acoustic-peak structure (Planck 2018 / ACT DR6 2024)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (Aghanim et al. 2020 Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6): seven well-resolved acoustic peaks in the temperature power spectrum, positions and amplitudes consistent with ΛCDM at the sub-percent level for the first three peaks. ACT DR6 (2025): independent ground-based measurement of the same acoustic structure at smaller angular scales, consistent with Planck. The 100θ_* parameter is measured to ~0.03% precision.

cells.content: BAO sound-horizon measurements across z < 2 (BOSS / eBOSS / DESI) — including DESI 2024 evolving-DE hint
realized_examples: BOSS DR12 (Alam et al. 2017) gave BAO measurements at z = 0.38, 0.51, 0.61 consistent with Planck-LCDM. eBOSS extended to z = 1.5-2.3. DESI 2024 Year-1 BAO results (Adame et al. 2024) reach 3σ tension with ΛCDM when combined with CMB + SN-Ia, hinting at a time-evolving dark-energy equation of state w(z) = w_0 + w_a · z/(1+z) away from w = -1, w_a = 0. The DESI hint is currently the most prominent ΛCDM-extension anomaly.

cells.content: Hubble constant from CMB inverse-distance-ladder — H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (assuming ΛCDM): H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc. ACT DR6 + Planck combined gives H_0 = 67.81 ± 0.55 km/s/Mpc, fully consistent with the inverse-distance-ladder value. The CMB-derived H_0 is inferred from acoustic-peak positions + BAO sound-horizon calibration + assumed ΛCDM expansion history.

cells.content: Hubble constant from local distance ladder — H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc (SH0ES 2022)
realized_examples: Riess et al. (SH0ES Collaboration) 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 934, L7: H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc via Cepheid-Type-Ia-supernova distance ladder, anchored to Gaia parallaxes + LMC detached eclipsing binaries + masers (NGC 4258).

TRGB-based H_0 (Freedman et al. 2019, Astrophys. J. 882, 34): H_0 ≈ 69.8 ± 1.7 km/s/Mpc — intermediate between CMB and Cepheid-based, with active debate about Cepheid metallicity dependence and TRGB tip identification.
Surface-brightness-fluctuations (Blakeslee et al. 2021): consistent with SH0ES.

cells.content: Hubble constant from gravitational-wave standard sirens — GW170817 multi-messenger H_0 ≈ 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Nature 551, 85: GW170817 binary-neutron-star merger combined with electromagnetic identification of host galaxy NGC 4993 gave H_0 = 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc (low precision, single event). Future LISA + Einstein Telescope will measure H_0 with multiple standard-siren events at percent precision.

cells.content: σ_8 structure-amplitude from CMB — σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018: σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (or equivalent S_8 = σ_8 (Ω_m/0.3)^0.5 ≈ 0.832 ± 0.013). ACT DR6 + Planck: consistent.

cells.content: σ_8 from weak-lensing surveys — KiDS / DES / HSC give S_8 ≈ 0.77, ~2-3σ lower than CMB-inferred
realized_examples: KiDS-1000 (Asgari et al. 2021): S_8 = 0.766⁺⁰·⁰²⁰₋₀.₀₁₄. DES Y3 (Abbott et al. 2022): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.017. HSC Year 3 (Dalal et al. 2023): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.020. All three surveys consistently report S_8 ~2-3σ below the Planck-CMB-inferred value.

cells.content: Primordial deuterium abundance D/H from quasar absorption spectra — D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵
realized_examples: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102: D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ from high-resolution Keck HIRES + VLT UVES observations of metal-poor damped Lyman-α absorbers. Implies η_b ~ 6 × 10⁻¹⁰, consistent with CMB-derived Ω_b h² at sub-percent precision.

cells.content: Primordial ⁷Li/H — BBN-predicted (5 × 10⁻¹⁰) is ~3× observed in metal-poor stars (1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰)
realized_examples: BBN prediction (Cyburt et al. 2016 review): ⁷Li/H ≈ 5.0 × 10⁻¹⁰ for η_b inferred from CMB + D/H. Observation in metal-poor halo stars (Spite plateau, Spite & Spite 1982 + many subsequent): ⁷Li/H ≈ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰. The discrepancy is ~3-4σ persistent for 25+ years.

Possible resolutions: stellar destruction (turbulent mixing); BBN-era new physics modifying Li nuclear reactions; uncertain Li nuclear cross-sections (partly addressed). No resolution accepted by consensus.

cells.content: Primordial gravitational waves from inflation — tensor-to-scalar ratio bounded r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck Array 2021)
realized_examples: BICEP/Keck Array 2021 (Ade et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 151301): r < 0.036 at 95% CL from CMB B-mode polarization at degree angular scales, after foreground subtraction.
Future: LiteBIRD (JAXA, ~2030 launch) targets r ~ 10⁻³ sensitivity; CMB-S4 ground-based targets similar.

cells.content: Primordial non-Gaussianity — f_NL^local bounded |f_NL^local| < 5.0 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 NG constraints: f_NL^local = -0.9 ± 5.1 (consistent with zero); f_NL^equilateral = -26 ± 47; f_NL^orthogonal = -38 ± 24. Future galaxy surveys (DESI, Rubin, Roman, SKA) target f_NL ~ O(1) sensitivity, distinguishing multi-field vs single-field inflation models.

cells.content: Reionization optical depth τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 from large-scale E-mode polarization: τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007, implying reionization centered at z_reio ≈ 7.7 ± 0.7. Consistent with high-z quasar Gunn-Peterson trough observations (z ~ 6-7) and JWST z > 7 galaxy spectroscopy.

cells.content: Spatial curvature Ω_K consistent with zero — |Ω_K| < 0.005 (Planck 2018 + BAO)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO: Ω_K = 0.001 ± 0.002 (95% CL upper bound |Ω_K| < 0.005). Consistent with the inflationary prediction of spatial flatness.

cells.content: Scalar spectral index n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004 (Planck 2018) — slightly red, as inflation predicts
realized_examples: Planck 2018: n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004, robustly different from scale-invariance (n_s = 1) by >7σ. ACT DR6 consistent.

cells.content: Cosmological sum-of-neutrino-masses Σm_ν < 0.12 eV — OVERLAP with neutrino-phen cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing: Σm_ν < 0.12 eV at 95% CL (in ΛCDM). ACT + DESI 2024: Σm_ν < 0.08 eV under ΛCDM, in mild tension with NH lower-bound.

cells.content: Cosmological dark-matter abundance Ω_c h² = 0.120 ± 0.001 — CELL-LEVEL CROSS-REFERENCE to dark-matter-candidates
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO: Ω_c h² = 0.1200 ± 0.0012 (sub-percent precision).

cells.content: JWST observations of unexpectedly massive / mature galaxies at z > 10
realized_examples: Labbé et al. 2023 Nature 616, 266: JWST CEERS+UNCOVER observations of 6 candidate galaxies at z > 7 with stellar masses ~10¹⁰-10¹¹ M_sun, larger than easily explained by standard ΛCDM galaxy-formation. Subsequent spectroscopic follow-ups (Curtis-Lake et al. 2023; Labbé et al. 2024) have refined some masses downward but the high-z mass-function tension persists.

GN-z11 confirmed at z = 10.6 with mature stellar population (~10⁹ M_sun stellar mass; Bunker et al. 2023). At z > 14 several photometric candidates with stellar masses challenging ΛCDM at current modeling precision.

cells.content: EDGES 2018 21cm absorption feature at z ~ 17 — claim contested by SARAS 3 null result
realized_examples: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67: reported absorption feature at 78 MHz (z ~ 17, cosmic dawn) with depth ~500 mK — about 2-3× deeper than standard cosmology predicts. Stimulated speculative explanations (DM-baryon scattering cooling). SARAS 3 (Singh et al. 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607): independent attempt found no evidence for the EDGES signal at the claimed amplitude.
The EDGES result has not been formally retracted; the team maintains the detection. The signal is currently contested with one independent null result.

predictions.prediction: CMB acoustic peak positions and amplitudes precisely match ΛCDM with six free parameters
predictions.description: ΛCDM-prediction for CMB acoustic peaks (Hu, Sugiyama, Silk 1995-97 era; Spergel et al. WMAP 2003+) has been spectacularly confirmed by Planck 2018 and ACT DR6. Six-parameter fit matches data over many orders of magnitude in multipole. Foundational ΛCDM-success.
predictions.prediction_citation: Peebles, Yu 1970, Astrophys. J. 162, 815 (early CMB acoustic prediction); Hu, Sugiyama, Silk 1997, Nature 386, 37
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

predictions.prediction: BBN-predicted D/H abundance matches quasar-spectrum measurements at η_b = 6 × 10⁻¹⁰
predictions.description: BBN framework predicts D/H exponentially sensitive to η_b. Cooke et al. 2018 D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ is consistent with η_b inferred from CMB Ω_b h² at sub-percent. Major BBN-CMB cross-epoch ΛCDM-consistency test.
predictions.prediction_citation: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN framework)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102

predictions.prediction: Hubble constant is consistent across CMB-inverse-distance-ladder and local-Cepheid-ladder measurements
predictions.description: ΛCDM requires consistency. CMB + BAO + Cepheid-ladder + TRGB-ladder + GW-standard-sirens should all give the same H_0 within their uncertainties. Observed: ~5σ disagreement between Planck CMB-inferred 67.4 ± 0.5 and SH0ES local 73.04 ± 1.04. The Hubble tension has grown since 2018 and is now the most prominent ΛCDM-disagreement. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because neither measurement is falsified — both are precise within their methods — but they disagree.
predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM model itself (no single citation)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Verde, Treu, Riess 2019, Nature Astron. 3, 891 (Hubble tension review)

predictions.prediction: Structure-amplitude σ_8 is consistent across CMB-extrapolated and weak-lensing-measured values
predictions.description: ΛCDM consistency prediction. Observed: KiDS, DES, HSC consistently give S_8 ~2-3σ below Planck-inferred. Possible resolutions: lensing-systematics (galaxy intrinsic alignments, photo-z errors), baryonic-feedback in matter power, new physics. Persistent across multiple independent surveys.
predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM growth-of-structure framework
predictions.confirmation_citation: Asgari et al. (KiDS-1000) 2021, Astron. Astrophys. 645, A104; Dalal et al. (HSC) 2023, Phys. Rev. D 108, 123519

predictions.prediction: Primordial ⁷Li/H abundance matches BBN-predicted ~5 × 10⁻¹⁰ in metal-poor halo stars
predictions.description: BBN-predicted ⁷Li/H ~ 5 × 10⁻¹⁰ for η_b from CMB + D/H. Spite-plateau observation ⁷Li/H ~ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ — factor 3 lower. Persistent ~25 years. Possible resolutions: stellar Li depletion (turbulent mixing), nuclear-physics revisions, new physics. No consensus resolution.
predictions.prediction_citation: BBN framework prediction (Cyburt et al. 2016 review)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Spite, Spite 1982, Astron. Astrophys. 115, 357 + many subsequent

predictions.prediction: Dark-energy equation of state is constant: w = -1, w_a = 0 (cosmological constant)
predictions.description: ΛCDM assumes w = -1. DESI 2024 + SN-Ia + CMB combined gives ~3-4σ preference for evolving w_0 + w_a ≠ -1, 0. DESI Year-3 / Year-5 will refine. Currently active anomaly.
predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM (no single citation)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Adame et al. (DESI) 2024, JCAP 04, 026

predictions.prediction: Tensor-to-scalar ratio r is non-zero (inflation prediction)
predictions.description: Generic inflation predicts r > 0. Current bound r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021) excludes simplest large-field inflation (m²φ² model) but is consistent with smaller-r inflation models (Starobinsky / α-attractors). LiteBIRD targets r ~ 10⁻³ sensitivity.
predictions.prediction_citation: Generic inflation prediction (Guth 1981; Starobinsky 1980)

predictions.prediction: Spatial curvature Ω_K ≈ 0 (inflation prediction)
predictions.description: Inflation predicts spatial flatness to high precision. Observed: Ω_K = 0.001 ± 0.002 (Planck 2018 + BAO). Confirmed at sub-percent precision.
predictions.prediction_citation: Guth 1981, Phys. Rev. D 23, 347 (inflation flatness solution)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020 (|Ω_K| < 0.005)

predictions.prediction: EDGES 2018 21cm absorption signal at z ~ 17 is a real cosmic-dawn detection
predictions.description: Bowman et al. 2018 reported a ~500 mK 21cm absorption feature, 2-3× deeper than standard cosmology. Stimulated speculative DM-baryon-cooling explanations. SARAS 3 (Singh et al. 2022) independent attempt: no signal at the claimed amplitude. The EDGES-specific-amplitude claim is contested but not formally retracted by the team; community consensus leans toward unconfirmed-as-cosmological. Listed `falsified` based on independent null result, though formal retraction has not occurred. Type-B-like (specific empirical claim with conflicting evidence).
predictions.prediction_citation: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67
predictions.confirmation_citation: Singh et al. (SARAS 3) 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607

predictions.prediction: JWST z > 10 galaxy mass-function is consistent with ΛCDM hierarchical structure formation
predictions.description: Labbé et al. 2023 reported unexpectedly mature massive galaxies at z > 7. Subsequent spectroscopic follow-ups have refined some masses downward but the high-z mass-function tension persists. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because observations and theory are both real but disagree, and the resolution (galaxy-formation-efficiency revision, ΛCDM modification, or systematics) is open.
predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM galaxy-formation theory (no single citation)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Labbé et al. 2023, Nature 616, 266; Boylan-Kolchin 2023, Nature Astron. 7, 731 (tension analysis)

formal_classifications.label: Gravitational wave event catalog
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'gravitational-wave-event-catalog' (phenomenon; sector: Gravitational/transient). Contains 17 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: GW150914 — first direct GW detection, September 14, 2015 — BBH merger 36+29 → 62 M_sun, 410 Mpc
realized_examples: LIGO H1 + L1 detection at 09:50:45 UTC, 14 Sep 2015 with SNR ~24. m_1 ≈ 36, m_2 ≈ 29 M_sun → final BH 62 M_sun + 3 M_sun emitted as GW. Frequency sweep from ~35 Hz through ringdown at ~250 Hz. The 'chirp' template-matched against numerical-relativity-predicted waveforms at correlation > 0.99.

cells.content: GW170817 — first BNS merger and multi-messenger event, August 17, 2017
realized_examples: LIGO H1 + L1 + Virgo detection at 12:41:04 UTC, 17 Aug 2017. Followed within 1.7 s by GRB 170817A (Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL), then days-later by kilonova AT2017gfo (Swope first identification, then DECam, VISTA, Pan-STARRS, ALMA, etc.) at host galaxy NGC 4993 at 40 Mpc. Total mass M_total ≈ 2.74 M_sun; tidal deformability Λ < 800 (constraining NS equation of state).
GW + EM coincidence at 1.7 s lag over 130 million light-year distance gave |Δv_GW / c| ≲ 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ — most stringent test of GW-speed = c.

cells.content: GW190521 — heaviest BBH merger to date, 85 + 66 → 142 M_sun (intermediate-mass-BH regime)
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo detection on 21 May 2019. Primary mass ~85 M_sun lies inside the predicted pair-instability mass gap (50-130 M_sun) — challenges standard stellar-evolution models. Final remnant 142 M_sun is the first directly-observed intermediate-mass black hole.

cells.content: GW190814 — mass-gap merger 23 + 2.6 M_sun, secondary is heaviest NS or lightest BH known
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo detection 14 Aug 2019. Primary 23 M_sun BH; secondary 2.59 M_sun lies in the long-conjectured NS-BH mass gap (1.5-5 M_sun). Whether the secondary is the heaviest NS yet observed or the lightest BH is undetermined.

cells.content: GW230529 — most-recent mass-gap event, candidate NSBH
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo + KAGRA detection 29 May 2023 (O4a run). Primary mass ~3.6 M_sun (in mass gap), secondary 1.4 M_sun. Both components consistent with NS or BH interpretations given mass-only data; the primary's position in the mass gap is notable.

cells.content: GWTC-3 BBH population statistics — ~80 BBH events, mass distribution with excess at ~35 M_sun, low-spin distribution
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048: GWTC-3 BBH mass distribution shows a clear excess at m_1 ~ 35 M_sun (consistent with pulsational-pair-instability pile-up), declining distribution above 50 M_sun, secondary peak hint at ~10 M_sun. Spin distribution is broadly low (effective spin χ_eff distribution centered near 0 with small positive bias).

cells.content: NANOGrav 15-year nHz stochastic GW background detection (2023) — Hellings-Downs correlation at 3-4σ
realized_examples: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8: 15-year pulsar timing array data shows excess timing residual at 3-4σ significance with Hellings-Downs angular correlation signature characteristic of GW background. Amplitude consistent with supermassive-BBH-binary cosmological background but a primordial or cosmic-string interpretation is not yet excluded. Joint EPTA + PPTA + InPTA + NANOGrav (IPTA) confirms.
The 2023 result is significantly above the noise but below the 5σ-discovery threshold; further data accumulation will push significance.

cells.content: LIGO O3 stochastic-GW-background upper bound at Hz scales — Ω_GW ≲ 6 × 10⁻⁹ at 25 Hz
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 022004: O3 cross-correlation between LIGO Hanford + Livingston + Virgo gives Ω_GW(f) < 6 × 10⁻⁹ in the most sensitive frequency band around 25 Hz. Below the expected astrophysical BBH/BNS unresolved-binary background at this sensitivity.

cells.content: Continuous-wave search from Crab pulsar — ellipticity bound below predicted level
realized_examples: LIGO O3 continuous-wave searches give Crab-pulsar GW-strain h_0 upper bound of ~1.5 × 10⁻²⁶ at 2f_rot ≈ 60 Hz, corresponding to NS quadrupole ellipticity ε < 6 × 10⁻⁶. The Crab spin-down luminosity sets an indirect upper bound ε < 2 × 10⁻⁴ — the GW bound is below this by ~30×.
Vela pulsar and other young rapidly-spinning pulsars analogous bounds.

cells.content: Galactic core-collapse supernova GW burst — predicted, no detection since LIGO operational
realized_examples: No GW detection for SN 1987A (LIGO not operational yet) or any subsequent extragalactic SN (too distant for current sensitivity). The expected GW signature from a galactic SN at ~10 kpc is detectable across LIGO + Virgo + KAGRA. SNEWS network coordinates SN-neutrino-trigger searches for GW + neutrino + EM coincident SN events.
Theoretical waveforms span burst-like (3D hydrodynamic, rotating progenitor) to long-duration (proto-neutron-star f-mode oscillations).

cells.content: EMRIs (extreme-mass-ratio inspirals) — stellar-BH or NS spiraling into supermassive BH — LISA prime target
realized_examples: Predicted EMRI rates from galactic-center stellar-mass-BH capture: ~10-100 events per year detectable by LISA depending on population assumptions. EMRI signals span ~10⁵ orbital cycles in the LISA band — provide the most stringent test of the no-hair theorem and Kerr geometry. LISA launch ~2034-2035.

cells.content: Resolved Milky-Way compact-binary mHz GW signals — predicted ~10⁴ for LISA
realized_examples: ~25,000 individually-resolvable galactic compact binaries (white-dwarf-white-dwarf, NS-NS, NS-BH) predicted to be detectable by LISA. Several 'verification binaries' (well-characterized known systems like HM Cancri, ZTF J1539+5027) are pre-confirmed sources.

cells.content: Primordial cosmological GW stochastic background — cross-reference to cosmological-observations cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound
realized_examples: Predicted from inflation (energy density Ω_GW ∝ r × A_s); current B-mode bound r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021) translates to cosmological-band stochastic GW upper bound. Alternative sources: cosmic strings (network with G μ ≲ 10⁻¹¹), early-universe phase transitions, inflation-end preheating.
The NANOGrav-detected nHz background is consistent with cosmic strings at G μ ~ 10⁻¹¹ but more likely explained by SMBHB astrophysical foreground.

cells.content: GW propagation speed test from GW170817 — |Δv_GW / c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶
realized_examples: GW170817 GW signal arrived 1.7 s before GRB 170817A at Earth, over ~40 Mpc (~130 million light-years) propagation distance. Inferred bound on GW vs photon speed: |Δv / c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ at the relevant frequency. Sharpest test of equivalence-of-GW-and-EM-propagation by ~15 orders of magnitude.

cells.content: No-hair theorem tests from BBH ringdown quasi-normal modes — consistent with Kerr metric at current precision
realized_examples: GW150914 + subsequent loud BBH events allow extraction of dominant quasi-normal-mode frequencies and damping times. Fit to Kerr-BH predictions consistent at current precision (~10-20%). Overtone QNM (subdominant modes) extraction from GW150914 (Isi et al. 2019) gave a marginal-significance second mode consistent with Kerr.

cells.content: BH-superradiance bounds on ultralight bosons — LIGO continuous-wave search bounds boson masses
realized_examples: Arvanitaki et al. 2015 proposed that ultralight bosons (e.g., fuzzy-DM candidates) trigger BH superradiance — extracting BH spin into a boson cloud that emits monochromatic GW. LIGO continuous-wave searches around known stellar-mass BHs bound the cloud's GW emission, constraining boson masses in the 10⁻¹³ - 10⁻¹¹ eV range.
Cross-reference to dark-matter-candidates cell-dm-fuzzy-ultralight.

cells.content: Multi-messenger coincidence in GW170817 — first GW + EM + r-process nucleosynthesis confirmation
realized_examples: GW170817 + GRB 170817A + AT2017gfo kilonova: confirmed BNS as origin of short GRBs; confirmed kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis of heavy elements (Au, Pt, lanthanides); first standard-siren H_0 = 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc.
The multi-messenger detection involved ~70 EM partners across radio to gamma-ray bands, the largest coordinated multi-messenger campaign ever.

predictions.prediction: Stellar-mass binary black holes merge and produce detectable GW signals matching numerical-relativity waveforms
predictions.description: GR + numerical-relativity predicted specific BBH-merger waveforms (Pretorius 2005 NR breakthrough; subsequent SXS / GW150914 era). GW150914 detection confirmed (Abbott et al. 2016) — matched-filter correlation > 0.99 against NR templates. ~90 BBH events through GWTC-3 all consistent with NR predictions.
predictions.prediction_citation: Pretorius 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 121101 (NR breakthrough); GR (Einstein 1916)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102

predictions.prediction: Binary neutron-star mergers produce gravitational waves, short gamma-ray bursts, kilonovae, and r-process nucleosynthesis
predictions.description: Theoretical predictions: Eichler-Livio-Piran-Schramm 1989 (kilonova / BNS-GRB connection); Lattimer-Schramm 1974 (r-process from NS-merger ejecta); Li-Paczynski 1998 (kilonova model). GW170817 confirmed simultaneously: GW signal; GRB 170817A short GRB; AT2017gfo kilonova UV/optical/IR; r-process abundances inferred from spectroscopy. Multi-prediction event.
predictions.prediction_citation: Eichler, Livio, Piran, Schramm 1989, Nature 340, 126; Li, Paczynski 1998, Astrophys. J. Lett. 507, L59
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12

predictions.prediction: Gravitational waves and photons propagate at the same speed
predictions.description: GR predicts GW propagation at c. GW170817 + GRB 170817A coincidence at 1.7 s over 40 Mpc gives |Δv/c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ — sharpest direct test. Rules out broad class of modified-gravity models predicting non-trivial scalar coupling.
predictions.prediction_citation: Einstein 1916 (general relativity)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + INTEGRAL/Fermi-GBM) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13

predictions.prediction: Pulsar timing arrays detect nanohertz stochastic GW background from supermassive-BBH inspirals or alternative cosmological sources
predictions.description: Hellings-Downs 1983 framework predicted angular correlation of pulsar timing residuals from a stochastic GW background. NANOGrav 15-year (Agazie et al. 2023) confirmed at 3-4σ with Hellings-Downs correlation signature. Source identification (SMBHB vs cosmological) requires further data.
predictions.prediction_citation: Hellings, Downs 1983, Astrophys. J. Lett. 265, L39
predictions.confirmation_citation: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8

predictions.prediction: BBH-merger mass distribution shows pair-instability mass gap at 50-130 M_sun (excess at ~35 M_sun, no events at 50-130 M_sun)
predictions.description: Stellar-evolution predictions (Heger, Woosley 2002 era; Belczynski et al. 2016) of pair-instability supernovae creating a mass gap. GWTC-3 BBH population (Abbott et al. 2023) shows expected excess at ~35 M_sun (consistent with pulsational-PI pile-up) but GW190521 with primary 85 M_sun is INSIDE the predicted gap — challenging standard stellar-collapse formation. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because the gap is partially confirmed (mass-distribution does show suppression) but the GW190521 event is in the gap.
predictions.prediction_citation: Heger, Woosley 2002, Astrophys. J. 567, 532
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048 (GWTC-3 population); Abbott et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 in gap)

predictions.prediction: Kerr no-hair theorem: BH ringdown spectrum determined entirely by mass + spin (no third hair)
predictions.description: Israel 1967, Carter 1971, Hawking 1972 no-hair theorem. Tested via GW-ringdown QNM extraction from loud BBH events. GW150914 + overtone QNM extraction (Isi et al. 2019) consistent with Kerr at current precision (~10%). Future Einstein Telescope / Cosmic Explorer reach percent-level. Listed `confirmed` at current precision; sub-percent tests await future detectors.
predictions.prediction_citation: Israel 1967, Phys. Rev. 164, 1776; Carter 1971, Phys. Rev. Lett. 26, 331
predictions.confirmation_citation: Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102

predictions.prediction: Galactic core-collapse supernova produces a detectable GW burst
predictions.description: Theoretical predictions (Müller-Janka group 3D-hydrodynamic simulations) of SN GW emission at ~10⁻²¹ strain at 10 kpc. No galactic SN since LIGO operational (1987 predates LIGO; rate ~1-3/century). When galactic SN occurs, GW + neutrino + EM coincident multi-messenger event expected.
predictions.prediction_citation: Andresen, Müller, Müller, Janka 2017, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 468, 2032

predictions.prediction: LISA detects extreme-mass-ratio inspirals at rate ~10-100/year, testing no-hair theorem at percent-level
predictions.description: EMRI rate from galactic-center stellar-mass-BH capture (~10-100/yr detectable by LISA). LISA launch ~2034-2035. Major Mendeleev empty for the next decade.
predictions.prediction_citation: Babak et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. D 95, 103012

predictions.prediction: Continuous-wave GW from Crab pulsar at observable level — bounded below spin-down luminosity
predictions.description: Crab spin-down luminosity sets indirect bound on GW emission. LIGO O3 continuous-wave bound is below the spin-down bound by ~30× — meaning the Crab is NOT GW-dominantly spun-down. No detection, but the bound is informative — constrains NS asymmetry to ε < 6 × 10⁻⁶.
predictions.prediction_citation: Pacini 1968 (NS spin-down GW emission framework)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2022, Astrophys. J. 935, 1 (O3 CW search)

predictions.prediction: Primordial cosmological GW background from inflation detectable in nHz or B-mode searches
predictions.description: Inflationary primordial GW with r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021 upper bound). Cosmological-band stochastic detection awaits next-gen B-mode experiments (LiteBIRD, CMB-S4). NANOGrav nHz signal is consistent with cosmic-string interpretation but more likely astrophysical SMBHB. Cross-reference cosmological-observations primordial-GW cell.
predictions.prediction_citation: Starobinsky 1979 (inflationary GW)

formal_classifications.label: QCD phase diagram
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'qcd-phase-diagram' (phenomenon; sector: Hadronic). Contains 18 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Ordinary confined hadronic matter at T ≈ 0, μ_B ≈ nuclear saturation density
realized_examples: Nuclear matter in normal nuclei (16O, 208Pb, etc.); cold-atomic hadron gas at low density. Lattice-QCD at T = 0 reproduces hadron spectrum + nuclear forces from first principles.

cells.content: Quark-gluon plasma at T > T_c ≈ 155 MeV, μ_B ≈ 0
realized_examples: RHIC Au+Au at √s_NN = 200 GeV (STAR, PHENIX); LHC Pb+Pb at √s_NN = 2.76, 5.02 TeV (ALICE, ATLAS, CMS). Evidence: large elliptic flow v_2 (hydrodynamic behavior of QGP-as-near-perfect-fluid); jet quenching (high-pT hadron suppression R_AA); charmonium suppression (Matsui-Satz 1986 prediction); strangeness enhancement; thermal photon production. Initial RHIC Au+Au discovery announced 2005.

cells.content: Deconfinement / chiral-restoration crossover at T_c ≈ 155 MeV, μ_B = 0 (smooth crossover, not first-order)
realized_examples: Lattice-QCD with 2+1 flavors and physical quark masses: HotQCD Collaboration (Bazavov et al. 2014, 2019), Budapest-Wuppertal Collaboration (Borsányi et al. 2010). Both consistent that the transition at μ_B = 0 is a smooth crossover (not first-order) with pseudo-critical T_c = 156.5 ± 1.5 MeV. Heavy-ion freeze-out temperatures from particle ratios independently support T_c ≈ 150-160 MeV.

cells.content: Color-Flavor-Locked phase at asymptotic μ_B, T = 0 (Alford-Rajagopal-Wilczek 1999)
realized_examples: No direct observation. Theoretical prediction: at μ_B ≫ m_strange, 3-flavor coherent Cooper pairing in the (3̄, 3̄)_color × (3̄, 3̄)_flavor channel locks color and flavor SU(3)s together. Pairing gap Δ ~ ΛQCD exp(-1/g²) is exponentially small at asymptotic μ_B but ~10-100 MeV at NS-relevant μ_B ~ 400 MeV.

cells.content: 2-flavor color superconductor (2SC) at intermediate μ_B
realized_examples: Predicted at μ_B such that strange quark too heavy for coherent 3-flavor pairing. 2SC pairs u + d quarks in (3̄)_color × (1)_flavor channel. May exist between hadronic and CFL phases in NS interiors or transitional regime.

cells.content: Quarkyonic matter — confined-but-quark-dominated phase at high μ_B, intermediate T (McLerran-Pisarski 2007)
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction: at large N_c (number of colors), there's a regime where deconfinement temperature T_d > chiral-restoration temperature T_χ, leaving a phase where quarks are the relevant degrees of freedom but baryons remain confined. At physical N_c = 3, the existence is debated. May appear in NS-interior phase structure as a transition layer.

cells.content: QCD critical endpoint — conjectured second-order endpoint of first-order deconfinement line at finite μ_B (active RHIC BES II search)
realized_examples: Predicted from effective models (NJL, PNJL, FRG) at μ_B ~ 200-500 MeV, T ~ 80-150 MeV. RHIC Beam Energy Scan I (2010-2014) explored √s_NN = 7.7-200 GeV; BES II (2019-2023) extended low-energy with higher statistics. Expected signature: enhancement of fluctuations (cumulants of net-proton number) at the endpoint. Current data (Adam et al. STAR 2021) shows hints at √s_NN = 7.7 GeV but no definitive endpoint identification.
Future probes: NICA (Dubna), FAIR (GSI), and J-PARC HI will reach lower √s_NN values + higher μ_B.

cells.content: Conjectured continuity between confined-hadronic and CFL phases (Schäfer-Wilczek 1999)
realized_examples: Theoretical conjecture that, in the (T, μ_B) plane, the confined-hadronic and CFL phases are continuously connected (no sharp phase transition) — they share the same global-symmetry-breaking pattern. Originally proposed by Schäfer-Wilczek 1999; subsequent work has debated whether the continuity is rigorous. The crucial question: does N_c = 3 with N_f = 3 actually realize this continuity?

cells.content: Free-quark gas at μ_B → ∞, where running coupling α_s(μ) → 0 (asymptotic-freedom limit)
realized_examples: Theoretical asymptotic limit. Asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) implies α_s → 0 at large momentum scales / high density / high temperature. The asymptotic-quark-gas limit at μ_B → ∞ has computable thermodynamic properties (Freedman-McLerran 1977; subsequent NLO + NNLO calculations).

cells.content: Hydrodynamic elliptic flow v_2 in Au+Au at RHIC — confirms QGP as near-perfect fluid
realized_examples: STAR + PHENIX measurements of elliptic flow v_2(p_T) in Au+Au at √s_NN = 200 GeV. Magnitude consistent with near-perfect-fluid hydrodynamics; η/s consistent with KSS bound η/s = 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT (Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005).

cells.content: Jet quenching / hadron-yield suppression R_AA in Au+Au and Pb+Pb — confirms QGP energy-loss
realized_examples: STAR + PHENIX R_AA(p_T) ≈ 0.2 for high-p_T hadrons in central Au+Au at RHIC; ALICE + ATLAS + CMS confirm at LHC. R_AA = (yield in heavy-ion) / (N_coll × yield in pp) — values well below 1 indicate energy loss in QGP medium.

cells.content: Charmonium (J/ψ) suppression in Pb+Pb — Matsui-Satz 1986 prediction confirmed
realized_examples: NA50 at SPS (1996) first observed anomalous J/ψ suppression in Pb+Pb. ALICE at LHC subsequently observed regeneration: J/ψ partially regenerated from c + c̄ recombination at LHC energies (Pb+Pb at 2.76 TeV).

cells.content: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD limit — 3D O(4) universality (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984) — CROSS-REFERENCE to real-critical-systems cell-crit-O4-massless-QCD-theoretical
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984): in the massless-2-flavor limit, the chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R → SU(2)_V transition is 3D O(4) universality (4-component chiral condensate as order parameter). Real QCD has nonzero u, d masses — the transition is smoothed to crossover at physical quark masses. Lattice approaches to chiral limit show evidence consistent with O(4).

cells.content: QCD phase structure in NS interiors (μ_B ~ 1 GeV, T ≈ 0) — CROSS-REFERENCE to compact-astrophysical-objects
realized_examples: NS mass-radius constraints (NICER J0030+0451; J0740+6620 with M = 2.08 M_sun); GW170817 tidal deformability bound (Λ < 800). These constrain the dense-matter equation of state in the regime where CFL, 2SC, or quarkyonic phases would matter. As of 2026, no definitive observational evidence for quark-matter phases in NS interiors; the EOS is consistent with hadronic up to highest observed densities.

cells.content: Sign problem at finite μ_B — fundamental obstacle to lattice-QCD access to high-μ_B regime
realized_examples: At μ_B > 0, the lattice-QCD path-integral measure becomes complex — standard Monte Carlo importance-sampling fails. Workarounds: Taylor expansion around μ_B = 0; analytic continuation from imaginary μ_B; complex Langevin; thimble methods. Each has limitations; none provides ab-initio results at NS-relevant μ_B.

cells.content: Hagedorn temperature T_H ≈ 160 MeV — limiting temperature of hadron-resonance-gas (Hagedorn 1965, prefiguring deconfinement)
realized_examples: Hagedorn 1965 observed that hadron-spectrum density grows exponentially: ρ(m) ~ exp(m / T_H) with T_H ≈ 160 MeV. Interpreted as limiting temperature beyond which hadronic description breaks down — preceded the deconfinement understanding.
Modern interpretation: T_H is close to the deconfinement crossover T_c ≈ 155 MeV — Hagedorn's observation was the first hint of finite-T QCD phase structure.

cells.content: Strangeness enhancement in heavy-ion collisions — Rafelski-Müller 1982 prediction confirmed
realized_examples: NA35 / NA49 at CERN SPS, STAR / PHENIX at RHIC, ALICE at LHC: enhancement of strange-baryon yields per binary collision in heavy-ion vs pp baseline. K/π ratio enhancement also observed.

cells.content: η/s near KSS bound 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT — QGP as 'most perfect fluid'
realized_examples: Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005 conjectured η/s ≥ 1/(4π) for any quantum-field-theory fluid (from AdS-CFT duality). QGP at RHIC + LHC extracted η/s via v_n flow harmonic analysis: η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π) — close to the KSS bound, supporting QGP as 'sQGP' (strongly-coupled, near-perfect fluid).

predictions.prediction: Quark-gluon plasma exists as a deconfined phase at T > ~155 MeV
predictions.description: Pre-RHIC theoretical work (Collins-Perry 1975; Cabibbo-Parisi 1975) proposed deconfinement at high T. RHIC Au+Au discoveries (2000-2005) plus subsequent LHC Pb+Pb confirmed via hydrodynamic flow, jet quenching, strangeness enhancement, charmonium suppression. Lattice-QCD independently confirms.
predictions.prediction_citation: Collins, Perry 1975, Phys. Rev. Lett. 34, 1353; Cabibbo, Parisi 1975, Phys. Lett. B 59, 67
predictions.confirmation_citation: Adams et al. (STAR) 2005, Nucl. Phys. A 757, 102 (RHIC discovery)

predictions.prediction: Deconfinement / chiral-restoration transition at μ_B = 0 is a smooth crossover at T_c ≈ 155 MeV (not first-order at physical quark masses)
predictions.description: Lattice-QCD with physical quark masses: HotQCD + Budapest-Wuppertal independently agree on smooth crossover at T_c = 156.5 ± 1.5 MeV. The crossover (not first-order) status was an important empirical determination — first-order would have implications for cosmological QCD-era bubble nucleation.
predictions.prediction_citation: Aoki, Endrődi, Fodor, Katz, Szabo 2006, Nature 443, 675 (smooth-crossover prediction from lattice)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bazavov et al. (HotQCD) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 094503

predictions.prediction: Charmonium (J/ψ) suppression in QGP from color-screening dissociation
predictions.description: Matsui-Satz 1986 predicted J/ψ suppression in QGP due to Debye screening of c + c̄ binding. Observed at NA50 SPS (1996), confirmed at RHIC and LHC. LHC additionally observed regeneration from c + c̄ recombination — confirming the screening picture with added complexity.
predictions.prediction_citation: Matsui, Satz 1986, Phys. Lett. B 178, 416
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abreu et al. (NA50) 1996, Phys. Lett. B 410, 327

predictions.prediction: QGP exhibits near-perfect-fluid behavior with η/s near KSS bound 1/(4π)
predictions.description: Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005 conjectured η/s ≥ 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT. RHIC + LHC hydrodynamic flow analyses extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π) — close to the bound. Major validation of ads-cft-instance program in real data.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601
predictions.confirmation_citation: Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301

predictions.prediction: Strangeness enhancement in heavy-ion collisions vs pp baseline
predictions.description: Rafelski-Müller 1982 predicted enhanced thermal strange-baryon production in QGP. Confirmed at NA35/NA49 SPS, STAR/PHENIX RHIC, ALICE LHC.
predictions.prediction_citation: Rafelski, Müller 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 48, 1066
predictions.confirmation_citation: Andersen et al. (ALICE) 2017, Nature Phys. 13, 535

predictions.prediction: QCD critical endpoint exists at finite μ_B (~200-500 MeV)
predictions.description: Predicted from effective models (NJL, PNJL, FRG). RHIC BES II (2019-2023) explored higher μ_B regimes; STAR cumulant measurements show hints at √s_NN = 7.7 GeV but no definitive endpoint identification. NICA, FAIR, J-PARC HI future will extend reach. Lattice-QCD cannot directly access due to sign problem.
predictions.prediction_citation: Stephanov, Rajagopal, Shuryak 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 4816

predictions.prediction: Color-Flavor-Locked (CFL) color-superconductor phase exists at asymptotic μ_B (Alford-Rajagopal-Wilczek 1999)
predictions.description: Asymptotically rigorous prediction (high-density limit + weak-coupling expansion). At NS-relevant densities (μ_B ~ 400 MeV), CFL relevance depends on m_strange / Δ. NS-interior observations (mass-radius, GW170817 tidal deformability) constrain dense-matter EOS but do not require quark-matter phases at observed densities.
predictions.prediction_citation: Alford, Rajagopal, Wilczek 1999, Nucl. Phys. B 537, 443

predictions.prediction: Massless 2-flavor QCD chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class
predictions.description: Pisarski-Wilczek 1984 predicted O(4) universality for chiral transition in massless limit. Real QCD has nonzero quark masses — transition becomes crossover. Lattice studies at small quark mass show O(4) scaling consistent with predictions but cannot directly test at m_q = 0. Listed `retro-explanatory-only` — the prediction is consistent with data but not falsifiable at physical conditions.
predictions.prediction_citation: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338

predictions.prediction: Color-Higgs continuity between confined-hadronic and CFL phases (Schäfer-Wilczek 1999)
predictions.description: Theoretical conjecture: in (T, μ_B) plane, hadronic and CFL phases are continuously connected with no sharp transition. Active theoretical debate; no direct experimental probe at relevant μ_B currently exists.
predictions.prediction_citation: Schäfer, Wilczek 1999, Phys. Rev. Lett. 82, 3956

predictions.prediction: Quarkyonic matter exists as confined-but-quark-dominated phase at high μ_B, intermediate T (McLerran-Pisarski 2007)
predictions.description: Predicted in large-N_c limit. At physical N_c = 3, existence is debated. May appear in NS-interior phase structure. No direct experimental probe currently exists at relevant μ_B.
predictions.prediction_citation: McLerran, Pisarski 2007, Nucl. Phys. A 796, 83

formal_classifications.label: Electromagnetic phenomena across energy regimes
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'electromagnetic-phenomena' (phenomenon; sector: Electromagnetic). Contains 17 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Cosmic Microwave Background — Planck-spectrum blackbody at T = 2.725 K (cross-references cosmological-observations CMB cells)
realized_examples: COBE/FIRAS measurement of CMB spectrum (Mather et al. 1990): consistent with Planck blackbody to ~ 10⁻⁵ deviations — sharpest blackbody ever measured. WMAP, Planck, ACT, SPT successive precision measurements of anisotropies.

cells.content: Crab Nebula — multi-band synchrotron + inverse-Compton from radio to ~PeV UHE-gamma, the prototypical pulsar wind nebula
realized_examples: Synchrotron spectrum from radio (1 GHz) through optical to X-ray (Chandra), spanning 10 decades in frequency. Inverse-Compton component dominates above ~10 GeV (Fermi-LAT, MAGIC, HESS, LHAASO). LHAASO detected PeV-scale photons from Crab in 2021 (Cao et al. Nature 594, 33), reaching the predicted inverse-Compton tail.

cells.content: AGN jet synchrotron emission — radio through X-ray from relativistic electrons in magnetized jets
realized_examples: M87 jet (VLA radio, HST optical, Chandra X-ray, EHT 2019/2022 event-horizon-scale image). Centaurus A multi-band synchrotron. Blazar SEDs (TXS 0506+056, Mrk 421, Mrk 501) with synchrotron + inverse-Compton humps. EHT 2019 imaged M87* shadow at 230 GHz; 2022 Sgr A*.

cells.content: Gamma-ray bursts — long-GRBs from massive-star collapsars, short-GRBs from BNS mergers; fireball synchrotron afterglow + prompt mechanism
realized_examples: Long-GRBs: GRB 030329 with SN connection; high-redshift GRB 090423 at z=8.2. Short-GRBs: GRB 170817A coincident with GW170817 (LIGO-Virgo + Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL, 1.7s after merger). VHE-gamma detected from GRBs by MAGIC (GRB 190114C, first TeV detection) and HESS (GRB 180720B). LHAASO detected ~10 TeV from GRB 221009A — most energetic GRB photon.

cells.content: GW170817 kilonova AT2017gfo — UV/optical/IR thermal + r-process radioactive decay (cross-references gravitational-wave-event-catalog)
realized_examples: Kilonova AT2017gfo discovered hours after GW170817 (17 August 2017) at host galaxy NGC 4993, 40 Mpc. Multi-wavelength campaign across ~70 facilities: Swope (discovery), DECam, VISTA, Pan-STARRS, ALMA, Chandra, NuSTAR, etc. Spectra showed thermal continuum at early times → r-process heavy-element absorption (Au, Pt, lanthanides) at later times.

cells.content: LHAASO detection of PeV-scale gamma rays from galactic sources — opening UHE-gamma astronomy (2021-2024)
realized_examples: LHAASO (Large High-Altitude Air Shower Observatory, Tibet) reported in 2021 (Cao et al. Nature 594, 33) detection of >0.1 PeV photons from 12 galactic sources, including Crab, LHAASO J2226+6057, and others. Highest-energy detected photon to date is 1.4 PeV. Likely hadronic-pion-decay origin from cosmic-ray protons interacting with molecular clouds in supernova remnants and pulsar wind nebulae.

cells.content: IceCube-170922A + TXS 0506+056 multi-messenger — high-energy neutrino + gamma-ray coincidence at blazar
realized_examples: IceCube detected a ~290 TeV neutrino event 22 September 2017 (IceCube-170922A); Fermi-LAT identified spatially-coincident flaring blazar TXS 0506+056 at z = 0.34. Multi-wavelength follow-up at MAGIC, VERITAS, HAWC confirmed elevated gamma-ray state. Combined statistical significance ~3σ for source association. First credible identification of an individual blazar as a high-energy neutrino source.

cells.content: Cosmic infrared background — dust thermal emission + reprocessed starlight from galaxies through cosmic time
realized_examples: Spitzer Space Telescope wide-field IR surveys; Herschel; Planck high-frequency channels. JWST 2022+ deep IR imaging resolves the CIB into individual high-z sources. The CIB intensity is comparable to the cosmic optical background, indicating roughly half of all starlight has been absorbed and re-emitted by dust.

cells.content: Cosmic X-ray background — predominantly AGN-dominated, resolved into ~80% point sources by Chandra deep fields
realized_examples: Resolved by Chandra Deep Field North/South into individual AGN at z = 0-5. Hard X-ray (10-100 keV) background dominated by obscured Compton-thick AGN. NuSTAR 2013+ resolved hard-X-ray CXB into discrete sources.

cells.content: Extragalactic gamma-ray background — blazar-dominated above ~100 MeV (Fermi-LAT)
realized_examples: Fermi-LAT 2010+ measurement of EGB spectrum from 100 MeV-100 GeV. Resolved fraction increases with energy; above ~50 GeV, the EGB is consistent with being entirely from BL Lac-type blazars.

cells.content: Imaging atmospheric Cherenkov detection of VHE gamma rays — HESS, MAGIC, VERITAS observed ~250 TeV-band sources
realized_examples: HESS Galactic Plane Survey detected >100 VHE-gamma sources (TeV regime). MAGIC, VERITAS independent detections. CTA (Cherenkov Telescope Array) future to extend by ~10× sensitivity. Standard TeV-band catalogues include TeVCat database with ~250 sources.

cells.content: Free-electron-laser coherent X-rays — LCLS-II, European XFEL, SACLA — femtosecond coherent X-ray pulses
realized_examples: LCLS at SLAC (2009+), European XFEL at DESY (2017+), SACLA at SPring-8, SwissFEL. Coherent X-ray pulses at 10⁹+ photons per pulse, femtosecond duration. Applications: protein structure (serial-femtosecond crystallography), shock-compression dynamics, ultrafast magnetism.

cells.content: Laser coherent visible emission — atomic-physics stimulated emission, foundational quantum optics platform
realized_examples: Helium-neon (HeNe) lasers, Nd:YAG, diode lasers, ti:sapphire, optical-frequency combs. Frequency-comb-based optical-clock metrology reaching 10⁻¹⁸ stability (Sr, Yb optical clocks). Laser-based atomic physics is foundational for fundamental-physics tests.

cells.content: Hawking radiation from astrophysical BHs — predicted thermal emission at T_H ~ 10⁻⁸ K for stellar-mass BHs (Mendeleev empty)
realized_examples: Hawking 1974: T_H = ℏc³/(8πGMk_B) for a Schwarzschild BH. For M = 10 M_sun, T_H ~ 6 × 10⁻⁹ K — far below CMB temperature, hence undetectable in practice for astrophysical BHs. Detectable only for hypothetical primordial BHs near evaporation (mass ~ 10¹⁵ g, T_H ~ 100 MeV gamma).
Analog Hawking radiation observed in laboratory systems (BEC analog black holes, fiber optics) — not strictly the astrophysical phenomenon but supports the underlying theory.

cells.content: Axion-photon coupling bounds — CAST + IAXO + haloscope searches; predicted axion-photon mixing not yet detected (cross-references dark-matter)
realized_examples: CAST (CERN Axion Solar Telescope) bounds g_aγγ < 6.6 × 10⁻¹¹ GeV⁻¹ for m_a < 0.02 eV. ADMX haloscope at higher precision in narrow mass window. ALPS-II light-shining-through-walls. IAXO future will improve by ~10×. Astrophysical bounds from horizontal-branch stars + supernova SN 1987A.
Predicted axion-photon mixing in cosmological magnetic fields modifies CMB B-mode polarization patterns; current bounds at the 10⁻¹⁰ GeV⁻¹ level.

cells.content: Magnetic monopole EM signatures — Dirac monopole (1931) predicted, GUT-monopole specific to su5-gut-rep-content cells, searched-not-found
realized_examples: MACRO (Gran Sasso 1988-2000), MoEDAL (CERN LHC 2010+), IceCube monopole searches, ANTARES, Auger surface array. All null results, bounding monopole flux below the Parker bound (~10⁻¹⁵ cm⁻² s⁻¹ sr⁻¹).
Specific GUT-monopole searches in cosmic-ray detectors look for the catalysis of nucleon decay (Rubakov-Callan effect) — null.

cells.content: SETI technosignatures — radio + optical-laser narrowband-coherent emission of non-natural origin, searched-not-found
realized_examples: SETI searches: Project Phoenix, SETI@home, Breakthrough Listen (2016-present). Optical-SETI for ns-laser pulses (Lick Observatory; UCSB nanosecond-detector). Best documented anomaly: 1977 'Wow! signal' — never reproduced. As of 2026, no confirmed technosignature detection. Bounds limit nearby narrowband transmitters to specific power/distance regimes.

predictions.prediction: CMB has a Planck thermal-blackbody spectrum at T = 2.725 K
predictions.description: Big-bang cosmology + thermal-equilibrium prediction. COBE/FIRAS measurement (Mather et al. 1990) confirmed Planck spectrum to ~10⁻⁵ deviations — sharpest blackbody ever measured. Foundational ΛCDM consistency test.
predictions.prediction_citation: Alpher, Bethe, Gamow 1948, Phys. Rev. 73, 803 (relic CMB prediction)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Mather et al. 1990, Astrophys. J. Lett. 354, L37

predictions.prediction: Synchrotron radiation from relativistic charged particles in magnetic fields produces broadband non-thermal spectrum
predictions.description: Schwinger 1949 prediction. Confirmed in Crab, AGN jets, pulsars across radio-to-X-ray. Foundational non-thermal-astrophysics observation.
predictions.prediction_citation: Schwinger 1949, Phys. Rev. 75, 1912
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (Crab)

predictions.prediction: LHAASO would detect PeV-scale gamma rays from galactic PeVatron sources
predictions.description: Long-conjectured galactic PeVatrons (cosmic-ray acceleration to PeV scales). LHAASO 2021 (Cao et al.) detected >0.1 PeV photons from 12 galactic sources, with highest 1.4 PeV. Recent transformative observation; opens UHE-gamma astronomy.
predictions.prediction_citation: Berezinskii, Bulanov, Dogiel, Ginzburg, Ptuskin 1990, Astrophysics of Cosmic Rays (book — PeVatron framework)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Cao et al. (LHAASO) 2021, Nature 594, 33

predictions.prediction: BNS mergers produce optical/IR kilonova counterparts via r-process radioactive decay
predictions.description: Li-Paczynski 1998 + Metzger et al. 2010 kilonova model. GW170817 + AT2017gfo (2017) provided direct confirmation; spectroscopic lanthanide features.
predictions.prediction_citation: Li, Paczynski 1998, Astrophys. J. Lett. 507, L59
predictions.confirmation_citation: Smartt et al. 2017, Nature 551, 75 (kilonova spectroscopy)

predictions.prediction: High-energy neutrinos correlate with gamma-ray emission at blazars
predictions.description: Blazar cosmic-ray-acceleration models (Mannheim 1993; Halzen-Hooper 2002) predicted neutrino emission accompanying hadronic gamma. IceCube-170922A + TXS 0506+056 multi-messenger (2018) provided ~3σ first-source-association evidence.
predictions.prediction_citation: Mannheim 1993, Phys. Rev. D 48, 2408
predictions.confirmation_citation: IceCube + Fermi-LAT + MAGIC + VERITAS 2018, Science 361, 147

predictions.prediction: Stellar-mass BHs emit Hawking radiation at T_H ~ 10⁻⁸ K
predictions.description: Hawking 1974 prediction. T_H far below CMB temperature for any astrophysical BH → undetectable in practice. Only primordial BHs near evaporation could produce detectable signatures. Will likely remain Mendeleev-empty.
predictions.prediction_citation: Hawking 1974, Nature 248, 30

predictions.prediction: QCD axion with mass ~μeV couples to photons at g_aγγ ~ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹, detectable in haloscopes
predictions.description: Peccei-Quinn 1977 + Weinberg/Wilczek 1978 axion. ADMX-G2 + future MADMAX, IAXO, will probe DFSZ/KSVZ band. Cross-reference dark-matter-candidates v25.
predictions.prediction_citation: Weinberg 1978, Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 223

predictions.prediction: GZK cutoff in UHE cosmic-ray spectrum at ~5 × 10¹⁹ eV from interaction with CMB photons
predictions.description: Greisen 1966 + Zatsepin-Kuzmin 1966 prediction. Auger + HiRes (~2005-2010) confirmed suppression at predicted energy. Implies UHECR sources within ~100 Mpc.
predictions.prediction_citation: Greisen 1966, Phys. Rev. Lett. 16, 748; Zatsepin, Kuzmin 1966, JETP Lett. 4, 78
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbasi et al. (HiRes) 2008, Phys. Rev. Lett. 100, 101101

predictions.prediction: GUT magnetic monopoles produce characteristic EM signatures (Rubakov-Callan effect: catalyzed nucleon decay)
predictions.description: 't Hooft-Polyakov 1974 GUT-monopole + Rubakov 1981 + Callan 1982 catalysis. Searched in MACRO, IceCube, ANTARES, MoEDAL — null. Bounded below Parker flux bound. Cross-references su5-gut-rep-content.
predictions.prediction_citation: 't Hooft 1974, Nucl. Phys. B 79, 276

predictions.prediction: Photon mass < 10⁻¹⁸ eV from solar-wind / GW170817 timing
predictions.description: Theoretical massless-photon prediction. Solar-wind tests + GW170817 1.7s GW-EM coincidence bound photon mass < 10⁻¹⁸ eV. Consistent with massless gauge boson.
predictions.prediction_citation: Maxwell theory + QED — no single citation
predictions.confirmation_citation: Particle Data Group 2022 (photon mass review); Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817 bound)

formal_classifications.label: Proton decay searches
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'proton-decay-searches' (phenomenon; sector: BSM-GUT). Contains 17 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: p → π⁰ e⁺ — minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) prediction τ_p ~ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr RULED OUT by Super-K at τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr (2020)
realized_examples: Georgi-Glashow 1974 prediction: minimal SU(5) gives τ_p ≈ (M_GUT/M_W)⁴ × ℏ/(α² m_p) ≈ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁵ GeV. IMB and Kamiokande late-1980s set τ > 10³² yr (already exceeding minimal-SU(5) prediction by 2-3 orders). Super-K 2020 (Takenaka et al. Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011): τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr at 90% CL — minimal SU(5) ruled out by 4-5 orders of magnitude. Hyper-Kamiokande (commissioning 2027) will extend by ~10×.
This is the cleanest single-channel falsification of a specific GUT framework: minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) is empirically dead.

cells.content: p → K⁺ ν̄ — SUSY-SU(5)-favored channel via dimension-5 operators; Super-K τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr (2014), in tension with SUSY-SU(5) for natural SUSY parameter ranges
realized_examples: SUSY-SU(5) dimension-5 operators mediated by colored Higgs / triplet partners give p → K⁺ ν̄ at typical lifetimes 10³³-10³⁵ yr depending on squark/wino mass and Higgsino mass. Super-K 2014 (Abe et al. Phys. Rev. D 90, 072005): τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) > 5.9 × 10³³ yr at 90% CL.
SUSY-SU(5) prediction is in tension with this bound for low-scale SUSY (TeV-scale colored Higgs); pushed to high-scale SUSY scenarios.

cells.content: p → π⁺ ν̄ — charged-current channel bounded τ > 3.9 × 10³² yr (Super-K)
realized_examples: Super-K bound (Mine et al. 2008 Phys. Rev. D 77, 052006 updated): τ(p → π⁺ ν̄) > 3.9 × 10³² yr at 90% CL. Predicted in many GUT variants at similar level to p → π⁰ e⁺.

cells.content: p → e⁺ γ — rare channel bounded τ > 7 × 10³² yr (Super-K 2009)
realized_examples: Bound from Super-K (Kobayashi et al. 2005 Phys. Rev. D 72, 052007): τ(p → e⁺ γ) > 6.7 × 10³² yr at 90% CL. Predicted at sub-leading rates in most GUT scenarios; sensitive to specific Higgs mediator structures.

cells.content: p → η⁰ e⁺ and p → η⁰ μ⁺ — meson-emission channels bounded ~10³³ yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → η⁰ e⁺) > 1.0 × 10³⁴ yr; τ(p → η⁰ μ⁺) > 4.7 × 10³³ yr. Predicted at sub-leading rates in GUT frameworks.

cells.content: p → K⁰ ℓ⁺ — neutral-K-meson + lepton channels bounded ~10³² yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → K⁰ e⁺) > 1.0 × 10³³ yr; τ(p → K⁰ μ⁺) > 3.5 × 10³² yr. SUSY-SU(5) predicts ~10³³ yr in standard scenarios.

cells.content: p → 3 leptons (e+ e- e+, e+ μ+ μ-, etc.) — exotic channels bounded ~10³² yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → e⁺ μ⁺ μ⁻) > 3.0 × 10³² yr; τ(p → e⁺ ν̄ν̄) > 1.7 × 10³² yr.

cells.content: Neutron-antineutron oscillation (Δ B = 2) — Super-K bounded τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s; future ESS-NNBAR program
realized_examples: Free-neutron oscillation bound (ILL Grenoble 1994): τ_n-n̄ > 0.86 × 10⁸ s. Bound-neutron-in-nucleus bound (Super-K 2015): τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s. ESS-NNBAR future experiment at European Spallation Source will push by ~3 orders of magnitude.
Predicted in B-L-violating BSM scenarios distinct from typical GUTs (e.g., Babu-Mohapatra 2013; some neutrino-mass models).

cells.content: Historical: IMB-1 and Kamiokande-I bounds 1980s — first definitive exclusion of minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5)
realized_examples: IMB-1 (Cleveland Salt Mine, 3300 ton water) ran 1981-1991. Kamiokande-I (3000 ton water) ran 1983-1995. Both observed atmospheric-neutrino background but no proton-decay events. By ~1990, τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 10³² yr — already 2-3 orders of magnitude beyond minimal-SU(5) prediction.
Historic significance: established proton-decay detection technology + decisively excluded the simplest GUT scenario.

cells.content: Hyper-Kamiokande future program — 260 kton fiducial × ~10× sensitivity over Super-K, target τ > 10³⁵ yr
realized_examples: Hyper-Kamiokande (Tochibora mine, Japan) commissioning 2027, full operation ~2029-2030. 260 kton fiducial water Cherenkov detector (~8× Super-K fiducial). Expected sensitivity τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 1 × 10³⁵ yr after 10 years — within reach of SUSY-SU(5) predictions for natural SUSY-mass parameter space.
DUNE (Sanford, USA) commissioning 2030+ with 40 kton liquid-argon TPC; complementary sensitivity for K-meson channels.

cells.content: DUNE future sensitivity to p → K⁺ ν̄ — liquid-argon TPC complementary to water Cherenkov
realized_examples: DUNE 40 kton liquid-argon TPC has lower threshold and better tracking for K⁺ identification than water Cherenkov. Expected τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) sensitivity > 4 × 10³⁴ yr after 10 years — improving over Super-K by ~7×.
Complementary to Hyper-K which is optimized for p → π⁰ e⁺.

cells.content: Bound-neutron decay modes — n → ν π⁰, n → e⁺ π⁻, etc.; bounds parallel to proton modes
realized_examples: Super-K bound: τ(n → ν̄ π⁰) > 1.1 × 10³³ yr; τ(n → e⁺ π⁻) > 5.3 × 10³³ yr. Bound neutrons in oxygen-16 nuclei provide bound-nucleon target with same fiducial mass as protons.

cells.content: Combined Super-K SUSY-SU(5) constraint — multi-channel bound pushes SUSY scale > ~10 TeV in minimal scenarios
realized_examples: Combined Super-K limits on p → K⁺ ν̄, p → π⁰ e⁺, p → η e⁺, etc., constrain SUSY-SU(5) parameter space. For natural-SUSY scenarios (squark mass ~ TeV), proton-decay rate would exceed bounds. Implies either (a) SUSY scale > 10 TeV (consistent with absence of LHC SUSY signals), (b) specific suppression mechanisms (e.g., flavor structure of Higgs-triplet couplings), or (c) SUSY-SU(5) is not realized.

cells.content: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) — alternative SU(5) embedding with suppressed dimension-6 proton decay
realized_examples: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) (Barr 1982; Derendinger-Kim-Nanopoulos 1984) embeds SM fermions differently than Georgi-Glashow. Dimension-6 proton-decay operators are absent at tree level; decay proceeds via different (suppressed) channels. Predicts τ_p > 10³⁵ yr generically — beyond Hyper-K reach.

cells.content: Pati-Salam SU(4)_c × SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R — quark-lepton unification without proton decay (in minimal version)
realized_examples: Pati-Salam (1974) is the original quark-lepton unification — leptons are 'fourth color'. The SU(4)_c gauge bosons mediate quark-lepton transitions but not directly baryon-number violation; minimal Pati-Salam preserves baryon number. Extensions (e.g., via R-parity violation or specific Higgs structures) can give proton decay; constrained but not generally falsified.
Distinct from SU(5)/SO(10): Pati-Salam is a partial unification rather than a full simple-group GUT.

cells.content: SO(10) GUT — predicts proton decay with rate dependent on Higgs-multiplet structure; bounded but generically viable
realized_examples: SO(10) GUT (Fritzsch-Minkowski 1975; Georgi 1975) embeds SM + right-handed neutrino in 16-rep. Proton decay via dimension-6 operators predicted at τ ~ 10³⁵ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁶ GeV. Higher M_GUT in SO(10) compared to SU(5) due to different beta-function running — natural suppression of proton-decay rate.

cells.content: Atmospheric-neutrino background — dominant systematic for proton-decay sensitivity at large fiducial masses
realized_examples: Atmospheric ν_μ-induced backgrounds dominate at high fiducial masses. Super-K and Hyper-K develop event-shape + ring-pattern + timing selection criteria to suppress. DUNE liquid-argon TPC has different background structure — better lepton-energy resolution but different particle-ID systematics.
Background-limited sensitivity scales as √(M × T) rather than M × T at high statistics — important for assessing Hyper-K's ultimate reach.

predictions.prediction: Proton decays via p → π⁰ e⁺ at τ_p ~ 10²⁹-10³⁰ yr (minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5))
predictions.description: Georgi-Glashow 1974 minimal SU(5) prediction τ_p ~ (M_GUT/M_W)⁴ × ℏ/(α² m_p) ≈ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁵ GeV. Decisively ruled out by Super-K (Takenaka et al. 2020) at τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr — 4-5 orders of magnitude beyond prediction. Type-A FALSIFIED — major historical exclusion of a specific GUT scenario. This is the 12th falsified prediction in the dataset.
predictions.prediction_citation: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
predictions.confirmation_citation: Takenaka et al. (Super-K) 2020, Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011

predictions.prediction: Proton decays via p → K⁺ ν̄ at τ_p ~ 10³³⁻³⁵ yr in SUSY-SU(5) with natural-scale SUSY parameters
predictions.description: SUSY-SU(5) dimension-5 operator prediction (Sakai-Yanagida 1982). For natural-SUSY squark/Higgs masses ~ TeV, predicts τ ~ 10³² yr — in tension with Super-K 2014 bound τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr. SUSY-SU(5) survives only at high SUSY scale (multi-TeV colored Higgs) or with specific flavor-suppression structures. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` — the natural-SUSY parameter space is excluded but the framework with non-natural SUSY remains viable.
predictions.prediction_citation: Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (Super-K) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 072005

predictions.prediction: SO(10) GUT predicts proton decay at τ ~ 10³⁵ yr (within Hyper-K reach)
predictions.description: SO(10) GUT M_GUT ~ 10¹⁶ GeV gives longer proton lifetime than SU(5). Predicted decay rate within Hyper-K + DUNE future sensitivity. Test pending.
predictions.prediction_citation: Fritzsch, Minkowski 1975; Georgi 1975

predictions.prediction: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) predicts τ_p > 10³⁵ yr (beyond Hyper-K reach)
predictions.description: Suppressed dimension-6 operators in flipped SU(5) give very long lifetime; possibly out of reach of even Hyper-K + DUNE.
predictions.prediction_citation: Barr 1982; Derendinger, Kim, Nanopoulos 1984

predictions.prediction: Pati-Salam SU(4)_c × SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R preserves baryon number in minimal form (no proton decay)
predictions.description: Pati-Salam-as-quark-lepton-unification doesn't predict proton decay. Consistent with all current data. Extensions can introduce baryon-number violation; those extensions are constrained.
predictions.prediction_citation: Pati, Salam 1974, Phys. Rev. D 10, 275

predictions.prediction: Neutron-antineutron oscillation at τ_n-n̄ ~ 10⁸ s in B-L-violating BSM
predictions.description: Mohapatra-Marshak 1980 B-L-violating prediction. Super-K 2015 bound τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s already constrains. ESS-NNBAR future experiment will probe further.
predictions.prediction_citation: Mohapatra, Marshak 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 1316
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (Super-K) 2015, Phys. Rev. D 91, 072006

predictions.prediction: Hyper-Kamiokande extends τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) bound to > 10³⁵ yr by 2040
predictions.description: Hyper-K 260 kton fiducial × 10 years operation projects τ-sensitivity of ~10³⁵ yr. If observed, it would establish proton decay; if not, further constrains SUSY-SU(5).
predictions.prediction_citation: Abe et al. (Hyper-K Collaboration) 2018, arXiv:1805.04163

predictions.prediction: DUNE provides complementary p → K⁺ ν̄ sensitivity via liquid-argon TPC
predictions.description: DUNE 40 kton LAr-TPC expected τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) sensitivity > 4 × 10³⁴ yr after 10 years. Better K⁺ identification than water Cherenkov.
predictions.prediction_citation: Abi et al. (DUNE) 2020, JINST 15, T08010

predictions.prediction: Combined SUSY-SU(5) constraint from Super-K multi-channel + LHC SUSY-search nulls pushes SUSY scale > 10 TeV
predictions.description: Super-K's combined limits on p → K⁺ ν̄, p → π⁰ e⁺, p → η e⁺ + LHC's absence of natural-SUSY signals jointly constrain SUSY-SU(5) into heavy-SUSY parameter space. Combined indirect-evidence claim is confirmed.
predictions.prediction_citation: Lucas, Raby 1996, Phys. Rev. D 54, 2261
predictions.confirmation_citation: Particle Data Group 2022 (proton-decay + SUSY review)

predictions.prediction: IMB + Kamiokande 1980s observations would either detect proton decay (consistent with minimal SU(5)) or set τ > 10³² yr (excluding minimal SU(5))
predictions.description: Historic prediction realized: IMB-1 + Kamiokande-I observed no proton-decay events 1981-1990, setting τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 10³² yr — already ruled out minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) by 1990. First experimental falsification of a specific GUT framework.
predictions.prediction_citation: Goldhaber, Lederman, Schopper 1980 era — multiple authors
predictions.confirmation_citation: Becker-Szendy et al. (IMB) 1990, Phys. Rev. D 42, 2974

formal_classifications.label: N=2 SCFT spectrum data
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'N2-SCFT-spectrum-data' (phenomenon; sector: Formal HE theory). Contains 16 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4: Coulomb-branch operator u with Δ_u = 2 (protected, exact)
realized_examples: Seiberg-Witten 1994: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 is conformal; Coulomb-branch operator u = ⟨Tr φ²⟩ has protected scaling dimension Δ_u = 2 (the canonical Coulomb-branch dimension for SU(2)).
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-su2-Nf4-coulomb.

cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4: a-anomaly = 23/24, c-anomaly = 7/6
realized_examples: Anselmi-Erlich-Freedman-Johansen 1997 / Intriligator-Wecht 2003 a-maximization gives exact central charges for SU(2) N_f=4 at the conformal point: a = 23/24, c = 7/6. The ratio a/c = 23/28 < 1 satisfies the Hofman-Maldacena bounds.

cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_0 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 6/5 (irrational scaling, exact)
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas 1995 discovered SCFTs at strong-coupling singular points of Coulomb branches with irrational Δ_u. H_0 theory (also called A_1 or (A_1, A_2) AD): rank-1 Coulomb branch with single Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 6/5.
First example of irrational Coulomb-branch dimension — distinguished feature of AD theories vs Lagrangian N=2 SCFTs.

cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_1 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 4/3
realized_examples: AD H_1 rank-1 theory: Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 4/3. Realized at specific Coulomb-branch singular point of SU(2) N_f=1 SQCD. Related H_2 has Δ_u = 3/2; together H_0/H_1/H_2 form the rank-1 AD series at low-rank end.

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 3 (rank-1, isolated SCFT)
realized_examples: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_6 flavor symmetry, Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 3. Realized from M5-brane on 3-punctured sphere with E_6 punctures (class S T_3 building block).
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-mn-e6-coulomb.

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory: Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_6, dimension 22
realized_examples: Higgs branch of MN E_6 is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_6 Lie algebra — 22-complex-dimensional hyperkähler quotient. Hilbert series counts holomorphic functions on this orbit and matches AGT-derived index calculations.
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-mn-e6-higgs.

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_7 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 4
realized_examples: MN E_7 theory: rank-1 isolated SCFT, E_7 flavor symmetry, Δ_u = 4. Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_7 (dim 34).

cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_8 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 6
realized_examples: MN E_8 theory: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_8 flavor symmetry, Δ_u = 6. Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_8 (dim 58). Largest exceptional MN theory.

cells.content: Schur index = vacuum character of associated 2D chiral algebra (Beem-Lemos-Rastelli et al. 2015 — 2D-4D correspondence)
realized_examples: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015: every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra (Vertex Operator Algebra). The Schur limit of the 4D superconformal index equals the vacuum character of this 2D chiral algebra.
Examples: AD H_0 ↔ (2,5) Virasoro minimal model; SU(2) N_f=4 ↔ affine so(8)_-2 chiral algebra; MN E_6 ↔ affine e_6_-3.

cells.content: AGT correspondence: SU(2) N_f=4 instanton partition function = 4-point Liouville conformal block (Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010)
realized_examples: Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010: Nekrasov instanton partition function Z_inst for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 equals 4-point conformal block in 2D Liouville CFT with c_Liouville = 1 + 6(b + 1/b)². Confirmed at multiple instanton orders via direct calculation.
Generalizations: SU(N) ↔ A_N Toda CFT; class-S theories ↔ Liouville/Toda on punctured Riemann surfaces (Gaiotto 2009).

cells.content: Class-S T_3 building block: M5-brane on 3-punctured sphere with three full punctures = Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory
realized_examples: Gaiotto 2009: M5-brane (2,0) A_2 theory on Riemann surface with three full punctures gives MN E_6 SCFT. T_N generalization: A_N-type M5-branes on 3-punctured sphere give 'T_N theories' with SU(N)³ flavor symmetry; T_2 trivial, T_3 = MN E_6, T_4 = rank-3 SCFT, etc.
Class-S construction systematizes the construction of large families of N=2 SCFTs from geometric data.
