cells.content: Conformal bootstrap bounds on a / c ratio for N=2 SCFTs — Hofman-Maldacena 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 + tighter SUSY-aware bounds
realized_examples: Hofman-Maldacena 2008 derived universal bound 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 for any unitary 4D CFT. For N=2 specifically, tighter bounds exist (Beem-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2014). Known SCFTs (AD, MN, SU(2) N=2 SQCD) satisfy bounds with various margins.
Stress-tensor 4-point function bootstrap (Beem et al. 2014) gives rigorous-bounded N=2 bootstrap data.

cells.content: BPS spectrum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 at generic Coulomb-branch point — Seiberg-Witten geometry derived
realized_examples: Seiberg-Witten 1994: BPS spectrum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 derived from genus-1 SW curve. At generic Coulomb-branch point, BPS multiplet includes W-bosons (electrically charged) + dyons (magnetically charged) + quarks (matter hypermultiplets) — full lattice structure determined by SW periods.
Spectrum of BPS states changes discontinuously across Coulomb-branch 'walls of marginal stability' — Denef-Moore 2007+.

cells.content: Pestun localization of S⁴ partition function for SU(2) N=2 SQCD (2007 / 2012) — exact ZZ-matrix-integral expression
realized_examples: Pestun 2012, Commun. Math. Phys. 313, 71: SUSY localization on S⁴ for N=2* SU(2) gauge theory and N=2 SU(2) SQCD reduces the path integral to a finite-dimensional matrix integral over Coulomb-branch scalar VEV. Exact result valid at all coupling.
Extended to SU(N) and class-S theories via subsequent work (Nekrasov-Okounkov, Pestun-Zabzine, etc.).

cells.content: Nekrasov instanton partition function — exact instanton sum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD via Omega-background regularization
realized_examples: Nekrasov 2003: exact instanton partition function Z_inst(a, m_i, ε_1, ε_2) for SU(2) N=2 SQCD computed via Omega-background regularization on R⁴ (replacing Coulomb-branch parameters and masses by VEVs). Z_inst is a power series in instanton number; convergent.
AGT identifies Z_inst with 2D Liouville 4-point conformal block — gives independent verification.

cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_0 Schur index = (2, 5) Virasoro minimal model vacuum character
realized_examples: Beem-Rastelli-van Rees 2018+: associated 2D chiral algebra for AD H_0 = (2, 5) Virasoro minimal model. Schur index of H_0 = vacuum character of (2, 5) minimal model = q^(-1/60) (1 + q² + q³ + q⁴ + 2q⁵ + ...).
Pattern: AD (A_1, A_{2n-1}) theories ↔ (2, 2n+3) Virasoro minimal models.

predictions.prediction: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 is conformal at the origin of moduli space with exact Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 2
predictions.description: Seiberg-Witten 1994 proved exact conformality via vanishing of β-function at N_f = 2 N_c = 4. Coulomb-branch operator scaling dimension Δ_u = 2 protected by N=2 SUSY + R-symmetry. Foundational result.
predictions.prediction_citation: Seiberg, Witten 1994, Nucl. Phys. B 431, 484
predictions.confirmation_citation: Seiberg, Witten 1994 (rigorous within N=2 framework)

predictions.prediction: Argyres-Douglas theories exist with irrational Coulomb-branch operator scaling dimensions
predictions.description: Argyres-Douglas 1995 demonstrated SCFTs at singular Coulomb-branch points with irrational Δ_u (e.g., 6/5, 4/3, 3/2 for H_0, H_1, H_2). Rigorous within N=2 SCFT framework via a-maximization.
predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Douglas 1995, Nucl. Phys. B 448, 93
predictions.confirmation_citation: Subsequent a-maximization (Anselmi et al. 1997, Intriligator-Wecht 2003)

predictions.prediction: AGT correspondence: SU(N) N=2 SQCD instanton partition function = SU(N) Toda CFT conformal block
predictions.description: Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010: original SU(2) ↔ Liouville. Generalized to SU(N) ↔ A_{N-1} Toda by Wyllard 2009 + subsequent verifications. Confirmed at multiple instanton orders.
predictions.prediction_citation: Alday, Gaiotto, Tachikawa 2010, Lett. Math. Phys. 91, 167
predictions.confirmation_citation: Wyllard 2009; multiple subsequent verifications

predictions.prediction: Every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra; Schur index = chiral-algebra vacuum character
predictions.description: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015: 2D-4D correspondence proved at the level of Schur-sector operators. Many specific examples verified: AD ↔ Virasoro minimal models, SU(2) N_f=4 ↔ affine so(8)_{-2}, MN E_6 ↔ affine e_6_{-3}.
predictions.prediction_citation: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359
predictions.confirmation_citation: Beem et al. 2015 (constructive proof)

predictions.prediction: Class-S construction: M5-brane (2,0) theory on Riemann surface with punctures gives 4D N=2 SCFT with flavor symmetry determined by puncture data
predictions.description: Gaiotto 2009: systematic construction of 4D N=2 SCFTs from M5-branes wrapped on punctured Riemann surfaces. Specific identifications: T_3 = MN E_6, T_4 = rank-3 SCFT, etc. Multi-channel verifications via index calculations, Higgs-branch identifications, AGT.
predictions.prediction_citation: Gaiotto 2009, JHEP 12, 034
predictions.confirmation_citation: Gaiotto 2009 + many subsequent

predictions.prediction: Hofman-Maldacena bound 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 holds for any 4D unitary CFT — N=2 SCFTs satisfy with margin
predictions.description: Hofman-Maldacena 2008 derived universal a/c bound from energy-flux positivity in 4D CFT. All known N=2 SCFTs (AD, MN, SU(N) SQCDs, class-S) satisfy bound with various margins.
predictions.prediction_citation: Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012
predictions.confirmation_citation: All known N=2 SCFTs verified to satisfy

predictions.prediction: Pestun localization: 4D N=2 partition function on S⁴ reduces to finite-dimensional matrix integral
predictions.description: Pestun 2012: rigorous proof of S⁴ partition function localization for N=2* and N=2 SU(2) gauge theories. Extended to many other cases.
predictions.prediction_citation: Pestun 2012, Commun. Math. Phys. 313, 71
predictions.confirmation_citation: Pestun 2012 (constructive proof)

predictions.prediction: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 has Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 3 with E_6 flavor symmetry from minimal nilpotent orbit Higgs branch
predictions.description: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_6 flavor symmetry. Δ_u = 3 from a-maximization; Higgs branch = E_6 minimal nilpotent orbit (dim 22). Verified via M5-brane class-S construction (T_3) and AGT.
predictions.prediction_citation: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, Nucl. Phys. B 482, 142
predictions.confirmation_citation: Confirmed via independent class-S construction (Gaiotto 2009) and AGT (Alday et al. 2010)

predictions.prediction: Higher-rank Argyres-Douglas theories form an infinite series with distinct Coulomb-branch spectra
predictions.description: Rank-2 and higher AD theories systematically constructed (Argyres-Lotito-Lü-Martone 2018+, Cecotti-Del Zotto). Predicted Coulomb-branch spectra computed via a-maximization. Some specific cases verified via class-S, but the full pattern at higher ranks awaits more independent checks.
predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lü, Martone 2018, JHEP 05, 199

predictions.prediction: N=2 superconformal-bootstrap exclusion regions tighten with operator-dimension input — eventually identify all N=2 SCFTs?
predictions.description: Numerical N=2 bootstrap continues to refine bounds; conjecture that all 4D N=2 SCFTs eventually appear at kinks of bootstrap exclusion-region boundaries (Liendo-Rastelli 2014+). Active research area with no definitive completion.
predictions.prediction_citation: Liendo, Rastelli 2014, JHEP 05, 100

formal_classifications.label: SPT phases and anomaly-matching observations
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'SPT-phase-and-anomaly-matching-observations' (phenomenon; sector: Formal HE theory). Contains 16 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: π⁰ → γγ decay rate from Adler-Bell-Jackiw chiral anomaly: Γ(π⁰ → γγ) = (α² m_π³)/(64 π³ F_π²) — exact result, confirmed to ~few percent
realized_examples: Adler 1969 + Bell-Jackiw 1969: triangle-diagram chiral anomaly gives π⁰ → γγ amplitude unambiguously. Predicted rate ~ 7.7 eV partial width.
Experimental: Γ(π⁰ → γγ) ≈ 7.83 eV (PDG 2022) — agreement at sub-percent level after radiative corrections.
PrimEx-II experiment (2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 011803): direct measurement Γ(π⁰ → γγ) = 7.802 ± 0.117 eV, agreement with NLO prediction.

cells.content: SM gauge anomaly cancellation — requires precise charge quantization (Y_L + Y_R relations); requires color-flavor matching
realized_examples: SM SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)_Y has perturbative gauge anomalies that cancel only because of specific quark and lepton charge assignments. SU(3)³ trivially cancels (vectorlike); SU(2)² × U(1)_Y requires Σ Y_L = 0 within doublets; U(1)_Y³ requires 6 Y_L^3 + 3 Y_R^3 = 0; mixed SU(3)² × U(1)_Y, SU(2)² × U(1)_Y, gravitational × U(1)_Y all constrained.

Result: SM matter content is uniquely determined (up to overall normalization) by gauge anomaly cancellation + minimal Higgs content. Predicts charge quantization (electron charge = -3 × down-quark charge).

cells.content: 't Hooft anomaly matching forces chiral symmetry breaking in QCD: SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V — pion as Goldstone boson
realized_examples: 't Hooft 1980: matching axial-current anomalies between UV (quark-gluon) and IR (hadrons) is incompatible with unbroken chiral symmetry for QCD with N_c = 3 colors and N_f ≥ 2 flavors. Therefore chiral symmetry MUST break.
Frishman-Schwimmer-Banks-Yankielowicz 1981: detailed verification of 't Hooft anomaly matching for QCD chiral breaking pattern.
Experimental: hadronic spectrum verifies SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V breaking pattern via pion Goldstone bosons (M_π ≪ Λ_QCD), η-η' singlet anomaly, etc.

cells.content: Wess-Zumino-Witten term in chiral Lagrangian — determines η, η' decay rates and U(1)_A anomaly
realized_examples: Wess-Zumino 1971 + Witten 1983 (Nucl. Phys. B 223, 422): chiral anomaly requires non-trivial topological term (WZW term) in low-energy chiral Lagrangian. Term level n is quantized for consistency.
Predictions: η → π⁺ π⁻ γ (anomaly-derived branching), η' decay rates, π⁰ π⁰ → γγ scattering at threshold.
U(1)_A anomaly explains η' mass — would be Goldstone if not for anomaly (Witten 1979, Veneziano 1979).

cells.content: Witten SU(2) global anomaly — requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; consistency with 3 generations confirmed
realized_examples: Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324: SU(2) gauge theory has Z_2 global anomaly π_4(SU(2)) = Z_2 — partition function changes sign under large gauge transformations unless the matter content has even number of left-handed SU(2) doublets.
Result: SM SU(2)_L gauge theory requires even number of left-handed doublets per generation; SM has 4 per generation (1 quark doublet × 3 colors + 1 lepton doublet = 4) — anomaly cancels.

cells.content: 3D topological insulator Z_2 invariant (Fu-Kane-Mele 2007) — confirmed via Bi₂Se₃ ARPES surface-state observation
realized_examples: Fu, Kane, Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803: Z_2 topological invariant for time-reversal-symmetric 3D insulators. Two distinct classes: Z_2 = 0 (trivial) and Z_2 = 1 (topological).

Experimental confirmation: Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ family (Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101; Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398). ARPES sees single Dirac-cone surface state, characteristic of Z_2 = 1.
Cell-level overlap with topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family.

cells.content: Integer quantum Hall conductivity = Chern number × e²/h — TKNN formula (Thouless-Kohmoto-Nightingale-den Nijs 1982)
realized_examples: TKNN 1982: integer QH conductivity σ_{xy} = ν × e²/h with ν = first Chern number of Bloch-band Berry curvature integrated over Brillouin zone. Z-valued topological invariant.
Confirmed: QH plateau at ν = e²/h quantized to part-per-billion precision (von Klitzing 1980); resistance standard (R_K = h/e² ≈ 25812.807 Ω) defined by this effect.

cells.content: Fidkowski-Kitaev Z_8 classification — interacting 3D topological superconductors classified by Z_8 instead of Z (non-interacting collapse to 8 distinct phases)
realized_examples: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509: free-fermion classification of 1D topological superconductors gives Z (number of Majorana zero modes per end), but generic 4-fermion interactions reduce this to Z_8 — phases differing by 8 collapse to the trivial phase.
Generalization to higher dimensions and other symmetry classes: Wen 2016, Freed-Hopkins 2016, Kapustin-Thorngren 2014 cobordism analysis.

cells.content: Majorana zero modes at p-wave superconductor boundaries — anomaly-inflow boundary state of 1D topological SC (Kitaev chain)
realized_examples: Kitaev 2001, Phys.-Usp. 44, 131: 1D spinless p-wave superconductor has Majorana zero mode at each end — boundary anomaly inflow of bulk SPT order.

Signatures observed: zero-bias-conductance-peak (ZBCP) in InSb/Al nanowires (Mourik et al. 2012, Science 336, 1003); InAs/Al (Albrecht et al. 2016, Nature 531, 206). But: NOT all ZBCPs are MZMs — recent retraction of 2018 Microsoft claim (Zhang et al. 2018 retraction).
Cell-level overlap with topo-mat v22 cell-ti-Microsoft-InSb-Al-2018-retracted.

cells.content: Parity anomaly on 3D TI surface — single Dirac fermion has anomalous parity, half-integer Hall conductance σ_xy = (n + 1/2) e²/h
realized_examples: Niemi, Semenoff 1983; Redlich 1984: single 2+1D Dirac fermion has parity anomaly, giving half-integer Chern-Simons level. Manifested on 3D TI surface as half-integer Hall conductance under broken time-reversal.
Confirmed via observations of half-integer quantum anomalous Hall states in magnetically doped TIs (Chang et al. 2013, Science 340, 167; subsequent refinements).

cells.content: Haldane SPT phase — S=1 antiferromagnetic chain has symmetry-protected gapped phase with edge spin-1/2 modes
realized_examples: Haldane 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1153: prediction that integer-spin Heisenberg antiferromagnetic chains have gapped ground state with hidden topological order.
Confirmed: Ni(C₂H₈N₂)₂NO₂ClO₄ ('NENP') and other S=1 chain materials show Haldane-gap phenomenology; edge spin-1/2 states observed.
Modern classification: Haldane phase = SPT with SO(3) × Z_2^T protection; Z_2 invariant.

cells.content: QCD θ-vacuum anomaly + experimental |θ| < 10⁻¹⁰ from neutron EDM = strong-CP problem (Peccei-Quinn axion proposed solution)
realized_examples: QCD has θ-vacuum term L_θ = (θ g_s²/32 π²) G G̃ from non-perturbative anomaly. CP-violating term that contributes to neutron EDM.
Experimental: |d_n| < 1.8 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm (Abel et al. 2020 ultracold neutron EDM, Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 081803), constrains |θ| < 1.5 × 10⁻¹⁰.
Strong-CP problem: why is θ ~ 0 to such precision? Natural value would be ~ 1. Peccei-Quinn 1977 proposed dynamical resolution via axion.

cells.content: Chern-Simons level quantization — gauge invariance under large gauge transformations forces integer CS level
realized_examples: Witten 1989: 3D Chern-Simons gauge theory consistency requires integer level k. Generalizes to integer-quantization of topological terms in higher-dimensional theories.
Application: Wess-Zumino term level in chiral Lagrangian quantized as integer (n = N_c = 3 for QCD); fractional QH ν = 1/m levels protected by m-fold flux composite-fermion structure.

cells.content: Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain in ultracold-atom lattices — SPT phase with Zak-phase invariant directly measured
realized_examples: Atala et al. 2013, Nature Phys. 9, 795: Zak phase of SSH chain measured directly in ultracold-atom optical lattice via Ramsey interferometry.
More recent: photonic SSH (Naz et al. 2018), polariton SSH (Solnyshkov et al. 2017), trapped-ion SSH simulations.

cells.content: Cobordism classification of SPT phases (Freed-Hopkins 2016, Kapustin-Thorngren 2014+) — predicts new SPT phases without confirmed material realizations
realized_examples: Freed, Hopkins 2016, arXiv:1604.06527: SPT phases in d spatial dimensions with symmetry group G classified by reduced cobordism groups Ω^G_{d+1}. Cross-references freed-hopkins-cobordism v8.
Predicted new SPT phases: time-reversal-protected fermionic SPTs with various global symmetries (Z_2^T extensions, U(1) × Z_2^T, etc.); some confirmed (3D TI), others awaiting material realization.

cells.content: Callan-Harvey anomaly-inflow mechanism — boundary anomaly = bulk topological term; established by domain-wall fermion analysis
realized_examples: Callan, Harvey 1985, Nucl. Phys. B 250, 427: anomalies localized on boundaries / domain walls inflow from bulk topological terms. Generalizes from index theorems to QFT.
Modern applications: domain-wall fermions in lattice QCD (Kaplan 1992); 3D TI surface states; M-theory cobordism via anomaly inflow.

predictions.prediction: π⁰ → γγ decay rate is determined by triangle anomaly: Γ = (α² m_π³)/(64 π³ F_π²) ≈ 7.7 eV
predictions.description: Adler-Bell-Jackiw 1969: chiral anomaly fixes π⁰ → γγ rate exactly (no renormalization beyond NLO QCD corrections). PrimEx-II 2014 measurement Γ = 7.802 ± 0.117 eV matches NLO prediction at sub-percent precision.
predictions.prediction_citation: Adler 1969, Phys. Rev. 177, 2426; Bell, Jackiw 1969, Nuovo Cim. A 60, 47
predictions.confirmation_citation: Larin et al. (PrimEx-II) 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 011803

predictions.prediction: SM matter content is uniquely constrained (up to overall normalization) by gauge anomaly cancellation — predicts charge quantization
predictions.description: Bouchiat-Iliopoulos-Meyer 1972 + Geng-Marshak 1989: SM gauge anomaly cancellation requires specific quark and lepton charge assignments. Predicts e⁻ charge = -3 × down-quark charge — verified experimentally.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519
predictions.confirmation_citation: All SM particle charges measured consistent with prediction

predictions.prediction: Chiral symmetry MUST break in QCD ('t Hooft 1980 anomaly matching argument)
predictions.description: 't Hooft 1980 + Frishman-Schwimmer-Banks-Yankielowicz 1981: anomaly matching between UV (quark-gluon) and IR (hadron) descriptions is incompatible with unbroken chiral symmetry. Confirmed by hadronic spectrum showing SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V breaking pattern with pion Goldstones.
predictions.prediction_citation: 't Hooft 1980, in Recent Developments in Gauge Theories
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hadronic spectrum confirmation

predictions.prediction: Witten SU(2) global anomaly requires even number of left-handed SM doublets per generation
predictions.description: Witten 1982: SU(2) gauge theory has Z_2 global anomaly π_4(SU(2)) = Z_2. SM has 4 left-handed doublets per generation (3 quark color copies + 1 lepton) — anomaly cancels.
predictions.prediction_citation: Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324
predictions.confirmation_citation: SM matter content verified by collider data

predictions.prediction: 3D time-reversal-symmetric insulators are classified by Z_2 invariant; non-trivial class has single Dirac-cone surface state
predictions.description: Fu-Kane-Mele 2007 prediction of Z_2 invariant + Dirac-cone surface state confirmed by ARPES on Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ family (Hsieh et al. 2009 + many).
predictions.prediction_citation: Fu, Kane, Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101

predictions.prediction: Integer quantum Hall conductivity = first Chern number × e²/h (TKNN formula)
predictions.description: Thouless-Kohmoto-Nightingale-den Nijs 1982: σ_xy quantized to ν × e²/h with ν = first Chern number. Confirmed by von Klitzing 1980 + subsequent precision measurements at part-per-billion.
predictions.prediction_citation: Thouless, Kohmoto, Nightingale, den Nijs 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 405
predictions.confirmation_citation: von Klitzing, Dorda, Pepper 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 45, 494

predictions.prediction: Free-fermion Z classification of 1D topological superconductors collapses to Z_8 under generic 4-fermion interactions (Fidkowski-Kitaev)
predictions.description: Fidkowski-Kitaev 2010: free Majorana classification Z reduces to Z_8 under interactions. Theoretically confirmed via cobordism (Freed-Hopkins 2016) and explicit interaction analysis. Material realization in 1D topological SCs remains limited but framework rigorously established.
predictions.prediction_citation: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509
predictions.confirmation_citation: Freed, Hopkins 2016 (cobordism)

predictions.prediction: Parity anomaly on 3D TI surface gives half-integer Hall conductance σ_xy = (n + 1/2) e²/h under broken T-reversal
predictions.description: Niemi-Semenoff 1983 + Redlich 1984 parity anomaly prediction confirmed by quantum anomalous Hall observation in magnetically-doped TIs (Chang et al. 2013).
predictions.prediction_citation: Niemi, Semenoff 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 51, 2077
predictions.confirmation_citation: Chang et al. 2013, Science 340, 167

predictions.prediction: Haldane integer-spin chain has gapped ground state with edge spin-1/2 modes (SPT order)
predictions.description: Haldane 1983 prediction confirmed in NENP (1989) and many subsequent S=1 chain materials. Modern interpretation: Haldane phase = SO(3)-protected SPT phase with Z_2 invariant.
predictions.prediction_citation: Haldane 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1153
predictions.confirmation_citation: Renard et al. 1988, Europhys. Lett. 3, 945 (NENP)

predictions.prediction: QCD θ-vacuum should produce neutron EDM ~ θ × 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm — strong-CP problem (Peccei-Quinn solution proposed)
predictions.description: Naive expectation: θ ~ O(1) gives |d_n| ~ 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm. Observed bound |d_n| < 1.8 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm (Abel et al. 2020) requires |θ| < 1.5 × 10⁻¹⁰. Why so small? Peccei-Quinn axion proposed dynamical solution. No axion detected yet — strong-CP remains unresolved.
predictions.prediction_citation: Naive expectation from QCD instanton physics
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abel et al. 2020 nEDM bound creates tension

formal_classifications.label: Modified gravity alternatives
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'modified-gravity-alternatives' (phenomenon; sector: BSM-non-GUT-non-DM). Contains 16 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: MOND explains galaxy rotation curves without dark matter via low-acceleration modification — empirical success at galactic scales
realized_examples: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365 (MOND proposal): modify Newton's 2nd law for a < a_0 ~ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s².

Strong empirical successes: explains rotation curves of ~150 disk galaxies (SPARC sample, Lelli-McGaugh-Schombert 2016) with single fitted parameter a_0; explains Tully-Fisher relation; explains baryonic Tully-Fisher relation (McGaugh 2011) — slope and normalization match MOND prediction.

Failures: (i) cluster-scale missing mass — MOND requires additional 'cluster dark matter' (residual factor ~2 missing); (ii) Bullet Cluster (Clowe et al. 2006) — gravitational lensing centered on collisionless mass, not gas — strong evidence for DM independent of modified gravity.

cells.content: Bekenstein TeVeS — relativistic MOND completion — broadly falsified by GW170817 graviton-speed bound
realized_examples: Bekenstein 2004, Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509: Tensor-Vector-Scalar relativistic theory reducing to MOND in nonrelativistic limit. Predicted graviton speed c_T ≠ c due to vector field coupling.

GW170817 (Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13): GW + GRB 170817A coincidence → c_T - c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ × c over 40 Mpc propagation. Falsifies generic TeVeS classes predicting Δv ~ O(1) or O(0.1).
Skordis et al. 2021 RMOND: GW170817-compatible MOND extension still under investigation.

cells.content: f(R) gravity as dark-energy alternative — constrained by LSS growth + CMB; chameleon-screened to evade solar-system PPN
realized_examples: Hu-Sawicki 2007, Phys. Rev. D 76, 064004: f(R) = R - m² c1 (R/m²)^n / (c2 (R/m²)^n + 1) provides Λ-like dark energy. Chameleon screening evades solar-system bounds.
Constraints: cosmological LSS growth f σ_8 < 0.05% deviation from GR allowed; CMB constraints from Planck. Specific Hu-Sawicki f(R) with |f_R0| < 10⁻⁴ — viable but very-near-GR.
Cassini PPN bound γ - 1 < (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵ requires |f_R0| < 10⁻⁶ in unscreened regime.

cells.content: DGP brane-world gravity — broadly disfavored by cosmological LSS + ruled out for graviton speed by GW170817
realized_examples: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208: 5D Einstein-Hilbert + 4D induced Einstein-Hilbert on brane → 4D IR-modified gravity with crossover scale r_c.

Self-accelerating branch (proposed dark-energy alternative): conflicts with Type Ia SN cosmography (Maartens, Majerotto 2006), CMB+LSS data (Lombriser et al. 2009), and predicts Δv/c ~ O(H₀ r_c) which conflicts with GW170817.
Normal branch + ghost issues remain (Charmousis et al. 2006).

cells.content: Horndeski-class scalar-tensor theories — most subclasses broadly falsified by GW170817; only narrow surviving variants remain
realized_examples: Horndeski 1974 + modern revival (Deffayet-Esposito-Farese-Vikman 2009): most general 2nd-order scalar-tensor Lagrangian in 4D. Includes Galileons, kinetic gravity braiding, etc.
Pre-GW170817: large parameter space allowed.

Post-GW170817 (Lombriser-Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382; Creminelli-Vernizzi 2017; Sakstein-Jain 2017): Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ requires G_4(φ,X) function be independent of X (kinetic term) — kills G_5 and most of G_4. Only quintessence-like + non-minimally coupled subset survives.
Subsequently: 'Beyond-Horndeski' DHOST class (Crisostomi et al. 2016) similarly heavily constrained.

cells.content: dRGT massive gravity — graviton mass bounded m_g < 6 × 10⁻²³ eV; consistent with GW170817 within bounds
realized_examples: de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101: ghost-free massive-graviton Lagrangian. Predicts dispersion relation E² = p² c² + m_g² c⁴.
GW170817 + GW150914 LIGO/Virgo dispersion-test bounds: m_g < 1.76 × 10⁻²³ eV/c² (Abbott et al. 2021 GWTC-3 + various single-event analyses).
Solar-system + LLR bounds independently constrain m_g.

cells.content: Hassan-Rosen bimetric gravity (two interacting metrics) — broadly disfavored by GW170817 propagation-speed bound
realized_examples: Hassan, Rosen 2012, JHEP 02, 126: ghost-free bimetric extension of dRGT massive gravity, two interacting tensor fields with mass mixing.
Bimetric theories predict generically Δv/c ≠ 0 from kinetic mixing — broadly falsified by GW170817 unless tuned to be near-GR.
Cosmological solutions: bigravity tested as dark-energy alternative; tight constraints from CMB + LSS (Akrami et al. 2018).

cells.content: PSR J0737-3039 double-pulsar — confirms GR's quadrupole formula to ~0.01%, constrains scalar-tensor ω_BD > 40,000 (Cassini bound)
realized_examples: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050: 16 years of precision timing of PSR J0737-3039 (the double pulsar). Tests 7 post-Keplerian relations of GR simultaneously.
Confirms GR orbital decay (quadrupole GW emission) to 0.013%. Largest-effect non-GR alternative: Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor → tests put ω_BD > 1.4 × 10⁵ (combined with Cassini).
Hulse-Taylor PSR B1913+16 (1974+): first binary-pulsar; ~10% precision tests; tightened by J0737-3039.

cells.content: Solar-system PPN parameter γ — Cassini Shapiro-time-delay: γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵, constrains Brans-Dicke ω_BD > 40,000
realized_examples: Bertotti, Iess, Tortora 2003, Nature 425, 374: Cassini superior-conjunction Shapiro-time-delay measurement gives γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵. Most precise PPN test of GR.
Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor predicts γ - 1 = -1/(2 + ω_BD); Cassini bound → ω_BD > 40,000 (original Brans-Dicke 1961 predicted ω ~ 1).

cells.content: LIGO/Virgo binary-merger waveforms — tests of post-Newtonian + ringdown phase against GR; constrains massive-graviton + Horndeski variants
realized_examples: LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3 (Abbott et al. 2021, Astrophys. J. 932, 49): tests of various PN coefficients + parametrized inspiral / ringdown / merger phases vs. GR templates. Bounds on Brans-Dicke ω_BD, massive graviton m_g, Lorentz-violation parameters, dispersion test coefficients.
All current LIGO/Virgo tests consistent with GR; bounds on deviations placed.

cells.content: EHT M87 / Sgr A* BH shadow tests — shadow diameter consistent with Kerr-BH prediction; constrains modified-gravity BH variants
realized_examples: EHT M87 (2019) shadow diameter 42 ± 3 μas, consistent with Kerr-BH prediction at ~1% precision. EHT Sgr A* (2022) similar.
Constraints: non-Kerr 'hair' parameters bounded; BH shadow modifications from scalar-tensor or Horndeski variants constrained to < few % of Kerr value.
Active program: testing Kerr-no-hair theorem via near-horizon imaging.

cells.content: Chameleon / symmetron screening mechanisms — laboratory fifth-force bounds + Eöt-Wash + atomic-interferometry tests
realized_examples: Khoury, Weltman 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 171104 (chameleon proposal): density-dependent scalar mass evades solar-system constraints by screening in high-density regions.
Tests: Eöt-Wash torsion balance + Casimir-force experiments + atom-interferometry (Hamilton et al. 2015 atomic interferometer chameleon search) constrain parameter space.
Galaxy-cluster + cosmological constraints (CHIPS catalog, GR-tests).
Specific symmetron / chameleon coupling × mass parameter regions ruled out.

cells.content: Einstein-aether (Lorentz-violating gravity) — c_T - c constraints from GW170817 strongly disfavor most natural parameter space
realized_examples: Jacobson, Mattingly 2001, Phys. Rev. D 64, 024028: timelike vector field breaking local Lorentz symmetry, modifies GW propagation.
GW170817 bound: Einstein-aether's c_a parameters (kinetic vector couplings) constrained to |c_a| < 10⁻¹⁵; only highly-tuned points survive.
Hořava-Lifshitz gravity (related class) similarly constrained.

cells.content: MOND on galaxy-cluster scales — Bullet Cluster gravitational lensing shows mass offset from gas, strong evidence against pure MOND
realized_examples: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109: Bullet Cluster (1E 0657-56) merging cluster system — weak-lensing mass map is offset from X-ray gas (which traces baryons). Suggests collisionless dark matter spatially separated from baryons during cluster merger.

Pure MOND cannot account for this offset: gravitational lensing should follow baryonic mass distribution in MOND. Requires either 'cluster dark matter' (~ neutrinos with eV mass — ruled out by cosmology) or breakdown of MOND.
Additional cluster pairs: MACS J0025.4-1222 (Bradač et al. 2008), ZwCl 0024+17 — similar mass-baryon offsets.

cells.content: RMOND (Skordis-Złośnik 2021) — GW170817-compatible relativistic MOND with c_T = c by construction
realized_examples: Skordis, Złośnik 2021, Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 161302: 'RMOND' relativistic MOND theory designed to be GW170817-compatible (graviton speed = c by construction).
Reproduces MOND phenomenology at galactic scales; cosmological consequences under investigation; not yet fully tested against CMB + LSS data.
Highly tuned construction; whether 'natural' completion of MOND remains debated.

cells.content: Galileon screened-scalar gravity — Vainshtein screening keeps consistent with solar-system PPN; constrained by cosmological data
realized_examples: Nicolis, Rattazzi, Trincherini 2009, Phys. Rev. D 79, 064036: Galileon scalar field with derivative couplings. Vainshtein screening at small scales evades solar-system bounds.
Cosmological viability: large-scale-structure growth tests (Khoury 2010 review); recent post-GW170817 analyses tightly constrain Galileons.
Most natural cubic-Galileon dark-energy realizations ruled out by GW170817 (Sakstein-Jain 2017).

predictions.prediction: MOND with a_0 ~ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s² explains galaxy rotation curves without dark matter
predictions.description: Milgrom 1983 prediction confirmed at galactic scales: SPARC sample (Lelli-McGaugh-Schombert 2016) of ~150 disk galaxies — rotation curves fit with single a_0; baryonic Tully-Fisher relation matches MOND prediction.
predictions.prediction_citation: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365
predictions.confirmation_citation: McGaugh et al. 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 117, 201101 (SPARC); Lelli, McGaugh, Schombert 2016

predictions.prediction: MOND alone can explain galaxy-cluster missing mass without dark matter
predictions.description: Pure MOND falsified at cluster scales by Bullet Cluster (Clowe et al. 2006): gravitational-lensing mass is spatially offset from baryonic mass during cluster merger, requires non-baryonic dark matter or breakdown of MOND.
predictions.prediction_citation: Implicit MOND-only DM-replacement at all scales
predictions.confirmation_citation: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

predictions.prediction: TeVeS (Bekenstein 2004) relativistic MOND completion produces GW propagation speed c_T differing from c
predictions.description: TeVeS predicts c_T ≠ c from vector-field gravitational coupling. GW170817 (Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶) falsifies generic TeVeS classes. Only highly-tuned post-2017 extensions (RMOND) survive.
predictions.prediction_citation: Bekenstein 2004, Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509
predictions.confirmation_citation: Boran, Desai, Kahya, Woodard 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 041501

predictions.prediction: DGP self-accelerating branch reproduces cosmological dark-energy without Λ
predictions.description: DGP self-accelerating branch as DE alternative ruled out by combined Type Ia SN cosmography + LSS growth + CMB data (Lombriser et al. 2009). GW170817 further constrains the model.
predictions.prediction_citation: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208 (DGP); Deffayet 2001 (DGP cosmology)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Lombriser, Hu, Fang, Seljak 2009, Phys. Rev. D 80, 063536

predictions.prediction: Horndeski-class scalar-tensor theories produce graviton speed c_T deviating from c
predictions.description: GW170817 bound Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ rules out generic Horndeski G_5 and most G_4(φ,X) terms. Only narrow surviving subset (G_4(φ) function of φ only, no X-dependence) remains.
predictions.prediction_citation: Generic Horndeski expectation: O(1) modifications to c_T
predictions.confirmation_citation: Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382; Creminelli, Vernizzi 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 251302

predictions.prediction: Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor PPN parameter γ deviates significantly from GR value γ = 1
predictions.description: Original Brans-Dicke 1961 prediction γ ≠ 1 with ω_BD ~ O(1). Cassini Shapiro-time-delay (Bertotti et al. 2003): γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵, requires ω_BD > 4 × 10⁴ — falsifies original natural-parameter prediction.
predictions.prediction_citation: Brans, Dicke 1961, Phys. Rev. 124, 925
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bertotti, Iess, Tortora 2003, Nature 425, 374

predictions.prediction: GR's quadrupole GW emission formula predicts binary-pulsar orbital decay rate
predictions.description: Hulse-Taylor PSR B1913+16 + PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar timing confirms GR orbital decay to ~0.01% precision (Kramer et al. 2021). Multi-channel post-Keplerian tests all consistent with GR.
predictions.prediction_citation: Einstein 1916 + Peters-Mathews 1963 (GR quadrupole formula)
predictions.confirmation_citation: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050

predictions.prediction: Massive graviton (dRGT class) has mass m_g detectable via dispersion of GW signal
predictions.description: dRGT massive-gravity predicts E² = p² c² + m_g² c⁴ dispersion. LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3 bounds: m_g < 1.76 × 10⁻²³ eV. No detection; mass is bounded but could be just below current sensitivity. Future LISA + Einstein Telescope will tighten by 1-2 orders of magnitude.
predictions.prediction_citation: de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. 2021, Astrophys. J. 932, 49 (bounds only)

predictions.prediction: Modified-gravity BH solutions deviate from Kerr — EHT shadow imaging tests
predictions.description: Various modified-gravity proposals predict modified BH structure (hair, deviations from Kerr-no-hair). EHT M87 + Sgr A* shadow tests bound deviations to ~few %. Specific predictions not detected; future EHT-next + ngEHT will tighten.
predictions.prediction_citation: Various modified-gravity hairy-BH proposals
predictions.confirmation_citation: Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104 (bounds only)

predictions.prediction: Chameleon / symmetron screening allows light scalar fields to evade solar-system fifth-force tests
predictions.description: Khoury-Weltman 2004 prediction: chameleon screening evades fifth-force bounds in dense environments. Confirmed mechanism — chameleon-mass enhancement in laboratory + solar-system densities verified. Parameter-space bounded by Eöt-Wash + atomic-interferometry + cosmological tests (Hamilton et al. 2015 + others).
predictions.prediction_citation: Khoury, Weltman 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 171104
predictions.confirmation_citation: Hamilton et al. 2015, Science 349, 849 + various

formal_classifications.label: Macroscopic and classical phenomena
formal_classifications.description: Classification of type 'macroscopic-classical-phenomena' (phenomenon; sector: Macroscopic / classical). Contains 16 cells across 3 axes. Closure level: partial. Detailed prose description not embedded in this snapshot — click 'Refresh from live server' for the full record.

cells.content: Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range energy spectrum: E(k) ~ k^(-5/3) — confirmed at high Reynolds numbers in DNS and laboratory experiments
realized_examples: Kolmogorov 1941, Dokl. Akad. Nauk SSSR 30, 301: dimensional argument from energy-cascade rate ε gives E(k) ~ ε^(2/3) k^(-5/3) in inertial range.
DNS confirmation: at Re_λ ~ 1000+, inertial range spans 1-2 decades in k with measured spectral slope -1.67 ± 0.02 (consistent with -5/3 with multifractal correction).
Atmospheric / oceanographic measurements: -5/3 slope confirmed over wide range of conditions.

cells.content: Pipe flow transition to turbulence at Re ~ 2300 (Reynolds 1883)
realized_examples: Reynolds 1883, Phil. Trans. R. Soc. 174, 935: critical Reynolds number Re_c ~ 2000-2300 for transition from laminar to turbulent flow in pipe flow. Mechanism: linear-instability-amplified disturbances trigger nonlinear breakdown.

Modern: subcritical instability with finite-amplitude threshold; recent precise experiments + DNS (Avila et al. 2011, Science 333, 192) measure decay-vs-splitting balance of turbulent puffs gives Re_c = 2040 ± 10.

cells.content: Multifractal intermittency corrections to K41 — Sq(p) = ζ_p with anomalous scaling deviating from p/3
realized_examples: Frisch-Parisi multifractal model (1985): higher-order velocity-structure-function scaling Sq(p) = ζ_p deviates from p/3 (K41 prediction). Anomalous exponent reflects intermittency.
Measurements: ζ_4 ≈ 1.28 ± 0.02 vs K41 prediction 4/3 ≈ 1.33; ζ_6 ≈ 1.78 vs K41 prediction 2.0. Deviations grow with p — characteristic of multifractal cascades.
She-Lévêque 1994 (Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 336) ζ_p = p/9 + 2 - 2(2/3)^(p/3) provides excellent empirical fit.

cells.content: NIF inertial-confinement fusion ignition December 5, 2022 — Q_fusion = 1.54 (3.15 MJ output / 2.05 MJ laser drive)
realized_examples: Abu-Shawareb et al. (NIF) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 065102: 8 December 2022 shot achieved Q > 1 inertial-confinement fusion ignition. Target gain Q = E_fusion / E_laser = 1.54; 3.15 MJ fusion yield from 2.05 MJ laser drive.
First scientific breakeven (Q > 1) ever achieved in laboratory inertial-confinement fusion. Confirmed Lawson hot-spot ignition criterion via direct experimental realization.
Subsequent NIF shots have reproduced and exceeded the 2022 ignition result.

cells.content: JET D-T plasma December 2021 — 59 MJ fusion energy in 5-second pulse, Q ~ 0.33
realized_examples: JET 2021 DT campaign: 59 MJ fusion energy released over 5-second pulse — > 2× the 1997 JET DT energy record.
Q ~ 0.33 in D-T fusion — still substantially below breakeven, but validates ITER's D-T scaling predictions.
Continues into JET 2023 final campaign before decommissioning.

cells.content: MMS spacecraft direct measurement of magnetic-reconnection diffusion region in Earth's magnetotail (2017+)
realized_examples: Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission, NASA: 4 spacecraft in tetrahedral formation with high time resolution (30 ms) plasma + B-field measurements. First direct in-situ observation of electron-scale diffusion region in magnetic reconnection (Burch et al. 2016, Science 352, aaf2939).
Subsequent: high-time-resolution magnetotail reconnection events characterize Hall current systems, electron jet outflows, and ion-scale current sheets.

cells.content: Magnetic Reconnection Experiment (MRX, Princeton) — laboratory measurement of fast reconnection rates
realized_examples: Yamada et al. (MRX) 1997+: laboratory device sustaining controlled magnetic-reconnection geometry with diagnostic access. Measures reconnection rates, plasmoid dynamics, particle acceleration spectra.
Confirms transition from Sweet-Parker (slow, ∝ Lundquist^(-1/2)) to Petschek-like fast reconnection in high-Lundquist-number / collisionless regime.

cells.content: Solar flare magnetic-reconnection signature — Yohkoh / RHESSI / SDO / Hinode multi-spacecraft confirmations
realized_examples: Yohkoh 1991+ (X-ray): cusp-shaped reconnection-event observations in solar flares.
TRACE / SDO 2010+ (EUV imaging): plasmoid ejections, current-sheet brightening, post-flare loops consistent with reconnection.
RHESSI (hard X-ray): nonthermal electron acceleration at reconnection sites.

cells.content: Parker Solar Probe solar-wind kinetic-scale cascade — first measurements of inertial-range turbulence inside 50 R_sun
realized_examples: NASA Parker Solar Probe (2018+, NASA mission): close-approach (down to ~10 R_sun perihelion) solar-wind plasma + B-field measurements.
Confirms inertial-range turbulence Kolmogorov spectrum in near-Sun solar wind; characterizes 'switchback' structures and kinetic-scale plasma physics in young solar wind.
Bandyopadhyay et al. 2020, Astrophys. J. Suppl. 246, 48 (turbulent cascade measurements).

cells.content: Jarzynski equality ⟨e^(-βW)⟩ = e^(-βΔF) — confirmed by single-molecule RNA-pulling experiments
realized_examples: Jarzynski 1997, Phys. Rev. Lett. 78, 2690: rigorous nonequilibrium work relation connecting work distributions to free-energy differences.
Liphardt et al. 2002, Science 296, 1832: first single-molecule experimental verification using optical-tweezer RNA unfolding/refolding. Subsequent: Collin et al. 2005, Nature 437, 231 + many.
Generalizations: Crooks fluctuation theorem (Crooks 1999); steady-state fluctuation theorems.

cells.content: Crooks fluctuation theorem P_F(W) / P_R(-W) = e^(β(W-ΔF)) — verified in RNA pulling experiments
realized_examples: Crooks 1999, Phys. Rev. E 60, 2721: ratio of forward-protocol work distribution P_F(W) to reverse-protocol P_R(-W) given by exponential of work-minus-free-energy difference.

Collin et al. 2005, Nature 437, 231: experimental verification using optical-tweezer RNA hairpin unfolding/refolding. Plot of ln[P_F(W)/P_R(-W)] vs W gives slope β = 1/k_B T and intercept βΔF — extracts ΔF from far-from-equilibrium work measurements.

cells.content: Tokamak H-mode confinement (Wagner 1982) — L-mode to H-mode transition at sufficient heating power
realized_examples: Wagner et al. (ASDEX) 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 1408: discovery of high-confinement (H-mode) regime in tokamak with edge pedestal formation.
Confirmed in nearly all subsequent tokamaks: JET, DIII-D, JT-60, EAST. H-mode is baseline operating mode for ITER design.
Edge-localized-modes (ELMs) — quasi-periodic edge instabilities releasing pedestal energy — characterize H-mode operation. ELM mitigation is active research area.

cells.content: ITER projected first plasma 2034+, Q ≥ 10 fusion-power gain target
realized_examples: ITER is under construction at Cadarache, France (international 7-party collaboration). Target: 500 MW fusion from 50 MW heating (Q = 10) in 400-second pulses.
Updated timeline (2023): first plasma ~ 2034-2035 (delayed from earlier 2025 target); DT operation ~ 2039+.
Validates tokamak path to fusion-relevant Q values; will confirm or refine Lawson-criterion + alpha-particle heating predictions in burning plasma.

cells.content: Granular jamming transition at packing fraction φ_J ~ 0.64 — confirmed via mechanical and structural measurements
realized_examples: Liu, Nagel 1998, Nature 396, 21 (jamming framework): jamming transition where amorphous granular packing develops finite mechanical rigidity at critical packing fraction.
Confirmed: φ_J ~ 0.64 for monodisperse spheres; signature scaling Z ~ Z_c + (φ - φ_J)^(1/2) for contact number; bulk modulus K ~ (φ - φ_J)^(1/2).
O'Hern et al. 2003, Phys. Rev. E 68, 011306 (jamming transition scaling).

cells.content: Vicsek-model collective motion — confirmed by bacterial suspensions, colloidal active particles, in vitro cytoskeletal systems
realized_examples: Vicsek et al. 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 1226: minimal model of self-propelled particles aligning with neighbors → flocking transition.

Experimental realizations: Bacillus subtilis suspensions (Wensink et al. 2012); colloidal self-propelled particles (Bricard et al. 2013, Nature 503, 95); microtubule + kinesin in vitro (Sanchez et al. 2012, Nature 491, 431).
Confirmed long-range order in 2D self-propelled systems despite Mermin-Wagner prohibition for equilibrium systems.

cells.content: Rayleigh-Bénard convection Nu(Ra) ~ Ra^(1/3) at high Ra — confirmed across many orders of magnitude
realized_examples: Rayleigh 1916: convective instability at Ra > Ra_c ~ 1708.
Modern: at high Ra (~ 10^7-10^16), Nusselt-number scaling Nu ~ Ra^(0.31) measured precisely.
Cryogenic helium experiments + DNS: scaling-law breaks down at Ra > 10^13 transitioning to ultimate Ra^(1/2) regime (Niemela et al. 2000 + theoretical predictions Kraichnan 1962).

predictions.prediction: Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range energy spectrum E(k) ~ k^(-5/3) at high Reynolds numbers
predictions.description: Kolmogorov 1941 dimensional prediction confirmed at Re_λ ~ 1000+ in DNS, laboratory, atmospheric, and oceanographic measurements. Spectral slope -1.67 with small multifractal intermittency corrections.
predictions.prediction_citation: Kolmogorov 1941, Dokl. Akad. Nauk SSSR 30, 301
predictions.confirmation_citation: Frisch 1995, Turbulence (Cambridge) summarizing decades of confirmation

predictions.prediction: Pipe flow transition to turbulence at critical Reynolds number Re_c ~ 2300 (Reynolds 1883)
predictions.description: Reynolds 1883 original observation refined by modern decay-vs-splitting analysis (Avila et al. 2011) → Re_c = 2040 ± 10.
predictions.prediction_citation: Reynolds 1883, Phil. Trans. R. Soc. 174, 935
predictions.confirmation_citation: Avila et al. 2011, Science 333, 192

predictions.prediction: NIF inertial-confinement-fusion achieves Q > 1 ignition via hot-spot Lawson criterion
predictions.description: Lindl 1995 ICF hot-spot ignition prediction confirmed by NIF December 5 2022 shot: Q = 1.54 (3.15 MJ from 2.05 MJ drive). First laboratory Q > 1 fusion.
predictions.prediction_citation: Lawson 1957, Proc. Phys. Soc. B 70, 6 (Lawson criterion); Lindl 1995 hot-spot ICF
predictions.confirmation_citation: Abu-Shawareb et al. (NIF) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 065102

predictions.prediction: JET D-T plasma demonstrates substantial fusion energy yield in 5-second pulses
predictions.description: JET 2021 DT campaign: 59 MJ fusion energy in 5-second pulse — > 2× the 1997 record. Validates predicted DT plasma performance scaling for ITER.
predictions.prediction_citation: ITER Physics Basis 2007 + JET DT predictions
predictions.confirmation_citation: JET Team 2022 DT 2021 results

predictions.prediction: Fast magnetic reconnection rate ~ 0.1 c_A (Alfvén velocity) in collisionless plasma — Hall-reconnection / plasmoid-instability regime
predictions.description: Petschek 1964 + Drake-Shay-Swisdak Hall-reconnection theory + plasmoid-instability theory (Loureiro et al. 2007) predicts fast reconnection rate. Confirmed in MMS spacecraft observations (Earth magnetotail) and MRX laboratory experiment.
predictions.prediction_citation: Petschek 1964, NASA SP-50, 425; Drake, Shay 1998
predictions.confirmation_citation: Burch et al. (MMS) 2016, Science 352, aaf2939

predictions.prediction: Jarzynski equality ⟨e^(-βW)⟩ = e^(-βΔF) holds rigorously for arbitrary nonequilibrium protocols
predictions.description: Jarzynski 1997 rigorous proof confirmed in single-molecule RNA-pulling experiments (Liphardt et al. 2002) and many subsequent verifications across systems.
predictions.prediction_citation: Jarzynski 1997, Phys. Rev. Lett. 78, 2690
predictions.confirmation_citation: Liphardt et al. 2002, Science 296, 1832

predictions.prediction: Multifractal intermittency corrections to K41: structure-function scaling ζ_p deviates from p/3 at high p
predictions.description: She-Lévêque multifractal model (1994) gives explicit form for ζ_p deviating from K41 prediction p/3. Confirmed in high-precision DNS + experimental velocity-structure-function measurements.
predictions.prediction_citation: Frisch-Parisi multifractal model 1985; She, Lévêque 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 336
predictions.confirmation_citation: Many high-Re experimental + DNS confirmations

predictions.prediction: Tokamak H-mode transition at sufficient heating power — characterized edge pedestal formation
predictions.description: Wagner et al. ASDEX 1982 discovery of H-mode confinement regime confirmed in subsequent tokamaks (JET, DIII-D, EAST, etc.); standard operating mode underlying ITER design.
predictions.prediction_citation: Wagner et al. 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 1408
predictions.confirmation_citation: Universal tokamak H-mode reproduction since 1982

predictions.prediction: Vicsek-model self-propelled particles exhibit long-range orientational order in 2D — flocking transition
predictions.description: Vicsek et al. 1995 model prediction confirmed in bacterial suspensions, colloidal active particles, microtubule-kinesin in vitro systems. Active matter breaks Mermin-Wagner equilibrium prohibition on 2D long-range order.
predictions.prediction_citation: Vicsek et al. 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 1226
predictions.confirmation_citation: Bricard et al. 2013, Nature 503, 95 (colloidal); many subsequent

predictions.prediction: ITER will achieve fusion-power-gain Q ≥ 10 in burning-plasma regime
predictions.description: ITER design target: 500 MW fusion from 50 MW heating in 400-second pulses. First plasma now targeted 2034+; DT operation ~2039+. The Q ≥ 10 prediction tests scaling of tokamak confinement + alpha-particle self-heating in burning plasma regime — not previously achieved.
predictions.prediction_citation: ITER Physics Basis 2007, Nucl. Fusion 47

Trivial MTC: only the vacuum object 1. Fusion rules: 1 ⊗ 1 = 1. S-matrix is the 1×1 identity. Central charge c = 0.
realized_examples: Trivial gapped phase (no topological order)
Vec (the category of complex vector spaces, as a trivial braided fusion category)

all_cells.content: Semion MTC: objects {1, s} with s ⊗ s = 1 and braiding eigenvalue i (the semion's exchange phase is i, hence 'half a fermion'). Chiral central charge c = 1 (mod 8 for bosonic structure). Modular S = (1/√2) [[1,1],[1,-1]].
realized_examples: U(1)_2 Chern-Simons theory
Kalmeyer-Laughlin chiral spin liquid (proposed for triangular antiferromagnets)
Boundary of (2+1)D bosonic IQHE-like phase with σ_xy = 2 (in units of e²/h)

all_cells.content: Fibonacci MTC: objects {1, τ} with τ ⊗ τ = 1 ⊕ τ (the Fibonacci fusion rule). Quantum dimension d_τ = φ = (1+√5)/2 (the golden ratio). Central charge c = 14/5. Non-abelian — τ is a non-abelian anyon.
realized_examples: (G_2)_1 Chern-Simons theory
Yang-Lee non-unitary CFT (after a unitarization)
Predicted in certain fractional Chern insulators (no confirmed laboratory realization as of 2026)

all_cells.content: Lee-Yang MTC: non-unitary; objects {1, τ_LY} with τ_LY ⊗ τ_LY = 1 ⊕ τ_LY. Same fusion rule as Fibonacci but negative quantum dimension d_{τ_LY} = -1/φ < 0. Central charge c = -22/5.
realized_examples: Lee-Yang edge singularity in dilute Ising at imaginary magnetic field
Minimal model M(2,5) (non-unitary)

all_cells.content: Ising MTC: objects {1, σ, ψ} with fusion σ ⊗ σ = 1 ⊕ ψ, σ ⊗ ψ = σ, ψ ⊗ ψ = 1. Quantum dimensions: d_1 = d_ψ = 1, d_σ = √2. Central charge c = 1/2 (mod 8). Non-abelian — σ is a non-abelian anyon (Majorana-like).
realized_examples: Majorana zero modes at vortex cores of (p+ip) superconductors
Boundary of (2+1)D Kitaev's honeycomb model in the gapped non-abelian (B) phase
Predicted (with partial confirmation) in ν=5/2 FQHE Moore-Read Pfaffian state

all_cells.content: SU(2)_2 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1}. Central charge c = 3·2/(2+2) = 3/2. Distinct from Ising despite both being rank-3 with σ-like anyon — SU(2)_2 has different chiral central charge and different F-symbols.
realized_examples: SU(2)_2 WZW model
Effective theory of a chiral spin liquid with SU(2) global symmetry

all_cells.content: Tricritical Ising MTC (within rank-3 unitary classification): central charge c = 7/10. Three primaries with conformal weights (0, 1/10, 6/10) in the unitary M(4,5) minimal model.
realized_examples: Tricritical Ising phase transition in dilute Ising magnet
Boundary of supersymmetric (2+1)D topological superconductor

all_cells.content: Toric code MTC (Z_2 gauge theory): objects {1, e, m, ε} where ε = e⊗m. Fusion: e⊗e = m⊗m = ε⊗ε = 1; e⊗m = ε. Quantum dimensions all 1 (abelian). Chiral central charge c = 0 — non-chiral; time-reversal-invariant.
realized_examples: Kitaev's toric code on a square lattice (experimentally realized in superconducting qubits — Google 2023, IBM 2024 — and in neutral-atom arrays — Lukin group 2021)
Z_2 spin liquids (candidate materials: certain pyrochlores; α-RuCl_3 in narrow regime)
Boundary of (2+1)D Z_2 SPT in the bosonic case

all_cells.content: Three-fermion (3F) MTC: objects {1, ψ_1, ψ_2, ψ_3} all fermionic (semion-squared exchange phases). Fusion: ψ_i ⊗ ψ_j = ψ_k for i ≠ j ≠ k, ψ_i ⊗ ψ_i = 1. Central charge c = 4 (mod 8). Chiral, no time-reversal-invariant realization.
realized_examples: Predicted boundary of certain (2+1)D fermionic SPT with three-band structure (no confirmed material realization as of 2026)
U(1)_4 × U(1)_{-4} chiral phase with appropriate K-matrix

all_cells.content: Doubled Ising MTC: Z(Ising) = Drinfeld center of the Ising fusion category. Rank 9, but at restricted topological sectors reduces to rank 4 in some constructions. Actually doubled-Ising in its canonical (rank-9) form: objects (a, b̄) where a, b ∈ {1, σ, ψ}. Non-chiral: c = 0. Time-reversal-invariant.
realized_examples: Levin-Wen string-net model with Ising fusion data
Boundary of (3+1)D phase with non-abelian intrinsic topological order on the boundary

all_cells.content: SU(2)_3 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by isospin j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2}. Central charge c = 3·3/(3+2) = 9/5. The j=1/2 and j=3/2 anyons are non-abelian; j=1 is abelian (the Z_2 grading). Equivalent to a Fibonacci-extended construction.
realized_examples: Read-Rezayi parafermion states (k=3 case) in FQHE
Proposed at ν = 12/5 FQHE plateau
SU(2)_3 WZW model

all_cells.content: SU(2)_4 Chern-Simons MTC: objects labeled by j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2, 2}. Central charge c = 3·4/(4+2) = 2.
realized_examples: SU(2)_4 WZW model
Proposed in higher-Landau-level FQHE constructions

all_cells.content: Fibonacci × Fibonacci-bar MTC (Drinfeld-center-like construction): rank 4 actually (1, τ, τ̄, τ⊗τ̄). Or alternatively a rank-5 exceptional from doubled SO(3)_3 truncation. Central charge c = 0 (non-chiral, if doubled construction).
realized_examples: Drinfeld center Z(Fib) of Fibonacci fusion category
Levin-Wen string-net with Fibonacci input

all_cells.content: SU(2)_5 Chern-Simons MTC: rank 6, j ∈ {0, 1/2, 1, 3/2, 2, 5/2}. Central charge c = 3·5/(5+2) = 15/7.
realized_examples: SU(2)_5 WZW model

all_cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
2D 0-form discrete symmetry. Canonical examples: Z_2 of Ising CFT, S_3 of 3-state Potts, Z_n of Z_n parafermions. Anomaly structure: H^3(G, U(1)) ≃ Ω_3^G classification.
realized_examples: Ising CFT Z_2 spin-flip symmetry (anomaly-free)
Tricritical Ising Z_2 × Z_2 (with mixed anomaly)
3-state Potts S_3 symmetry

all_cells.content: 2D 0-form non-invertible Tambara-Yamagami symmetry. Kramers-Wannier-duality-like fusion: D × D = 1 + Z + ... (D = duality defect, Z = group elements). The fusion is non-invertible: D² is NOT 1.
realized_examples: Kramers-Wannier duality of 2D Ising (D acts as the self-duality)
Z_n × Z_n duality category for the rank-2 toric-code-like phase
Verlinde-line dualities in c=1 free-boson CFT

all_cells.content: 2D 0-form general fusion-category symmetry (Verlinde lines and beyond). Topological defect lines (TDLs) in 2D CFT carry fusion-category structure C; non-invertible TDLs occur whenever C is not pointed.
realized_examples: Verlinde lines of 2D rational CFTs (Petkova-Zuber 2001)
Topological defect lines in tricritical Ising minimal model (Frolich-Fuchs-Runkel-Schweigert 2007)
Non-invertible defects in c=1 free-boson at irrational radius

all_cells.content: 3D 0-form discrete symmetry. Examples: Z_2 of 3D Ising CFT at criticality, time-reversal Z_2^T of (2+1)D topological insulators in the AII class.
realized_examples: 3D Ising critical point Z_2 (above the Wilson-Fisher fixed point)
Z_2 time-reversal of topological insulators (AII symmetry class)
Z_4 chiral symmetry of certain (2+1)D Dirac fermions

all_cells.content: 3D 1-form discrete symmetry acting on line operators. Canonical case: Z_N 1-form symmetry of U(1)_N Chern-Simons theory (acts on Wilson lines).
realized_examples: Z_2 × Z_2 1-form of 3D Z_2 toric code (the e-line and m-line are 1-form symmetry charges)
Z_N 1-form of U(1)_N Chern-Simons theory
Z_N 1-form of Z_N gauge theory in (2+1)D

all_cells.content: 4D 0-form continuous Lie-group symmetry. Examples: SU(N_f) chiral flavor symmetry of N_f-flavor QCD; U(1)_B baryon number; U(1)_em electromagnetic gauge (when treated as global at the boundary).
realized_examples: SU(3)_L × SU(3)_R chiral flavor of 3-flavor QCD
SU(N) flavor symmetry of large-N QCD
U(1)_B baryon number in SM
SU(2) custodial symmetry of SM electroweak sector

all_cells.content: 4D 1-form center symmetry of pure SU(N) Yang-Mills. The Z_N center acts on Wilson lines, classifying them by N-ality. Spontaneously broken in deconfined (high-T) phase; unbroken in confined (low-T) phase. Order parameter: Polyakov loop.
realized_examples: Z_N 1-form of pure SU(N) YM in 4D (canonical Komargodski-Sulejmanpasic-Unsal example)
Z_2 magnetic 1-form of free 4D Maxwell theory ('t Hooft line)
Z_n 1-form of 4D Z_n gauge theory

all_cells.content: 4D 0-form non-invertible symmetry from ABJ anomaly. The chiral U(1)_A of QED is anomalous and naively breaks; recast as a NON-INVERTIBLE discrete Z-valued (or rational) symmetry by gauging instanton number. Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022; Cordova-Ohmori 2022.
realized_examples: U(1)_A of massless QED — recast as a fractional / non-invertible symmetry
Chiral symmetry of massless QCD — partial non-invertible structure
Theta-shifts in 4D N=4 SYM viewed as non-invertible symmetry

all_cells.content: 4D 2-group structure mixing 0-form and 1-form symmetries via Postnikov class. Canonical example: axion-photon system where the axion shift symmetry (0-form U(1)) mixes with the photon 1-form Z by anomaly inflow.
realized_examples: Axion-photon system (the canonical 4D 2-group)
Theta-term-shift mixing with electromagnetic 1-form in 4D
Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2-group of 4D N=2 theories

all_cells.content: 4D 2-form discrete symmetry acting on 2D defects. Generators are codimension-3 in 4D = 1D in bulk SymTFT. Examples: 2-form of free 2-form gauge theory in 4D; emergent 2-form in certain (3+1)D phases.
realized_examples: Free 2-form gauge theory in 4D (Bantay-Vafa-like models)
Emergent 2-form symmetry in (3+1)D Z_2 topological order

all_cells.content: 6D 2-form center symmetry of the (2,0) theory. The 6D N=(2,0) SCFT has 2-form symmetry corresponding to the center of the gauge group (A_n, D_n, E_6,7,8 types). Acts on M2-brane-like 2D surface operators. Canonical SymTFT example linking 6D dynamics to 7D Chern-Simons-like invertible TFT.
realized_examples: 6D N=(2,0) theory of type A_N (= N M5-branes) has Z_N 2-form symmetry
6D (2,0) of type D_n has Z_2 2-form symmetry
6D (2,0) of type E_n has trivial 2-form symmetry

all_cells.content: 3D 1-form non-invertible symmetry. Acts on line operators with fusion-category structure (no group inverse). Canonical: certain Chern-Simons-matter theories where dualities yield non-invertible 1-form structure.
realized_examples: Certain 3D N=4 quiver gauge theories with non-trivial duality structure
3D ABJM at level k with non-invertible defect symmetries

all_cells.content: 5D 0-form discrete symmetry. 5D theories (e.g., 5D N=1 SCFTs from M-theory on Calabi-Yau singularities) carry intricate global symmetry structure including discrete 0-form factors classified by Ω_5^{SO × BG}.
realized_examples: 5D N=1 E_n SCFTs from M-theory on del Pezzo surfaces
5D Seiberg theories with various flavor symmetries

all_cells.content: 2D 1-form symmetry: acts on 1-dim defects (= boundaries in 2D bulk). For 2D theories with boundaries, 1-form symmetry classifies boundary state structure; for closed 2D theories, the 1-form acts trivially on the empty surface — the symmetry is structurally vacuous in the closed-spacetime sector.
realized_examples: Boundary classification of 2D Ising under Z_2 1-form (boundary states organized by Z_2)

all_cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded

SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 flavors at the conformal point. Coulomb branch is 1D, parameterized by u = ⟨Tr(φ²)⟩. The Seiberg-Witten curve is the canonical example: y² = (x − u)(x² − Λ²) deformed by N_f=4 flavor masses, which at the conformal point is the original Seiberg-Witten SU(2) Nf=4 curve.
realized_examples: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 fundamental flavors (Seiberg-Witten 1994)
Tested via Lagrangian construction; canonical worked example

all_cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 flavors: Higgs branch is the symmetric product Sym²(C²)/Z_2, of complex dim 4 / 2 = 2 in some parameterization; the hyperkähler quaternionic dimension equals 3 for the full SO(8) flavor structure. Equivalently, the Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of so(8) at this conformal point.
realized_examples: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 (the same theory as the Coulomb-branch cell — SCFTs have both branches)
Realized as a Nakajima quiver variety for the affine D_4 Dynkin diagram

all_cells.content: A_1 (or H_0) Argyres-Douglas theory: rank-1 Coulomb branch with singular point at the origin where the SU(2) SQCD theory with N_f=1 develops mutually non-local light states (an electric monopole and a magnetic monopole simultaneously massless). Non-Lagrangian SCFT.
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas H_0 = simplest AD theory
Singular point on the SU(2) N_f=1 Coulomb branch
Coulomb-branch operator with non-integer scaling dimension Δ = 6/5

all_cells.content: A_1 Argyres-Douglas H_0: no Higgs branch (rank 0). The theory has no flavor symmetry that could parameterize a hyperkähler moduli space. This is a generic feature of the simplest AD theories: they have non-trivial Coulomb branches but trivial Higgs branches.
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas H_0 has trivial flavor sector

all_cells.content: E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: rank-1 Coulomb branch with single Coulomb-branch operator of scaling dimension Δ = 3. The Seiberg-Witten curve is a genus-1 elliptic fibration over the Coulomb branch with E_6 monodromy structure.
realized_examples: E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996 (the canonical rank-1 SCFT with exceptional flavor symmetry)
Realized as the theory living on a single M5-brane probe of an E_6 singularity

all_cells.content: E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_6, of complex dimension 22 (quaternionic dimension 11). Hyperkähler manifold with E_6 isometry; one of the canonical 'symplectic singularities' in the Beauville-Bogomolov classification.
realized_examples: Minimal nilpotent orbit O_min(e_6), the closure of the highest-root orbit
Realized as a hyperkähler cone with E_6 holomorphic isometry

all_cells.content: E_7 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_7, of complex dimension 34 (quaternionic dim 17).
realized_examples: Minimal nilpotent orbit O_min(e_7)

all_cells.content: E_8 Minahan-Nemeschansky theory: Higgs branch is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_8, of complex dim 58 (quaternionic dim 29).
realized_examples: Minimal nilpotent orbit O_min(e_8) — the largest exceptional example

all_cells.content: T_3 theory (= class-S type A_2 on 3-punctured sphere): rank-2 Coulomb branch with 2 Coulomb-branch operators of dimensions (2, 3). The simplest non-Lagrangian class-S theory; equivalent to E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky in its rank-1 limit but here treated as the parent rank-2 class-S construction.
realized_examples: T_3 = T[A_2] theory on 3-punctured sphere
Realized via M5-brane construction on the 3-punctured sphere with maximal regular punctures

all_cells.content: T_3 = T[A_2] theory: Higgs branch is a known hyperkähler manifold of complex dim 10 (quaternionic dim 5), realized as a moment map / quiver variety.
realized_examples: T_3 Higgs branch as moment map level set for SU(3)³ flavor symmetry

all_cells.content: N=4 SYM with SU(2) gauge group: Coulomb branch is 1D, parameterized by u = ⟨Tr(φ²)⟩. The Seiberg-Witten curve is trivial (Λ → 0 in the SU(2) Nf=4 family); the prepotential is exactly classical. The full N=4 SCFT has a U-duality structure (SL(2,Z)) acting on coupling τ.
realized_examples: N=4 SU(2) SYM in the gauge-conformal phase
Mass-deformation to SU(2) Nf=4 recovers Seiberg-Witten

all_cells.content: N=4 SU(2) SYM: Higgs branch is the symmetric product Sym²(C²)/Z_2, which equals the minimal nilpotent orbit of sl(2) (closed up to closure). Hyperkähler quotient of C² × C² / Z_2 by the diagonal SU(2). Quaternionic dim = 3.
realized_examples: N=4 SU(2) Higgs branch as 2-instanton moduli space
Mirror to the Coulomb branch in the 3D N=4 reduction

all_cells.content: N=2* mass-deformation of N=4 SU(N) SYM: mixed branch structure where the mass parameter m interpolates between N=4 (m=0) and pure N=2 SYM (m→∞). Both Higgs and Coulomb branches are non-trivial; their intersection captures the mass-deformation dynamics.
realized_examples: N=2* SU(N) SYM with adjoint hypermultiplet mass m
Continuum interpolation between N=4 and pure-N=2

all_cells.content: Free N=2 vector multiplet (rank 1): Coulomb branch is the complex plane C, parameterized by the vev of the free scalar φ. No interactions; Seiberg-Witten prepotential is classical kinetic term only.
realized_examples: Single free N=2 vector multiplet
The U(1) case of N=2 abelian gauge theory

all_cells.content: Free N=2 hypermultiplet (rank 1): Higgs branch is the quaternionic plane H = C², parameterized by the two complex scalars of the hyper. No interactions.
realized_examples: Single free N=2 hypermultiplet

all_cells.content: Class-S theory of D-type, e.g., T[D_3] = SO(6) on a 3-punctured sphere: rank-3 Coulomb branch with 3 Coulomb-branch operators of specific dimensions.
realized_examples: T[D_3] on 3-punctured sphere
Realized via M5-brane construction with Z_2 orbifold action

all_cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded
Light pseudoscalar mesons (J^P^C = 0-+): the SU(3) flavor octet π, K, η plus singlet η'.
realized_examples: K+ (494 MeV), K0 (498 MeV), K- (494 MeV), K0-bar (498 MeV)
η (548 MeV), η' (958 MeV)

all_cells.content: Light vector mesons (J^P^C = 1--): ρ, ω, K*, φ — the SU(3) flavor nonet.
realized_examples: ω (783 MeV)
φ (1020 MeV)

all_cells.content: Light tensor mesons (J^P^C = 2++): a_2, f_2, K_2*, f_2'.
Lowest-lying baryon octet (J^P = 1/2+): N (nucleon doublet), Λ, Σ, Ξ — the SU(3) flavor octet of spin-1/2 baryons.
realized_examples: proton p (938.3 MeV), neutron n (939.6 MeV)
Λ (1116 MeV)

all_cells.content: Lowest-lying baryon decuplet (J^P = 3/2+): Δ, Σ*, Ξ*, Ω — the SU(3) flavor decuplet.
J/ψ and other charmonium 1-- states: c-cbar bound states with J^P^C = 1--.
realized_examples: J/ψ (3097 MeV) — the canonical charmonium discovery (1974)
ψ(4040), ψ(4160), ψ(4415) — higher charmonium states

all_cells.content: Υ and other bottomonium 1-- states: b-bbar bound states with J^P^C = 1--.
realized_examples: Υ(1S) (9460 MeV) — bottomonium ground state, discovered 1977

all_cells.content: Charm-baryon 1/2+ states: Λ_c, Σ_c, Ξ_c, Ω_c.
Bottom-baryon 1/2+ states: Λ_b, Σ_b, Ξ_b, Ω_b.
Doubly-charm baryon: Ξ_cc++ (3621 MeV), confirmed by LHCb 2017. The first doubly-heavy baryon. Doubly-bottom baryons Ξ_bb, Ω_bb predicted at ~10 GeV but not yet observed.
realized_examples: Ξ_cc++ (3621 MeV) — observed LHCb 2017
Ξ_bc, Ξ_bb, Ω_bb, B_c+ family — predicted but unobserved at various masses

all_cells.content: X(3872): the first confirmed exotic tetraquark candidate. Discovered Belle 2003. Mass 3872 MeV, J^P^C = 1++, narrow width.
realized_examples: X(3872) — Belle 2003, confirmed by BaBar, CDF, D0, LHCb, CMS

all_cells.content: Hidden-charm pentaquarks: P_c(4380), P_c(4312), P_c(4440), P_c(4457). Discovered LHCb 2015 and 2019 in Λ_b decays.
realized_examples: P_c(4312), P_c(4440), P_c(4457) — LHCb 2019 (refined spectroscopy)
P_cs(4459) — strange pentaquark, LHCb 2020

all_cells.content: Charged charmonium / bottomonium-like tetraquark states: Z_c(3900), Z_c(4020), Z_b(10610), Z_b(10650). Definitively non-standard (charged charmonium implies tetraquark content).
realized_examples: Z_c(3900) — BESIII / Belle 2013
Z_c(4020) — BESIII 2013

all_cells.content: Lowest scalar glueball: lattice QCD predicts mass ≈ 1500-1700 MeV with J^P^C = 0++. Mendeleev-style empty cell — predicted but not unambiguously identified. Candidate states f_0(1500) and f_0(1710) have significant glueball admixture but mix with conventional q-qbar states.
realized_examples: f_0(1500) — leading glueball candidate (disputed)
f_0(1710) — alternative glueball candidate (disputed)
Lattice prediction: m ≈ 1730 MeV (Morningstar-Peardon 1999), 1500 MeV (recent SU(3) lattice)

all_cells.content: Light 1-+ hybrid meson: predicted by lattice QCD at ≈ 1.8-2.0 GeV. The 1-+ J^P^C is exotic (forbidden for pure q-qbar mesons), so observation of any 1-+ light meson is direct evidence of hybrid content. Candidates π_1(1400) and π_1(1600) exist with disputed interpretations; η_1(1855) discovered by BESIII 2022 is the cleanest candidate to date.
realized_examples: π_1(1400) — early candidate, contested
π_1(1600) — better-established candidate, hybrid interpretation favored but not universal
η_1(1855) — BESIII 2022 discovery, isoscalar hybrid candidate

all_cells.content: d*(2380) dibaryon candidate: a 6-quark state with quantum numbers I(J^P) = 0(3+), mass ≈ 2380 MeV, observed by WASA-at-COSY 2014. The first dibaryon resonance with credible experimental support. Status: partial — observed by one collaboration with consistent signal across multiple decay modes; awaits independent confirmation at higher statistics.
realized_examples: d*(2380) — WASA-at-COSY 2014, mass 2380 MeV, width 70 MeV

all_cells.content: T_cc+(3875): doubly-charm tetraquark with quark content ccūd̄, J^P=1+, observed by LHCb 2021. The first confirmed doubly-heavy tetraquark and an unambiguously exotic state.
realized_examples: T_cc+(3875) — LHCb 2021/2022, mass 3875 MeV, very narrow width

all_cells.content: Predicted doubly-bottom tetraquark T_bb (bbū-d̄, J^P=1+) at mass ≈ 10.4 GeV. Predicted to be stable against strong decay (Karliner-Rosner 2017; Eichten-Quigg 2017). Mendeleev empty cell — strong theoretical prediction, no experimental observation as of 2026.
realized_examples: T_bb (bbū-d̄) — predicted at m ≈ 10.4 GeV, J^P = 1+
T_bbb-bar — predicted higher-mass extension

all_cells.content: structurally-excluded
structurally-excluded

ν=1/3 Laughlin state. Hall conductance σ_xy = (1/3)e²/h. Quasiparticles have fractional charge e/3 with abelian fractional statistics (exchange phase 2π/3). Ground-state degeneracy 3 on torus. The foundational FQH state — Tsui-Stormer-Gossard 1982 discovery; Laughlin 1983 theory.
realized_examples: ν=1/3 in GaAs heterostructures — original 1982 discovery (Tsui, Stormer, Gossard)
ν=1/3 in graphene (Du et al. 2009; Bolotin et al. 2009)
ν=1/3 in ZnO/MgZnO 2DEG

all_cells.content: ν=1/5 Laughlin state. Quasiparticle charge e/5; abelian statistics with phase 2π/5. Ground-state degeneracy 5 on torus.
realized_examples: ν=1/5 in high-mobility GaAs (Pan et al. 2002)

all_cells.content: ν=2/5 Jain composite-fermion state. The first 'non-Laughlin' Jain state. Quasiparticle charge e/5. Abelian statistics.
realized_examples: ν=2/5 in GaAs (Willett et al. 1987)

all_cells.content: ν=3/7 Jain state. CF filling 3.
realized_examples: ν=3/7 in GaAs (Pan et al. 2003)

all_cells.content: ν=4/9 Jain state. CF filling 4. Approaching the half-filled Landau level limit.
realized_examples: ν=4/9 in GaAs (Pan et al. 2003)

all_cells.content: ν=2/3 particle-hole conjugate of ν=1/3. Same anyon content as 1/3 by PH symmetry within the LLL.
realized_examples: ν=2/3 in GaAs (early FQH experiments)

all_cells.content: ν=5/2 Moore-Read Pfaffian candidate. Half-filled second Landau level with predicted non-abelian Ising anyon quasiparticles. Pfaffian wavefunction Pf(1/(z_i - z_j)) times Laughlin factor. Universal TQC NOT supported by Ising anyons alone (requires supplementation).
realized_examples: ν=5/2 plateau in highest-mobility GaAs heterostructures (Willett et al. 1987)
Specific Pfaffian-state identification: contested (Pfaffian vs anti-Pfaffian vs PH-Pfaffian)

all_cells.content: ν=5/2 anti-Pfaffian alternative. Particle-hole conjugate of Moore-Read Pfaffian within the second Landau level. Different anyonic content (anti-Ising statistics) from original Pfaffian. One of three competing candidates for the 5/2 state.
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction (Levin-Halperin-Rosenow 2007; Lee-Ryu-Nayak-Fisher 2007)
Not unambiguously identified experimentally; competes with Pfaffian and PH-Pfaffian

all_cells.content: ν=5/2 PH-Pfaffian candidate. Particle-hole-symmetric pairing pattern proposed by Son 2015 in the composite-Dirac-fermion framework. Distinct anyon content from Pfaffian and anti-Pfaffian. Thermal Hall measurements (Banerjee 2018) initially appeared consistent with PH-Pfaffian; later analyses dispute this.
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction (Son 2015)
Possibly favored by thermal Hall data (Banerjee et al. 2018) but disputed

all_cells.content: ν=12/5 Read-Rezayi state with Fibonacci anyons. Would support UNIVERSAL topological quantum computation by braiding alone. The only proposed FQH state with Fibonacci anyons; observation would be a landmark.
realized_examples: ν=12/5 plateau observed in highest-mobility GaAs (Xia et al. 2004; Choi et al. 2008)
Identification as Read-Rezayi state: partial evidence (numerical match), not unambiguously confirmed
Particle-hole conjugate at ν=13/5 also observed

all_cells.content: ν=7/2 Moore-Read Pfaffian analog. Half-filled third Landau level. Less studied than 5/2; the analog non-abelian Ising-anyon state in the third LL.
realized_examples: ν=7/2 plateau observed (less mobile samples)

all_cells.content: ν=8/3 CF state. In the second Landau level. Particle-hole conjugate of 7/3 (also a Jain state).
realized_examples: ν=8/3 in GaAs second LL (Pan et al. 1999)

all_cells.content: ν=4/11 unconventional state. Predicted via second-generation Jain hierarchy (composite-fermion-of-composite-fermion); observed at higher mobility. Partial identification.
realized_examples: ν=4/11 plateau observed (Pan et al. 2003) — robust but identification debated

all_cells.content: ν=7/5 Read-Rezayi Z_4 parafermion candidate. Particle-hole conjugate of 12/5 within the LLL. PREDICTED Fibonacci-like or Z_4-parafermion anyons. NOT confirmed.
realized_examples: ν=7/5 plateau weakly observed (less robust than 12/5)
Theoretical Read-Rezayi prediction not confirmed by experiment

all_cells.content: ν=3/8 bosonic hierarchy state. Less robust; observed in highest-mobility samples.
realized_examples: ν=3/8 weak plateau (Pan et al.)

all_cells.content: Predicted Fibonacci-anyon state at unconventional ν. Mendeleev empty cell. Various theoretical proposals for FQH states supporting Fibonacci anyons beyond 12/5; observation would enable universal TQC.
realized_examples: No experimental observation of Fibonacci anyons at any ν as of 2026
Predicted at multiple specific ν values in various theoretical proposals

all_cells.content: ν=1/9 Laughlin state. Very fragile (extremely small gap); observed only in highest-mobility specimens.
realized_examples: ν=1/9 weakly observed in cleanest samples

structurally-excluded

Canonical white dwarfs: M ≈ 0.6 M_sun, R ≈ R_Earth (~6000 km), supported by electron degeneracy pressure. Cooling timescales ~ Gyr. Mass-radius relation is well-measured for individuals including Sirius B (M ≈ 1.02 M_sun, R ≈ 0.0084 R_sun) and 40 Eridani B.
realized_examples: Sirius B — paradigmatic WD, M ≈ 1.02 M_sun, R ≈ 0.0084 R_sun (Bond et al. 2017 HST astrometric mass)
40 Eridani B — closest WD-triple system to Earth
Procyon B — high-mass WD in nearby binary
Van Maanen 2 — first isolated WD discovered (1917)

all_cells.content: White dwarfs near the Chandrasekhar limit M_Ch ≈ 1.4 M_sun (for nonrotating, nonmagnetic, μ_e = 2 composition). Approach to M_Ch leads to instability (electron capture, pyconuclear reactions) and is the precursor to type-Ia supernovae or accretion-induced collapse to neutron stars.
realized_examples: RX J0648.0-4418 — accreting massive WD
ZTF J153932.16+502738.8 — eclipsing ultramassive double WD inspiral
WD 1856+534 — high-mass WD with transiting giant planet

all_cells.content: Ultramassive WDs with M ≳ 1.1 M_sun, formed in mergers of WD pairs or massive progenitors. Crystallizing interiors (Coulomb crystallization at low T) and possible neon-capture-driven collapse to NSs are predicted; the latter constitutes the accretion-induced-collapse formation channel for low-mass NSs.
realized_examples: ZTF J1901+1458 — M ≈ 1.35 M_sun ultramassive WD with strong B field (Caiazzo et al. 2021)
WD J0551+4135 — ultramassive WD with composition suggesting merger origin (Hollands et al. 2020)

all_cells.content: Canonical neutron stars: M ≈ 1.4 M_sun (the median observed NS mass), R ≈ 11-13 km. Surface gravity GM/(Rc²) ≈ 0.2 — general-relativistic regime. Crust + core structure: outer crust (Coulomb lattice of nuclei in electron gas), inner crust (neutron-drip + neutron superfluid), outer core (npe + possibly μ matter), inner core (composition uncertain — hyperons, kaon condensate, quark matter all proposed).
