realized_examples: PSR J0030+0451 — NICER mass-radius measurement M = 1.34 (+0.15/-0.16) M_sun, R = 12.71 (+1.14/-1.19) km (Miller et al. 2019, Riley et al. 2019)
PSR B1913+16 (Hulse-Taylor) — first binary pulsar, M ≈ 1.44 M_sun
PSR J0737-3039A/B — double pulsar system
PSR J0437-4715 — NICER mass-radius (Choudhury et al. 2024)

all_cells.content: Massive neutron stars with M ≳ 2 M_sun, constraining the TOV maximum mass from below. These observations rule out soft hadronic-only EOS and disfavor first-order quark-matter phase transitions at densities below ~3-4 n_0.
realized_examples: PSR J0740+6620 — M = 2.08 ± 0.07 M_sun (Cromartie et al. 2020; Fonseca et al. 2021); NICER + radio mass-radius: M = 2.072 M_sun, R = 12.39 km (Miller et al. 2021)
PSR J0348+0432 — M = 2.01 ± 0.04 M_sun (Antoniadis et al. 2013)
PSR J1614-2230 — M ≈ 1.93 M_sun (Demorest et al. 2010; revised in Arzoumanian et al. 2018)
PSR J0952-0607 — claimed M ≈ 2.35 M_sun, the most-massive known NS as of 2024 (Romani et al. 2022; mass updated; observational systematics under continued review)

all_cells.content: Magnetars: neutron stars with extreme magnetic fields B ~ 10^14-10^15 G (~10^4 × canonical NS). Phenomenology includes soft-gamma-repeater (SGR) bursts, anomalous X-ray pulsar (AXP) emission, giant flares (10^46 erg in seconds), and occasional Fast-Radio-Burst (FRB) associations.
realized_examples: SGR 1806-20 — 2004 giant flare, brightest extrasolar event ever recorded
SGR J1935+2154 — magnetar associated with FRB 200428, the first galactic FRB
1E 1841-045 — paradigmatic AXP
PSR J1119-6127 — magnetar-pulsar transition object

all_cells.content: Hypermassive neutron-star remnants: short-lived (~ms-1s) products of binary NS coalescence with total mass exceeding M_TOV. Differential rotation supports against immediate collapse; viscous evolution drives collapse to BH on ms-s timescales.
realized_examples: GW170817 post-merger — total mass ≈ 2.74 M_sun (above M_TOV); remnant identity (immediate BH, short-lived hypermassive NS, or transient supramassive NS) constrained by EM observations and kilonova ejecta mass; consensus disfavors prompt collapse, favors short-lived hypermassive NS → BH (Margalit, Metzger 2017; Bauswein et al. 2017)

all_cells.content: Stellar-mass black holes in X-ray binaries: M ~ 5-30 M_sun, identified by accretion-disk X-ray emission and dynamical mass measurements of the companion. The first BH identifications were in this cell (Cyg X-1).
realized_examples: Cyg X-1 — M ≈ 21.2 M_sun (Miller-Jones et al. 2021), the first BH discovered
GRS 1915+105 — M ≈ 10.1 M_sun, microquasar with relativistic jets
V404 Cygni — M ≈ 9 M_sun, transient BH X-ray binary
LMC X-1 — M ≈ 10.9 M_sun, persistent BH HMXB
GX 339-4 — transient BH X-ray binary, M ≈ 5-7 M_sun

all_cells.content: Stellar-mass black holes in merging compact binaries, catalogued by LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA gravitational-wave observations. As of the O4 run (2024-26), GWTC-4 contains ~150+ confident BBH mergers spanning component masses ~5-80 M_sun. Mass distribution shows distinctive features: a low-mass-gap edge near ~3-5 M_sun, a pile-up at ~10 M_sun, and a partially-suppressed pair-instability gap at ~50-100 M_sun.
realized_examples: GW150914 — first GW detection, M_1 ≈ 36 M_sun, M_2 ≈ 29 M_sun, BBH merger at z ≈ 0.09
GW190521 — total mass ≈ 150 M_sun; final BH ≈ 142 M_sun straddles intermediate-mass BH regime
GW200115 — first confirmed NS-BH merger, with BH M ≈ 5.7 M_sun, NS M ≈ 1.5 M_sun
GW190412 — first confidently asymmetric-mass-ratio BBH merger (q ≈ 0.28)

all_cells.content: The lower-mass-gap region: 3-5 M_sun. X-ray-binary observations of the 1990s-2010s found very few BHs below ~5 M_sun, suggesting a gap between the maximum NS mass (~2.3 M_sun) and the minimum BH mass (~5 M_sun). GW observations are beginning to populate this gap (GW200115 with BH M ≈ 5.7 M_sun, GW190425, GW230529 — see below).
realized_examples: GW230529_181500 — compact-object merger with primary mass 2.5-4.5 M_sun (LVK Collaboration 2024); occupies the lower mass-gap region
GW200115 — NS-BH merger with BH at lower edge of gap

all_cells.content: Intermediate-mass black holes, 10^2-10^5 M_sun. The largest empty zone in the BH mass distribution. Astrophysically expected as: (i) seeds of SMBHs (Volonteri-style seed scenarios); (ii) end-products of runaway collisions in dense stellar clusters; (iii) accreted descendants of Pop-III stellar BHs.
realized_examples: GW190521 final BH at ≈ 142 M_sun — the strongest single IMBH candidate from GW astronomy
HLX-1 (in ESO 243-49) — hyperluminous X-ray source, M ≈ 10^4 M_sun candidate (debated)
NGC 6166 ULX-1 candidates — multiple IMBH-mass-range candidates
M82 X-1 — possible IMBH (debated)

all_cells.content: Supermassive black holes at galactic centers. Stellar-dynamical mass measurements (S-star orbits at the Galactic Center), maser-dynamics measurements (NGC 4258), and event-horizon-scale imaging (EHT for Sgr A* and M87*) provide the cleanest individual mass constraints. SMBHs at galactic centers correlate with host galaxy properties (M-σ relation).
realized_examples: Sgr A* — M ≈ 4.297 ± 0.012 × 10^6 M_sun (GRAVITY Collaboration 2022); EHT imaging (EHT Collaboration 2022) confirms shadow size consistent with Kerr metric prediction
M87* — M ≈ 6.5 × 10^9 M_sun (EHT Collaboration 2019); first EHT image of a BH shadow
NGC 4258 — M ≈ 4 × 10^7 M_sun, gold-standard SMBH mass via H2O megamaser
Andromeda M31 nucleus — M ≈ 1.4 × 10^8 M_sun

all_cells.content: Supermassive black holes powering active galactic nuclei (AGN) and quasars: M ~ 10^7-10^10 M_sun. Mass measurements via reverberation mapping (variability time-lag of broad-line region), single-epoch virial estimators, or stellar/gas dynamics for nearby AGN. The most massive known SMBHs reach ~ 10^10 M_sun (ultramassive cell may host some).
realized_examples: TON 618 — M ≈ 6.6 × 10^10 M_sun, one of the most-massive known SMBHs (mass via H-beta line FWHM, single-epoch)
3C 273 — first quasar identified (1963), M ≈ 9 × 10^8 M_sun
NGC 1275 — Perseus cluster central AGN, M ≈ 8 × 10^8 M_sun
Pop-III-descendant quasars at z > 6 (J0313-1806 at z ≈ 7.6, M ≈ 1.6 × 10^9 M_sun — challenging early-Universe seed scenarios)

all_cells.content: Quark stars (strange quark stars): hypothetical compact objects composed of stable strange quark matter (SQM), predicted if Bodmer-Witten conjecture (SQM as true QCD ground state at zero pressure) holds. Mass-radius relation differs from NS: R increases with M for low-mass SQM stars, opposite to NS trend.

Primordial black holes in the asteroid-mass window: M ~ 10^17-10^22 g (~ 10^-15 to 10^-11 M_sun). This window remains open for PBHs being a substantial DM fraction; constraints from femto/picolensing, Hawking-evaporation gamma rays, and neutron-star capture rates only partially close it.

Primordial black holes in the LIGO mass window: M ~ 1-100 M_sun. Once proposed as a substantial DM fraction (especially after GW150914), the window is now strongly excluded as ≳ 10% DM by combined microlensing (OGLE, EROS, MACHO), GW merger-rate statistics, CMB-accretion limits, and dwarf-galaxy survival constraints.

Exotic compact objects: boson stars, gravastars, fuzzballs, dark stars, anisotropic stars, wormholes — proposed BH-mimickers that match BH external metric beyond the horizon scale but differ at horizon scale (no horizon, soft surface, etc.). Each model predicts a distinct GW-ringdown 'echo' signature or distinct shadow morphology potentially detectable at next-generation precision.
HgTe/CdTe quantum well above critical thickness ~6.3 nm
realized_examples: Molecular-beam-epitaxy-grown HgTe quantum wells (Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang theoretical prediction 2006; König et al. transport observation 2007). The quantum-spin-Hall phase with longitudinal conductance σ_xx = 2e²/h plateau is observed.

all_cells.content: Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ — the canonical 3D TI family
realized_examples: Bi₂Se₃ — single Dirac cone at Γ on the surface confirmed by ARPES (Xia, Hsieh 2009). Bi₂Te₃ and Sb₂Te₃ — analogous family members with larger bulk gap and stronger surface Dirac cones. STM imaging confirms surface state robustness to non-magnetic disorder.

all_cells.content: Monolayer 1T'-WTe₂
realized_examples: Monolayer 1T'-WTe₂ exfoliated from bulk crystals. Quantum spin Hall edge conductance observed up to ~100 K — substantially higher than HgTe. STM verifies edge state density.

all_cells.content: Bismuthene — monolayer Bi(111) on SiC substrate
realized_examples: Bismuthene grown epitaxially on SiC(0001). Spin-orbit-induced gap of ~0.8 eV — the largest known 2D-TI gap. Edge states verified by ARPES and STM.

all_cells.content: SmB₆ — topological Kondo insulator candidate
realized_examples: SmB₆ single crystals. Bulk Kondo insulating behavior confirmed; surface state interpretation contested — some experiments support a topological surface state (ARPES of in-gap states, surface conduction at low T), others interpret the in-gap features as non-topological.

all_cells.content: SnTe and Pb₁₋ₓSnₓTe — topological crystalline insulators
realized_examples: SnTe single crystals (Tanaka et al. 2012 ARPES). Pb₁₋ₓSnₓTe alloy with x > 0.32 shows the band-inverted phase. Surface Dirac cones at L points of the bulk BZ confirmed; symmetry-broken splitting under uniaxial strain confirmed (Okada et al. 2013 STM).

all_cells.content: FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ — superconducting iron chalcogenide with claimed Majorana surface states
realized_examples: Single crystals of FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅. Wang et al. 2018 reported zero-bias conductance peaks at vortex cores via STM, interpreted as Majorana zero modes. Subsequent experiments report inconsistent vortex-to-vortex peak behavior; alternative non-topological interpretations (Caroli-de Gennes-Matricon states, Yu-Shiba-Rusinov bound states from impurities) remain viable.

all_cells.content: Sr₂RuO₄ — long-debated chiral p-wave superconductor candidate
realized_examples: Sr₂RuO₄ single crystals; long thought to be a p_x + ip_y triplet superconductor (Mackenzie-Maeno 2003). 2019 NMR Knight-shift measurements (Pustogow et al. 2019) showed the spin susceptibility drops in the superconducting state — inconsistent with the equal-spin-pairing triplet scenario. The pairing symmetry is now actively re-debated; the original chiral-p-wave claim is substantially weakened though not formally retracted.

all_cells.content: InSb/Al nanowire — the retracted 2018 Majorana zero-mode claim
realized_examples: Zhang et al. 2018 Nature paper claimed quantized 2e²/h conductance peaks attributable to Majorana zero modes in an InSb/Al hybrid nanowire. The paper was retracted in 2021 after re-analysis of the original data revealed that signatures consistent with MZMs were instead disorder-induced Andreev bound states, with the published quantization claim not supported by the underlying data.

all_cells.content: TaAs, NbAs, TaP, NbP — the canonical Weyl semimetal family
realized_examples: TaAs single crystals (Lv et al. 2015 ARPES; Xu et al. 2015 surface Fermi arcs). Surface Fermi-arc spectra confirmed; bulk Weyl-point band crossings confirmed via soft-X-ray ARPES at varying photon energies.

all_cells.content: Co₃Sn₂S₂ — magnetic Weyl semimetal
realized_examples: Co₃Sn₂S₂ single crystals. Below T_C ~ 175 K, ferromagnetic ordering breaks time-reversal. Anomalous Hall effect with large intrinsic contribution, surface Fermi arcs imaged by STM (Morali et al. 2019), are consistent with bulk magnetic Weyl points.

all_cells.content: Cd₃As₂ — 3D Dirac semimetal
realized_examples: Cd₃As₂ single crystals. ARPES (Neupane et al. 2014) confirms two bulk 3D Dirac points along the k_z axis protected by C₄ rotational symmetry. Magnetoresistance studies show the predicted negative longitudinal magnetoresistance from the chiral anomaly.

all_cells.content: MnBi₂Te₄ and (MnBi₂Te₄)(Bi₂Te₃)ₙ family — intrinsic magnetic TI / axion insulator candidate
realized_examples: MnBi₂Te₄ single crystals — antiferromagnetic ordering below T_N ~ 25 K. Quantum anomalous Hall effect observed in thin flakes (Deng et al. 2020 Science). Axion-insulator response (θ = π) signatures reported but contested in terms of cleanliness of the bulk vs surface contributions.

all_cells.content: Bismuth (rhombohedral Bi) — first confirmed higher-order topological insulator
realized_examples: Rhombohedral bismuth (Bi) — STM imaging of 1D conducting hinge states between (111) and (110) surface facets (Schindler et al. 2018 Nature Phys.). Bulk classification: a HOTI with 1D hinge modes at intersections of surface facets where the second-order topological invariant changes.

all_cells.content: Fractional topological insulator (3D analog of FQHE) — predicted, no confirmed material
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Theoretical proposals from Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang (2010) and subsequent works construct 3D class AII insulators with fractional axion angle θ = π/k for integer k > 1, protected by time-reversal and a global U(1) charge symmetry.

all_cells.content: Intrinsic bulk axion insulator with cleanly realized θ = π (no surface complication)
realized_examples: No material. Theoretical proposals require a 3D antiferromagnetic insulator whose surface gap can be opened cleanly and whose axion response can be measured via topological magnetoelectric effect (image-monopole signature near surface). MnBi₂Te₄ partly realizes this but with experimental complications.

all_cells.content: Class CI 3D topological superconductor in a real material — no clear candidate
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Class CI requires both T² = +1 (time-reversal) and C² = +1 (particle-hole) — a combination rare in superconductors with strong spin-orbit coupling but predicted to support nontrivial 3D topology with ℤ invariant.

all_cells.content: Higher-order topological superconductor with helical Majorana hinge modes — predicted
realized_examples: No confirmed material. Theoretical proposals (Khalaf 2018; Geier et al. 2018) construct higher-order DIII TSCs with helical Majorana hinge modes at codimension-2 boundaries. Candidate platforms (iron-based superconductors with crystalline symmetry) have been suggested but not confirmed.

all_cells.content: Solar mixing angle θ_12 ≈ 33.45°
realized_examples: KamLAND reactor + SNO solar combined analyses give sin²(θ_12) ≈ 0.307 ± 0.013; the precision is dominated by KamLAND long-baseline reactor measurements of Δm²_21 and θ_12 together.

all_cells.content: Atmospheric mixing angle θ_23 ≈ 49° (NH) with octant ambiguity
realized_examples: Super-Kamiokande atmospheric-ν fit, MINOS, T2K, NOvA disappearance measurements. sin²(θ_23) ≈ 0.55 (NH best-fit, NuFIT 2024); the octant (θ_23 below or above 45°) is not yet definitively resolved.

all_cells.content: Reactor mixing angle θ_13 ≈ 8.6°
realized_examples: Daya Bay reactor short-baseline experiment in China measured sin²(2θ_13) = 0.0856 ± 0.0029 (2022); RENO and Double Chooz are consistent.

all_cells.content: Leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP
realized_examples: T2K Collaboration 2020 Nature report best-fit δ_CP ≈ -π/2 in NH (CP-violating); NOvA 2024 results report best-fit closer to δ_CP ≈ 0 in NH. The two experiments are in slight tension on δ_CP at the ~1.5σ level.

all_cells.content: Solar mass-squared difference Δm²_21 ≈ 7.4 × 10⁻⁵ eV²
realized_examples: KamLAND + solar global fit: Δm²_21 = (7.42 ± 0.21) × 10⁻⁵ eV² (NuFIT 2024).

all_cells.content: Atmospheric mass-squared difference |Δm²_31| ≈ 2.5 × 10⁻³ eV² (sign open)
realized_examples: T2K, NOvA, MINOS, IceCube atmospheric fits all give |Δm²_31| ≈ 2.51 × 10⁻³ eV². The sign distinguishes normal hierarchy (Δm²_31 > 0) from inverted hierarchy (Δm²_31 < 0).

all_cells.content: Mass hierarchy: normal (NH, m1<m2<m3) vs inverted (IH, m3<m1<m2)
realized_examples: Global fits including NOvA, T2K, atmospheric data, and cosmological mass bounds slightly prefer NH over IH at ~2-3σ. JUNO, DUNE, and IceCube-Upgrade are designed to resolve this at >5σ within the next decade.

all_cells.content: Absolute electron-neutrino mass bound from tritium-endpoint
realized_examples: KATRIN Collaboration 2022 Nature: m(ν_e) < 0.8 eV at 90% CL (combined first three measurement campaigns). Final KATRIN target: 0.2 eV sensitivity.

all_cells.content: Cosmological sum-of-masses bound Σm_ν < 0.12 eV
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing: Σm_ν < 0.12 eV (95% CL, ΛCDM). Tighter bounds from CMB-S4 are anticipated in the late 2020s — the experiment should detect non-zero Σm_ν if the value is ≳ 0.04 eV (the lower bound implied by NH).
ACT + DESI 2024 combined results give Σm_ν < 0.08 eV (95% CL) under ΛCDM, in mild tension with the NH lower bound.

all_cells.content: Dirac vs Majorana character of neutrino mass
realized_examples: No direct experimental test resolves Dirac vs Majorana definitively. Majorana character would manifest in observable lepton-number-violating 0νββ rate. Current 0νββ searches bound m_ββ ≲ 150 meV (KamLAND-Zen 2024). Next-generation experiments target m_ββ ≲ 10-20 meV (LEGEND-1000, nEXO).

all_cells.content: Neutrinoless double-beta decay search programs
realized_examples: KamLAND-Zen (Xe-136) 2024: T_1/2^{0νββ} > 2.3 × 10^{26} yr, corresponding to m_ββ < 36-156 meV (matrix-element dependent). LEGEND-200 (Ge-76) currently taking data. CUPID (Mo-100), nEXO design at next-generation sensitivity m_ββ ≲ 10 meV.

all_cells.content: Heidelberg-Moscow 76Ge 0νββ claim (Klapdor-Kleingrothaus 2001/2006) — FALSIFIED
realized_examples: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001 / 2006 claimed a 0νββ signal in Ge-76 corresponding to m_ββ ≈ 0.32 eV. GERDA Phase II (Agostini et al. 2020) and Majorana Demonstrator (Alvis et al. 2019) decisively excluded the claimed signal: GERDA reached T_1/2 > 1.8 × 10^{26} yr, ruling out the Heidelberg-Moscow claim by orders of magnitude. The 2001/2006 claim is one of the cleanest experimental falsifications in neutrino physics.

all_cells.content: LSND 1996 νe-appearance interpreted as eV-scale sterile neutrino — FALSIFIED in original form
realized_examples: LSND Collaboration 1996, 2001 reported νe appearance in a ν_μ beam at L/E suggesting Δm² ~ 1 eV² oscillation; interpretation: a fourth sterile neutrino at eV mass scale. Subsequent dedicated short-baseline searches: MiniBooNE saw partial confirmation but with energy-spectrum complications; IceCube-DeepCore and MINOS+ excluded the LSND-interpretation 3+1-sterile parameter space at high CL. MicroBooNE 2022 saw no excess in the expected LSND parameter space.

Reactor antineutrino anomaly and Gallium anomaly briefly reignited eV-scale sterile interest but recent reactor flux re-evaluations (HKSS / SM2018) and BEST 2022 results have not converged on a single sterile parameter space consistent across experiments.

all_cells.content: Remaining eV-scale sterile-ν parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies
realized_examples: BEST 2022 Gallium-anomaly results show ~4σ ν_e disappearance consistent with a sterile-ν oscillation at Δm² ~ 1 eV². STEREO and PROSPECT reactor experiments DID NOT confirm reactor anomaly; recent reactor-flux re-evaluations (HKSS, SM2018) reduced the predicted antineutrino flux, partly explaining the original anomaly without sterile neutrinos.

all_cells.content: keV-scale sterile-ν as warm-dark-matter candidate
realized_examples: X-ray line searches (XMM-Newton, NuSTAR, Suzaku) have placed bounds on the sterile-ν → ν + γ decay X-ray line in galaxy clusters. The 2014 ~3.5 keV unidentified line in Perseus and other clusters was tentatively interpreted as sterile-ν decay (m_s ~ 7 keV); subsequent observations (deeper Chandra, XMM, and 2020 Hitomi data) significantly weakened but did not definitively eliminate this interpretation. Structure-formation bounds (Lyman-α, dwarf-galaxy abundance) limit warm-DM masses to m_s ≳ a few keV.

all_cells.content: Type-I see-saw mechanism for neutrino mass generation
realized_examples: No direct experimental test. The see-saw type-I mechanism postulates right-handed neutrinos N_R with large Majorana masses M_R ≫ electroweak scale (typically GeV-TeV-scale for low-scale see-saw; up to ~10^14 GeV for GUT-scale see-saw); the light neutrino mass is generated as m_ν ≈ y_ν² v² / M_R, naturally explaining the smallness of m_ν.

all_cells.content: Leptogenesis as origin of cosmological matter-antimatter asymmetry
realized_examples: No direct experimental test possible at terrestrial experiments. Connections: requires Majorana neutrinos (cf. cell-nu-Dirac-vs-Majorana); requires CP violation in the lepton sector (cf. cell-nu-deltaCP) — though low-energy δ_CP need not match the high-energy CP phases relevant to leptogenesis without additional symmetry assumptions.

all_cells.content: Supernova 1987A neutrino burst detection
realized_examples: SN 1987A burst neutrinos detected at Kamiokande-II (11 events), IMB (8 events), Baksan (5 events). Total energy released ~3 × 10^{53} erg, consistent with core-collapse neutrino emission models. Sub-bound on neutrino mass from time-of-flight spread: m_ν ≲ ~10 eV (loose).
Future galactic SN burst would yield O(10^4) events in Super-K, IceCube, JUNO, and DUNE — a major motivation for the SuperNova Early Warning System (SNEWS) network.

all_cells.content: Liquid-gas critical points in pure fluids — H₂O (T_c = 647 K), CO₂ (T_c = 304 K), Xe (T_c = 290 K)
realized_examples: High-pressure light-scattering and density measurements near the critical point of CO₂, Xe, H₂O, methane, ethane. Critical exponents (ν, η, γ) measured to few-percent precision and consistent with 3D-Ising bootstrap predictions: ν = 0.630, η = 0.036, γ = 1.24.

all_cells.content: Uniaxial antiferromagnets FeF₂ (T_N = 78 K), MnF₂ (T_N = 67 K)
realized_examples: Neutron scattering measurements of order-parameter exponent β at the Néel transition. FeF₂ and MnF₂ are textbook uniaxial antiferromagnets — their order parameter is a 1-component spin staggered magnetization, giving Z_2 symmetry → 3D-Ising universality.

all_cells.content: Binary-fluid critical demixing — 3-methylpentane + nitroethane and similar mixtures
realized_examples: Light-scattering and refractive-index measurements at the upper consolute critical point of binary miscible-fluid systems near the demixing transition. Order parameter is the concentration difference; symmetry is Z_2 (concentration-swap) → 3D Ising.

all_cells.content: 4He superfluid λ-transition at T_λ = 2.17 K — THE 4He λ-puzzle cell
realized_examples: Lipa et al. 1996, 2003 Phys. Rev. B microgravity experiments aboard space shuttle (and ground-based heat-capacity measurements at sub-μK precision near T_λ): measured ν = 0.6709 ± 0.0001 and α = -0.0127 ± 0.0003. Chester et al. 2020 Phys. Rev. D bootstrap for 3D O(2): ν = 0.6719(7), α = -0.0157(20). The two values differ by ~5σ — the current 'λ-puzzle' in critical phenomena.

all_cells.content: Planar (easy-plane) magnetic Curie / Néel transitions — gadolinium intermetallics, BaCo(PO₃)₂
realized_examples: Neutron scattering of planar (XY-symmetry) ferromagnets / antiferromagnets near their Curie / Néel temperature. Exponents consistent with 3D O(2) universality at the few-percent level — less precise than 4He λ but qualitatively confirming universality.

all_cells.content: Isotropic 3D Heisenberg ferromagnets / antiferromagnets — Ni (T_C = 627 K), EuO (T_C = 69 K), RbMnF₃ (T_N = 83 K)
realized_examples: Neutron scattering, magnetization, and specific-heat measurements of isotropic 3D ferromagnets and antiferromagnets give exponents consistent with 3D O(3) bootstrap: ν ≈ 0.7117, η ≈ 0.038, γ ≈ 1.396.

all_cells.content: BKT transition in 2D thin 4He superfluid films at T_BKT ≈ 1-2 K (film-dependent)
realized_examples: Bishop, Reppy 1978 Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 1727 — torsional-oscillator measurements of thin 4He films revealing the universal superfluid-density jump ρ_s(T_BKT⁻)/(k_B T_BKT) = 2 m²/(π ℏ²), confirming Kosterlitz-Thouless prediction.

all_cells.content: 3He-4He mixture tricritical point at T_t ≈ 0.87 K, x_t ≈ 0.67
realized_examples: Specific-heat and concentration measurements of 3He-4He liquid mixtures near the tricritical point where the λ-transition line meets the first-order phase-separation line. Exponents show mean-field-like behavior with logarithmic corrections, as predicted by Riedel-Wegner theory (tricritical d_c = 3).

all_cells.content: Self-avoiding random walks for polymers in dilute good-solvent solution — R_g ~ N^ν with ν ≈ 0.588
realized_examples: Light-scattering measurements of radius of gyration vs degree of polymerization for polystyrene, PMMA, and other polymers in good solvent. ν ≈ 0.588, matching the bootstrap-equivalent 3D-SAW prediction (which corresponds to the 3D O(N→0) limit of the Wilson-Fisher series).

all_cells.content: 3D percolation universality class — cluster-mass scaling exponent τ ≈ 2.189
realized_examples: Site / bond percolation on 3D lattices (cubic, BCC, FCC) — Monte Carlo simulations match analytic predictions for cluster-size exponents. Experimental realizations in sol-gel transitions, microemulsion conductivity, and some glass-formers show qualitative agreement with percolation exponents.

all_cells.content: Lifshitz points in modulated magnetic systems — MnP, NaV₂O₅ candidate observations
realized_examples: MnP phase diagram exhibits a candidate Lifshitz point where ferromagnetic, helical, and paramagnetic phases meet. Neutron-scattering measurements partly confirm Lifshitz-class exponents but the realization is less clean than Wilson-Fisher classes.

all_cells.content: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD — 3D O(4) universality (theoretical only)
realized_examples: Pisarski-Wilczek 1984 prediction: in the massless 2-flavor limit, the chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R / SU(2)_V transition is in the 3D O(4) universality class (4-component order parameter = chiral condensate). Lattice QCD simulations at very small but nonzero quark mass show evidence consistent with O(4) scaling but interpretation requires extrapolation.

all_cells.content: 2D Ising model — exactly solved (Onsager 1944), ν = 1, η = 1/4
realized_examples: Onsager 1944 exact solution gives exact critical exponents. Experimental realizations in thin magnetic films (limited cleanliness) and in lattice-model simulations match perfectly. The 2D Ising CFT has central charge c = 1/2 and is the chiral Ising MTC.

all_cells.content: 3D O(N≥5) universality classes — no clean material realization
realized_examples: Theoretical bootstrap-precision exponents exist for 3D O(N) for any N (Chester et al. 2021, 2022). No clean material realization of N ≥ 5. Some candidates (deconfined-criticality multi-component order parameters) have been proposed but the universality identification is debated.

all_cells.content: Ordinary surface critical exponents in bulk-critical magnets — neutron-reflectometry on Ni, EuO surfaces
realized_examples: Neutron reflectometry of magnetic films at the bulk Curie point reveals modified critical exponents at the surface — the 'ordinary' surface universality class (Diehl 1986).

all_cells.content: Surface-extraordinary universality class — predicted, no clean material realization
realized_examples: Diehl 1986 predicted the 'extraordinary' surface universality where surface ordering exceeds bulk ordering at the bulk-critical point. No clean experimental realization confirmed; some recent quantum-Hall-edge realizations have been proposed.

all_cells.content: Rare-earth Heisenberg ferromagnet EuS (T_C = 16.5 K), other Eu chalcogenides
realized_examples: Neutron-scattering and specific-heat measurements of EuS at its Curie point give ν and γ consistent with 3D-Heisenberg universality at the few-percent level. EuS is canonical because it has nearly-isotropic Heisenberg exchange and minimal lattice anisotropy.

all_cells.content: Isobutyric acid + water binary-fluid critical demixing
realized_examples: Light scattering and refractive-index measurements of isobutyric-acid + water mixtures near the upper consolute critical point. Exponents fit 3D-Ising precisely.

all_cells.content: Canonical WIMP miracle — thermal-relic GeV-TeV particle with weak-scale annihilation cross-section
realized_examples: LZ Collaboration 2024 Phys. Rev. Lett. result: spin-independent cross-section σ_SI < 2.2 × 10⁻⁴⁸ cm² at m_χ = 36 GeV (90% CL); XENONnT 2023 Phys. Rev. Lett. equivalent bounds. Canonical SUSY-neutralino-LSP parameter space at ~weak-scale-cross-section is essentially excluded.
Heavy-WIMP candidates (TeV-PeV scale, with non-standard freeze-out / non-thermal-relic histories) remain viable; the exclusion applies to the canonical thermal-relic WIMP-miracle prediction.

all_cells.content: QCD axion in DFSZ / KSVZ models — sub-eV mass linked to Peccei-Quinn scale
realized_examples: ADMX (Axion Dark Matter Experiment) at U. Washington — most-stringent search for sub-eV QCD axion via axion-photon coupling g_aγγ in a high-Q microwave cavity in strong B-field. ADMX-G2 (2018-2024) exclusion of m_a in 2.66-4.2 μeV range at DFSZ sensitivity. HAYSTAC (Yale) and ABRACADABRA (MIT) extending to higher masses. Other haloscope programs (ORGAN, QUAX, CAPP) ongoing.

all_cells.content: Axion-like particles (ALPs) — generic pseudoscalar bosons in wider mass-coupling space
realized_examples: ALPS-II at DESY (light-shining-through-walls), IAXO (axion helioscope), MADMAX (high-mass haloscope), ADMX-Sidecar — multi-pronged ALP search programs. ALPs decoupled from the strong-CP-solving requirement allow wider g_aγγ - m_a parameter space.

all_cells.content: Sterile neutrino at keV scale as warm-DM candidate (shared with neutrino-phen cell-nu-sterile-keV-WarmDM)
realized_examples: X-ray line searches (XMM-Newton, NuSTAR) bound sterile-ν → γ + ν decay rate; 2014 Bulbul et al. and Boyarsky et al. tentatively detected ~3.5 keV X-ray line in galaxy clusters, suggesting sterile-ν mass ~7 keV. Subsequent observations (Dessert-Rodd-Safdi 2020 Science 367, 1465) significantly weakened the 3.5 keV claim. Structure-formation bounds from Lyman-α and Milky Way satellite-galaxy counts bound m_s ≳ few keV.

Dodelson-Widrow oscillation-production gives a relic abundance dependent on sterile-active mixing; Shi-Fuller resonant production gives alternative cosmological histories. Parameter space partially excluded but not eliminated.

all_cells.content: eV-scale sterile neutrino as primary DM — shared with neutrino-phen cells cell-nu-sterile-eV-LSND-claim-FALSIFIED and cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension
realized_examples: eV-scale sterile-ν as primary DM is largely excluded — Σm_ν cosmological bound (< 0.12 eV from Planck) and active-sterile mixing constraints leave a narrow window where eV-sterile could be a fraction of DM but not the bulk. Some remaining parameter space from BEST gallium-anomaly results.

all_cells.content: Asteroid-mass primordial black holes (10¹⁷-10²² g) as viable DM — shared with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-asteroid-mass
realized_examples: Microlensing surveys (Subaru HSC for short-duration events, OGLE-IV) bound PBH fraction at most masses but leave the asteroid-mass-to-sublunar window (M ≈ 10¹⁷-10²² g) less constrained. PBH-asteroid-mass remains viable as the bulk of DM in this narrow window.

all_cells.content: PBHs in LIGO-mass window (1-100 M_sun) as 100% DM — excluded (shared with compact-astro cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded)
realized_examples: LIGO-Virgo binary-merger rate in the 10-100 M_sun window is incompatible with PBHs constituting 100% of DM — the implied merger rate exceeds the observed LVK rate by orders of magnitude. PBH fraction in this mass window is bounded at f_PBH ≲ 10⁻³.

all_cells.content: Fuzzy / ultralight dark matter — bosonic field at m ~ 10⁻²² eV (de Broglie wavelength ~ kpc)
realized_examples: Pulsar timing array bounds on m_φ ≳ 10⁻²³ eV; Lyman-α forest bounds (Iršič et al. 2017) on m_φ ≳ 2 × 10⁻²¹ eV. Galaxy rotation-curve and dwarf-galaxy-core analyses partially favor or exclude depending on mass. Subhalo / cosmic-web observations partially constrain.
Active galactic-nuclei light-curve searches (superradiance) constrain ultralight bosons coupled to BH spins — Arvanitaki et al. 2015.

all_cells.content: Self-interacting dark matter — σ/m ~ 0.1-10 cm²/g, motivated by small-scale-structure problems
realized_examples: Bullet Cluster (Markevitch et al. 2004) bounds σ/m < 0.7 cm²/g for hot DM-DM collisions; cluster offset measurements give similar bounds. Galaxy-scale observations (cored profiles, dwarf-galaxy diversity, fewer-than-expected satellites) suggest σ/m ~ 0.1-1 cm²/g at galactic-velocity scales — implying velocity-dependent cross-sections (Yukawa-like).
Specific particle realizations: Yukawa-coupled hidden-sector mediators with sub-GeV mass; dark photon coupling; ELDER dark matter.

all_cells.content: Asymmetric dark matter — relic abundance set by a separate dark asymmetry, paralleling baryogenesis
realized_examples: Specific UV completions: hidden-sector baryogenesis with dark-baryon-number; pangenesis (Hall-Marsano-Cui 2010); aidnogenesis (Davoudiasl-Kim-Murayama). Models predict m_DM ~ few GeV (set by ratio Ω_DM/Ω_b ≈ 5 and dark-asymmetry size ~ baryon asymmetry).

all_cells.content: Mirror dark matter — parallel sector of mirror baryons with mirror-baryogenesis
realized_examples: Limited direct testability — mirror-baryon-baryon kinetic mixing with photon (via U(1)_em - U(1)'_em) is constrained but limited. Mirror-DM scenarios remain theoretically motivated by particle-physics symmetry but lack distinctive predictions distinguishable from other hidden-sector DM.

all_cells.content: Dark photon as portal between SM and dark sector — eV-GeV mass with kinetic mixing ε ~ 10⁻³ - 10⁻⁹
realized_examples: Heavy Photon Search (HPS) at JLab, NA64 at CERN, beam-dump constraints (E137, NuCal, U70), DM search experiments interpreted in dark-photon framework. Parameter-space exclusion plots in (m_A', ε) plane have multi-decade reach across mass and kinetic-mixing scales.
Distinct from QCD-axion / ALP — dark photon is a vector (spin-1) mediator, not pseudoscalar.

all_cells.content: Gravitino LSP in SUSY scenarios — TeV-PeV mass, produced from heavy-NLSP late decays
realized_examples: Big-bang-nucleosynthesis (BBN) bounds constrain late gravitino-producing decays — abundant gravitinos produced by NLSP decays during BBN would disrupt light-element abundances. Gravitino-LSP scenarios are restricted to specific NLSP-mass / lifetime windows.
LHC searches for gravitino-LSP signatures (mono-photon + missing energy, displaced vertices) provide complementary bounds in the high-scale-SUSY-mediation parameter space.

all_cells.content: MACHOs as primary halo DM — brown dwarfs, faint stars, planetary-mass compact objects — falsified by microlensing surveys
realized_examples: EROS-2 (2007) and OGLE-IV microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 (8% of halo DM) across the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound that total baryonic mass is far less than Ω_DM, MACHOs cannot constitute the bulk of dark matter.

Distinguishes MACHO-baryonic-compact from primordial-black-hole-compact: PBHs are non-baryonic (formed from gravitational collapse of cosmological density fluctuations) and not subject to the BBN constraint on baryonic matter.

all_cells.content: DAMA/LIBRA annual modulation interpretation as WIMP signal — FALSIFIED by XENON/LZ/PandaX null results
realized_examples: DAMA/NaI (1996-2002), DAMA/LIBRA (2003-2024) reported ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) scintillator detector, consistent with expected WIMP-Earth-velocity-modulation signal. The interpreted parameter space (m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm²) was excluded by XENON1T, XENONnT, LZ at multiple orders of magnitude below the implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100 results using NaI(Tl) target have not reproduced the modulation signal.

all_cells.content: CDMS-II silicon detector 2010 3-event excess — FALSIFIED by subsequent SuperCDMS exclusions
realized_examples: CDMS-II 2010 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 expected background in silicon detectors, interpreted as a hint of low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. Subsequent CDMS-Lite (2014) and SuperCDMS (2018+) silicon-detector exclusions ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint.

all_cells.content: String-axiverse — multitude of ALPs predicted by string compactifications across decades of mass scales
realized_examples: No specific axiverse-ALP has been detected. Theoretical motivation: string compactifications give large numbers of axion-like pseudoscalars with masses log-distributed across many orders of magnitude (Arvanitaki et al. 2010). Some specific candidates: heavy ALPs from Calabi-Yau compactifications, ultra-light axions from string moduli, fuzzy-DM-like axions.

all_cells.content: Supermassive PBHs as seeds for galactic-center SMBHs (cross-reference to compact-astro SMBH cells)
realized_examples: High-redshift SMBH formation (z > 6 quasars with M_BH > 10⁹ M_sun observed by JWST 2023) is challenging for stellar-collapse seed scenarios. PBH-seeded SMBH growth from massive primordial seeds (M ~ 10⁵-10⁶ M_sun) is one of several proposed solutions.

all_cells.content: CMB temperature anisotropy acoustic-peak structure (Planck 2018 / ACT DR6 2024)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (Aghanim et al. 2020 Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6): seven well-resolved acoustic peaks in the temperature power spectrum, positions and amplitudes consistent with ΛCDM at the sub-percent level for the first three peaks. ACT DR6 (2025): independent ground-based measurement of the same acoustic structure at smaller angular scales, consistent with Planck. The 100θ_* parameter is measured to ~0.03% precision.

all_cells.content: BAO sound-horizon measurements across z < 2 (BOSS / eBOSS / DESI) — including DESI 2024 evolving-DE hint
realized_examples: BOSS DR12 (Alam et al. 2017) gave BAO measurements at z = 0.38, 0.51, 0.61 consistent with Planck-LCDM. eBOSS extended to z = 1.5-2.3. DESI 2024 Year-1 BAO results (Adame et al. 2024) reach 3σ tension with ΛCDM when combined with CMB + SN-Ia, hinting at a time-evolving dark-energy equation of state w(z) = w_0 + w_a · z/(1+z) away from w = -1, w_a = 0. The DESI hint is currently the most prominent ΛCDM-extension anomaly.

all_cells.content: Hubble constant from CMB inverse-distance-ladder — H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 (assuming ΛCDM): H_0 = 67.4 ± 0.5 km/s/Mpc. ACT DR6 + Planck combined gives H_0 = 67.81 ± 0.55 km/s/Mpc, fully consistent with the inverse-distance-ladder value. The CMB-derived H_0 is inferred from acoustic-peak positions + BAO sound-horizon calibration + assumed ΛCDM expansion history.

all_cells.content: Hubble constant from local distance ladder — H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc (SH0ES 2022)
realized_examples: Riess et al. (SH0ES Collaboration) 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 934, L7: H_0 = 73.04 ± 1.04 km/s/Mpc via Cepheid-Type-Ia-supernova distance ladder, anchored to Gaia parallaxes + LMC detached eclipsing binaries + masers (NGC 4258).

TRGB-based H_0 (Freedman et al. 2019, Astrophys. J. 882, 34): H_0 ≈ 69.8 ± 1.7 km/s/Mpc — intermediate between CMB and Cepheid-based, with active debate about Cepheid metallicity dependence and TRGB tip identification.
Surface-brightness-fluctuations (Blakeslee et al. 2021): consistent with SH0ES.

all_cells.content: Hubble constant from gravitational-wave standard sirens — GW170817 multi-messenger H_0 ≈ 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Nature 551, 85: GW170817 binary-neutron-star merger combined with electromagnetic identification of host galaxy NGC 4993 gave H_0 = 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc (low precision, single event). Future LISA + Einstein Telescope will measure H_0 with multiple standard-siren events at percent precision.

all_cells.content: σ_8 structure-amplitude from CMB — σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018: σ_8 = 0.811 ± 0.006 (or equivalent S_8 = σ_8 (Ω_m/0.3)^0.5 ≈ 0.832 ± 0.013). ACT DR6 + Planck: consistent.

all_cells.content: σ_8 from weak-lensing surveys — KiDS / DES / HSC give S_8 ≈ 0.77, ~2-3σ lower than CMB-inferred
realized_examples: KiDS-1000 (Asgari et al. 2021): S_8 = 0.766⁺⁰·⁰²⁰₋₀.₀₁₄. DES Y3 (Abbott et al. 2022): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.017. HSC Year 3 (Dalal et al. 2023): S_8 = 0.776 ± 0.020. All three surveys consistently report S_8 ~2-3σ below the Planck-CMB-inferred value.

all_cells.content: Primordial deuterium abundance D/H from quasar absorption spectra — D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵
realized_examples: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102: D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ from high-resolution Keck HIRES + VLT UVES observations of metal-poor damped Lyman-α absorbers. Implies η_b ~ 6 × 10⁻¹⁰, consistent with CMB-derived Ω_b h² at sub-percent precision.

all_cells.content: Primordial ⁷Li/H — BBN-predicted (5 × 10⁻¹⁰) is ~3× observed in metal-poor stars (1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰)
realized_examples: BBN prediction (Cyburt et al. 2016 review): ⁷Li/H ≈ 5.0 × 10⁻¹⁰ for η_b inferred from CMB + D/H. Observation in metal-poor halo stars (Spite plateau, Spite & Spite 1982 + many subsequent): ⁷Li/H ≈ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰. The discrepancy is ~3-4σ persistent for 25+ years.

Possible resolutions: stellar destruction (turbulent mixing); BBN-era new physics modifying Li nuclear reactions; uncertain Li nuclear cross-sections (partly addressed). No resolution accepted by consensus.

all_cells.content: Primordial gravitational waves from inflation — tensor-to-scalar ratio bounded r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck Array 2021)
realized_examples: BICEP/Keck Array 2021 (Ade et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 151301): r < 0.036 at 95% CL from CMB B-mode polarization at degree angular scales, after foreground subtraction.
Future: LiteBIRD (JAXA, ~2030 launch) targets r ~ 10⁻³ sensitivity; CMB-S4 ground-based targets similar.

all_cells.content: Primordial non-Gaussianity — f_NL^local bounded |f_NL^local| < 5.0 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 NG constraints: f_NL^local = -0.9 ± 5.1 (consistent with zero); f_NL^equilateral = -26 ± 47; f_NL^orthogonal = -38 ± 24. Future galaxy surveys (DESI, Rubin, Roman, SKA) target f_NL ~ O(1) sensitivity, distinguishing multi-field vs single-field inflation models.

all_cells.content: Reionization optical depth τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007 (Planck 2018)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 from large-scale E-mode polarization: τ_reio = 0.054 ± 0.007, implying reionization centered at z_reio ≈ 7.7 ± 0.7. Consistent with high-z quasar Gunn-Peterson trough observations (z ~ 6-7) and JWST z > 7 galaxy spectroscopy.

all_cells.content: Spatial curvature Ω_K consistent with zero — |Ω_K| < 0.005 (Planck 2018 + BAO)
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO: Ω_K = 0.001 ± 0.002 (95% CL upper bound |Ω_K| < 0.005). Consistent with the inflationary prediction of spatial flatness.

all_cells.content: Scalar spectral index n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004 (Planck 2018) — slightly red, as inflation predicts
realized_examples: Planck 2018: n_s = 0.965 ± 0.004, robustly different from scale-invariance (n_s = 1) by >7σ. ACT DR6 consistent.

all_cells.content: Cosmological sum-of-neutrino-masses Σm_ν < 0.12 eV — OVERLAP with neutrino-phen cell-nu-Sum-mass-Planck
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing: Σm_ν < 0.12 eV at 95% CL (in ΛCDM). ACT + DESI 2024: Σm_ν < 0.08 eV under ΛCDM, in mild tension with NH lower-bound.

all_cells.content: Cosmological dark-matter abundance Ω_c h² = 0.120 ± 0.001 — CELL-LEVEL CROSS-REFERENCE to dark-matter-candidates
realized_examples: Planck 2018 + BAO: Ω_c h² = 0.1200 ± 0.0012 (sub-percent precision).

all_cells.content: JWST observations of unexpectedly massive / mature galaxies at z > 10
realized_examples: Labbé et al. 2023 Nature 616, 266: JWST CEERS+UNCOVER observations of 6 candidate galaxies at z > 7 with stellar masses ~10¹⁰-10¹¹ M_sun, larger than easily explained by standard ΛCDM galaxy-formation. Subsequent spectroscopic follow-ups (Curtis-Lake et al. 2023; Labbé et al. 2024) have refined some masses downward but the high-z mass-function tension persists.

GN-z11 confirmed at z = 10.6 with mature stellar population (~10⁹ M_sun stellar mass; Bunker et al. 2023). At z > 14 several photometric candidates with stellar masses challenging ΛCDM at current modeling precision.

all_cells.content: EDGES 2018 21cm absorption feature at z ~ 17 — claim contested by SARAS 3 null result
realized_examples: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67: reported absorption feature at 78 MHz (z ~ 17, cosmic dawn) with depth ~500 mK — about 2-3× deeper than standard cosmology predicts. Stimulated speculative explanations (DM-baryon scattering cooling). SARAS 3 (Singh et al. 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607): independent attempt found no evidence for the EDGES signal at the claimed amplitude.
The EDGES result has not been formally retracted; the team maintains the detection. The signal is currently contested with one independent null result.

all_cells.content: GW150914 — first direct GW detection, September 14, 2015 — BBH merger 36+29 → 62 M_sun, 410 Mpc
realized_examples: LIGO H1 + L1 detection at 09:50:45 UTC, 14 Sep 2015 with SNR ~24. m_1 ≈ 36, m_2 ≈ 29 M_sun → final BH 62 M_sun + 3 M_sun emitted as GW. Frequency sweep from ~35 Hz through ringdown at ~250 Hz. The 'chirp' template-matched against numerical-relativity-predicted waveforms at correlation > 0.99.

all_cells.content: GW170817 — first BNS merger and multi-messenger event, August 17, 2017
realized_examples: LIGO H1 + L1 + Virgo detection at 12:41:04 UTC, 17 Aug 2017. Followed within 1.7 s by GRB 170817A (Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL), then days-later by kilonova AT2017gfo (Swope first identification, then DECam, VISTA, Pan-STARRS, ALMA, etc.) at host galaxy NGC 4993 at 40 Mpc. Total mass M_total ≈ 2.74 M_sun; tidal deformability Λ < 800 (constraining NS equation of state).
GW + EM coincidence at 1.7 s lag over 130 million light-year distance gave |Δv_GW / c| ≲ 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ — most stringent test of GW-speed = c.

all_cells.content: GW190521 — heaviest BBH merger to date, 85 + 66 → 142 M_sun (intermediate-mass-BH regime)
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo detection on 21 May 2019. Primary mass ~85 M_sun lies inside the predicted pair-instability mass gap (50-130 M_sun) — challenges standard stellar-evolution models. Final remnant 142 M_sun is the first directly-observed intermediate-mass black hole.

all_cells.content: GW190814 — mass-gap merger 23 + 2.6 M_sun, secondary is heaviest NS or lightest BH known
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo detection 14 Aug 2019. Primary 23 M_sun BH; secondary 2.59 M_sun lies in the long-conjectured NS-BH mass gap (1.5-5 M_sun). Whether the secondary is the heaviest NS yet observed or the lightest BH is undetermined.

all_cells.content: GW230529 — most-recent mass-gap event, candidate NSBH
realized_examples: LIGO + Virgo + KAGRA detection 29 May 2023 (O4a run). Primary mass ~3.6 M_sun (in mass gap), secondary 1.4 M_sun. Both components consistent with NS or BH interpretations given mass-only data; the primary's position in the mass gap is notable.

all_cells.content: GWTC-3 BBH population statistics — ~80 BBH events, mass distribution with excess at ~35 M_sun, low-spin distribution
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048: GWTC-3 BBH mass distribution shows a clear excess at m_1 ~ 35 M_sun (consistent with pulsational-pair-instability pile-up), declining distribution above 50 M_sun, secondary peak hint at ~10 M_sun. Spin distribution is broadly low (effective spin χ_eff distribution centered near 0 with small positive bias).

all_cells.content: NANOGrav 15-year nHz stochastic GW background detection (2023) — Hellings-Downs correlation at 3-4σ
realized_examples: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8: 15-year pulsar timing array data shows excess timing residual at 3-4σ significance with Hellings-Downs angular correlation signature characteristic of GW background. Amplitude consistent with supermassive-BBH-binary cosmological background but a primordial or cosmic-string interpretation is not yet excluded. Joint EPTA + PPTA + InPTA + NANOGrav (IPTA) confirms.
The 2023 result is significantly above the noise but below the 5σ-discovery threshold; further data accumulation will push significance.

all_cells.content: LIGO O3 stochastic-GW-background upper bound at Hz scales — Ω_GW ≲ 6 × 10⁻⁹ at 25 Hz
realized_examples: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 022004: O3 cross-correlation between LIGO Hanford + Livingston + Virgo gives Ω_GW(f) < 6 × 10⁻⁹ in the most sensitive frequency band around 25 Hz. Below the expected astrophysical BBH/BNS unresolved-binary background at this sensitivity.

all_cells.content: Continuous-wave search from Crab pulsar — ellipticity bound below predicted level
realized_examples: LIGO O3 continuous-wave searches give Crab-pulsar GW-strain h_0 upper bound of ~1.5 × 10⁻²⁶ at 2f_rot ≈ 60 Hz, corresponding to NS quadrupole ellipticity ε < 6 × 10⁻⁶. The Crab spin-down luminosity sets an indirect upper bound ε < 2 × 10⁻⁴ — the GW bound is below this by ~30×.
Vela pulsar and other young rapidly-spinning pulsars analogous bounds.

all_cells.content: Galactic core-collapse supernova GW burst — predicted, no detection since LIGO operational
realized_examples: No GW detection for SN 1987A (LIGO not operational yet) or any subsequent extragalactic SN (too distant for current sensitivity). The expected GW signature from a galactic SN at ~10 kpc is detectable across LIGO + Virgo + KAGRA. SNEWS network coordinates SN-neutrino-trigger searches for GW + neutrino + EM coincident SN events.
Theoretical waveforms span burst-like (3D hydrodynamic, rotating progenitor) to long-duration (proto-neutron-star f-mode oscillations).

all_cells.content: EMRIs (extreme-mass-ratio inspirals) — stellar-BH or NS spiraling into supermassive BH — LISA prime target
realized_examples: Predicted EMRI rates from galactic-center stellar-mass-BH capture: ~10-100 events per year detectable by LISA depending on population assumptions. EMRI signals span ~10⁵ orbital cycles in the LISA band — provide the most stringent test of the no-hair theorem and Kerr geometry. LISA launch ~2034-2035.

all_cells.content: Resolved Milky-Way compact-binary mHz GW signals — predicted ~10⁴ for LISA
realized_examples: ~25,000 individually-resolvable galactic compact binaries (white-dwarf-white-dwarf, NS-NS, NS-BH) predicted to be detectable by LISA. Several 'verification binaries' (well-characterized known systems like HM Cancri, ZTF J1539+5027) are pre-confirmed sources.

all_cells.content: Primordial cosmological GW stochastic background — cross-reference to cosmological-observations cell-cosm-primordial-GW-r-bound
realized_examples: Predicted from inflation (energy density Ω_GW ∝ r × A_s); current B-mode bound r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021) translates to cosmological-band stochastic GW upper bound. Alternative sources: cosmic strings (network with G μ ≲ 10⁻¹¹), early-universe phase transitions, inflation-end preheating.
The NANOGrav-detected nHz background is consistent with cosmic strings at G μ ~ 10⁻¹¹ but more likely explained by SMBHB astrophysical foreground.

all_cells.content: GW propagation speed test from GW170817 — |Δv_GW / c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶
realized_examples: GW170817 GW signal arrived 1.7 s before GRB 170817A at Earth, over ~40 Mpc (~130 million light-years) propagation distance. Inferred bound on GW vs photon speed: |Δv / c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ at the relevant frequency. Sharpest test of equivalence-of-GW-and-EM-propagation by ~15 orders of magnitude.

all_cells.content: No-hair theorem tests from BBH ringdown quasi-normal modes — consistent with Kerr metric at current precision
realized_examples: GW150914 + subsequent loud BBH events allow extraction of dominant quasi-normal-mode frequencies and damping times. Fit to Kerr-BH predictions consistent at current precision (~10-20%). Overtone QNM (subdominant modes) extraction from GW150914 (Isi et al. 2019) gave a marginal-significance second mode consistent with Kerr.

all_cells.content: BH-superradiance bounds on ultralight bosons — LIGO continuous-wave search bounds boson masses
realized_examples: Arvanitaki et al. 2015 proposed that ultralight bosons (e.g., fuzzy-DM candidates) trigger BH superradiance — extracting BH spin into a boson cloud that emits monochromatic GW. LIGO continuous-wave searches around known stellar-mass BHs bound the cloud's GW emission, constraining boson masses in the 10⁻¹³ - 10⁻¹¹ eV range.
Cross-reference to dark-matter-candidates cell-dm-fuzzy-ultralight.

all_cells.content: Multi-messenger coincidence in GW170817 — first GW + EM + r-process nucleosynthesis confirmation
realized_examples: GW170817 + GRB 170817A + AT2017gfo kilonova: confirmed BNS as origin of short GRBs; confirmed kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis of heavy elements (Au, Pt, lanthanides); first standard-siren H_0 = 70⁺¹⁹₋₈ km/s/Mpc.
The multi-messenger detection involved ~70 EM partners across radio to gamma-ray bands, the largest coordinated multi-messenger campaign ever.

all_cells.content: Ordinary confined hadronic matter at T ≈ 0, μ_B ≈ nuclear saturation density
realized_examples: Nuclear matter in normal nuclei (16O, 208Pb, etc.); cold-atomic hadron gas at low density. Lattice-QCD at T = 0 reproduces hadron spectrum + nuclear forces from first principles.

all_cells.content: Quark-gluon plasma at T > T_c ≈ 155 MeV, μ_B ≈ 0
realized_examples: RHIC Au+Au at √s_NN = 200 GeV (STAR, PHENIX); LHC Pb+Pb at √s_NN = 2.76, 5.02 TeV (ALICE, ATLAS, CMS). Evidence: large elliptic flow v_2 (hydrodynamic behavior of QGP-as-near-perfect-fluid); jet quenching (high-pT hadron suppression R_AA); charmonium suppression (Matsui-Satz 1986 prediction); strangeness enhancement; thermal photon production. Initial RHIC Au+Au discovery announced 2005.

all_cells.content: Deconfinement / chiral-restoration crossover at T_c ≈ 155 MeV, μ_B = 0 (smooth crossover, not first-order)
realized_examples: Lattice-QCD with 2+1 flavors and physical quark masses: HotQCD Collaboration (Bazavov et al. 2014, 2019), Budapest-Wuppertal Collaboration (Borsányi et al. 2010). Both consistent that the transition at μ_B = 0 is a smooth crossover (not first-order) with pseudo-critical T_c = 156.5 ± 1.5 MeV. Heavy-ion freeze-out temperatures from particle ratios independently support T_c ≈ 150-160 MeV.

all_cells.content: Color-Flavor-Locked phase at asymptotic μ_B, T = 0 (Alford-Rajagopal-Wilczek 1999)
realized_examples: No direct observation. Theoretical prediction: at μ_B ≫ m_strange, 3-flavor coherent Cooper pairing in the (3̄, 3̄)_color × (3̄, 3̄)_flavor channel locks color and flavor SU(3)s together. Pairing gap Δ ~ ΛQCD exp(-1/g²) is exponentially small at asymptotic μ_B but ~10-100 MeV at NS-relevant μ_B ~ 400 MeV.

all_cells.content: 2-flavor color superconductor (2SC) at intermediate μ_B
realized_examples: Predicted at μ_B such that strange quark too heavy for coherent 3-flavor pairing. 2SC pairs u + d quarks in (3̄)_color × (1)_flavor channel. May exist between hadronic and CFL phases in NS interiors or transitional regime.

all_cells.content: Quarkyonic matter — confined-but-quark-dominated phase at high μ_B, intermediate T (McLerran-Pisarski 2007)
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction: at large N_c (number of colors), there's a regime where deconfinement temperature T_d > chiral-restoration temperature T_χ, leaving a phase where quarks are the relevant degrees of freedom but baryons remain confined. At physical N_c = 3, the existence is debated. May appear in NS-interior phase structure as a transition layer.

all_cells.content: QCD critical endpoint — conjectured second-order endpoint of first-order deconfinement line at finite μ_B (active RHIC BES II search)
realized_examples: Predicted from effective models (NJL, PNJL, FRG) at μ_B ~ 200-500 MeV, T ~ 80-150 MeV. RHIC Beam Energy Scan I (2010-2014) explored √s_NN = 7.7-200 GeV; BES II (2019-2023) extended low-energy with higher statistics. Expected signature: enhancement of fluctuations (cumulants of net-proton number) at the endpoint. Current data (Adam et al. STAR 2021) shows hints at √s_NN = 7.7 GeV but no definitive endpoint identification.
Future probes: NICA (Dubna), FAIR (GSI), and J-PARC HI will reach lower √s_NN values + higher μ_B.

all_cells.content: Conjectured continuity between confined-hadronic and CFL phases (Schäfer-Wilczek 1999)
realized_examples: Theoretical conjecture that, in the (T, μ_B) plane, the confined-hadronic and CFL phases are continuously connected (no sharp phase transition) — they share the same global-symmetry-breaking pattern. Originally proposed by Schäfer-Wilczek 1999; subsequent work has debated whether the continuity is rigorous. The crucial question: does N_c = 3 with N_f = 3 actually realize this continuity?

all_cells.content: Free-quark gas at μ_B → ∞, where running coupling α_s(μ) → 0 (asymptotic-freedom limit)
realized_examples: Theoretical asymptotic limit. Asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) implies α_s → 0 at large momentum scales / high density / high temperature. The asymptotic-quark-gas limit at μ_B → ∞ has computable thermodynamic properties (Freedman-McLerran 1977; subsequent NLO + NNLO calculations).

all_cells.content: Hydrodynamic elliptic flow v_2 in Au+Au at RHIC — confirms QGP as near-perfect fluid
realized_examples: STAR + PHENIX measurements of elliptic flow v_2(p_T) in Au+Au at √s_NN = 200 GeV. Magnitude consistent with near-perfect-fluid hydrodynamics; η/s consistent with KSS bound η/s = 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT (Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005).

all_cells.content: Jet quenching / hadron-yield suppression R_AA in Au+Au and Pb+Pb — confirms QGP energy-loss
realized_examples: STAR + PHENIX R_AA(p_T) ≈ 0.2 for high-p_T hadrons in central Au+Au at RHIC; ALICE + ATLAS + CMS confirm at LHC. R_AA = (yield in heavy-ion) / (N_coll × yield in pp) — values well below 1 indicate energy loss in QGP medium.

all_cells.content: Charmonium (J/ψ) suppression in Pb+Pb — Matsui-Satz 1986 prediction confirmed
realized_examples: NA50 at SPS (1996) first observed anomalous J/ψ suppression in Pb+Pb. ALICE at LHC subsequently observed regeneration: J/ψ partially regenerated from c + c̄ recombination at LHC energies (Pb+Pb at 2.76 TeV).

all_cells.content: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD limit — 3D O(4) universality (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984) — CROSS-REFERENCE to real-critical-systems cell-crit-O4-massless-QCD-theoretical
realized_examples: Theoretical prediction (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984): in the massless-2-flavor limit, the chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R → SU(2)_V transition is 3D O(4) universality (4-component chiral condensate as order parameter). Real QCD has nonzero u, d masses — the transition is smoothed to crossover at physical quark masses. Lattice approaches to chiral limit show evidence consistent with O(4).

all_cells.content: QCD phase structure in NS interiors (μ_B ~ 1 GeV, T ≈ 0) — CROSS-REFERENCE to compact-astrophysical-objects
realized_examples: NS mass-radius constraints (NICER J0030+0451; J0740+6620 with M = 2.08 M_sun); GW170817 tidal deformability bound (Λ < 800). These constrain the dense-matter equation of state in the regime where CFL, 2SC, or quarkyonic phases would matter. As of 2026, no definitive observational evidence for quark-matter phases in NS interiors; the EOS is consistent with hadronic up to highest observed densities.

all_cells.content: Sign problem at finite μ_B — fundamental obstacle to lattice-QCD access to high-μ_B regime
realized_examples: At μ_B > 0, the lattice-QCD path-integral measure becomes complex — standard Monte Carlo importance-sampling fails. Workarounds: Taylor expansion around μ_B = 0; analytic continuation from imaginary μ_B; complex Langevin; thimble methods. Each has limitations; none provides ab-initio results at NS-relevant μ_B.

all_cells.content: Hagedorn temperature T_H ≈ 160 MeV — limiting temperature of hadron-resonance-gas (Hagedorn 1965, prefiguring deconfinement)
realized_examples: Hagedorn 1965 observed that hadron-spectrum density grows exponentially: ρ(m) ~ exp(m / T_H) with T_H ≈ 160 MeV. Interpreted as limiting temperature beyond which hadronic description breaks down — preceded the deconfinement understanding.
Modern interpretation: T_H is close to the deconfinement crossover T_c ≈ 155 MeV — Hagedorn's observation was the first hint of finite-T QCD phase structure.

all_cells.content: Strangeness enhancement in heavy-ion collisions — Rafelski-Müller 1982 prediction confirmed
realized_examples: NA35 / NA49 at CERN SPS, STAR / PHENIX at RHIC, ALICE at LHC: enhancement of strange-baryon yields per binary collision in heavy-ion vs pp baseline. K/π ratio enhancement also observed.

all_cells.content: η/s near KSS bound 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT — QGP as 'most perfect fluid'
realized_examples: Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005 conjectured η/s ≥ 1/(4π) for any quantum-field-theory fluid (from AdS-CFT duality). QGP at RHIC + LHC extracted η/s via v_n flow harmonic analysis: η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π) — close to the KSS bound, supporting QGP as 'sQGP' (strongly-coupled, near-perfect fluid).

all_cells.content: Cosmic Microwave Background — Planck-spectrum blackbody at T = 2.725 K (cross-references cosmological-observations CMB cells)
realized_examples: COBE/FIRAS measurement of CMB spectrum (Mather et al. 1990): consistent with Planck blackbody to ~ 10⁻⁵ deviations — sharpest blackbody ever measured. WMAP, Planck, ACT, SPT successive precision measurements of anisotropies.

all_cells.content: Crab Nebula — multi-band synchrotron + inverse-Compton from radio to ~PeV UHE-gamma, the prototypical pulsar wind nebula
realized_examples: Synchrotron spectrum from radio (1 GHz) through optical to X-ray (Chandra), spanning 10 decades in frequency. Inverse-Compton component dominates above ~10 GeV (Fermi-LAT, MAGIC, HESS, LHAASO). LHAASO detected PeV-scale photons from Crab in 2021 (Cao et al. Nature 594, 33), reaching the predicted inverse-Compton tail.

all_cells.content: AGN jet synchrotron emission — radio through X-ray from relativistic electrons in magnetized jets
realized_examples: M87 jet (VLA radio, HST optical, Chandra X-ray, EHT 2019/2022 event-horizon-scale image). Centaurus A multi-band synchrotron. Blazar SEDs (TXS 0506+056, Mrk 421, Mrk 501) with synchrotron + inverse-Compton humps. EHT 2019 imaged M87* shadow at 230 GHz; 2022 Sgr A*.

all_cells.content: Gamma-ray bursts — long-GRBs from massive-star collapsars, short-GRBs from BNS mergers; fireball synchrotron afterglow + prompt mechanism
realized_examples: Long-GRBs: GRB 030329 with SN connection; high-redshift GRB 090423 at z=8.2. Short-GRBs: GRB 170817A coincident with GW170817 (LIGO-Virgo + Fermi-GBM + INTEGRAL, 1.7s after merger). VHE-gamma detected from GRBs by MAGIC (GRB 190114C, first TeV detection) and HESS (GRB 180720B). LHAASO detected ~10 TeV from GRB 221009A — most energetic GRB photon.

all_cells.content: GW170817 kilonova AT2017gfo — UV/optical/IR thermal + r-process radioactive decay (cross-references gravitational-wave-event-catalog)
realized_examples: Kilonova AT2017gfo discovered hours after GW170817 (17 August 2017) at host galaxy NGC 4993, 40 Mpc. Multi-wavelength campaign across ~70 facilities: Swope (discovery), DECam, VISTA, Pan-STARRS, ALMA, Chandra, NuSTAR, etc. Spectra showed thermal continuum at early times → r-process heavy-element absorption (Au, Pt, lanthanides) at later times.

all_cells.content: LHAASO detection of PeV-scale gamma rays from galactic sources — opening UHE-gamma astronomy (2021-2024)
realized_examples: LHAASO (Large High-Altitude Air Shower Observatory, Tibet) reported in 2021 (Cao et al. Nature 594, 33) detection of >0.1 PeV photons from 12 galactic sources, including Crab, LHAASO J2226+6057, and others. Highest-energy detected photon to date is 1.4 PeV. Likely hadronic-pion-decay origin from cosmic-ray protons interacting with molecular clouds in supernova remnants and pulsar wind nebulae.

all_cells.content: IceCube-170922A + TXS 0506+056 multi-messenger — high-energy neutrino + gamma-ray coincidence at blazar
realized_examples: IceCube detected a ~290 TeV neutrino event 22 September 2017 (IceCube-170922A); Fermi-LAT identified spatially-coincident flaring blazar TXS 0506+056 at z = 0.34. Multi-wavelength follow-up at MAGIC, VERITAS, HAWC confirmed elevated gamma-ray state. Combined statistical significance ~3σ for source association. First credible identification of an individual blazar as a high-energy neutrino source.

all_cells.content: Cosmic infrared background — dust thermal emission + reprocessed starlight from galaxies through cosmic time
realized_examples: Spitzer Space Telescope wide-field IR surveys; Herschel; Planck high-frequency channels. JWST 2022+ deep IR imaging resolves the CIB into individual high-z sources. The CIB intensity is comparable to the cosmic optical background, indicating roughly half of all starlight has been absorbed and re-emitted by dust.

all_cells.content: Cosmic X-ray background — predominantly AGN-dominated, resolved into ~80% point sources by Chandra deep fields
realized_examples: Resolved by Chandra Deep Field North/South into individual AGN at z = 0-5. Hard X-ray (10-100 keV) background dominated by obscured Compton-thick AGN. NuSTAR 2013+ resolved hard-X-ray CXB into discrete sources.

all_cells.content: Extragalactic gamma-ray background — blazar-dominated above ~100 MeV (Fermi-LAT)
realized_examples: Fermi-LAT 2010+ measurement of EGB spectrum from 100 MeV-100 GeV. Resolved fraction increases with energy; above ~50 GeV, the EGB is consistent with being entirely from BL Lac-type blazars.

all_cells.content: Imaging atmospheric Cherenkov detection of VHE gamma rays — HESS, MAGIC, VERITAS observed ~250 TeV-band sources
realized_examples: HESS Galactic Plane Survey detected >100 VHE-gamma sources (TeV regime). MAGIC, VERITAS independent detections. CTA (Cherenkov Telescope Array) future to extend by ~10× sensitivity. Standard TeV-band catalogues include TeVCat database with ~250 sources.

all_cells.content: Free-electron-laser coherent X-rays — LCLS-II, European XFEL, SACLA — femtosecond coherent X-ray pulses
realized_examples: LCLS at SLAC (2009+), European XFEL at DESY (2017+), SACLA at SPring-8, SwissFEL. Coherent X-ray pulses at 10⁹+ photons per pulse, femtosecond duration. Applications: protein structure (serial-femtosecond crystallography), shock-compression dynamics, ultrafast magnetism.

all_cells.content: Laser coherent visible emission — atomic-physics stimulated emission, foundational quantum optics platform
realized_examples: Helium-neon (HeNe) lasers, Nd:YAG, diode lasers, ti:sapphire, optical-frequency combs. Frequency-comb-based optical-clock metrology reaching 10⁻¹⁸ stability (Sr, Yb optical clocks). Laser-based atomic physics is foundational for fundamental-physics tests.

all_cells.content: Hawking radiation from astrophysical BHs — predicted thermal emission at T_H ~ 10⁻⁸ K for stellar-mass BHs (Mendeleev empty)
realized_examples: Hawking 1974: T_H = ℏc³/(8πGMk_B) for a Schwarzschild BH. For M = 10 M_sun, T_H ~ 6 × 10⁻⁹ K — far below CMB temperature, hence undetectable in practice for astrophysical BHs. Detectable only for hypothetical primordial BHs near evaporation (mass ~ 10¹⁵ g, T_H ~ 100 MeV gamma).
Analog Hawking radiation observed in laboratory systems (BEC analog black holes, fiber optics) — not strictly the astrophysical phenomenon but supports the underlying theory.

all_cells.content: Axion-photon coupling bounds — CAST + IAXO + haloscope searches; predicted axion-photon mixing not yet detected (cross-references dark-matter)
realized_examples: CAST (CERN Axion Solar Telescope) bounds g_aγγ < 6.6 × 10⁻¹¹ GeV⁻¹ for m_a < 0.02 eV. ADMX haloscope at higher precision in narrow mass window. ALPS-II light-shining-through-walls. IAXO future will improve by ~10×. Astrophysical bounds from horizontal-branch stars + supernova SN 1987A.
Predicted axion-photon mixing in cosmological magnetic fields modifies CMB B-mode polarization patterns; current bounds at the 10⁻¹⁰ GeV⁻¹ level.

all_cells.content: Magnetic monopole EM signatures — Dirac monopole (1931) predicted, GUT-monopole specific to su5-gut-rep-content cells, searched-not-found
realized_examples: MACRO (Gran Sasso 1988-2000), MoEDAL (CERN LHC 2010+), IceCube monopole searches, ANTARES, Auger surface array. All null results, bounding monopole flux below the Parker bound (~10⁻¹⁵ cm⁻² s⁻¹ sr⁻¹).
Specific GUT-monopole searches in cosmic-ray detectors look for the catalysis of nucleon decay (Rubakov-Callan effect) — null.

all_cells.content: SETI technosignatures — radio + optical-laser narrowband-coherent emission of non-natural origin, searched-not-found
realized_examples: SETI searches: Project Phoenix, SETI@home, Breakthrough Listen (2016-present). Optical-SETI for ns-laser pulses (Lick Observatory; UCSB nanosecond-detector). Best documented anomaly: 1977 'Wow! signal' — never reproduced. As of 2026, no confirmed technosignature detection. Bounds limit nearby narrowband transmitters to specific power/distance regimes.

all_cells.content: p → π⁰ e⁺ — minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) prediction τ_p ~ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr RULED OUT by Super-K at τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr (2020)
realized_examples: Georgi-Glashow 1974 prediction: minimal SU(5) gives τ_p ≈ (M_GUT/M_W)⁴ × ℏ/(α² m_p) ≈ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁵ GeV. IMB and Kamiokande late-1980s set τ > 10³² yr (already exceeding minimal-SU(5) prediction by 2-3 orders). Super-K 2020 (Takenaka et al. Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011): τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr at 90% CL — minimal SU(5) ruled out by 4-5 orders of magnitude. Hyper-Kamiokande (commissioning 2027) will extend by ~10×.
This is the cleanest single-channel falsification of a specific GUT framework: minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) is empirically dead.

all_cells.content: p → K⁺ ν̄ — SUSY-SU(5)-favored channel via dimension-5 operators; Super-K τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr (2014), in tension with SUSY-SU(5) for natural SUSY parameter ranges
realized_examples: SUSY-SU(5) dimension-5 operators mediated by colored Higgs / triplet partners give p → K⁺ ν̄ at typical lifetimes 10³³-10³⁵ yr depending on squark/wino mass and Higgsino mass. Super-K 2014 (Abe et al. Phys. Rev. D 90, 072005): τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) > 5.9 × 10³³ yr at 90% CL.
SUSY-SU(5) prediction is in tension with this bound for low-scale SUSY (TeV-scale colored Higgs); pushed to high-scale SUSY scenarios.

all_cells.content: p → π⁺ ν̄ — charged-current channel bounded τ > 3.9 × 10³² yr (Super-K)
realized_examples: Super-K bound (Mine et al. 2008 Phys. Rev. D 77, 052006 updated): τ(p → π⁺ ν̄) > 3.9 × 10³² yr at 90% CL. Predicted in many GUT variants at similar level to p → π⁰ e⁺.

all_cells.content: p → e⁺ γ — rare channel bounded τ > 7 × 10³² yr (Super-K 2009)
realized_examples: Bound from Super-K (Kobayashi et al. 2005 Phys. Rev. D 72, 052007): τ(p → e⁺ γ) > 6.7 × 10³² yr at 90% CL. Predicted at sub-leading rates in most GUT scenarios; sensitive to specific Higgs mediator structures.

all_cells.content: p → η⁰ e⁺ and p → η⁰ μ⁺ — meson-emission channels bounded ~10³³ yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → η⁰ e⁺) > 1.0 × 10³⁴ yr; τ(p → η⁰ μ⁺) > 4.7 × 10³³ yr. Predicted at sub-leading rates in GUT frameworks.

all_cells.content: p → K⁰ ℓ⁺ — neutral-K-meson + lepton channels bounded ~10³² yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → K⁰ e⁺) > 1.0 × 10³³ yr; τ(p → K⁰ μ⁺) > 3.5 × 10³² yr. SUSY-SU(5) predicts ~10³³ yr in standard scenarios.

all_cells.content: p → 3 leptons (e+ e- e+, e+ μ+ μ-, etc.) — exotic channels bounded ~10³² yr
realized_examples: Super-K bounds: τ(p → e⁺ μ⁺ μ⁻) > 3.0 × 10³² yr; τ(p → e⁺ ν̄ν̄) > 1.7 × 10³² yr.

all_cells.content: Neutron-antineutron oscillation (Δ B = 2) — Super-K bounded τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s; future ESS-NNBAR program
realized_examples: Free-neutron oscillation bound (ILL Grenoble 1994): τ_n-n̄ > 0.86 × 10⁸ s. Bound-neutron-in-nucleus bound (Super-K 2015): τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s. ESS-NNBAR future experiment at European Spallation Source will push by ~3 orders of magnitude.
Predicted in B-L-violating BSM scenarios distinct from typical GUTs (e.g., Babu-Mohapatra 2013; some neutrino-mass models).

all_cells.content: Historical: IMB-1 and Kamiokande-I bounds 1980s — first definitive exclusion of minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5)
realized_examples: IMB-1 (Cleveland Salt Mine, 3300 ton water) ran 1981-1991. Kamiokande-I (3000 ton water) ran 1983-1995. Both observed atmospheric-neutrino background but no proton-decay events. By ~1990, τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 10³² yr — already 2-3 orders of magnitude beyond minimal-SU(5) prediction.
Historic significance: established proton-decay detection technology + decisively excluded the simplest GUT scenario.

all_cells.content: Hyper-Kamiokande future program — 260 kton fiducial × ~10× sensitivity over Super-K, target τ > 10³⁵ yr
realized_examples: Hyper-Kamiokande (Tochibora mine, Japan) commissioning 2027, full operation ~2029-2030. 260 kton fiducial water Cherenkov detector (~8× Super-K fiducial). Expected sensitivity τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 1 × 10³⁵ yr after 10 years — within reach of SUSY-SU(5) predictions for natural SUSY-mass parameter space.
DUNE (Sanford, USA) commissioning 2030+ with 40 kton liquid-argon TPC; complementary sensitivity for K-meson channels.

all_cells.content: DUNE future sensitivity to p → K⁺ ν̄ — liquid-argon TPC complementary to water Cherenkov
realized_examples: DUNE 40 kton liquid-argon TPC has lower threshold and better tracking for K⁺ identification than water Cherenkov. Expected τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) sensitivity > 4 × 10³⁴ yr after 10 years — improving over Super-K by ~7×.
Complementary to Hyper-K which is optimized for p → π⁰ e⁺.

all_cells.content: Bound-neutron decay modes — n → ν π⁰, n → e⁺ π⁻, etc.; bounds parallel to proton modes
realized_examples: Super-K bound: τ(n → ν̄ π⁰) > 1.1 × 10³³ yr; τ(n → e⁺ π⁻) > 5.3 × 10³³ yr. Bound neutrons in oxygen-16 nuclei provide bound-nucleon target with same fiducial mass as protons.

all_cells.content: Combined Super-K SUSY-SU(5) constraint — multi-channel bound pushes SUSY scale > ~10 TeV in minimal scenarios
realized_examples: Combined Super-K limits on p → K⁺ ν̄, p → π⁰ e⁺, p → η e⁺, etc., constrain SUSY-SU(5) parameter space. For natural-SUSY scenarios (squark mass ~ TeV), proton-decay rate would exceed bounds. Implies either (a) SUSY scale > 10 TeV (consistent with absence of LHC SUSY signals), (b) specific suppression mechanisms (e.g., flavor structure of Higgs-triplet couplings), or (c) SUSY-SU(5) is not realized.

all_cells.content: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) — alternative SU(5) embedding with suppressed dimension-6 proton decay
realized_examples: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) (Barr 1982; Derendinger-Kim-Nanopoulos 1984) embeds SM fermions differently than Georgi-Glashow. Dimension-6 proton-decay operators are absent at tree level; decay proceeds via different (suppressed) channels. Predicts τ_p > 10³⁵ yr generically — beyond Hyper-K reach.

all_cells.content: Pati-Salam SU(4)_c × SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R — quark-lepton unification without proton decay (in minimal version)
realized_examples: Pati-Salam (1974) is the original quark-lepton unification — leptons are 'fourth color'. The SU(4)_c gauge bosons mediate quark-lepton transitions but not directly baryon-number violation; minimal Pati-Salam preserves baryon number. Extensions (e.g., via R-parity violation or specific Higgs structures) can give proton decay; constrained but not generally falsified.
Distinct from SU(5)/SO(10): Pati-Salam is a partial unification rather than a full simple-group GUT.

all_cells.content: SO(10) GUT — predicts proton decay with rate dependent on Higgs-multiplet structure; bounded but generically viable
realized_examples: SO(10) GUT (Fritzsch-Minkowski 1975; Georgi 1975) embeds SM + right-handed neutrino in 16-rep. Proton decay via dimension-6 operators predicted at τ ~ 10³⁵ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁶ GeV. Higher M_GUT in SO(10) compared to SU(5) due to different beta-function running — natural suppression of proton-decay rate.

all_cells.content: Atmospheric-neutrino background — dominant systematic for proton-decay sensitivity at large fiducial masses
realized_examples: Atmospheric ν_μ-induced backgrounds dominate at high fiducial masses. Super-K and Hyper-K develop event-shape + ring-pattern + timing selection criteria to suppress. DUNE liquid-argon TPC has different background structure — better lepton-energy resolution but different particle-ID systematics.
Background-limited sensitivity scales as √(M × T) rather than M × T at high statistics — important for assessing Hyper-K's ultimate reach.

all_cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4: Coulomb-branch operator u with Δ_u = 2 (protected, exact)
realized_examples: Seiberg-Witten 1994: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 is conformal; Coulomb-branch operator u = ⟨Tr φ²⟩ has protected scaling dimension Δ_u = 2 (the canonical Coulomb-branch dimension for SU(2)).
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-su2-Nf4-coulomb.

all_cells.content: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4: a-anomaly = 23/24, c-anomaly = 7/6
realized_examples: Anselmi-Erlich-Freedman-Johansen 1997 / Intriligator-Wecht 2003 a-maximization gives exact central charges for SU(2) N_f=4 at the conformal point: a = 23/24, c = 7/6. The ratio a/c = 23/28 < 1 satisfies the Hofman-Maldacena bounds.

all_cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_0 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 6/5 (irrational scaling, exact)
realized_examples: Argyres-Douglas 1995 discovered SCFTs at strong-coupling singular points of Coulomb branches with irrational Δ_u. H_0 theory (also called A_1 or (A_1, A_2) AD): rank-1 Coulomb branch with single Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 6/5.
First example of irrational Coulomb-branch dimension — distinguished feature of AD theories vs Lagrangian N=2 SCFTs.

all_cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_1 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 4/3
realized_examples: AD H_1 rank-1 theory: Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 4/3. Realized at specific Coulomb-branch singular point of SU(2) N_f=1 SQCD. Related H_2 has Δ_u = 3/2; together H_0/H_1/H_2 form the rank-1 AD series at low-rank end.

all_cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 3 (rank-1, isolated SCFT)
realized_examples: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_6 flavor symmetry, Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 3. Realized from M5-brane on 3-punctured sphere with E_6 punctures (class S T_3 building block).
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-mn-e6-coulomb.

all_cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory: Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_6, dimension 22
realized_examples: Higgs branch of MN E_6 is the minimal nilpotent orbit of e_6 Lie algebra — 22-complex-dimensional hyperkähler quotient. Hilbert series counts holomorphic functions on this orbit and matches AGT-derived index calculations.
Cross-references higgs-coulomb-branches cell-hcb-mn-e6-higgs.

all_cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_7 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 4
realized_examples: MN E_7 theory: rank-1 isolated SCFT, E_7 flavor symmetry, Δ_u = 4. Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_7 (dim 34).

all_cells.content: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_8 theory: Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 6
realized_examples: MN E_8 theory: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_8 flavor symmetry, Δ_u = 6. Higgs branch = minimal nilpotent orbit of E_8 (dim 58). Largest exceptional MN theory.

all_cells.content: Schur index = vacuum character of associated 2D chiral algebra (Beem-Lemos-Rastelli et al. 2015 — 2D-4D correspondence)
realized_examples: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015: every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra (Vertex Operator Algebra). The Schur limit of the 4D superconformal index equals the vacuum character of this 2D chiral algebra.
