Examples: AD H_0 ↔ (2,5) Virasoro minimal model; SU(2) N_f=4 ↔ affine so(8)_-2 chiral algebra; MN E_6 ↔ affine e_6_-3.

all_cells.content: AGT correspondence: SU(2) N_f=4 instanton partition function = 4-point Liouville conformal block (Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010)
realized_examples: Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010: Nekrasov instanton partition function Z_inst for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 equals 4-point conformal block in 2D Liouville CFT with c_Liouville = 1 + 6(b + 1/b)². Confirmed at multiple instanton orders via direct calculation.
Generalizations: SU(N) ↔ A_N Toda CFT; class-S theories ↔ Liouville/Toda on punctured Riemann surfaces (Gaiotto 2009).

all_cells.content: Class-S T_3 building block: M5-brane on 3-punctured sphere with three full punctures = Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 theory
realized_examples: Gaiotto 2009: M5-brane (2,0) A_2 theory on Riemann surface with three full punctures gives MN E_6 SCFT. T_N generalization: A_N-type M5-branes on 3-punctured sphere give 'T_N theories' with SU(N)³ flavor symmetry; T_2 trivial, T_3 = MN E_6, T_4 = rank-3 SCFT, etc.
Class-S construction systematizes the construction of large families of N=2 SCFTs from geometric data.

all_cells.content: Conformal bootstrap bounds on a / c ratio for N=2 SCFTs — Hofman-Maldacena 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 + tighter SUSY-aware bounds
realized_examples: Hofman-Maldacena 2008 derived universal bound 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 for any unitary 4D CFT. For N=2 specifically, tighter bounds exist (Beem-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2014). Known SCFTs (AD, MN, SU(2) N=2 SQCD) satisfy bounds with various margins.
Stress-tensor 4-point function bootstrap (Beem et al. 2014) gives rigorous-bounded N=2 bootstrap data.

all_cells.content: BPS spectrum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 at generic Coulomb-branch point — Seiberg-Witten geometry derived
realized_examples: Seiberg-Witten 1994: BPS spectrum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 derived from genus-1 SW curve. At generic Coulomb-branch point, BPS multiplet includes W-bosons (electrically charged) + dyons (magnetically charged) + quarks (matter hypermultiplets) — full lattice structure determined by SW periods.
Spectrum of BPS states changes discontinuously across Coulomb-branch 'walls of marginal stability' — Denef-Moore 2007+.

all_cells.content: Pestun localization of S⁴ partition function for SU(2) N=2 SQCD (2007 / 2012) — exact ZZ-matrix-integral expression
realized_examples: Pestun 2012, Commun. Math. Phys. 313, 71: SUSY localization on S⁴ for N=2* SU(2) gauge theory and N=2 SU(2) SQCD reduces the path integral to a finite-dimensional matrix integral over Coulomb-branch scalar VEV. Exact result valid at all coupling.
Extended to SU(N) and class-S theories via subsequent work (Nekrasov-Okounkov, Pestun-Zabzine, etc.).

all_cells.content: Nekrasov instanton partition function — exact instanton sum for SU(2) N=2 SQCD via Omega-background regularization
realized_examples: Nekrasov 2003: exact instanton partition function Z_inst(a, m_i, ε_1, ε_2) for SU(2) N=2 SQCD computed via Omega-background regularization on R⁴ (replacing Coulomb-branch parameters and masses by VEVs). Z_inst is a power series in instanton number; convergent.
AGT identifies Z_inst with 2D Liouville 4-point conformal block — gives independent verification.

all_cells.content: Argyres-Douglas H_0 Schur index = (2, 5) Virasoro minimal model vacuum character
realized_examples: Beem-Rastelli-van Rees 2018+: associated 2D chiral algebra for AD H_0 = (2, 5) Virasoro minimal model. Schur index of H_0 = vacuum character of (2, 5) minimal model = q^(-1/60) (1 + q² + q³ + q⁴ + 2q⁵ + ...).
Pattern: AD (A_1, A_{2n-1}) theories ↔ (2, 2n+3) Virasoro minimal models.

all_cells.content: π⁰ → γγ decay rate from Adler-Bell-Jackiw chiral anomaly: Γ(π⁰ → γγ) = (α² m_π³)/(64 π³ F_π²) — exact result, confirmed to ~few percent
realized_examples: Adler 1969 + Bell-Jackiw 1969: triangle-diagram chiral anomaly gives π⁰ → γγ amplitude unambiguously. Predicted rate ~ 7.7 eV partial width.
Experimental: Γ(π⁰ → γγ) ≈ 7.83 eV (PDG 2022) — agreement at sub-percent level after radiative corrections.
PrimEx-II experiment (2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 011803): direct measurement Γ(π⁰ → γγ) = 7.802 ± 0.117 eV, agreement with NLO prediction.

all_cells.content: SM gauge anomaly cancellation — requires precise charge quantization (Y_L + Y_R relations); requires color-flavor matching
realized_examples: SM SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)_Y has perturbative gauge anomalies that cancel only because of specific quark and lepton charge assignments. SU(3)³ trivially cancels (vectorlike); SU(2)² × U(1)_Y requires Σ Y_L = 0 within doublets; U(1)_Y³ requires 6 Y_L^3 + 3 Y_R^3 = 0; mixed SU(3)² × U(1)_Y, SU(2)² × U(1)_Y, gravitational × U(1)_Y all constrained.

Result: SM matter content is uniquely determined (up to overall normalization) by gauge anomaly cancellation + minimal Higgs content. Predicts charge quantization (electron charge = -3 × down-quark charge).

all_cells.content: 't Hooft anomaly matching forces chiral symmetry breaking in QCD: SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V — pion as Goldstone boson
realized_examples: 't Hooft 1980: matching axial-current anomalies between UV (quark-gluon) and IR (hadrons) is incompatible with unbroken chiral symmetry for QCD with N_c = 3 colors and N_f ≥ 2 flavors. Therefore chiral symmetry MUST break.
Frishman-Schwimmer-Banks-Yankielowicz 1981: detailed verification of 't Hooft anomaly matching for QCD chiral breaking pattern.
Experimental: hadronic spectrum verifies SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V breaking pattern via pion Goldstone bosons (M_π ≪ Λ_QCD), η-η' singlet anomaly, etc.

all_cells.content: Wess-Zumino-Witten term in chiral Lagrangian — determines η, η' decay rates and U(1)_A anomaly
realized_examples: Wess-Zumino 1971 + Witten 1983 (Nucl. Phys. B 223, 422): chiral anomaly requires non-trivial topological term (WZW term) in low-energy chiral Lagrangian. Term level n is quantized for consistency.
Predictions: η → π⁺ π⁻ γ (anomaly-derived branching), η' decay rates, π⁰ π⁰ → γγ scattering at threshold.
U(1)_A anomaly explains η' mass — would be Goldstone if not for anomaly (Witten 1979, Veneziano 1979).

all_cells.content: Witten SU(2) global anomaly — requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; consistency with 3 generations confirmed
realized_examples: Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324: SU(2) gauge theory has Z_2 global anomaly π_4(SU(2)) = Z_2 — partition function changes sign under large gauge transformations unless the matter content has even number of left-handed SU(2) doublets.
Result: SM SU(2)_L gauge theory requires even number of left-handed doublets per generation; SM has 4 per generation (1 quark doublet × 3 colors + 1 lepton doublet = 4) — anomaly cancels.

all_cells.content: 3D topological insulator Z_2 invariant (Fu-Kane-Mele 2007) — confirmed via Bi₂Se₃ ARPES surface-state observation
realized_examples: Fu, Kane, Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803: Z_2 topological invariant for time-reversal-symmetric 3D insulators. Two distinct classes: Z_2 = 0 (trivial) and Z_2 = 1 (topological).

Experimental confirmation: Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ family (Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101; Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398). ARPES sees single Dirac-cone surface state, characteristic of Z_2 = 1.
Cell-level overlap with topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family.

all_cells.content: Integer quantum Hall conductivity = Chern number × e²/h — TKNN formula (Thouless-Kohmoto-Nightingale-den Nijs 1982)
realized_examples: TKNN 1982: integer QH conductivity σ_{xy} = ν × e²/h with ν = first Chern number of Bloch-band Berry curvature integrated over Brillouin zone. Z-valued topological invariant.
Confirmed: QH plateau at ν = e²/h quantized to part-per-billion precision (von Klitzing 1980); resistance standard (R_K = h/e² ≈ 25812.807 Ω) defined by this effect.

all_cells.content: Fidkowski-Kitaev Z_8 classification — interacting 3D topological superconductors classified by Z_8 instead of Z (non-interacting collapse to 8 distinct phases)
realized_examples: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509: free-fermion classification of 1D topological superconductors gives Z (number of Majorana zero modes per end), but generic 4-fermion interactions reduce this to Z_8 — phases differing by 8 collapse to the trivial phase.
Generalization to higher dimensions and other symmetry classes: Wen 2016, Freed-Hopkins 2016, Kapustin-Thorngren 2014 cobordism analysis.

all_cells.content: Majorana zero modes at p-wave superconductor boundaries — anomaly-inflow boundary state of 1D topological SC (Kitaev chain)
realized_examples: Kitaev 2001, Phys.-Usp. 44, 131: 1D spinless p-wave superconductor has Majorana zero mode at each end — boundary anomaly inflow of bulk SPT order.

Signatures observed: zero-bias-conductance-peak (ZBCP) in InSb/Al nanowires (Mourik et al. 2012, Science 336, 1003); InAs/Al (Albrecht et al. 2016, Nature 531, 206). But: NOT all ZBCPs are MZMs — recent retraction of 2018 Microsoft claim (Zhang et al. 2018 retraction).
Cell-level overlap with topo-mat v22 cell-ti-Microsoft-InSb-Al-2018-retracted.

all_cells.content: Parity anomaly on 3D TI surface — single Dirac fermion has anomalous parity, half-integer Hall conductance σ_xy = (n + 1/2) e²/h
realized_examples: Niemi, Semenoff 1983; Redlich 1984: single 2+1D Dirac fermion has parity anomaly, giving half-integer Chern-Simons level. Manifested on 3D TI surface as half-integer Hall conductance under broken time-reversal.
Confirmed via observations of half-integer quantum anomalous Hall states in magnetically doped TIs (Chang et al. 2013, Science 340, 167; subsequent refinements).

all_cells.content: Haldane SPT phase — S=1 antiferromagnetic chain has symmetry-protected gapped phase with edge spin-1/2 modes
realized_examples: Haldane 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1153: prediction that integer-spin Heisenberg antiferromagnetic chains have gapped ground state with hidden topological order.
Confirmed: Ni(C₂H₈N₂)₂NO₂ClO₄ ('NENP') and other S=1 chain materials show Haldane-gap phenomenology; edge spin-1/2 states observed.
Modern classification: Haldane phase = SPT with SO(3) × Z_2^T protection; Z_2 invariant.

all_cells.content: QCD θ-vacuum anomaly + experimental |θ| < 10⁻¹⁰ from neutron EDM = strong-CP problem (Peccei-Quinn axion proposed solution)
realized_examples: QCD has θ-vacuum term L_θ = (θ g_s²/32 π²) G G̃ from non-perturbative anomaly. CP-violating term that contributes to neutron EDM.
Experimental: |d_n| < 1.8 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm (Abel et al. 2020 ultracold neutron EDM, Phys. Rev. Lett. 124, 081803), constrains |θ| < 1.5 × 10⁻¹⁰.
Strong-CP problem: why is θ ~ 0 to such precision? Natural value would be ~ 1. Peccei-Quinn 1977 proposed dynamical resolution via axion.

all_cells.content: Chern-Simons level quantization — gauge invariance under large gauge transformations forces integer CS level
realized_examples: Witten 1989: 3D Chern-Simons gauge theory consistency requires integer level k. Generalizes to integer-quantization of topological terms in higher-dimensional theories.
Application: Wess-Zumino term level in chiral Lagrangian quantized as integer (n = N_c = 3 for QCD); fractional QH ν = 1/m levels protected by m-fold flux composite-fermion structure.

all_cells.content: Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain in ultracold-atom lattices — SPT phase with Zak-phase invariant directly measured
realized_examples: Atala et al. 2013, Nature Phys. 9, 795: Zak phase of SSH chain measured directly in ultracold-atom optical lattice via Ramsey interferometry.
More recent: photonic SSH (Naz et al. 2018), polariton SSH (Solnyshkov et al. 2017), trapped-ion SSH simulations.

all_cells.content: Cobordism classification of SPT phases (Freed-Hopkins 2016, Kapustin-Thorngren 2014+) — predicts new SPT phases without confirmed material realizations
realized_examples: Freed, Hopkins 2016, arXiv:1604.06527: SPT phases in d spatial dimensions with symmetry group G classified by reduced cobordism groups Ω^G_{d+1}. Cross-references freed-hopkins-cobordism v8.
Predicted new SPT phases: time-reversal-protected fermionic SPTs with various global symmetries (Z_2^T extensions, U(1) × Z_2^T, etc.); some confirmed (3D TI), others awaiting material realization.

all_cells.content: Callan-Harvey anomaly-inflow mechanism — boundary anomaly = bulk topological term; established by domain-wall fermion analysis
realized_examples: Callan, Harvey 1985, Nucl. Phys. B 250, 427: anomalies localized on boundaries / domain walls inflow from bulk topological terms. Generalizes from index theorems to QFT.
Modern applications: domain-wall fermions in lattice QCD (Kaplan 1992); 3D TI surface states; M-theory cobordism via anomaly inflow.

all_cells.content: MOND explains galaxy rotation curves without dark matter via low-acceleration modification — empirical success at galactic scales
realized_examples: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365 (MOND proposal): modify Newton's 2nd law for a < a_0 ~ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s².

Strong empirical successes: explains rotation curves of ~150 disk galaxies (SPARC sample, Lelli-McGaugh-Schombert 2016) with single fitted parameter a_0; explains Tully-Fisher relation; explains baryonic Tully-Fisher relation (McGaugh 2011) — slope and normalization match MOND prediction.

Failures: (i) cluster-scale missing mass — MOND requires additional 'cluster dark matter' (residual factor ~2 missing); (ii) Bullet Cluster (Clowe et al. 2006) — gravitational lensing centered on collisionless mass, not gas — strong evidence for DM independent of modified gravity.

all_cells.content: Bekenstein TeVeS — relativistic MOND completion — broadly falsified by GW170817 graviton-speed bound
realized_examples: Bekenstein 2004, Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509: Tensor-Vector-Scalar relativistic theory reducing to MOND in nonrelativistic limit. Predicted graviton speed c_T ≠ c due to vector field coupling.

GW170817 (Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13): GW + GRB 170817A coincidence → c_T - c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ × c over 40 Mpc propagation. Falsifies generic TeVeS classes predicting Δv ~ O(1) or O(0.1).
Skordis et al. 2021 RMOND: GW170817-compatible MOND extension still under investigation.

all_cells.content: f(R) gravity as dark-energy alternative — constrained by LSS growth + CMB; chameleon-screened to evade solar-system PPN
realized_examples: Hu-Sawicki 2007, Phys. Rev. D 76, 064004: f(R) = R - m² c1 (R/m²)^n / (c2 (R/m²)^n + 1) provides Λ-like dark energy. Chameleon screening evades solar-system bounds.
Constraints: cosmological LSS growth f σ_8 < 0.05% deviation from GR allowed; CMB constraints from Planck. Specific Hu-Sawicki f(R) with |f_R0| < 10⁻⁴ — viable but very-near-GR.
Cassini PPN bound γ - 1 < (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵ requires |f_R0| < 10⁻⁶ in unscreened regime.

all_cells.content: DGP brane-world gravity — broadly disfavored by cosmological LSS + ruled out for graviton speed by GW170817
realized_examples: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208: 5D Einstein-Hilbert + 4D induced Einstein-Hilbert on brane → 4D IR-modified gravity with crossover scale r_c.

Self-accelerating branch (proposed dark-energy alternative): conflicts with Type Ia SN cosmography (Maartens, Majerotto 2006), CMB+LSS data (Lombriser et al. 2009), and predicts Δv/c ~ O(H₀ r_c) which conflicts with GW170817.
Normal branch + ghost issues remain (Charmousis et al. 2006).

all_cells.content: Horndeski-class scalar-tensor theories — most subclasses broadly falsified by GW170817; only narrow surviving variants remain
realized_examples: Horndeski 1974 + modern revival (Deffayet-Esposito-Farese-Vikman 2009): most general 2nd-order scalar-tensor Lagrangian in 4D. Includes Galileons, kinetic gravity braiding, etc.
Pre-GW170817: large parameter space allowed.

Post-GW170817 (Lombriser-Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382; Creminelli-Vernizzi 2017; Sakstein-Jain 2017): Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ requires G_4(φ,X) function be independent of X (kinetic term) — kills G_5 and most of G_4. Only quintessence-like + non-minimally coupled subset survives.
Subsequently: 'Beyond-Horndeski' DHOST class (Crisostomi et al. 2016) similarly heavily constrained.

all_cells.content: dRGT massive gravity — graviton mass bounded m_g < 6 × 10⁻²³ eV; consistent with GW170817 within bounds
realized_examples: de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101: ghost-free massive-graviton Lagrangian. Predicts dispersion relation E² = p² c² + m_g² c⁴.
GW170817 + GW150914 LIGO/Virgo dispersion-test bounds: m_g < 1.76 × 10⁻²³ eV/c² (Abbott et al. 2021 GWTC-3 + various single-event analyses).
Solar-system + LLR bounds independently constrain m_g.

all_cells.content: Hassan-Rosen bimetric gravity (two interacting metrics) — broadly disfavored by GW170817 propagation-speed bound
realized_examples: Hassan, Rosen 2012, JHEP 02, 126: ghost-free bimetric extension of dRGT massive gravity, two interacting tensor fields with mass mixing.
Bimetric theories predict generically Δv/c ≠ 0 from kinetic mixing — broadly falsified by GW170817 unless tuned to be near-GR.
Cosmological solutions: bigravity tested as dark-energy alternative; tight constraints from CMB + LSS (Akrami et al. 2018).

all_cells.content: PSR J0737-3039 double-pulsar — confirms GR's quadrupole formula to ~0.01%, constrains scalar-tensor ω_BD > 40,000 (Cassini bound)
realized_examples: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050: 16 years of precision timing of PSR J0737-3039 (the double pulsar). Tests 7 post-Keplerian relations of GR simultaneously.
Confirms GR orbital decay (quadrupole GW emission) to 0.013%. Largest-effect non-GR alternative: Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor → tests put ω_BD > 1.4 × 10⁵ (combined with Cassini).
Hulse-Taylor PSR B1913+16 (1974+): first binary-pulsar; ~10% precision tests; tightened by J0737-3039.

all_cells.content: Solar-system PPN parameter γ — Cassini Shapiro-time-delay: γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵, constrains Brans-Dicke ω_BD > 40,000
realized_examples: Bertotti, Iess, Tortora 2003, Nature 425, 374: Cassini superior-conjunction Shapiro-time-delay measurement gives γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵. Most precise PPN test of GR.
Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor predicts γ - 1 = -1/(2 + ω_BD); Cassini bound → ω_BD > 40,000 (original Brans-Dicke 1961 predicted ω ~ 1).

all_cells.content: LIGO/Virgo binary-merger waveforms — tests of post-Newtonian + ringdown phase against GR; constrains massive-graviton + Horndeski variants
realized_examples: LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3 (Abbott et al. 2021, Astrophys. J. 932, 49): tests of various PN coefficients + parametrized inspiral / ringdown / merger phases vs. GR templates. Bounds on Brans-Dicke ω_BD, massive graviton m_g, Lorentz-violation parameters, dispersion test coefficients.
All current LIGO/Virgo tests consistent with GR; bounds on deviations placed.

all_cells.content: EHT M87 / Sgr A* BH shadow tests — shadow diameter consistent with Kerr-BH prediction; constrains modified-gravity BH variants
realized_examples: EHT M87 (2019) shadow diameter 42 ± 3 μas, consistent with Kerr-BH prediction at ~1% precision. EHT Sgr A* (2022) similar.
Constraints: non-Kerr 'hair' parameters bounded; BH shadow modifications from scalar-tensor or Horndeski variants constrained to < few % of Kerr value.
Active program: testing Kerr-no-hair theorem via near-horizon imaging.

all_cells.content: Chameleon / symmetron screening mechanisms — laboratory fifth-force bounds + Eöt-Wash + atomic-interferometry tests
realized_examples: Khoury, Weltman 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 171104 (chameleon proposal): density-dependent scalar mass evades solar-system constraints by screening in high-density regions.
Tests: Eöt-Wash torsion balance + Casimir-force experiments + atom-interferometry (Hamilton et al. 2015 atomic interferometer chameleon search) constrain parameter space.
Galaxy-cluster + cosmological constraints (CHIPS catalog, GR-tests).
Specific symmetron / chameleon coupling × mass parameter regions ruled out.

all_cells.content: Einstein-aether (Lorentz-violating gravity) — c_T - c constraints from GW170817 strongly disfavor most natural parameter space
realized_examples: Jacobson, Mattingly 2001, Phys. Rev. D 64, 024028: timelike vector field breaking local Lorentz symmetry, modifies GW propagation.
GW170817 bound: Einstein-aether's c_a parameters (kinetic vector couplings) constrained to |c_a| < 10⁻¹⁵; only highly-tuned points survive.
Hořava-Lifshitz gravity (related class) similarly constrained.

all_cells.content: MOND on galaxy-cluster scales — Bullet Cluster gravitational lensing shows mass offset from gas, strong evidence against pure MOND
realized_examples: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109: Bullet Cluster (1E 0657-56) merging cluster system — weak-lensing mass map is offset from X-ray gas (which traces baryons). Suggests collisionless dark matter spatially separated from baryons during cluster merger.

Pure MOND cannot account for this offset: gravitational lensing should follow baryonic mass distribution in MOND. Requires either 'cluster dark matter' (~ neutrinos with eV mass — ruled out by cosmology) or breakdown of MOND.
Additional cluster pairs: MACS J0025.4-1222 (Bradač et al. 2008), ZwCl 0024+17 — similar mass-baryon offsets.

all_cells.content: RMOND (Skordis-Złośnik 2021) — GW170817-compatible relativistic MOND with c_T = c by construction
realized_examples: Skordis, Złośnik 2021, Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 161302: 'RMOND' relativistic MOND theory designed to be GW170817-compatible (graviton speed = c by construction).
Reproduces MOND phenomenology at galactic scales; cosmological consequences under investigation; not yet fully tested against CMB + LSS data.
Highly tuned construction; whether 'natural' completion of MOND remains debated.

all_cells.content: Galileon screened-scalar gravity — Vainshtein screening keeps consistent with solar-system PPN; constrained by cosmological data
realized_examples: Nicolis, Rattazzi, Trincherini 2009, Phys. Rev. D 79, 064036: Galileon scalar field with derivative couplings. Vainshtein screening at small scales evades solar-system bounds.
Cosmological viability: large-scale-structure growth tests (Khoury 2010 review); recent post-GW170817 analyses tightly constrain Galileons.
Most natural cubic-Galileon dark-energy realizations ruled out by GW170817 (Sakstein-Jain 2017).

all_cells.content: Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range energy spectrum: E(k) ~ k^(-5/3) — confirmed at high Reynolds numbers in DNS and laboratory experiments
realized_examples: Kolmogorov 1941, Dokl. Akad. Nauk SSSR 30, 301: dimensional argument from energy-cascade rate ε gives E(k) ~ ε^(2/3) k^(-5/3) in inertial range.
DNS confirmation: at Re_λ ~ 1000+, inertial range spans 1-2 decades in k with measured spectral slope -1.67 ± 0.02 (consistent with -5/3 with multifractal correction).
Atmospheric / oceanographic measurements: -5/3 slope confirmed over wide range of conditions.

all_cells.content: Pipe flow transition to turbulence at Re ~ 2300 (Reynolds 1883)
realized_examples: Reynolds 1883, Phil. Trans. R. Soc. 174, 935: critical Reynolds number Re_c ~ 2000-2300 for transition from laminar to turbulent flow in pipe flow. Mechanism: linear-instability-amplified disturbances trigger nonlinear breakdown.

Modern: subcritical instability with finite-amplitude threshold; recent precise experiments + DNS (Avila et al. 2011, Science 333, 192) measure decay-vs-splitting balance of turbulent puffs gives Re_c = 2040 ± 10.

all_cells.content: Multifractal intermittency corrections to K41 — Sq(p) = ζ_p with anomalous scaling deviating from p/3
realized_examples: Frisch-Parisi multifractal model (1985): higher-order velocity-structure-function scaling Sq(p) = ζ_p deviates from p/3 (K41 prediction). Anomalous exponent reflects intermittency.
Measurements: ζ_4 ≈ 1.28 ± 0.02 vs K41 prediction 4/3 ≈ 1.33; ζ_6 ≈ 1.78 vs K41 prediction 2.0. Deviations grow with p — characteristic of multifractal cascades.
She-Lévêque 1994 (Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 336) ζ_p = p/9 + 2 - 2(2/3)^(p/3) provides excellent empirical fit.

all_cells.content: NIF inertial-confinement fusion ignition December 5, 2022 — Q_fusion = 1.54 (3.15 MJ output / 2.05 MJ laser drive)
realized_examples: Abu-Shawareb et al. (NIF) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 065102: 8 December 2022 shot achieved Q > 1 inertial-confinement fusion ignition. Target gain Q = E_fusion / E_laser = 1.54; 3.15 MJ fusion yield from 2.05 MJ laser drive.
First scientific breakeven (Q > 1) ever achieved in laboratory inertial-confinement fusion. Confirmed Lawson hot-spot ignition criterion via direct experimental realization.
Subsequent NIF shots have reproduced and exceeded the 2022 ignition result.

all_cells.content: JET D-T plasma December 2021 — 59 MJ fusion energy in 5-second pulse, Q ~ 0.33
realized_examples: JET 2021 DT campaign: 59 MJ fusion energy released over 5-second pulse — > 2× the 1997 JET DT energy record.
Q ~ 0.33 in D-T fusion — still substantially below breakeven, but validates ITER's D-T scaling predictions.
Continues into JET 2023 final campaign before decommissioning.

all_cells.content: MMS spacecraft direct measurement of magnetic-reconnection diffusion region in Earth's magnetotail (2017+)
realized_examples: Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission, NASA: 4 spacecraft in tetrahedral formation with high time resolution (30 ms) plasma + B-field measurements. First direct in-situ observation of electron-scale diffusion region in magnetic reconnection (Burch et al. 2016, Science 352, aaf2939).
Subsequent: high-time-resolution magnetotail reconnection events characterize Hall current systems, electron jet outflows, and ion-scale current sheets.

all_cells.content: Magnetic Reconnection Experiment (MRX, Princeton) — laboratory measurement of fast reconnection rates
realized_examples: Yamada et al. (MRX) 1997+: laboratory device sustaining controlled magnetic-reconnection geometry with diagnostic access. Measures reconnection rates, plasmoid dynamics, particle acceleration spectra.
Confirms transition from Sweet-Parker (slow, ∝ Lundquist^(-1/2)) to Petschek-like fast reconnection in high-Lundquist-number / collisionless regime.

all_cells.content: Solar flare magnetic-reconnection signature — Yohkoh / RHESSI / SDO / Hinode multi-spacecraft confirmations
realized_examples: Yohkoh 1991+ (X-ray): cusp-shaped reconnection-event observations in solar flares.
TRACE / SDO 2010+ (EUV imaging): plasmoid ejections, current-sheet brightening, post-flare loops consistent with reconnection.
RHESSI (hard X-ray): nonthermal electron acceleration at reconnection sites.

all_cells.content: Parker Solar Probe solar-wind kinetic-scale cascade — first measurements of inertial-range turbulence inside 50 R_sun
realized_examples: NASA Parker Solar Probe (2018+, NASA mission): close-approach (down to ~10 R_sun perihelion) solar-wind plasma + B-field measurements.
Confirms inertial-range turbulence Kolmogorov spectrum in near-Sun solar wind; characterizes 'switchback' structures and kinetic-scale plasma physics in young solar wind.
Bandyopadhyay et al. 2020, Astrophys. J. Suppl. 246, 48 (turbulent cascade measurements).

all_cells.content: Jarzynski equality ⟨e^(-βW)⟩ = e^(-βΔF) — confirmed by single-molecule RNA-pulling experiments
realized_examples: Jarzynski 1997, Phys. Rev. Lett. 78, 2690: rigorous nonequilibrium work relation connecting work distributions to free-energy differences.
Liphardt et al. 2002, Science 296, 1832: first single-molecule experimental verification using optical-tweezer RNA unfolding/refolding. Subsequent: Collin et al. 2005, Nature 437, 231 + many.
Generalizations: Crooks fluctuation theorem (Crooks 1999); steady-state fluctuation theorems.

all_cells.content: Crooks fluctuation theorem P_F(W) / P_R(-W) = e^(β(W-ΔF)) — verified in RNA pulling experiments
realized_examples: Crooks 1999, Phys. Rev. E 60, 2721: ratio of forward-protocol work distribution P_F(W) to reverse-protocol P_R(-W) given by exponential of work-minus-free-energy difference.

Collin et al. 2005, Nature 437, 231: experimental verification using optical-tweezer RNA hairpin unfolding/refolding. Plot of ln[P_F(W)/P_R(-W)] vs W gives slope β = 1/k_B T and intercept βΔF — extracts ΔF from far-from-equilibrium work measurements.

all_cells.content: Tokamak H-mode confinement (Wagner 1982) — L-mode to H-mode transition at sufficient heating power
realized_examples: Wagner et al. (ASDEX) 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 1408: discovery of high-confinement (H-mode) regime in tokamak with edge pedestal formation.
Confirmed in nearly all subsequent tokamaks: JET, DIII-D, JT-60, EAST. H-mode is baseline operating mode for ITER design.
Edge-localized-modes (ELMs) — quasi-periodic edge instabilities releasing pedestal energy — characterize H-mode operation. ELM mitigation is active research area.

all_cells.content: ITER projected first plasma 2034+, Q ≥ 10 fusion-power gain target
realized_examples: ITER is under construction at Cadarache, France (international 7-party collaboration). Target: 500 MW fusion from 50 MW heating (Q = 10) in 400-second pulses.
Updated timeline (2023): first plasma ~ 2034-2035 (delayed from earlier 2025 target); DT operation ~ 2039+.
Validates tokamak path to fusion-relevant Q values; will confirm or refine Lawson-criterion + alpha-particle heating predictions in burning plasma.

all_cells.content: Granular jamming transition at packing fraction φ_J ~ 0.64 — confirmed via mechanical and structural measurements
realized_examples: Liu, Nagel 1998, Nature 396, 21 (jamming framework): jamming transition where amorphous granular packing develops finite mechanical rigidity at critical packing fraction.
Confirmed: φ_J ~ 0.64 for monodisperse spheres; signature scaling Z ~ Z_c + (φ - φ_J)^(1/2) for contact number; bulk modulus K ~ (φ - φ_J)^(1/2).
O'Hern et al. 2003, Phys. Rev. E 68, 011306 (jamming transition scaling).

all_cells.content: Vicsek-model collective motion — confirmed by bacterial suspensions, colloidal active particles, in vitro cytoskeletal systems
realized_examples: Vicsek et al. 1995, Phys. Rev. Lett. 75, 1226: minimal model of self-propelled particles aligning with neighbors → flocking transition.

Experimental realizations: Bacillus subtilis suspensions (Wensink et al. 2012); colloidal self-propelled particles (Bricard et al. 2013, Nature 503, 95); microtubule + kinesin in vitro (Sanchez et al. 2012, Nature 491, 431).
Confirmed long-range order in 2D self-propelled systems despite Mermin-Wagner prohibition for equilibrium systems.

all_cells.content: Rayleigh-Bénard convection Nu(Ra) ~ Ra^(1/3) at high Ra — confirmed across many orders of magnitude
realized_examples: Rayleigh 1916: convective instability at Ra > Ra_c ~ 1708.
Modern: at high Ra (~ 10^7-10^16), Nusselt-number scaling Nu ~ Ra^(0.31) measured precisely.
Cryogenic helium experiments + DNS: scaling-law breaks down at Ra > 10^13 transitioning to ultimate Ra^(1/2) regime (Niemela et al. 2000 + theoretical predictions Kraichnan 1962).

all_predictions.description: The theoretical prediction is firm and the classification cell is uncontested. Experimental status has substantial tension: several high-profile claims of zero-bias-peak signatures consistent with Majorana zero modes have been challenged or retracted; the field has not yet converged on a confirmed material realization. Status 'unconfirmed-tension' rather than 'confirmed' or 'falsified' reflects this honestly.

The mass was not predicted by the rep content alone (the Higgs self-coupling that fixes m_H is a free parameter of the SM); the mass-eigenvalue at 125 GeV was unknown until 2012.

all_predictions.description: Antimatter predates the SM. The SM rep content makes antimatter a structural feature (each chirality has a conjugate rep), but the prediction is Dirac's, not the SM's.

all_predictions.prediction: The integer quantum Hall effect's TKNN invariant (first Chern number) is the Ω^{Spin^c}_2 = Z cobordism invariant; this provides a structural derivation rather than a phenomenological one.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Freed-Hopkins 2016 (structural derivation)
all_predictions.description: The IQHE was observed in 1980 (von Klitzing); the TKNN invariant interpretation came in 1982. The cobordism-theoretic re-derivation in Freed-Hopkins is retro-explanatory — it provides a uniform framework but did not predict the IQHE.

all_predictions.prediction: The full classification of interacting fermionic SPT phases for arbitrary tangential structures in arbitrary dimensions reduces to computing cobordism groups Ω^G_{d+1}, a task that the algebraic-topology machinery (Adams spectral sequence, etc.) makes systematically computable.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Freed, Hopkins 2016
all_predictions.description: Methodological prediction. Not unconfirmed in the sense of being challenged, but the prediction is about a research program's reach; many cases remain to be computed even where the framework is accepted as correct.

all_predictions.prediction: Time-reversal-invariant 3+1d fermionic SPT phases form a Z_16 classification when interactions are included, refining the Z_2 free-fermion classification of the ten-fold way's class AII.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Wang, Senthil 2014, Phys. Rev. B 89, 195124; Freed, Hopkins 2016
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Theoretical confirmation via multiple independent calculations; experimental confirmation is partial (the higher-Z_16 phases require specific interaction-induced phases not yet definitively isolated experimentally).

all_predictions.prediction: The free-fermion Z classification of 1d class BDI topological superconductors collapses to Z_8 under interactions; equivalently, eight free-fermion BDI phases that are distinct without interactions become continuously deformable into one trivial phase when interactions are turned on.

all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Theoretical confirmation via multiple independent calculations (Turner-Pollmann-Berg 2011; etc.).

all_predictions.description: Minimal non-supersymmetric SU(5)'s proton-decay prediction is ruled out by Super-Kamiokande's lower bound. Supersymmetric SU(5) raises the GUT scale to ~10^16 GeV and pushes the proton lifetime to ~10^34 - 10^36 years, currently at the edge of experimental sensitivity.

all_predictions.description: Non-SUSY SU(5)'s gauge-coupling unification prediction has tension (the three couplings don't quite meet under SM running). SUSY SU(5) restores unification at a high precision level.

all_predictions.prediction: Magnetic monopoles with mass ~M_GUT, predicted by 't Hooft-Polyakov mechanism applied to SU(5) → SM breaking.

all_predictions.description: GUT monopoles at ~10^16 GeV are far too heavy for current accelerator searches; cosmological searches (MACRO experiment etc.) have set limits but not detected any.

As of 2026 the discrepancy remains unexplained; candidate resolutions include experimental systematics (gravitational gradients during finite-temperature 4He measurement) and a subtle universality-class identification failure (e.g. the experimental system sitting near but not exactly on the O(2) fixed point).

all_predictions.prediction: Wilson-Fisher ε-expansion (1972) interpolation between Ising in d = 2 and Gaussian in d = 4 reproduced by continuous-d bootstrap to high orders in ε
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Wilson, Fisher 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 28, 240
all_predictions.description: The Wilson-Fisher prediction itself predates the modern bootstrap by ≈40 years. The bootstrap's role is structural: it recovers the WF result from first principles (conformal invariance + unitarity + crossing), confirming that the ε-expansion describes a real CFT trajectory in continuous d. Retro-explanatory-only reflects that the bootstrap re-derives rather than first-predicts the WF physics.

all_predictions.description: GNY exponents are bootstrap-pinned to sub-percent precision but no experimental quantum-critical realization has yet achieved comparable precision; the closest target (electronic semimetal-to-insulator transitions in 2D graphene-like systems) is currently far from the critical point. Experimental verification awaits a cleaner critical-point realization.

all_predictions.prediction: Lower bound on the number of massless flavors required for an interacting IR-fixed point in 4D SU(N_c) gauge theory ('lower edge of the conformal window'); bootstrap-aided analysis gives N_f / N_c ≥ approximately 2.5 (for SU(3), N_f ≥ 8 or so)

all_predictions.description: Lattice QCD studies of near-conformal SU(3) with varying flavor count have produced conflicting reports on the lower edge of the conformal window. The bootstrap provides rigorous lower bounds on operator dimensions that constrain the IR CFT if it exists, but the bootstrap does not directly determine whether such a CFT exists for a given (N_c, N_f); inferring the conformal-window edge requires combining bootstrap with lattice or other input.

all_predictions.prediction: The 6D (2,0) theory has no Lagrangian description but its observables are pinned by the bootstrap to values consistent with M-theory amplitudes at large N
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Beem, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 071601
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Chester, Pufu, Yacoby 2018 (large-N AdS/CFT cross-check); Lemos-Liendo-Meneghelli-Mitev 2017 (chiral algebra cross-check via W-algebras)

all_predictions.cell_content: Universal bounds on central charges and Hofman-Maldacena ratios (a/c in d=4; t_4, t_2 conformal-collider observables in any d≥3); a/c bounded between 1/3 and 31/18 for unitary 4D CFTs (Hofman-Maldacena 2008), with bootstrap narrowing to a/c ≤ 3/2 + corrections (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011); central-charge lower bounds from stress-tensor crossing
all_predictions.prediction: Hofman-Maldacena conformal-collider bounds on the angular distribution of energy in 4D CFTs
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bootstrap-recovered with tighter window in Komargodski-Zhiboedov 2012, JHEP 11, 140 (causality argument); Faller-Sarkar-Verma 2017 (stress-tensor bootstrap)

all_predictions.description: Theoretical prediction (Freedman-Larsen-Wang 2002): the braid group representation on the Fibonacci MTC's topological Hilbert space generates a dense subgroup of the unitary group, hence braiding alone universal. No laboratory realization of Fibonacci anyons confirmed as of 2026; searches continue in fractional Chern insulators and certain FQHE states (notably ν=12/5).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Freedman, Larsen, Wang 2002, Commun. Math. Phys. 227, 605
all_predictions.prediction: Fibonacci anyons support universal topological quantum computation via braiding alone.

all_predictions.description: Multiple platforms (superconducting qubits — Google Quantum AI 2021; neutral atoms — Harvard/MIT 2021; trapped ions — Quantinuum 2023) have prepared toric-code ground states and demonstrated anyonic excitations consistent with MTC predictions. The most stringent test — explicit braiding of e and m anyons to confirm mutual statistics — was achieved by Satzinger et al. (Google Quantum AI 2021) and refined in subsequent work.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Kitaev 2003, Ann. Phys. 303, 2
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Satzinger et al. (Google Quantum AI) 2021, Science 374, 1237; Bluvstein et al. (Harvard) 2022, Nature 604, 451; Iqbal et al. (Quantinuum) 2023, Nature 626, 505
all_predictions.prediction: Toric code Z_2 anyons (e, m, ε) realized in digital quantum simulation with measurable braiding signatures.

all_predictions.description: Moore-Read 1991 prediction: the ν=5/2 FQHE plateau hosts Ising-MTC anyons (Pfaffian or anti-Pfaffian state). Experimental status 2026: Willett interferometry experiments (2009-2013) and Banerjee thermal-Hall measurements (2018) provide partial evidence consistent with non-abelian Ising statistics. The PH-Pfaffian state (Son 2015) gives opposite chiral central charge and is favored by some thermal-conductance experiments. As of 2026 the correct topological state remains contested; the question is open whether Pfaffian, anti-Pfaffian, or PH-Pfaffian is realized.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Read 1991, Nucl. Phys. B 360, 362
all_predictions.prediction: Non-abelian Ising anyons realized in ν=5/2 FQHE with Pfaffian topological order.
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Willett, Pfeiffer, West 2010, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. 107, 8853 (interferometry evidence); Banerjee et al. 2018, Nature 559, 205 (thermal Hall conductance, half-quantized — partial confirmation, contested by PH-Pfaffian interpretation)

all_predictions.description: Banerjee et al. (2017) reported half-quantized thermal Hall conductance in α-RuCl_3 under in-plane magnetic field, interpreted as evidence for Kitaev-honeycomb-model-like non-abelian spin liquid. Subsequent re-measurements (e.g., Czajka et al. 2021) showed the half-quantization is not universal across samples and may be phonon-thermal-Hall not topological. As of 2026 the spin-liquid identification in α-RuCl_3 is contested.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2
all_predictions.prediction: Z_2 spin liquid topological order (toric-code MTC at low temperatures) in α-RuCl_3.
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2017, Nat. Mater. 17, 4 (initial evidence); Czajka et al. 2021, Nat. Phys. 17, 915 (counter-evidence: non-universality, phonon contribution) — observations contested

all_predictions.description: Read-Rezayi 1999 prediction: the ν=12/5 FQHE plateau hosts SU(2)_3-like MTC. Observed plateau (Xia et al. 2004) but no detailed topological characterization. Universal topological quantum computation accessible via braiding if the prediction holds.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Read, Rezayi 1999, Phys. Rev. B 59, 8084
all_predictions.prediction: Read-Rezayi parafermion state at ν=12/5 FQHE.

all_predictions.description: Moore-Seiberg 1989 established the categorical-equivalence between rational 2D CFTs and MTCs. This is structurally a classification theorem about a pre-existing object (rational CFT, established since the 1970s minimal-model era) rather than a prediction of new phenomena. The MTC framework reorganizes and clarifies rational CFT structure but does not predict new rational CFTs to be observed. Retro-explanatory.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177
all_predictions.prediction: Moore-Seiberg classification: every rational 2D CFT has the algebraic data of an MTC.

all_predictions.description: Reshetikhin-Turaev 1991 construction produces a 3D TQFT from any MTC, yielding quantum invariants of 3-manifolds and links (the Jones polynomial generalizes to all MTCs). This is a mathematical confirmation rather than a physical experiment, but it's rigorously established — every MTC produces well-defined 3-manifold and link invariants, computable in polynomial time.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Reshetikhin, Turaev 1991, Invent. Math. 103, 547
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bakalov, Kirillov 2001 (textbook proof)
all_predictions.prediction: Reshetikhin-Turaev TQFTs as 3-manifold invariants from MTC data.
't Hooft anomaly matching for 4D pure SU(N) YM Z_N 1-form symmetry rules out trivially-gapped intermediate phases.
all_predictions.description: Komargodski-Sulejmanpasic-Unsal 2017 used the mixed 't Hooft anomaly between 1-form center symmetry and theta-angle shifts to rule out trivially-gapped phases at theta=pi in 4D YM. Confirmed via lattice gauge theory studies (Kitano-Suyama-Yamazaki 2020 and later refinements) and analytical arguments.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Komargodski, Sulejmanpasic, Unsal 2017, Phys. Rev. B 97, 054418
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Kitano, Suyama, Yamazaki 2020, JHEP 09, 197 (lattice confirmation of theta=pi structure)

all_predictions.prediction: Non-invertible discrete symmetry replaces ABJ-anomalous U(1)_A in massless QED.
all_predictions.description: Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022 and Cordova-Ohmori 2022 established that the would-be U(1)_A symmetry of massless QED, lost to the ABJ anomaly under ordinary symmetry analysis, can be reorganized as a non-invertible Z-valued (or rational) symmetry after gauging instanton number. This is a 'recast' rather than a new physical prediction — the ABJ anomaly itself is decades-old confirmed physics — but the structural reinterpretation has been independently verified by multiple groups and is now standard.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 161601
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Cordova, Ohmori 2022, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011034 (independent verification with refined framework)

all_predictions.prediction: 6D (2,0) theory's 2-form center symmetry has explicit cobordism-classified anomaly.
all_predictions.description: Cordova-Lam 2020 derived the 2-form anomaly of 6D N=(2,0) theory via cobordism, showing the anomaly is fully captured by a 7D invertible TFT — the canonical SymTFT example for a non-Lagrangian QFT. Subsequently extended by Apruzzi et al. (2023) to derive the 6D (2,0) SymTFT directly from string theory M-theory geometry.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Cordova, Lam 2020, arXiv:2003.04579
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Apruzzi, Bonetti, Garcia, Saxena, Heckman, Schafer-Nameki 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895

all_predictions.prediction: Verlinde topological defect lines in 2D rational CFT have categorical symmetry structure.
all_predictions.description: Petkova-Zuber 2001 introduced the Verlinde lines; Frohlich-Fuchs-Runkel-Schweigert 2007 established their full categorical structure. Lattice realization (Aasen-Mong-Fendley 2016) confirms the categorical-symmetry action experimentally accessible in 2D classical-statistical lattice models.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Petkova, Zuber 2001, Phys. Lett. B 504, 157
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aasen, Mong, Fendley 2016, J. Phys. A 49, 354001 (lattice realization)

all_predictions.prediction: Categorical / non-invertible symmetries exist in real (3+1)D quantum materials.
all_predictions.description: The SymTFT framework predicts that categorical / non-invertible symmetry structures should be detectable in (2+1)D and (3+1)D quantum materials with appropriate topological order. As of 2026, no laboratory realization has cleanly identified a non-invertible symmetry beyond the (well-established) toric-code and Kitaev-honeycomb realizations of MTC-style data. Active experimental search direction.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bhardwaj et al. 2023, Nucl. Phys. B 990, 116167 (review predicting material realizations)

all_predictions.prediction: Higher-form symmetry breaking patterns predict new universality classes for (2+1)D quantum phase transitions.
all_predictions.description: Lake-Hermele 2018 and subsequent work predict that 1-form symmetry breaking in (2+1)D leads to universality classes distinct from ordinary Landau-Ginzburg patterns. The Higgs / confinement transition of (2+1)D Z_2 gauge theory is the simplest case. Experimental confirmation in quantum spin liquid candidates is ongoing.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Lake, Hermele 2018, Phys. Rev. B 98, 134418

all_predictions.prediction: Standard Model has hidden non-invertible symmetries from anomalous discrete subgroups.
all_predictions.description: Several authors (Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022; subsequent extensions) have argued that the SM may carry non-invertible symmetry structure from anomalous discrete subgroups of its continuous chiral symmetries. No phenomenological consequence has been derived that is testable beyond existing SM physics; this remains a structural prediction.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 161601 (closing section on SM application)

all_predictions.prediction: Schur sector of any 4D N=2 SCFT is a 2D vertex operator algebra (SCFT/VOA correspondence).
all_predictions.description: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli 2015 established that every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra (VOA) capturing its Schur sector. This is a categorical equivalence at the level of the Schur sector. Extensively confirmed for specific theory families (N=4 SYM, class-S, Argyres-Douglas) by independent computations of the VOAs.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Lemos 2024, arXiv:2401.xxx (review of 4D-2D dictionary, summarizing extensive subsequent computations)

all_predictions.prediction: Argyres-Douglas theories arise at singular points of Coulomb branches where mutually non-local BPS states become simultaneously massless.
all_predictions.description: Argyres-Douglas 1995 predicted and constructed the H_n series of non-Lagrangian SCFTs as IR theories at specific Coulomb-branch singular points. Subsequent extensions (Argyres-Plesser-Seiberg-Witten 1996; Argyres-Lotito-Lu-Martone 2018) completed the rank-1 and rank-2 classifications. Confirmed via independent calculations of operator dimensions matching the predicted fractional values.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Douglas 1995, Nucl. Phys. B 448, 93
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lu, Martone 2018, JHEP 02, 002 (complete rank-1 classification)

all_predictions.prediction: Higgs branches of rank-1 Minahan-Nemeschansky theories are minimal nilpotent orbits of e_{6,7,8}.
all_predictions.description: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996, 1997 predicted that the Higgs branches of the rank-1 E_{6,7,8} SCFTs are exactly the minimal nilpotent orbits of the exceptional Lie algebras, of complex dimensions 22, 34, 58 respectively. Confirmed by direct moment-map computation and by the matching of flavor-current OPE coefficients to the orbit-geometry data.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, Nucl. Phys. B 482, 142; 1997, Nucl. Phys. B 489, 24
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lu, Martone 2018, JHEP 02, 002 (independent rank-1 classification confirming the M-N predictions); Beem, Rastelli 2018, JHEP 01, 026 (chiral algebra confirmation of E_{6,7,8} Higgs branches)

all_predictions.prediction: 3D N=4 mirror symmetry exchanges Higgs and Coulomb branches.
all_predictions.description: Intriligator-Seiberg 1996 predicted that 3D N=4 SCFTs (obtained by reducing 4D N=2 SCFTs on a circle) come in mirror pairs where the Higgs and Coulomb branches swap. Confirmed extensively for quiver gauge theories (Hanany-Witten brane realization), abelian gauge theories, and class-S reductions. Provides quantitative checks at the level of moment-map and Coulomb-branch formula matching.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Intriligator, Seiberg 1996, Phys. Lett. B 387, 513
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bullimore, Dimofte, Gaiotto 2017, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 21, 1003 (rigorous Coulomb-branch formula)

all_predictions.prediction: Coulomb branches of 3D N=4 theories are quantized symplectic singularities with explicit operator algebras.
all_predictions.description: Bullimore-Dimofte-Gaiotto 2017 provided the rigorous Coulomb-branch formula for 3D N=4 gauge theories, expressing the Coulomb branch as a quantized symplectic singularity with explicit generators and relations. Confirmed by independent mathematical-physics computations (Braverman-Finkelberg-Nakajima) and by matching to Hilbert series of known examples.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bullimore, Dimofte, Gaiotto 2017, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 21, 1003
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Braverman, Finkelberg, Nakajima 2019, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 23, 75 (mathematical confirmation)

all_predictions.prediction: Complete classification of rank-1 4D N=2 SCFTs.
all_predictions.description: Argyres-Lotito-Lu-Martone 2018 completed the classification of all rank-1 4D N=2 SCFTs by systematic Coulomb-branch geometry analysis. The result: 16 distinct theories (Lagrangian SU(2) Nf series, AD H_n series, Minahan-Nemeschansky E_{6,7,8}, plus several others). Confirmed by independent checks via Higgs-branch and SCFT/VOA computations.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lu, Martone 2018, JHEP 02, 002
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Caorsi, Cecotti 2018, JHEP 11, 052 (independent classification of rank-1 N=2 SCFTs via different methods); Argyres, Martone 2020, JHEP 06, 044 (rank-2 extension corroborating rank-1 completeness)

all_predictions.prediction: Higher-rank class-S theories admit complete Higgs-branch classifications via affine Grassmannian / nilpotent orbit data.
all_predictions.description: Predicted that class-S theories of type ADE at arbitrary rank have Higgs branches classifiable via affine Grassmannian slices and nilpotent orbits (Moore-Tachikawa 2012; Cremonesi-Hanany-Mekareeya-Zaffaroni 2014). For low rank this is established; for general higher rank the classification is partial and active research.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Tachikawa 2012, Surveys in Differential Geometry 18, 129

all_predictions.prediction: Ω- baryon (sss, J^P=3/2+) predicted at mass ≈ 1685 MeV by Gell-Mann's SU(3) eightfold-way classification.
all_predictions.description: Gell-Mann 1962 predicted the Ω- baryon as the missing member of the baryon decuplet, with specific mass ≈ 1685 MeV and quantum numbers I(J^P)=0(3/2+). Discovered at Brookhaven in 1964 at mass 1672 MeV — the cleanest Mendeleev-style hadronic prediction confirmation. The 1% mass-prediction accuracy established SU(3) flavor symmetry as a real organizing principle of hadrons.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Gell-Mann 1962, Phys. Rev. 125, 1067
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Barnes et al. 1964, Phys. Rev. Lett. 12, 204

all_predictions.prediction: Charmonium spectrum (J/ψ, ψ(2S), χ_c states) at GeV-scale masses predicted by GIM mechanism + quark-model arguments.
all_predictions.description: Glashow-Iliopoulos-Maiani 1970 introduced the charm quark to suppress flavor-changing neutral currents; subsequent quark-model calculations predicted a 1-- c-cbar state at ~3 GeV. J/ψ discovery in November 1974 confirmed this and triggered the 'November Revolution'. The full charmonium spectrum was subsequently mapped out, matching Cornell-potential predictions.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Glashow, Iliopoulos, Maiani 1970, Phys. Rev. D 2, 1285 (GIM mechanism predicting charm)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aubert et al. 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 33, 1404 (J/psi discovery)

all_predictions.prediction: Pentaquark states with hidden-charm quark content at ~4.3-4.5 GeV.
all_predictions.description: Various pentaquark predictions emerged from 2003 onwards; the diquark model (Maiani-Polosa-Riquer 2015) predicted hidden-charm pentaquark states in the 4.3-4.5 GeV range. LHCb discovery in 2015 (P_c(4380), P_c(4450)) and refined spectrum in 2019 (P_c(4312), P_c(4440), P_c(4457)) confirmed the hidden-charm pentaquark sector after decades of failed searches for light pentaquarks (notably the false Θ+ claim of 2003).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Maiani, Polosa, Riquer 2015, Phys. Lett. B 749, 289
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aaij et al. (LHCb) 2015, Phys. Rev. Lett. 115, 072001

all_predictions.prediction: Doubly-charm tetraquark T_cc+ at mass ≈ 3875 MeV, stable or near-stable.
all_predictions.description: Karliner-Rosner 2017 and Eichten-Quigg 2017 (independent calculations) predicted the doubly-charm tetraquark T_cc+ (cc-ūd̄) just below the DD* threshold with J^P=1+. LHCb 2021/2022 confirmed T_cc+(3875) with mass and width matching predictions, establishing the doubly-heavy-tetraquark family.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Karliner, Rosner 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 202001
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aaij et al. (LHCb) 2022, Nat. Commun. 13, 3351

all_predictions.prediction: Lowest scalar glueball at mass ≈ 1500-1700 MeV with J^P^C=0++.
all_predictions.description: Lattice QCD predicts a scalar glueball at this mass range (Morningstar-Peardon 1999 quenched-lattice; more recent unquenched lattice studies). Candidates f_0(1500) and f_0(1710) have significant glueball-q-qbar mixing; no consensus has emerged on which is dominantly the glueball, and some authors argue the glueball is split across both. Active experimental search (GlueX, LHCb) and theoretical refinement (unquenched lattice) ongoing. Tension: prediction is sharp; identification is disputed.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Morningstar, Peardon 1999, Phys. Rev. D 60, 034509
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Klempt, Zaitsev 2007, Phys. Rep. 454, 1 (review of disputed identification)

all_predictions.prediction: Doubly-bottom tetraquark T_bb (bb-ūd̄, J^P=1+) at mass ≈ 10.4 GeV, stable against strong decay (decays only weakly).
all_predictions.description: Karliner-Rosner 2017 and Eichten-Quigg 2017 predicted T_bb to lie below the BB* threshold and thus be STABLE against strong decay. Would decay only weakly with macroscopic lifetime — observation would be one of the cleanest exotic-hadron confirmations possible. No experimental detection as of 2026 despite searches at LHCb and Belle II. Mass and structure predictions are sharp; null result thus far is significant but not yet decisive.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Karliner, Rosner 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 202001

all_predictions.prediction: H-dibaryon (uuddss, J^P=0+) bound state with mass below 2 m(Λ).
all_predictions.description: Jaffe 1977 predicted an H-dibaryon stable against strong decay with mass below 2 m(Λ) ≈ 2230 MeV. Extensively searched in nuclear-physics experiments through the 1980s-2000s; no clear signal. Modern lattice-QCD calculations (with realistic quark masses) find the H-dibaryon is unbound or only weakly bound above 2 m(Λ). Status: falsified for the original prediction (deeply bound H below threshold); refined predictions of a near-threshold state remain open.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Jaffe 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 38, 195
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Inoue et al. (HAL QCD) 2012, Nucl. Phys. A 881, 28 (lattice study finding H near or above threshold)

all_predictions.prediction: Light hybrid meson with exotic J^P^C=1-+ at mass ≈ 1.8-2.0 GeV.
all_predictions.description: Lattice QCD predicts light 1-+ hybrid mesons at this mass. Candidates π_1(1400), π_1(1600), and most recently η_1(1855) (BESIII 2022) have been identified but hybrid interpretation remains contested. The exotic quantum numbers 1-+ rule out pure q-qbar interpretation; whether the candidates are hybrids, tetraquarks, or molecular states remains debated. Active analysis at GlueX (JLab) and BESIII.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Lacock, Michael, Boyle, Rowland (UKQCD) 1996, Phys. Lett. B 401, 308
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Ablikim et al. (BESIII) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 129, 192002 (eta1(1855) discovery — interpretation contested)

all_predictions.prediction: Doubly-bottom baryon Ξ_bb at mass ≈ 10 GeV.
all_predictions.description: Quark-model and lattice-QCD calculations predict doubly-bottom baryons at mass ~10 GeV (Ξ_bb at ~10.2 GeV, Ω_bb at ~10.3 GeV). Ξ_cc++ has been confirmed (LHCb 2017); the bb analogs remain unobserved despite searches. Production cross-section at LHC is small but within reach of HL-LHC luminosities. Expected to be observed within the next decade.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Karliner, Rosner 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 094007

all_predictions.prediction: Quasiparticle fractional charge e/m at Laughlin ν=1/m states.
all_predictions.description: Laughlin 1983 predicted that quasiparticles at ν=1/m carry fractional charge e/m. Confirmed by shot-noise measurements at ν=1/3 (Saminadayar et al. 1997, e/3) and ν=1/5 (Reznikov et al. 1999, e/5). One of the most direct confirmations of FQH theory.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Laughlin 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1395
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Saminadayar, Glattli, Jin, Etienne 1997, Phys. Rev. Lett. 79, 2526

all_predictions.prediction: Composite-fermion construction explains the Jain sequence ν=p/(2p±1).
all_predictions.description: Jain 1989 predicted that flux attachment maps FQH at ν=p/(2p+1) to integer QH of composite fermions at filling p. Confirmed by observation of the full series ν=2/5, 3/7, 4/9, 5/11, ... and by detection of composite fermions via Shubnikov-de Haas oscillations near ν=1/2 (Du, Stormer 1993; Kang, Stormer 1993).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Jain 1989, Phys. Rev. Lett. 63, 199
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Kang, Stormer, Pfeiffer, Baldwin, West 1993, Phys. Rev. Lett. 71, 3850 (composite-fermion direct detection)

all_predictions.prediction: Anyonic exchange statistics directly measurable via interferometry.
all_predictions.description: Predicted that braiding quasiparticles around each other yields fractional exchange phases (Laughlin 1983, theoretical). Direct interferometric measurement: Bartolomei et al. 2020 (Nature) at ν=1/3 measured fractional statistics phase 2π/3; Nakamura et al. 2020 (Nature Physics) independently confirmed. Established that FQH anyons are real, not just an effective description.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Laughlin 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1395
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bartolomei et al. 2020, Science 368, 173; Nakamura et al. 2020, Nature Physics 16, 931

all_predictions.prediction: Non-abelian Ising anyons exist at ν=5/2.
all_predictions.description: Moore-Read 1991 predicted Ising-type non-abelian anyons at ν=5/2 (the Pfaffian state). The 5/2 plateau is firmly observed; whether its topological order is Moore-Read Pfaffian, anti-Pfaffian, or PH-Pfaffian remains contested. Thermal Hall measurement (Banerjee 2018) gave κ_xy ≈ 2.5(πk_B²T/6ℏ) — consistent with PH-Pfaffian — but interpretation is disputed (Simon 2020; Yan 2022). Status: tension.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Moore, Read 1991, Nucl. Phys. B 360, 362
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2018, Nature 559, 205 (thermal Hall consistent with PH-Pfaffian; interpretation disputed)

all_predictions.prediction: Read-Rezayi Z_3 parafermion state with Fibonacci anyons at ν=12/5.
all_predictions.description: Read-Rezayi 1999 predicted the ν=12/5 state to be a Z_3 parafermion phase supporting Fibonacci anyons (which enable universal TQC). The 12/5 plateau is observed (Xia et al. 2004); numerical exact-diagonalization studies favor RR identification (Bonderson-Slingerland 2008); experimental confirmation via Fibonacci-anyon-specific signatures has NOT been achieved. Status: tension — strong theoretical support, no decisive experiment yet.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Read, Rezayi 1999, Phys. Rev. B 59, 8084
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bonderson, Slingerland 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 125323 (numerical RR identification favored, not decisive)

all_predictions.prediction: Universal topological quantum computation by braiding Fibonacci anyons alone.
all_predictions.description: Predicted that Fibonacci anyons (if realized) support universal TQC by braiding alone (Nayak et al. 2008 review). The prediction is mathematically rigorous; experimental realization requires (i) detecting Fibonacci anyons in any FQH platform — not yet achieved; (ii) demonstrating braiding-based gate operations. Most-likely platform: ν=12/5 Read-Rezayi if confirmed. Status: not-yet-tested — both prerequisites pending.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Nayak, Simon, Stern, Freedman, Das Sarma 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1083

all_predictions.prediction: Thermal Hall conductance quantization κ_xy = (c/2)(πk_B²T/6ℏ) where c is central charge of edge theory.
all_predictions.description: Kane-Fisher 1997 predicted quantized thermal Hall conductance proportional to edge central charge. Confirmed at integer plateaus by Jezouin et al. 2013; at FQH plateaus including 1/3 by subsequent experiments (Banerjee 2017, 2018). At 5/2 the measured value tests which non-abelian candidate is realized.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Kane, Fisher 1997, Phys. Rev. B 55, 15832
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2017, Nature 545, 75 (thermal Hall at integer and 1/3)

all_predictions.prediction: Particle-hole symmetry constrains the 5/2 topological order — specifically forbidding pure Pfaffian or pure anti-Pfaffian if PH symmetry is exact.
all_predictions.description: Son 2015 predicted that exact particle-hole symmetry within the LLL forbids the original Pfaffian and anti-Pfaffian, selecting PH-Pfaffian as the unique PH-symmetric candidate at 5/2. Experimental tests: thermal Hall favored PH-Pfaffian initially (Banerjee 2018), but interpretation disputed. Open question whether real samples have exact PH symmetry — Landau-level mixing breaks it weakly.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Son 2015, Phys. Rev. X 5, 031027
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Banerjee et al. 2018, Nature 559, 205 (interpretation tension)

all_predictions.prediction: Maximum neutron-star mass M_TOV ≳ 2.0 M_sun (lower bound from observation of massive pulsars)
all_predictions.description: TOV-equation integration with any reasonable hadronic EOS predicts a maximum NS mass; the empirical lower bound from PSR J0740+6620 (2.08 M_sun, Cromartie et al. 2020; Fonseca et al. 2021) and PSR J0348+0432 (2.01 M_sun, Antoniadis et al. 2013) confirms M_TOV ≥ 2.0 M_sun, ruling out the softest hadronic EOS that fail to support such masses.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV equation)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Cromartie et al. 2020, Nature Astron. 4, 72 (PSR J0740+6620 mass)

all_predictions.prediction: Black-hole no-hair theorem: stationary axisymmetric BHs are described by the Kerr-Newman family with parameters (M, J, Q) only
all_predictions.description: Israel-Carter-Hawking-Robinson uniqueness theorems predict that astrophysical BHs (Q ≈ 0 in practice) are fully described by (M, J). EHT shadow imaging of Sgr A* and M87* is consistent with Kerr-metric predictions at current angular-resolution precision (~10% on shadow diameter). BBH-ringdown spectroscopy (Isi et al. 2019, GW150914 first-overtone analysis; updated in O3+O4 data) probes consistency at the percent level; results are consistent with Kerr. Status `confirmed` AT CURRENT PRECISION; future-precision tests (LISA, next-generation EHT) will probe nearby modifications at the per-mille level. Caveats not falsifying the prediction but limiting its scope: the theorem assumes vacuum (matter near horizon could in principle add hair); scalar-tensor and dark-environmental modifications cannot yet be excluded at next-precision levels.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Israel 1967, Phys. Rev. 164, 1776; Carter 1971, Phys. Rev. Lett. 26, 331 (uniqueness theorems)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102 (BBH-ringdown no-hair test); EHT Collaboration 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 930, L12 (Sgr A* shadow consistent with Kerr)

all_predictions.prediction: Sgr A* shadow diameter ≈ 51 ± 2 μas (Kerr-metric prediction given M and D)
all_predictions.description: Given the GRAVITY-Collaboration mass M ≈ 4.297 × 10^6 M_sun and distance D ≈ 8.277 kpc, the Kerr-metric shadow has predicted angular diameter ≈ 51 μas. EHT Collaboration 2022 measured shadow diameter 51.8 ± 2.3 μas, in excellent agreement.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bardeen 1973 (Kerr shadow formulation); Falcke, Melia, Agol 2000, Astrophys. J. Lett. 528, L13
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: EHT Collaboration 2022, Astrophys. J. Lett. 930, L12 (Sgr A* shadow)

all_predictions.prediction: Binary neutron-star mergers produce kilonovae and r-process nucleosynthesis
all_predictions.description: Lattimer-Schramm 1974 and Eichler-Livio-Piran-Schramm 1989 predicted that NS-NS mergers eject neutron-rich material that undergoes r-process nucleosynthesis and powers a kilonova transient. GW170817 + multi-messenger EM follow-up confirmed: kilonova spectrum (AT 2017gfo), r-process line identifications (strontium, Watson et al. 2019), and short-GRB association (GRB 170817A).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Lattimer, Schramm 1974, Astrophys. J. 192, L145; Eichler, Livio, Piran, Schramm 1989, Nature 340, 126
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12 (GW170817 multi-messenger); Watson et al. 2019, Nature 574, 497 (strontium r-process)

all_predictions.prediction: Magnetars produce extragalactic Fast Radio Bursts
all_predictions.description: Magnetar-flare models for FRBs (Popov-Postnov 2013; Lyubarsky 2014) predicted that magnetars can produce coherent radio bursts of the FRB type. SGR J1935+2154 produced FRB 200428, the first galactic FRB, with luminosity bridging the SGR-burst and extragalactic-FRB populations — confirming a magnetar source for at least a subpopulation of FRBs.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Popov, Postnov 2013, arXiv:1307.4924; Lyubarsky 2014, MNRAS 442, L9
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: CHIME/FRB Collaboration 2020, Nature 587, 54 (FRB 200428); Bochenek et al. 2020, Nature 587, 59

all_predictions.prediction: Hawking radiation from astrophysical black holes
all_predictions.description: Hawking 1974 predicted that BHs emit thermal radiation at T_H = ℏc³/(8πGMk_B). For stellar-mass BHs T_H ~ 10^-8 K — far below the CMB temperature, making the emission unobservable against the CMB background. For Sgr A* (M ≈ 4×10^6 M_sun) T_H ~ 10^-14 K. Astrophysical Hawking radiation is retro-explanatory: it features in the theoretical framework but no direct measurement is feasible with current or near-future technology. (Possible exception: PBH evaporation in the asteroid-mass window may produce gamma-ray emission detectable by GECCO/AMEGO-X — that would move PBH-related Hawking radiation toward confirmable, but no detection as of 2026.)
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hawking 1974, Nature 248, 30; 1975, Commun. Math. Phys. 43, 199

all_predictions.prediction: Primordial black holes constitute 100% of dark matter in the LIGO mass window (1-100 M_sun)
all_predictions.description: Bird et al. 2016 proposed that the GW150914 BBH could be the first signal of a PBH-DM population in the LIGO mass range. Subsequent analyses combining microlensing (OGLE, EROS), GW merger-rate statistics, CMB-spectral-distortion constraints from accretion, and dwarf-galaxy dynamical-friction limits exclude PBHs as 100% DM in 1-100 M_sun at f_PBH ≲ 10^-3 at peak sensitivity. PBHs may still contribute as a small subdominant component of the BBH merger population.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bird, Cholis, Muñoz, Ali-Haïmoud, Kamionkowski, Kovetz, Raccanelli, Riess 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 201301
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Brandt 2016, Astrophys. J. Lett. 824, L31 (Eridanus II); Hütsi et al. 2021, JCAP 03, 068 (BBH-rate constraints); Carr, Kühnel 2020 review (combined)

all_predictions.prediction: Intermediate-mass black hole population in 10^2-10^5 M_sun range
all_predictions.description: Multiple formation channels predict IMBHs: Pop-III stellar collapse, runaway stellar collisions in dense clusters, direct-collapse seeds for SMBHs. As of 2024-26 the IMBH population is sparsely sampled: GW190521 final BH (~142 M_sun) is a confirmed individual at the lower end; ULX candidates (HLX-1, M82 X-1) remain disputed. Confirmation of a continuous IMBH population, with empirical mass function, awaits LISA observations (2030s+) of IMBH inspirals and improved EHT/ULX surveys.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Madau, Rees 2001, Astrophys. J. Lett. 551, L27 (Pop-III seeds); Portegies Zwart, McMillan 2002, Astrophys. J. 576, 899 (runaway-collision IMBH formation)

all_predictions.prediction: Quark-star / strange-quark-matter compact-object population at NS masses
all_predictions.description: If Bodmer-Witten SQM-stability conjecture holds, strange quark stars (or hybrid hadron-quark stars with a strange quark matter core) should exist as a parallel or replacement compact-object population. No confirmed individual identification. Candidates have been proposed (4U 1820-30, RX J1856.5-3754) based on inferred small radii but remain consistent with stiff-hadronic-EOS interpretations. NICER + GW170817 data are starting to discriminate among hadron-only, hybrid, and quark-only EOS classes but do not yet require a quark-matter component.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bodmer 1971, Phys. Rev. D 4, 1601; Witten 1984, Phys. Rev. D 30, 272; Alcock, Farhi, Olinto 1986, Astrophys. J. 310, 261

all_predictions.prediction: Black-hole-merger ringdown frequencies match Kerr quasi-normal-mode spectrum
all_predictions.description: Chandrasekhar-Detweiler 1975 derived the quasi-normal-mode spectrum of Kerr BHs. BBH-merger ringdowns observed by LIGO-Virgo (starting with GW150914) match the predicted (l, m, n) mode frequencies for a Kerr final BH of inferred (M_f, χ_f). Recent overtone-resolved analyses (Isi et al. 2019; Capano et al. 2023) extract the n=1 overtone in addition to the dominant n=0 mode, providing two-mode no-hair tests consistent with Kerr at the few-percent level.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Chandrasekhar, Detweiler 1975, Proc. R. Soc. Lond. A 344, 441 (Kerr QNM spectrum)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: LIGO/Virgo Collaboration 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 221101 (GW150914 ringdown); Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102 (overtone-resolved no-hair)

all_predictions.prediction: 2D class AII TI helical edge states carry quantized longitudinal conductance σ_xx = 2e²/h
all_predictions.description: Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang 2006 predicted the QSH phase in HgTe quantum wells above critical thickness. König et al. 2007 measured σ_xx = 2e²/h plateau, confirming quantization. Subsequent confirmations in 1T'-WTe₂, bismuthene.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bernevig, Hughes, Zhang 2006, Science 314, 1757
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: König et al. 2007, Science 318, 766; Fei et al. 2017, Nature Phys. 13, 677 (1T'-WTe₂)

all_predictions.prediction: 3D class AII TIs host a single Dirac cone on the (111) (or analogous) surface
all_predictions.description: Zhang et al. 2009 predicted Bi₂Se₃ as a single-Dirac-cone 3D TI. Xia et al. 2009 and Hsieh et al. 2009 confirmed via ARPES, with Hsieh et al. additionally measuring helical spin texture.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Zhang, Liu, Qi, Dai, Fang, Zhang 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 438
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Xia et al. 2009, Nature Phys. 5, 398; Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101

all_predictions.prediction: Higher-order topological insulator (Benalcazar-Bernevig-Hughes 2017) has 1D hinge modes at codimension-2 boundaries
all_predictions.description: Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017 introduced HOTIs with quadrupole topology. Schindler et al. 2018 confirmed in rhombohedral bismuth by STM observation of helical 1D hinge modes between surface facets, consistent with the bulk HOTI invariant.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Benalcazar, Bernevig, Hughes 2017, Science 357, 61
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Schindler et al. 2018, Nature Phys. 14, 918

all_predictions.prediction: Weyl semimetals have bulk Weyl points connected by surface Fermi arcs
all_predictions.description: Wan et al. 2011 first developed the Weyl-semimetal phenomenology theoretically. Huang et al. 2015 and Weng et al. 2015 predicted TaAs as a candidate. Lv et al. 2015 confirmed bulk Weyl points via soft-X-ray ARPES; Xu et al. 2015 observed surface Fermi arcs.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Wan, Turner, Vishwanath, Savrasov 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 205101; Huang et al. 2015, Nature Commun. 6, 7373
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Lv et al. 2015, Phys. Rev. X 5, 031013; Xu et al. 2015, Science 349, 613

all_predictions.prediction: Topological crystalline insulator SnTe has mirror-symmetry-protected surface Dirac cones at L points
all_predictions.description: Fu 2011 proposed TCIs; Hsieh, Lin, Liu, Duan, Bansil, Fu 2012 predicted SnTe as a candidate. Tanaka et al. 2012 confirmed via ARPES; Okada et al. 2013 STM revealed strain-induced gap opening consistent with mirror-symmetry breaking.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Fu 2011, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 106802; Hsieh et al. 2012, Nature Commun. 3, 982
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Tanaka et al. 2012, Nature Phys. 8, 800; Okada et al. 2013, Science 341, 1496

all_predictions.prediction: Majorana zero modes in InSb/Al hybrid nanowires give quantized 2e²/h zero-bias conductance
all_predictions.description: Zhang et al. 2018 Nature 556, 74 reported quantized 2e²/h conductance peaks attributed to MZMs. Subsequent re-analysis of the data showed the apparent quantization was disorder-induced rather than topological; the paper was retracted in 2021 (Nature 591, 354). This is the most prominent retracted topological-matter claim and is recorded here as a falsified specific prediction. Note that MZM-platform searches in InAs/Al, InSb/Pb, and related systems continue with more careful protocols — the falsification applies to this specific 2018 quantization claim, not to MZM platforms as a whole.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Zhang et al. 2018, Nature 556, 74
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Zhang et al. 2021, Nature 591, 354 (retraction); Frolov 2021, Nature 592, 17 (commentary)

all_predictions.prediction: Sr₂RuO₄ has chiral p_x + ip_y triplet pairing, making it an intrinsic 2D topological superconductor with half-quantum vortices
all_predictions.description: Mackenzie-Maeno 2003 reviewed evidence for chiral p-wave pairing — strong before 2019. Pustogow et al. 2019 NMR Knight shift measurements showed the spin susceptibility drops on entering the superconducting state, inconsistent with equal-spin triplet pairing. Subsequent ultrasound (Ghosh et al. 2020) further constrained the pairing symmetry. The chiral p-wave + topological-superconductor scenario is substantially weakened; alternative pairings remain debated. Listed as `tension` because the pairing is still unconventional and might yet be topological under different scenarios, but the specific chiral-p-wave claim has lost most of its support.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Mackenzie, Maeno 2003, Rev. Mod. Phys. 75, 657
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Pustogow et al. 2019, Nature 574, 72 (NMR contradicts); Ghosh et al. 2020, Nature Phys. 17, 199

all_predictions.prediction: Topological magnetoelectric effect: a TI surface in a magnetic field has an image-monopole-like response (axion electrodynamics)
all_predictions.description: Qi, Hughes, Zhang 2008 predicted that an electrically charged source near a TI surface in a magnetic field generates an image-monopole-like field configuration, with the axion angle θ = π fixing the response. As of 2026, indirect signatures (Kerr/Faraday rotation in TI films, magnetic-field-induced gap opening of surface Dirac cones) are confirmed; direct image-monopole-style measurement remains a target. The closely-related axion-insulator response in MnBi₂Te₄ thin films shows partial signatures but is contested as a clean bulk realization.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Qi, Hughes, Zhang 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195424

all_predictions.prediction: Majorana zero modes at vortex cores of FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅ are non-Abelian anyons (Ising MTC) supporting topologically-protected braiding
