all_predictions.description: Wang et al. 2018 reported zero-bias conductance peaks at vortex cores in FeTe₀.₅₅Se₀.₄₅, consistent with MZMs of a 2D class D effective surface TSC. The braiding-protected non-Abelian statistics has not been demonstrated. Subsequent experiments report vortex-to-vortex inconsistency in the zero-bias peak presence; impurity-induced Yu-Shiba-Rusinov bound states remain a viable alternative explanation. Listed as `tension` because the signatures are striking but alternative interpretations are not ruled out.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hosur, Ghaemi, Mong, Vishwanath 2011, Phys. Rev. Lett. 107, 097001 (general theory of MZMs in vortex cores of SC + TI surfaces); Hu et al. 2018 (FeTe/Se prediction)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Wang et al. 2018, Science 362, 333 (vortex zero-bias); Machida et al. 2019, Nature Mater. 18, 811 (vortex-dependence concerns)

all_predictions.prediction: Fractional topological insulator (3D analog of FQHE) exists in some strongly-correlated 3D material with time-reversal symmetry
all_predictions.description: Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang 2010 and subsequent works constructed fractional 3D TIs with fractional axion angle θ = π/k for integer k > 1, protected by time-reversal and global U(1). The phenomenon is theoretically clean — parton constructions confirm consistency, and the topological-magnetoelectric response is fractionalized. No material realization exists. This is the longest-standing open Mendeleev cell of 3D-topological-matter physics.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Maciejko, Qi, Karch, Zhang 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 105, 246809; Swingle, Barkeshli, McGreevy, Senthil 2011, Phys. Rev. B 83, 195139

all_predictions.prediction: Solar neutrinos undergo flavor-changing oscillation (large-mixing-angle MSW solution); θ_12 large
all_predictions.description: Mikheyev-Smirnov-Wolfenstein matter-effect predicted resonant flavor conversion in the solar interior. SNO 2002 directly confirmed the missing solar electron-neutrino flux was converted to ν_μ + ν_τ. KamLAND reactor confirmed the LMA-MSW solution with sin²(θ_12) ≈ 0.31. Final precision-coincidence of solar + reactor measurements.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Wolfenstein 1978, Phys. Rev. D 17, 2369; Mikheyev, Smirnov 1985, Yad. Fiz. 42, 1441
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Ahmad et al. (SNO) 2002, Phys. Rev. Lett. 89, 011301; Eguchi et al. (KamLAND) 2003, Phys. Rev. Lett. 90, 021802

all_predictions.prediction: Atmospheric neutrinos undergo flavor oscillation with Δm² ~ 10⁻³ eV² and near-maximal θ_23
all_predictions.description: Super-Kamiokande 1998 reported zenith-angle dependence of atmospheric ν_μ flux indicating ν_μ → ν_τ oscillation with Δm² ≈ 2 × 10⁻³ eV² and large mixing. MINOS, T2K, NOvA, and IceCube subsequently confirmed and precisely measured |Δm²_31| and sin²(θ_23).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Pontecorvo 1957/1968 (oscillation framework); Wolfenstein 1978 (matter effects)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Fukuda et al. (Super-K) 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 1562; Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004

all_predictions.prediction: Reactor mixing angle θ_13 is non-zero
all_predictions.description: Pre-2012 upper bounds (CHOOZ) on θ_13 were below the level required for resolving leptonic CP violation; some theoretical frameworks (tribimaximal mixing) predicted θ_13 = 0. The 2012 measurements by Daya Bay (and RENO, Double Chooz) established sin²(2θ_13) ≈ 0.085, opening the CP-violation measurement program.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bilenky, Pontecorvo 1978, Phys. Rep. 41, 225 (3-flavor framework); Daya Bay design proposal 2007
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: An et al. (Daya Bay) 2012, Phys. Rev. Lett. 108, 171803

all_predictions.prediction: Cosmological probes can bound the sum of neutrino masses below 1 eV
all_predictions.description: Hu-Eisenstein-Tegmark 1998 framework for neutrino mass effects on structure formation. Planck 2018 + BAO + lensing established Σm_ν < 0.12 eV at 95% CL. ACT + DESI 2024 tightened to ~0.08 eV.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hu, Eisenstein, Tegmark 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 80, 5255
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

all_predictions.prediction: Mass hierarchy is normal (m_1 < m_2 < m_3)
all_predictions.description: Current global fits show NH preferred over IH at ~2-3σ but not at 5σ. The slight tension is between (a) atmospheric data + NOvA which slightly prefer NH and (b) the absolute mass-bound tension between cosmology and the IH lower bound (IH requires Σm_ν ≳ 0.1 eV, which is in mild tension with ACT+DESI 2024). DUNE and JUNO are designed to definitively resolve at >5σ.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: No specific prediction; the preference for NH emerged from global fits over ~2020-2024
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Capozzi, Lisi, Marrone, Palazzo 2021, Prog. Part. Nucl. Phys. 102, 48 (global fits showing NH preference)

all_predictions.prediction: Leptonic CP-violating phase δ_CP ≈ -π/2
all_predictions.description: T2K 2020 best-fit δ_CP ≈ -π/2 with hint of CP violation. NOvA 2022 best-fit closer to 0 in NH (or non-trivial in IH). The two experiments are in slight tension at the ~1.5σ level. DUNE is designed to measure δ_CP at 10° precision over 10 years.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hyper-K and DUNE design documents 2017-2020 (forecasts for CP-violation discovery)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (T2K) 2020, Nature 580, 339; Acero et al. (NOvA) 2022, Phys. Rev. D 106, 032004

all_predictions.prediction: Heidelberg-Moscow claim: 76Ge 0νββ signal at T_1/2 ~ 1.2 × 10^{25} yr, m_ββ ≈ 0.3 eV
all_predictions.description: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001/2006 claimed a 0νββ signal in 76Ge. GERDA Phase II 2020 reached T_1/2 > 1.8 × 10^{26} yr, ruling out the claimed signal by more than an order of magnitude. This is the canonical falsified specific claim in 0νββ history. Distinguished from a 'Majorana hypothesis falsification' — the Majorana hypothesis remains open; only the specific Heidelberg-Moscow claim of positive detection is falsified.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Klapdor-Kleingrothaus et al. 2001, Mod. Phys. Lett. A 16, 2409
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Agostini et al. (GERDA) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 252502 (decisive exclusion)

all_predictions.prediction: LSND νe-appearance is explained by a sterile neutrino with Δm² ~ 1 eV² and sin²(2θ_μe) ~ 0.003 (3+1 model)
all_predictions.description: LSND 2001 reported an excess interpretable as a 3+1 sterile neutrino oscillation. IceCube-DeepCore 2020 search for atmospheric ν_μ disappearance excluded the 3+1 parameter space at high CL. MicroBooNE 2022 (and the broader SBN program) excluded the LSND-implied parameter space for the original 3+1 interpretation. Listed `falsified` for the specific LSND-derived 3+1 interpretation; a different eV-scale sterile parameter space from reactor / Gallium anomalies (cell-nu-sterile-eV-tension) remains in tension rather than falsified.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Aguilar et al. (LSND) 2001, Phys. Rev. D 64, 112007
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aartsen et al. (IceCube) 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141801; Acero et al. (MicroBooNE) 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 241801

all_predictions.prediction: Neutrinos are Majorana particles, with m_ββ ≈ 10-50 meV detectable by next-generation 0νββ experiments
all_predictions.description: If neutrinos are Majorana (favored on grounds of seesaw + leptogenesis), m_ββ should be detectable by LEGEND-1000, nEXO, and other next-gen 0νββ experiments. Current sensitivity (KamLAND-Zen 2024) bounds m_ββ < ~150 meV, an order of magnitude above the favored Majorana-NH prediction range. Listed `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the prediction is well-defined but the experimental sensitivity is not yet there.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980; Avignone, Elliott, Engel 2008 (review of m_ββ expectations)

all_predictions.prediction: Galactic core-collapse supernova produces detectable neutrino burst
all_predictions.description: Core-collapse SN models (Bethe-Wilson 1985 onwards) predict ~10^{53} erg neutrino emission over ~10 s. SN 1987A burst confirmed at Kamiokande-II, IMB, and Baksan; the energy, time profile, and event count match canonical core-collapse models.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bethe, Wilson 1985, Astrophys. J. 295, 14; Burrows, Lattimer 1986
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Hirata et al. 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1490; Bionta et al. 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 1494

all_predictions.prediction: 3D Ising critical exponents are universal across liquid-gas critical points, uniaxial magnets, and binary-fluid mixtures
all_predictions.description: Universality predicted by Kadanoff (1966), Wilson, Fisher (1972) RG theory. Independent precision measurements in CO₂, Xe, H₂O liquid-gas critical points (Sengers reviews), FeF₂ and MnF₂ uniaxial antiferromagnets (Hutchings 1972, Belanger 1987), and binary-fluid mixtures (Beysens 1982) all give consistent exponents ν ≈ 0.630, η ≈ 0.04. Bootstrap-precision values (Kos-Poland-Simmons-Duffin 2014, El-Showk 2014) are consistent with measurements at the few-percent level.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Kadanoff 1966, Physics 2, 263; Wilson, Fisher 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 28, 240
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Sengers, Levelt Sengers 1986, Annu. Rev. Phys. Chem. 37, 189; Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin 2014, JHEP 11, 109

all_predictions.prediction: 3D O(2)/XY ν ≈ 0.6719 from bootstrap (Chester et al. 2020) — matches 4He λ-transition exponent
all_predictions.description: The Chester et al. 2020 bootstrap result ν = 0.6719(7) is in ~5σ tension with the Lipa et al. 2003 satellite-microgravity 4He λ-transition measurement ν = 0.6709(1). This is the most prominent quantitative theory-experiment tension currently in critical phenomena — the '4He λ-puzzle.' Possible resolutions are open: bootstrap systematic underestimate, residual gravitational rounding in the satellite experiment, or higher-order corrections to scaling on either side. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because the underlying universality assignment (4He → 3D O(2)) is confirmed but the bootstrap-precision exponent value is in tension.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 084017
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Lipa, Nissen, Stricker, Swanson, Chui 2003, Phys. Rev. B 68, 174518 (4He λ measurement)

all_predictions.prediction: BKT transition in 2D 4He films has universal superfluid-density jump ρ_s(T_BKT⁻)/(k_B T_BKT) = 2 m² / (π ℏ²)
all_predictions.description: Kosterlitz, Thouless 1973 predicted that the 2D XY model has a topological phase transition driven by vortex unbinding, with a universal jump in superfluid density at the transition. Bishop and Reppy 1978 measured the jump in thin 4He films using torsional oscillators and confirmed the predicted universal value — one of the cleanest universal-jump observations in critical phenomena.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Kosterlitz, Thouless 1973, J. Phys. C 6, 1181
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bishop, Reppy 1978, Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 1727

all_predictions.prediction: 3D O(3)/Heisenberg ν ≈ 0.7117 — matches Ni / EuO / EuS Curie-point measurements
all_predictions.description: Bootstrap result (Chester et al. 2021): 3D O(3) ν = 0.7117(5). Measurements in Ni, EuO, EuS at their Curie points give ν ≈ 0.71 at the few-percent level (limited by measurement precision rather than discrepancy). Consistent within current precision.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Hohenemser, Rosov, Kleinhammes 1989, Hyperfine Interact. 49, 267 (Ni)

all_predictions.prediction: Polymer scaling exponent ν ≈ 0.588 in 3D dilute polymer solutions in good solvent (SAW exponent)
all_predictions.description: de Gennes 1972 mapped the SAW problem onto the n → 0 limit of the O(N) Wilson-Fisher fixed point, predicting ν ≈ 0.588 in 3D. Light-scattering measurements of polystyrene, PMMA, and other polymers in good solvent (Schäfer-Kappeler 1985 and subsequent) confirm at the few-percent level.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: de Gennes 1972, Phys. Lett. A 38, 339
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Schäfer, Kappeler 1985, J. Phys. Paris 46, 1853; Belohorec, Nickel 1997 Monte Carlo

all_predictions.prediction: Tricritical exponents in 3He-4He mixtures are mean-field with logarithmic corrections (d_c = 3 for tricritical)
all_predictions.description: Riedel-Wegner 1972 predicted that 3D is the upper critical dimension for tricritical phase transitions, giving mean-field-like exponents with logarithmic corrections. Goellner-Behringer-Meyer 1973 specific-heat measurements of 3He-4He mixtures at the tricritical point confirm.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Riedel, Wegner 1972, Phys. Rev. Lett. 29, 349
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Goellner, Behringer, Meyer 1973, J. Low Temp. Phys. 13, 113

all_predictions.prediction: Chiral phase transition in massless 2-flavor QCD is in 3D O(4) universality class
all_predictions.description: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984 predicted O(4) universality in the chiral limit of 2-flavor QCD. Real QCD has nonzero u, d quark masses, which break the exact chiral SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R symmetry — the predicted phase transition becomes a smooth crossover at physical quark masses. Lattice QCD at small but nonzero quark masses is consistent with O(4) scaling in the extrapolation, but cannot directly test the prediction at zero quark mass. Listed `retro-explanatory-only` — the prediction is consistent with what data exists but is not falsifiable at physical conditions.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338

all_predictions.prediction: 3D O(5), O(6), and higher-N Wilson-Fisher fixed points exist as theoretical CFTs with calculable exponents — no clean material realization yet
all_predictions.description: Bootstrap (Chester et al. 2022 and earlier) gives precision exponents for any 3D O(N). Some proposed deconfined-quantum-criticality scenarios may realize high-N Wilson-Fisher fixed points but the universality assignments remain contested. No clean experimental realization confirmed.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Chester et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (O(N) bootstrap)

all_predictions.prediction: Surface-extraordinary critical universality class is realizable in materials with enhanced surface ordering
all_predictions.description: Diehl 1986 predicted the 'extraordinary' surface universality where the surface orders more strongly than the bulk near the bulk-critical point. Bootstrap-precision analysis (Padayasi et al. 2022) gives the surface exponents but no clean experimental realization is confirmed. Some quantum-Hall-edge realizations have been proposed.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Diehl 1986, Phase Transitions and Critical Phenomena vol. 10

all_predictions.prediction: Universality holds across all microscopic realizations of a given symmetry / dimension combination
all_predictions.description: The cornerstone of critical phenomena. Predicted by Kadanoff (1966), formalized via RG by Wilson (1971-72), and confirmed empirically across dozens of independent systems sharing the same universality class (3D Ising: liquid-gas + uniaxial magnets + binary fluids; 3D O(3) Heisenberg: Ni + EuO + EuS + RbMnF₃; etc.). This is the structural fact that makes the Periodic-Table-of-Universality entry meaningful.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Kadanoff 1966, Physics 2, 263; Wilson 1971, Phys. Rev. B 4, 3174
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Multiple independent measurements across cells — the universality prediction has been tested by every system-class transition tabulated in this FC

all_predictions.prediction: Canonical WIMP miracle: thermal-relic GeV-TeV particle with weak-scale annihilation cross-section gives Ω_DM ≈ 0.27 naturally and is detectable by direct-detection experiments
all_predictions.description: Lee-Weinberg 1977 established the WIMP-miracle relic-abundance calculation. SUSY-LSP and analogous candidates motivated three decades of WIMP-search programs. LZ 2024 (Aalbers et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002) and XENONnT 2023 placed σ_SI bounds orders of magnitude below typical WIMP-miracle predictions. Canonical SUSY-neutralino-LSP parameter space is essentially excluded; non-canonical heavy-WIMP variants remain viable. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` rather than falsified because (a) heavy-WIMP / non-thermal-WIMP scenarios remain alive, (b) the community uses 'constrained' or 'largely excluded' rather than falsified, (c) the WIMP-miracle as a class prediction is weakened but specific WIMP subclasses survive.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Lee, Weinberg 1977, Phys. Rev. Lett. 39, 165
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aalbers et al. (LZ) 2024, Phys. Rev. Lett. 132, 041002 (most stringent constraint)

all_predictions.prediction: DAMA/LIBRA NaI(Tl) annual modulation is a WIMP signal
all_predictions.description: Bernabei et al. 1996-2024 observe ~12σ annual modulation in NaI(Tl) detector at DAMA/LNGS. Interpreted as WIMP-Earth-velocity modulation with m_WIMP ~ 10 GeV, σ_SI ~ 10⁻⁴⁰ cm². XENON1T (2018), XENONnT (2023), and LZ (2024) all excluded the parameter space orders of magnitude below the DAMA-implied cross-section. ANAIS-112 (2024) and COSINE-100, using NaI(Tl) targets similar to DAMA, have not reproduced the modulation in WIMP-compatible parameter space. The WIMP interpretation is falsified; the modulation itself remains an open puzzle (possible systematics: cosmic-ray seasonal variation, muon contamination, environmental).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bernabei et al. (DAMA/NaI / LIBRA) 1996-present
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aprile et al. (XENON1T) 2018, Phys. Rev. Lett. 121, 111302; Adhikari et al. (ANAIS-112) 2024, arXiv:2404.17348 (NaI-target null result)

all_predictions.prediction: MACHOs (faint baryonic compact objects) constitute the bulk of galactic-halo dark matter
all_predictions.description: Pre-1990s MACHO hypothesis. EROS-2 (Tisserand et al. 2007) and OGLE-IV (Wyrzykowski et al. 2011) microlensing surveys bound f_MACHO < 0.08 of halo DM in the planetary-to-stellar mass range. Combined with BBN bound on baryonic matter, MACHOs cannot be the bulk of DM. Type-A falsified prediction (a specific BSM-or-near-BSM hypothesis ruled out).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Paczynski 1986, Astrophys. J. 304, 1 (MACHO proposal)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Tisserand et al. (EROS-2) 2007, Astron. Astrophys. 469, 387

all_predictions.prediction: QCD axion in DFSZ / KSVZ band with m_a ~ μeV-meV and g_aγγ ~ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹ is the dark matter
all_predictions.description: Theoretical prediction from Peccei-Quinn 1977 + Weinberg 1978 + Wilczek 1978 plus misalignment cosmology gives axion-DM with mass linked to PQ-breaking scale. ADMX-G2 has excluded m_a in 2.66-4.2 μeV at DFSZ sensitivity (Bartram et al. 2021); the full cosmologically-allowed window (m_a from ~ neV to ~ meV) requires years more of haloscope scanning. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the prediction is well-defined and the experiment is in progress.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Preskill, Wise, Wilczek 1983, Phys. Lett. B 120, 127 (misalignment cosmology); Abbott, Sikivie 1983

all_predictions.prediction: Sterile neutrino at m_s ~ 7 keV decays to γ + ν producing a detectable X-ray line in galaxy clusters
all_predictions.description: Bulbul et al. 2014 and Boyarsky et al. 2014 reported a ~3.5 keV unidentified X-ray line in stacked observations of galaxy clusters, interpretable as decaying 7 keV sterile-ν dark matter. Subsequent observations (Dessert-Rodd-Safdi 2020 Science 367, 1465; Foster et al. 2020) significantly weakened the line claim using deeper observations and improved background modeling. The sterile-ν-DM interpretation is not falsified but the specific 3.5 keV line claim is substantially weakened.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Dodelson, Widrow 1994, Phys. Rev. Lett. 72, 17 (sterile-ν DM); Bulbul et al. 2014 (3.5 keV line)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Dessert, Rodd, Safdi 2020, Science 367, 1465 (weakening of 3.5 keV line)

all_predictions.prediction: PBHs in the LIGO mass window (10-100 M_sun) constitute the bulk of dark matter
all_predictions.description: v21 falsified prediction (cell-compact-PBH-LIGO-window-excluded). LIGO-Virgo-KAGRA binary-merger rates incompatible with PBH-as-100%-DM in this mass window — implied PBH merger rates exceed observed rates by orders of magnitude. Hütsi-Raidal-Vaskonen 2021 derived f_PBH ≲ 10⁻³ in LIGO window. Cross-reference: same falsified prediction recorded in compact-astrophysical-objects v21; here it appears as falsified prediction for `excluded-as-major-fraction` cell.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Carr, Kühnel, Sandstad 2016, Phys. Rev. D 94, 083504 (PBH-DM in LIGO window)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Hütsi, Raidal, Vaskonen 2021, Phys. Rev. D 103, 063510

all_predictions.prediction: CDMS-II silicon-detector 2010 3-event excess is a WIMP signal at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV
all_predictions.description: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013 reported 3 candidate events vs ~1 background in silicon detectors, hinting at low-mass WIMP at m_WIMP ~ 8 GeV. SuperCDMS subsequent exclusions (Agnese et al. 2018 Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002) ruled out the parameter space implied by the 3-event hint at high significance. Type-B falsified-specific-claim — low-statistics hint excluded by increased exposure.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Agnese et al. (CDMS-II) 2013, Phys. Rev. Lett. 111, 251301
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Agnese et al. (SuperCDMS) 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 022002

all_predictions.prediction: Fuzzy / ultralight dark matter at m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV explains galaxy-scale small-scale-structure anomalies (core-cusp, missing satellites)
all_predictions.description: Hu-Barkana-Gruzinov 2000 proposed fuzzy DM. Iršič et al. 2017 Lyman-α bounds m_φ ≳ 2 × 10⁻²¹ eV, in tension with the original 10⁻²² eV motivation. Galaxy rotation-curve analyses partially favor m_φ ~ 10⁻²² eV but Lyman-α and Milky Way satellite counts disfavor. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` — different observations point in different directions.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hu, Barkana, Gruzinov 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 85, 1158
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Iršič et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 031302 (Lyman-α tension)

all_predictions.prediction: Self-interacting dark matter with velocity-dependent σ/m solves galactic core-cusp + dwarf-galaxy-diversity problems
all_predictions.description: Spergel-Steinhardt 2000 motivated SIDM. Observational evidence for SIDM-style halo structure is suggestive but not conclusive; specific particle-physics realizations (Yukawa-mediated dark sector) remain to be confirmed. Status `unconfirmed-not-yet-tested` — the framework is internally consistent but requires identifying specific particle realization.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Spergel, Steinhardt 2000, Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 3760

all_predictions.prediction: Asymmetric dark matter explains the Ω_DM / Ω_b ≈ 5 coincidence via shared asymmetry-generation
all_predictions.description: Nussinov 1985 + subsequent work. Connects baryogenesis to DM-genesis. Predicts m_DM ~ 1-15 GeV. Sub-GeV-DM direct-detection programs (SuperCDMS, SENSEI, DAMIC) target the predicted mass range; no signal so far.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Nussinov 1985, Phys. Lett. B 165, 55; Petraki, Volkas 2013 review

all_predictions.prediction: CMB acoustic peak positions and amplitudes precisely match ΛCDM with six free parameters
all_predictions.description: ΛCDM-prediction for CMB acoustic peaks (Hu, Sugiyama, Silk 1995-97 era; Spergel et al. WMAP 2003+) has been spectacularly confirmed by Planck 2018 and ACT DR6. Six-parameter fit matches data over many orders of magnitude in multipole. Foundational ΛCDM-success.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Peebles, Yu 1970, Astrophys. J. 162, 815 (early CMB acoustic prediction); Hu, Sugiyama, Silk 1997, Nature 386, 37
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020, Astron. Astrophys. 641, A6

all_predictions.prediction: BBN-predicted D/H abundance matches quasar-spectrum measurements at η_b = 6 × 10⁻¹⁰
all_predictions.description: BBN framework predicts D/H exponentially sensitive to η_b. Cooke et al. 2018 D/H = (2.527 ± 0.030) × 10⁻⁵ is consistent with η_b inferred from CMB Ω_b h² at sub-percent. Major BBN-CMB cross-epoch ΛCDM-consistency test.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN framework)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Cooke, Pettini, Steidel 2018, Astrophys. J. 855, 102

all_predictions.prediction: Hubble constant is consistent across CMB-inverse-distance-ladder and local-Cepheid-ladder measurements
all_predictions.description: ΛCDM requires consistency. CMB + BAO + Cepheid-ladder + TRGB-ladder + GW-standard-sirens should all give the same H_0 within their uncertainties. Observed: ~5σ disagreement between Planck CMB-inferred 67.4 ± 0.5 and SH0ES local 73.04 ± 1.04. The Hubble tension has grown since 2018 and is now the most prominent ΛCDM-disagreement. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because neither measurement is falsified — both are precise within their methods — but they disagree.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM model itself (no single citation)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Verde, Treu, Riess 2019, Nature Astron. 3, 891 (Hubble tension review)

all_predictions.prediction: Structure-amplitude σ_8 is consistent across CMB-extrapolated and weak-lensing-measured values
all_predictions.description: ΛCDM consistency prediction. Observed: KiDS, DES, HSC consistently give S_8 ~2-3σ below Planck-inferred. Possible resolutions: lensing-systematics (galaxy intrinsic alignments, photo-z errors), baryonic-feedback in matter power, new physics. Persistent across multiple independent surveys.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM growth-of-structure framework
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Asgari et al. (KiDS-1000) 2021, Astron. Astrophys. 645, A104; Dalal et al. (HSC) 2023, Phys. Rev. D 108, 123519

all_predictions.prediction: Primordial ⁷Li/H abundance matches BBN-predicted ~5 × 10⁻¹⁰ in metal-poor halo stars
all_predictions.description: BBN-predicted ⁷Li/H ~ 5 × 10⁻¹⁰ for η_b from CMB + D/H. Spite-plateau observation ⁷Li/H ~ 1.6 × 10⁻¹⁰ — factor 3 lower. Persistent ~25 years. Possible resolutions: stellar Li depletion (turbulent mixing), nuclear-physics revisions, new physics. No consensus resolution.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: BBN framework prediction (Cyburt et al. 2016 review)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Spite, Spite 1982, Astron. Astrophys. 115, 357 + many subsequent

all_predictions.prediction: Dark-energy equation of state is constant: w = -1, w_a = 0 (cosmological constant)
all_predictions.description: ΛCDM assumes w = -1. DESI 2024 + SN-Ia + CMB combined gives ~3-4σ preference for evolving w_0 + w_a ≠ -1, 0. DESI Year-3 / Year-5 will refine. Currently active anomaly.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM (no single citation)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Adame et al. (DESI) 2024, JCAP 04, 026

all_predictions.prediction: Tensor-to-scalar ratio r is non-zero (inflation prediction)
all_predictions.description: Generic inflation predicts r > 0. Current bound r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021) excludes simplest large-field inflation (m²φ² model) but is consistent with smaller-r inflation models (Starobinsky / α-attractors). LiteBIRD targets r ~ 10⁻³ sensitivity.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Generic inflation prediction (Guth 1981; Starobinsky 1980)

all_predictions.prediction: Spatial curvature Ω_K ≈ 0 (inflation prediction)
all_predictions.description: Inflation predicts spatial flatness to high precision. Observed: Ω_K = 0.001 ± 0.002 (Planck 2018 + BAO). Confirmed at sub-percent precision.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Guth 1981, Phys. Rev. D 23, 347 (inflation flatness solution)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020 (|Ω_K| < 0.005)

all_predictions.prediction: EDGES 2018 21cm absorption signal at z ~ 17 is a real cosmic-dawn detection
all_predictions.description: Bowman et al. 2018 reported a ~500 mK 21cm absorption feature, 2-3× deeper than standard cosmology. Stimulated speculative DM-baryon-cooling explanations. SARAS 3 (Singh et al. 2022) independent attempt: no signal at the claimed amplitude. The EDGES-specific-amplitude claim is contested but not formally retracted by the team; community consensus leans toward unconfirmed-as-cosmological. Listed `falsified` based on independent null result, though formal retraction has not occurred. Type-B-like (specific empirical claim with conflicting evidence).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bowman et al. (EDGES) 2018, Nature 555, 67
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Singh et al. (SARAS 3) 2022, Nature Astron. 6, 607

all_predictions.prediction: JWST z > 10 galaxy mass-function is consistent with ΛCDM hierarchical structure formation
all_predictions.description: Labbé et al. 2023 reported unexpectedly mature massive galaxies at z > 7. Subsequent spectroscopic follow-ups have refined some masses downward but the high-z mass-function tension persists. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because observations and theory are both real but disagree, and the resolution (galaxy-formation-efficiency revision, ΛCDM modification, or systematics) is open.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: ΛCDM galaxy-formation theory (no single citation)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Labbé et al. 2023, Nature 616, 266; Boylan-Kolchin 2023, Nature Astron. 7, 731 (tension analysis)

all_predictions.prediction: Stellar-mass binary black holes merge and produce detectable GW signals matching numerical-relativity waveforms
all_predictions.description: GR + numerical-relativity predicted specific BBH-merger waveforms (Pretorius 2005 NR breakthrough; subsequent SXS / GW150914 era). GW150914 detection confirmed (Abbott et al. 2016) — matched-filter correlation > 0.99 against NR templates. ~90 BBH events through GWTC-3 all consistent with NR predictions.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Pretorius 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 121101 (NR breakthrough); GR (Einstein 1916)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102

all_predictions.prediction: Binary neutron-star mergers produce gravitational waves, short gamma-ray bursts, kilonovae, and r-process nucleosynthesis
all_predictions.description: Theoretical predictions: Eichler-Livio-Piran-Schramm 1989 (kilonova / BNS-GRB connection); Lattimer-Schramm 1974 (r-process from NS-merger ejecta); Li-Paczynski 1998 (kilonova model). GW170817 confirmed simultaneously: GW signal; GRB 170817A short GRB; AT2017gfo kilonova UV/optical/IR; r-process abundances inferred from spectroscopy. Multi-prediction event.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Eichler, Livio, Piran, Schramm 1989, Nature 340, 126; Li, Paczynski 1998, Astrophys. J. Lett. 507, L59
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + EM partners) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L12

all_predictions.prediction: Gravitational waves and photons propagate at the same speed
all_predictions.description: GR predicts GW propagation at c. GW170817 + GRB 170817A coincidence at 1.7 s over 40 Mpc gives |Δv/c| < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ — sharpest direct test. Rules out broad class of modified-gravity models predicting non-trivial scalar coupling.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Einstein 1916 (general relativity)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC + INTEGRAL/Fermi-GBM) 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13

all_predictions.prediction: Pulsar timing arrays detect nanohertz stochastic GW background from supermassive-BBH inspirals or alternative cosmological sources
all_predictions.description: Hellings-Downs 1983 framework predicted angular correlation of pulsar timing residuals from a stochastic GW background. NANOGrav 15-year (Agazie et al. 2023) confirmed at 3-4σ with Hellings-Downs correlation signature. Source identification (SMBHB vs cosmological) requires further data.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hellings, Downs 1983, Astrophys. J. Lett. 265, L39
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Agazie et al. (NANOGrav) 2023, Astrophys. J. Lett. 951, L8

all_predictions.prediction: BBH-merger mass distribution shows pair-instability mass gap at 50-130 M_sun (excess at ~35 M_sun, no events at 50-130 M_sun)
all_predictions.description: Stellar-evolution predictions (Heger, Woosley 2002 era; Belczynski et al. 2016) of pair-instability supernovae creating a mass gap. GWTC-3 BBH population (Abbott et al. 2023) shows expected excess at ~35 M_sun (consistent with pulsational-PI pile-up) but GW190521 with primary 85 M_sun is INSIDE the predicted gap — challenging standard stellar-collapse formation. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` because the gap is partially confirmed (mass-distribution does show suppression) but the GW190521 event is in the gap.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Heger, Woosley 2002, Astrophys. J. 567, 532
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2023, Phys. Rev. X 13, 011048 (GWTC-3 population); Abbott et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 in gap)

all_predictions.prediction: Kerr no-hair theorem: BH ringdown spectrum determined entirely by mass + spin (no third hair)
all_predictions.description: Israel 1967, Carter 1971, Hawking 1972 no-hair theorem. Tested via GW-ringdown QNM extraction from loud BBH events. GW150914 + overtone QNM extraction (Isi et al. 2019) consistent with Kerr at current precision (~10%). Future Einstein Telescope / Cosmic Explorer reach percent-level. Listed `confirmed` at current precision; sub-percent tests await future detectors.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Israel 1967, Phys. Rev. 164, 1776; Carter 1971, Phys. Rev. Lett. 26, 331
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Isi, Giesler, Farr, Scheel, Teukolsky 2019, Phys. Rev. Lett. 123, 111102

all_predictions.prediction: Galactic core-collapse supernova produces a detectable GW burst
all_predictions.description: Theoretical predictions (Müller-Janka group 3D-hydrodynamic simulations) of SN GW emission at ~10⁻²¹ strain at 10 kpc. No galactic SN since LIGO operational (1987 predates LIGO; rate ~1-3/century). When galactic SN occurs, GW + neutrino + EM coincident multi-messenger event expected.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Andresen, Müller, Müller, Janka 2017, Mon. Not. R. Astron. Soc. 468, 2032

all_predictions.prediction: LISA detects extreme-mass-ratio inspirals at rate ~10-100/year, testing no-hair theorem at percent-level
all_predictions.description: EMRI rate from galactic-center stellar-mass-BH capture (~10-100/yr detectable by LISA). LISA launch ~2034-2035. Major Mendeleev empty for the next decade.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Babak et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. D 95, 103012

all_predictions.prediction: Continuous-wave GW from Crab pulsar at observable level — bounded below spin-down luminosity
all_predictions.description: Crab spin-down luminosity sets indirect bound on GW emission. LIGO O3 continuous-wave bound is below the spin-down bound by ~30× — meaning the Crab is NOT GW-dominantly spun-down. No detection, but the bound is informative — constrains NS asymmetry to ε < 6 × 10⁻⁶.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Pacini 1968 (NS spin-down GW emission framework)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. (LVC) 2022, Astrophys. J. 935, 1 (O3 CW search)

all_predictions.prediction: Primordial cosmological GW background from inflation detectable in nHz or B-mode searches
all_predictions.description: Inflationary primordial GW with r < 0.036 (BICEP/Keck 2021 upper bound). Cosmological-band stochastic detection awaits next-gen B-mode experiments (LiteBIRD, CMB-S4). NANOGrav nHz signal is consistent with cosmic-string interpretation but more likely astrophysical SMBHB. Cross-reference cosmological-observations primordial-GW cell.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Starobinsky 1979 (inflationary GW)

all_predictions.prediction: Quark-gluon plasma exists as a deconfined phase at T > ~155 MeV
all_predictions.description: Pre-RHIC theoretical work (Collins-Perry 1975; Cabibbo-Parisi 1975) proposed deconfinement at high T. RHIC Au+Au discoveries (2000-2005) plus subsequent LHC Pb+Pb confirmed via hydrodynamic flow, jet quenching, strangeness enhancement, charmonium suppression. Lattice-QCD independently confirms.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Collins, Perry 1975, Phys. Rev. Lett. 34, 1353; Cabibbo, Parisi 1975, Phys. Lett. B 59, 67
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Adams et al. (STAR) 2005, Nucl. Phys. A 757, 102 (RHIC discovery)

all_predictions.prediction: Deconfinement / chiral-restoration transition at μ_B = 0 is a smooth crossover at T_c ≈ 155 MeV (not first-order at physical quark masses)
all_predictions.description: Lattice-QCD with physical quark masses: HotQCD + Budapest-Wuppertal independently agree on smooth crossover at T_c = 156.5 ± 1.5 MeV. The crossover (not first-order) status was an important empirical determination — first-order would have implications for cosmological QCD-era bubble nucleation.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Aoki, Endrődi, Fodor, Katz, Szabo 2006, Nature 443, 675 (smooth-crossover prediction from lattice)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bazavov et al. (HotQCD) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 094503

all_predictions.prediction: Charmonium (J/ψ) suppression in QGP from color-screening dissociation
all_predictions.description: Matsui-Satz 1986 predicted J/ψ suppression in QGP due to Debye screening of c + c̄ binding. Observed at NA50 SPS (1996), confirmed at RHIC and LHC. LHC additionally observed regeneration from c + c̄ recombination — confirming the screening picture with added complexity.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Matsui, Satz 1986, Phys. Lett. B 178, 416
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abreu et al. (NA50) 1996, Phys. Lett. B 410, 327

all_predictions.prediction: QGP exhibits near-perfect-fluid behavior with η/s near KSS bound 1/(4π)
all_predictions.description: Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005 conjectured η/s ≥ 1/(4π) from AdS-CFT. RHIC + LHC hydrodynamic flow analyses extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π) — close to the bound. Major validation of ads-cft-instance program in real data.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301

all_predictions.prediction: Strangeness enhancement in heavy-ion collisions vs pp baseline
all_predictions.description: Rafelski-Müller 1982 predicted enhanced thermal strange-baryon production in QGP. Confirmed at NA35/NA49 SPS, STAR/PHENIX RHIC, ALICE LHC.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Rafelski, Müller 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 48, 1066
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Andersen et al. (ALICE) 2017, Nature Phys. 13, 535

all_predictions.prediction: QCD critical endpoint exists at finite μ_B (~200-500 MeV)
all_predictions.description: Predicted from effective models (NJL, PNJL, FRG). RHIC BES II (2019-2023) explored higher μ_B regimes; STAR cumulant measurements show hints at √s_NN = 7.7 GeV but no definitive endpoint identification. NICA, FAIR, J-PARC HI future will extend reach. Lattice-QCD cannot directly access due to sign problem.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Stephanov, Rajagopal, Shuryak 1998, Phys. Rev. Lett. 81, 4816

all_predictions.prediction: Color-Flavor-Locked (CFL) color-superconductor phase exists at asymptotic μ_B (Alford-Rajagopal-Wilczek 1999)
all_predictions.description: Asymptotically rigorous prediction (high-density limit + weak-coupling expansion). At NS-relevant densities (μ_B ~ 400 MeV), CFL relevance depends on m_strange / Δ. NS-interior observations (mass-radius, GW170817 tidal deformability) constrain dense-matter EOS but do not require quark-matter phases at observed densities.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Alford, Rajagopal, Wilczek 1999, Nucl. Phys. B 537, 443

all_predictions.prediction: Massless 2-flavor QCD chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class
all_predictions.description: Pisarski-Wilczek 1984 predicted O(4) universality for chiral transition in massless limit. Real QCD has nonzero quark masses — transition becomes crossover. Lattice studies at small quark mass show O(4) scaling consistent with predictions but cannot directly test at m_q = 0. Listed `retro-explanatory-only` — the prediction is consistent with data but not falsifiable at physical conditions.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338

all_predictions.prediction: Color-Higgs continuity between confined-hadronic and CFL phases (Schäfer-Wilczek 1999)
all_predictions.description: Theoretical conjecture: in (T, μ_B) plane, hadronic and CFL phases are continuously connected with no sharp transition. Active theoretical debate; no direct experimental probe at relevant μ_B currently exists.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Schäfer, Wilczek 1999, Phys. Rev. Lett. 82, 3956

all_predictions.prediction: Quarkyonic matter exists as confined-but-quark-dominated phase at high μ_B, intermediate T (McLerran-Pisarski 2007)
all_predictions.description: Predicted in large-N_c limit. At physical N_c = 3, existence is debated. May appear in NS-interior phase structure. No direct experimental probe currently exists at relevant μ_B.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: McLerran, Pisarski 2007, Nucl. Phys. A 796, 83

all_predictions.prediction: CMB has a Planck thermal-blackbody spectrum at T = 2.725 K
all_predictions.description: Big-bang cosmology + thermal-equilibrium prediction. COBE/FIRAS measurement (Mather et al. 1990) confirmed Planck spectrum to ~10⁻⁵ deviations — sharpest blackbody ever measured. Foundational ΛCDM consistency test.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Alpher, Bethe, Gamow 1948, Phys. Rev. 73, 803 (relic CMB prediction)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Mather et al. 1990, Astrophys. J. Lett. 354, L37

all_predictions.prediction: Synchrotron radiation from relativistic charged particles in magnetic fields produces broadband non-thermal spectrum
all_predictions.description: Schwinger 1949 prediction. Confirmed in Crab, AGN jets, pulsars across radio-to-X-ray. Foundational non-thermal-astrophysics observation.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Schwinger 1949, Phys. Rev. 75, 1912
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (Crab)

all_predictions.prediction: LHAASO would detect PeV-scale gamma rays from galactic PeVatron sources
all_predictions.description: Long-conjectured galactic PeVatrons (cosmic-ray acceleration to PeV scales). LHAASO 2021 (Cao et al.) detected >0.1 PeV photons from 12 galactic sources, with highest 1.4 PeV. Recent transformative observation; opens UHE-gamma astronomy.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Berezinskii, Bulanov, Dogiel, Ginzburg, Ptuskin 1990, Astrophysics of Cosmic Rays (book — PeVatron framework)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Cao et al. (LHAASO) 2021, Nature 594, 33

all_predictions.prediction: BNS mergers produce optical/IR kilonova counterparts via r-process radioactive decay
all_predictions.description: Li-Paczynski 1998 + Metzger et al. 2010 kilonova model. GW170817 + AT2017gfo (2017) provided direct confirmation; spectroscopic lanthanide features.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Li, Paczynski 1998, Astrophys. J. Lett. 507, L59
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Smartt et al. 2017, Nature 551, 75 (kilonova spectroscopy)

all_predictions.prediction: High-energy neutrinos correlate with gamma-ray emission at blazars
all_predictions.description: Blazar cosmic-ray-acceleration models (Mannheim 1993; Halzen-Hooper 2002) predicted neutrino emission accompanying hadronic gamma. IceCube-170922A + TXS 0506+056 multi-messenger (2018) provided ~3σ first-source-association evidence.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Mannheim 1993, Phys. Rev. D 48, 2408
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: IceCube + Fermi-LAT + MAGIC + VERITAS 2018, Science 361, 147

all_predictions.prediction: Stellar-mass BHs emit Hawking radiation at T_H ~ 10⁻⁸ K
all_predictions.description: Hawking 1974 prediction. T_H far below CMB temperature for any astrophysical BH → undetectable in practice. Only primordial BHs near evaporation could produce detectable signatures. Will likely remain Mendeleev-empty.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hawking 1974, Nature 248, 30

all_predictions.prediction: QCD axion with mass ~μeV couples to photons at g_aγγ ~ 10⁻¹⁵ GeV⁻¹, detectable in haloscopes
all_predictions.description: Peccei-Quinn 1977 + Weinberg/Wilczek 1978 axion. ADMX-G2 + future MADMAX, IAXO, will probe DFSZ/KSVZ band. Cross-reference dark-matter-candidates v25.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Weinberg 1978, Phys. Rev. Lett. 40, 223

all_predictions.prediction: GZK cutoff in UHE cosmic-ray spectrum at ~5 × 10¹⁹ eV from interaction with CMB photons
all_predictions.description: Greisen 1966 + Zatsepin-Kuzmin 1966 prediction. Auger + HiRes (~2005-2010) confirmed suppression at predicted energy. Implies UHECR sources within ~100 Mpc.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Greisen 1966, Phys. Rev. Lett. 16, 748; Zatsepin, Kuzmin 1966, JETP Lett. 4, 78
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbasi et al. (HiRes) 2008, Phys. Rev. Lett. 100, 101101

all_predictions.prediction: GUT magnetic monopoles produce characteristic EM signatures (Rubakov-Callan effect: catalyzed nucleon decay)
all_predictions.description: 't Hooft-Polyakov 1974 GUT-monopole + Rubakov 1981 + Callan 1982 catalysis. Searched in MACRO, IceCube, ANTARES, MoEDAL — null. Bounded below Parker flux bound. Cross-references su5-gut-rep-content.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: 't Hooft 1974, Nucl. Phys. B 79, 276

all_predictions.prediction: Photon mass < 10⁻¹⁸ eV from solar-wind / GW170817 timing
all_predictions.description: Theoretical massless-photon prediction. Solar-wind tests + GW170817 1.7s GW-EM coincidence bound photon mass < 10⁻¹⁸ eV. Consistent with massless gauge boson.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Maxwell theory + QED — no single citation
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Particle Data Group 2022 (photon mass review); Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817 bound)

all_predictions.prediction: Proton decays via p → π⁰ e⁺ at τ_p ~ 10²⁹-10³⁰ yr (minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5))
all_predictions.description: Georgi-Glashow 1974 minimal SU(5) prediction τ_p ~ (M_GUT/M_W)⁴ × ℏ/(α² m_p) ≈ 10²⁹⁻³⁰ yr for M_GUT ~ 10¹⁵ GeV. Decisively ruled out by Super-K (Takenaka et al. 2020) at τ > 2.4 × 10³⁴ yr — 4-5 orders of magnitude beyond prediction. Type-A FALSIFIED — major historical exclusion of a specific GUT scenario. This is the 12th falsified prediction in the dataset.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Takenaka et al. (Super-K) 2020, Phys. Rev. D 102, 112011

all_predictions.prediction: Proton decays via p → K⁺ ν̄ at τ_p ~ 10³³⁻³⁵ yr in SUSY-SU(5) with natural-scale SUSY parameters
all_predictions.description: SUSY-SU(5) dimension-5 operator prediction (Sakai-Yanagida 1982). For natural-SUSY squark/Higgs masses ~ TeV, predicts τ ~ 10³² yr — in tension with Super-K 2014 bound τ > 5.9 × 10³³ yr. SUSY-SU(5) survives only at high SUSY scale (multi-TeV colored Higgs) or with specific flavor-suppression structures. Listed `unconfirmed-tension` — the natural-SUSY parameter space is excluded but the framework with non-natural SUSY remains viable.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (Super-K) 2014, Phys. Rev. D 90, 072005

all_predictions.prediction: SO(10) GUT predicts proton decay at τ ~ 10³⁵ yr (within Hyper-K reach)
all_predictions.description: SO(10) GUT M_GUT ~ 10¹⁶ GeV gives longer proton lifetime than SU(5). Predicted decay rate within Hyper-K + DUNE future sensitivity. Test pending.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Fritzsch, Minkowski 1975; Georgi 1975

all_predictions.prediction: Flipped SU(5) × U(1) predicts τ_p > 10³⁵ yr (beyond Hyper-K reach)
all_predictions.description: Suppressed dimension-6 operators in flipped SU(5) give very long lifetime; possibly out of reach of even Hyper-K + DUNE.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Barr 1982; Derendinger, Kim, Nanopoulos 1984

all_predictions.prediction: Pati-Salam SU(4)_c × SU(2)_L × SU(2)_R preserves baryon number in minimal form (no proton decay)
all_predictions.description: Pati-Salam-as-quark-lepton-unification doesn't predict proton decay. Consistent with all current data. Extensions can introduce baryon-number violation; those extensions are constrained.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Pati, Salam 1974, Phys. Rev. D 10, 275

all_predictions.prediction: Neutron-antineutron oscillation at τ_n-n̄ ~ 10⁸ s in B-L-violating BSM
all_predictions.description: Mohapatra-Marshak 1980 B-L-violating prediction. Super-K 2015 bound τ_n-n̄ > 4.7 × 10⁸ s already constrains. ESS-NNBAR future experiment will probe further.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Mohapatra, Marshak 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 1316
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abe et al. (Super-K) 2015, Phys. Rev. D 91, 072006

all_predictions.prediction: Hyper-Kamiokande extends τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) bound to > 10³⁵ yr by 2040
all_predictions.description: Hyper-K 260 kton fiducial × 10 years operation projects τ-sensitivity of ~10³⁵ yr. If observed, it would establish proton decay; if not, further constrains SUSY-SU(5).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Abe et al. (Hyper-K Collaboration) 2018, arXiv:1805.04163

all_predictions.prediction: DUNE provides complementary p → K⁺ ν̄ sensitivity via liquid-argon TPC
all_predictions.description: DUNE 40 kton LAr-TPC expected τ(p → K⁺ ν̄) sensitivity > 4 × 10³⁴ yr after 10 years. Better K⁺ identification than water Cherenkov.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Abi et al. (DUNE) 2020, JINST 15, T08010

all_predictions.prediction: Combined SUSY-SU(5) constraint from Super-K multi-channel + LHC SUSY-search nulls pushes SUSY scale > 10 TeV
all_predictions.description: Super-K's combined limits on p → K⁺ ν̄, p → π⁰ e⁺, p → η e⁺ + LHC's absence of natural-SUSY signals jointly constrain SUSY-SU(5) into heavy-SUSY parameter space. Combined indirect-evidence claim is confirmed.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Lucas, Raby 1996, Phys. Rev. D 54, 2261
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Particle Data Group 2022 (proton-decay + SUSY review)

all_predictions.prediction: IMB + Kamiokande 1980s observations would either detect proton decay (consistent with minimal SU(5)) or set τ > 10³² yr (excluding minimal SU(5))
all_predictions.description: Historic prediction realized: IMB-1 + Kamiokande-I observed no proton-decay events 1981-1990, setting τ(p → π⁰ e⁺) > 10³² yr — already ruled out minimal Georgi-Glashow SU(5) by 1990. First experimental falsification of a specific GUT framework.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Goldhaber, Lederman, Schopper 1980 era — multiple authors
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Becker-Szendy et al. (IMB) 1990, Phys. Rev. D 42, 2974

all_predictions.prediction: SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 is conformal at the origin of moduli space with exact Coulomb-branch operator dimension Δ_u = 2
all_predictions.description: Seiberg-Witten 1994 proved exact conformality via vanishing of β-function at N_f = 2 N_c = 4. Coulomb-branch operator scaling dimension Δ_u = 2 protected by N=2 SUSY + R-symmetry. Foundational result.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Seiberg, Witten 1994, Nucl. Phys. B 431, 484
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Seiberg, Witten 1994 (rigorous within N=2 framework)

all_predictions.prediction: Argyres-Douglas theories exist with irrational Coulomb-branch operator scaling dimensions
all_predictions.description: Argyres-Douglas 1995 demonstrated SCFTs at singular Coulomb-branch points with irrational Δ_u (e.g., 6/5, 4/3, 3/2 for H_0, H_1, H_2). Rigorous within N=2 SCFT framework via a-maximization.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Douglas 1995, Nucl. Phys. B 448, 93
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Subsequent a-maximization (Anselmi et al. 1997, Intriligator-Wecht 2003)

all_predictions.prediction: AGT correspondence: SU(N) N=2 SQCD instanton partition function = SU(N) Toda CFT conformal block
all_predictions.description: Alday-Gaiotto-Tachikawa 2010: original SU(2) ↔ Liouville. Generalized to SU(N) ↔ A_{N-1} Toda by Wyllard 2009 + subsequent verifications. Confirmed at multiple instanton orders.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Alday, Gaiotto, Tachikawa 2010, Lett. Math. Phys. 91, 167
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Wyllard 2009; multiple subsequent verifications

all_predictions.prediction: Every 4D N=2 SCFT has an associated 2D chiral algebra; Schur index = chiral-algebra vacuum character
all_predictions.description: Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli-van Rees 2015: 2D-4D correspondence proved at the level of Schur-sector operators. Many specific examples verified: AD ↔ Virasoro minimal models, SU(2) N_f=4 ↔ affine so(8)_{-2}, MN E_6 ↔ affine e_6_{-3}.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Beem et al. 2015 (constructive proof)

all_predictions.prediction: Class-S construction: M5-brane (2,0) theory on Riemann surface with punctures gives 4D N=2 SCFT with flavor symmetry determined by puncture data
all_predictions.description: Gaiotto 2009: systematic construction of 4D N=2 SCFTs from M5-branes wrapped on punctured Riemann surfaces. Specific identifications: T_3 = MN E_6, T_4 = rank-3 SCFT, etc. Multi-channel verifications via index calculations, Higgs-branch identifications, AGT.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Gaiotto 2009, JHEP 12, 034
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Gaiotto 2009 + many subsequent

all_predictions.prediction: Hofman-Maldacena bound 1/2 ≤ a/c ≤ 3/2 holds for any 4D unitary CFT — N=2 SCFTs satisfy with margin
all_predictions.description: Hofman-Maldacena 2008 derived universal a/c bound from energy-flux positivity in 4D CFT. All known N=2 SCFTs (AD, MN, SU(N) SQCDs, class-S) satisfy bound with various margins.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: All known N=2 SCFTs verified to satisfy

all_predictions.prediction: Pestun localization: 4D N=2 partition function on S⁴ reduces to finite-dimensional matrix integral
all_predictions.description: Pestun 2012: rigorous proof of S⁴ partition function localization for N=2* and N=2 SU(2) gauge theories. Extended to many other cases.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Pestun 2012, Commun. Math. Phys. 313, 71
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Pestun 2012 (constructive proof)

all_predictions.prediction: Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6 has Coulomb-branch operator at Δ_u = 3 with E_6 flavor symmetry from minimal nilpotent orbit Higgs branch
all_predictions.description: Minahan-Nemeschansky 1996: rank-1 isolated SCFT with E_6 flavor symmetry. Δ_u = 3 from a-maximization; Higgs branch = E_6 minimal nilpotent orbit (dim 22). Verified via M5-brane class-S construction (T_3) and AGT.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, Nucl. Phys. B 482, 142
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Confirmed via independent class-S construction (Gaiotto 2009) and AGT (Alday et al. 2010)

all_predictions.prediction: Higher-rank Argyres-Douglas theories form an infinite series with distinct Coulomb-branch spectra
all_predictions.description: Rank-2 and higher AD theories systematically constructed (Argyres-Lotito-Lü-Martone 2018+, Cecotti-Del Zotto). Predicted Coulomb-branch spectra computed via a-maximization. Some specific cases verified via class-S, but the full pattern at higher ranks awaits more independent checks.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Argyres, Lotito, Lü, Martone 2018, JHEP 05, 199

all_predictions.prediction: N=2 superconformal-bootstrap exclusion regions tighten with operator-dimension input — eventually identify all N=2 SCFTs?
all_predictions.description: Numerical N=2 bootstrap continues to refine bounds; conjecture that all 4D N=2 SCFTs eventually appear at kinks of bootstrap exclusion-region boundaries (Liendo-Rastelli 2014+). Active research area with no definitive completion.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Liendo, Rastelli 2014, JHEP 05, 100

all_predictions.prediction: π⁰ → γγ decay rate is determined by triangle anomaly: Γ = (α² m_π³)/(64 π³ F_π²) ≈ 7.7 eV
all_predictions.description: Adler-Bell-Jackiw 1969: chiral anomaly fixes π⁰ → γγ rate exactly (no renormalization beyond NLO QCD corrections). PrimEx-II 2014 measurement Γ = 7.802 ± 0.117 eV matches NLO prediction at sub-percent precision.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Adler 1969, Phys. Rev. 177, 2426; Bell, Jackiw 1969, Nuovo Cim. A 60, 47
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Larin et al. (PrimEx-II) 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113, 011803

all_predictions.prediction: SM matter content is uniquely constrained (up to overall normalization) by gauge anomaly cancellation — predicts charge quantization
all_predictions.description: Bouchiat-Iliopoulos-Meyer 1972 + Geng-Marshak 1989: SM gauge anomaly cancellation requires specific quark and lepton charge assignments. Predicts e⁻ charge = -3 × down-quark charge — verified experimentally.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: All SM particle charges measured consistent with prediction

all_predictions.prediction: Chiral symmetry MUST break in QCD ('t Hooft 1980 anomaly matching argument)
all_predictions.description: 't Hooft 1980 + Frishman-Schwimmer-Banks-Yankielowicz 1981: anomaly matching between UV (quark-gluon) and IR (hadron) descriptions is incompatible with unbroken chiral symmetry. Confirmed by hadronic spectrum showing SU(N_f)_L × SU(N_f)_R → SU(N_f)_V breaking pattern with pion Goldstones.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: 't Hooft 1980, in Recent Developments in Gauge Theories
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Hadronic spectrum confirmation

all_predictions.prediction: Witten SU(2) global anomaly requires even number of left-handed SM doublets per generation
all_predictions.description: Witten 1982: SU(2) gauge theory has Z_2 global anomaly π_4(SU(2)) = Z_2. SM has 4 left-handed doublets per generation (3 quark color copies + 1 lepton) — anomaly cancels.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: SM matter content verified by collider data

all_predictions.prediction: 3D time-reversal-symmetric insulators are classified by Z_2 invariant; non-trivial class has single Dirac-cone surface state
all_predictions.description: Fu-Kane-Mele 2007 prediction of Z_2 invariant + Dirac-cone surface state confirmed by ARPES on Bi₂Se₃, Bi₂Te₃, Sb₂Te₃ family (Hsieh et al. 2009 + many).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Fu, Kane, Mele 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 98, 106803
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Hsieh et al. 2009, Nature 460, 1101

all_predictions.prediction: Integer quantum Hall conductivity = first Chern number × e²/h (TKNN formula)
all_predictions.description: Thouless-Kohmoto-Nightingale-den Nijs 1982: σ_xy quantized to ν × e²/h with ν = first Chern number. Confirmed by von Klitzing 1980 + subsequent precision measurements at part-per-billion.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Thouless, Kohmoto, Nightingale, den Nijs 1982, Phys. Rev. Lett. 49, 405
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: von Klitzing, Dorda, Pepper 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 45, 494

all_predictions.prediction: Free-fermion Z classification of 1D topological superconductors collapses to Z_8 under generic 4-fermion interactions (Fidkowski-Kitaev)
all_predictions.description: Fidkowski-Kitaev 2010: free Majorana classification Z reduces to Z_8 under interactions. Theoretically confirmed via cobordism (Freed-Hopkins 2016) and explicit interaction analysis. Material realization in 1D topological SCs remains limited but framework rigorously established.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010, Phys. Rev. B 81, 134509
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Freed, Hopkins 2016 (cobordism)

all_predictions.prediction: Parity anomaly on 3D TI surface gives half-integer Hall conductance σ_xy = (n + 1/2) e²/h under broken T-reversal
all_predictions.description: Niemi-Semenoff 1983 + Redlich 1984 parity anomaly prediction confirmed by quantum anomalous Hall observation in magnetically-doped TIs (Chang et al. 2013).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Niemi, Semenoff 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 51, 2077
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Chang et al. 2013, Science 340, 167

all_predictions.prediction: Haldane integer-spin chain has gapped ground state with edge spin-1/2 modes (SPT order)
all_predictions.description: Haldane 1983 prediction confirmed in NENP (1989) and many subsequent S=1 chain materials. Modern interpretation: Haldane phase = SO(3)-protected SPT phase with Z_2 invariant.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Haldane 1983, Phys. Rev. Lett. 50, 1153
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Renard et al. 1988, Europhys. Lett. 3, 945 (NENP)

all_predictions.prediction: QCD θ-vacuum should produce neutron EDM ~ θ × 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm — strong-CP problem (Peccei-Quinn solution proposed)
all_predictions.description: Naive expectation: θ ~ O(1) gives |d_n| ~ 10⁻¹⁶ e·cm. Observed bound |d_n| < 1.8 × 10⁻²⁶ e·cm (Abel et al. 2020) requires |θ| < 1.5 × 10⁻¹⁰. Why so small? Peccei-Quinn axion proposed dynamical solution. No axion detected yet — strong-CP remains unresolved.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Naive expectation from QCD instanton physics
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abel et al. 2020 nEDM bound creates tension

all_predictions.prediction: MOND with a_0 ~ 1.2 × 10⁻¹⁰ m/s² explains galaxy rotation curves without dark matter
all_predictions.description: Milgrom 1983 prediction confirmed at galactic scales: SPARC sample (Lelli-McGaugh-Schombert 2016) of ~150 disk galaxies — rotation curves fit with single a_0; baryonic Tully-Fisher relation matches MOND prediction.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: McGaugh et al. 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 117, 201101 (SPARC); Lelli, McGaugh, Schombert 2016

all_predictions.prediction: MOND alone can explain galaxy-cluster missing mass without dark matter
all_predictions.description: Pure MOND falsified at cluster scales by Bullet Cluster (Clowe et al. 2006): gravitational-lensing mass is spatially offset from baryonic mass during cluster merger, requires non-baryonic dark matter or breakdown of MOND.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Implicit MOND-only DM-replacement at all scales
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

all_predictions.prediction: TeVeS (Bekenstein 2004) relativistic MOND completion produces GW propagation speed c_T differing from c
all_predictions.description: TeVeS predicts c_T ≠ c from vector-field gravitational coupling. GW170817 (Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶) falsifies generic TeVeS classes. Only highly-tuned post-2017 extensions (RMOND) survive.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Bekenstein 2004, Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Boran, Desai, Kahya, Woodard 2018, Phys. Rev. D 97, 041501

all_predictions.prediction: DGP self-accelerating branch reproduces cosmological dark-energy without Λ
all_predictions.description: DGP self-accelerating branch as DE alternative ruled out by combined Type Ia SN cosmography + LSS growth + CMB data (Lombriser et al. 2009). GW170817 further constrains the model.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Dvali, Gabadadze, Porrati 2000, Phys. Lett. B 485, 208 (DGP); Deffayet 2001 (DGP cosmology)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Lombriser, Hu, Fang, Seljak 2009, Phys. Rev. D 80, 063536

all_predictions.prediction: Horndeski-class scalar-tensor theories produce graviton speed c_T deviating from c
all_predictions.description: GW170817 bound Δv/c < 7 × 10⁻¹⁶ rules out generic Horndeski G_5 and most G_4(φ,X) terms. Only narrow surviving subset (G_4(φ) function of φ only, no X-dependence) remains.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Generic Horndeski expectation: O(1) modifications to c_T
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382; Creminelli, Vernizzi 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 251302

all_predictions.prediction: Brans-Dicke scalar-tensor PPN parameter γ deviates significantly from GR value γ = 1
all_predictions.description: Original Brans-Dicke 1961 prediction γ ≠ 1 with ω_BD ~ O(1). Cassini Shapiro-time-delay (Bertotti et al. 2003): γ - 1 = (2.1 ± 2.3) × 10⁻⁵, requires ω_BD > 4 × 10⁴ — falsifies original natural-parameter prediction.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Brans, Dicke 1961, Phys. Rev. 124, 925
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bertotti, Iess, Tortora 2003, Nature 425, 374

all_predictions.prediction: GR's quadrupole GW emission formula predicts binary-pulsar orbital decay rate
all_predictions.description: Hulse-Taylor PSR B1913+16 + PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar timing confirms GR orbital decay to ~0.01% precision (Kramer et al. 2021). Multi-channel post-Keplerian tests all consistent with GR.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Einstein 1916 + Peters-Mathews 1963 (GR quadrupole formula)
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050

all_predictions.prediction: Massive graviton (dRGT class) has mass m_g detectable via dispersion of GW signal
all_predictions.description: dRGT massive-gravity predicts E² = p² c² + m_g² c⁴ dispersion. LIGO/Virgo GWTC-3 bounds: m_g < 1.76 × 10⁻²³ eV. No detection; mass is bounded but could be just below current sensitivity. Future LISA + Einstein Telescope will tighten by 1-2 orders of magnitude.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: de Rham, Gabadadze, Tolley 2010, Phys. Rev. Lett. 106, 231101
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Abbott et al. 2021, Astrophys. J. 932, 49 (bounds only)

all_predictions.prediction: Modified-gravity BH solutions deviate from Kerr — EHT shadow imaging tests
all_predictions.description: Various modified-gravity proposals predict modified BH structure (hair, deviations from Kerr-no-hair). EHT M87 + Sgr A* shadow tests bound deviations to ~few %. Specific predictions not detected; future EHT-next + ngEHT will tighten.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Various modified-gravity hairy-BH proposals
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104 (bounds only)

all_predictions.prediction: Chameleon / symmetron screening allows light scalar fields to evade solar-system fifth-force tests
all_predictions.description: Khoury-Weltman 2004 prediction: chameleon screening evades fifth-force bounds in dense environments. Confirmed mechanism — chameleon-mass enhancement in laboratory + solar-system densities verified. Parameter-space bounded by Eöt-Wash + atomic-interferometry + cosmological tests (Hamilton et al. 2015 + others).
all_predictions.prediction_citation: Khoury, Weltman 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 171104
all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Hamilton et al. 2015, Science 349, 849 + various

all_predictions.prediction: Kolmogorov K41 inertial-range energy spectrum E(k) ~ k^(-5/3) at high Reynolds numbers
all_predictions.description: Kolmogorov 1941 dimensional prediction confirmed at Re_λ ~ 1000+ in DNS, laboratory, atmospheric, and oceanographic measurements. Spectral slope -1.67 with small multifractal intermittency corrections.

all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Liphardt et al. 2002, Science 296, 1832

all_predictions.confirmation_citation: Bricard et al. 2013, Nature 503, 95 (colloidal); many subsequent

all_predictions.prediction: ITER will achieve fusion-power-gain Q ≥ 10 in burning-plasma regime
all_predictions.description: ITER design target: 500 MW fusion from 50 MW heating in 400-second pulses. First plasma now targeted 2034+; DT operation ~2039+. The Q ≥ 10 prediction tests scaling of tokamak confinement + alpha-particle self-heating in burning plasma regime — not previously achieved.
all_predictions.prediction_citation: ITER Physics Basis 2007, Nucl. Fusion 47

all_edges.description: McKay correspondence: each finite subgroup Γ ⊂ SU(2) has a McKay quiver built from tensor products of the defining 2-dim rep with all irreps of Γ; the McKay quiver is the affine ADE Dynkin diagram of the corresponding Lie algebra. The bijection runs symmetrically: given an ADE Lie algebra, the corresponding SU(2) subgroup is recovered as the binary polyhedral group of matching ADE type. Canonical direction (subgroups → Lie algebras) reflects McKay's original construction direction.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

all_edges.description: Brieskorn-Grothendieck: resolution of a Du Val singularity attaches a configuration of (-2)-curves on a non-singular replacement; the intersection graph of these curves is the ADE Dynkin diagram. The Lie algebra structure (Dynkin diagram, root system) is read off the singularity. Per v15 spec convention, 'A derives-from B' means A's structure is read off B; here the Lie algebra (A) is derived from the singularity (B).
citations: Brieskorn 1966, Math. Ann. 166, 76
Brieskorn 1968, Actes Congr. Internat. Math. Nice 2, 279
Slodowy 1980, Simple Singularities and Simple Algebraic Groups (LNM 815)

all_edges.description: Quotient construction: each Du Val singularity is realized as ℂ²/Γ where Γ ⊂ SU(2) is the corresponding ADE finite subgroup; the singularity is derived from the group action. Direction: 'ade-du-val derives-from ade-su2-subgroups' since the singularity's structure is read off the group.
citations: Klein 1884
Slodowy 1980, LNM 815

all_edges.description: Gabriel's theorem: connected representation-finite quivers are exactly the ADE quivers (those with ADE underlying graph). Moreover, indecomposable representations of an ADE quiver are in bijection with positive roots of the corresponding ADE root system. The bijection is symmetric: given a Lie algebra, the corresponding quiver is the underlying Dynkin diagram with any orientation; given a representation-finite quiver, its underlying graph is the Dynkin diagram.
citations: Gabriel 1972, Manuscripta Mathematica 6, 71
Bernstein, Gelfand, Ponomarev 1973, Russian Math. Surveys 28, 17

all_edges.description: Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber classification: each ADE Cartan type corresponds to a modular invariant of the SU(2)_k WZW model (with appropriate level matching). A-series at all levels; D-series at even levels; E_6/E_7/E_8 at sporadic levels 10, 16, 28. The bijection is between Cartan types and modular-invariant structural patterns; the level axis adds a parametric refinement.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Commun. Math. Phys. 113, 1

all_edges.description: Bridgeland-King-Reid: derived equivalence between coherent sheaves on the minimal resolution of a Du Val singularity and representations of the McKay quiver of the corresponding finite SU(2) subgroup. The equivalence is functorial and runs symmetrically; canonical direction reflects the original 2001 result's statement.
citations: Bridgeland, King, Reid 2001, J. Amer. Math. Soc. 14, 535

all_edges.description: McKay quiver: each finite SU(2) subgroup Γ has a canonical quiver (the McKay quiver) whose vertices are irreps of Γ and edges record the tensor decomposition of the defining 2-dim rep ⊗ V_i; the McKay quiver of a finite SU(2) subgroup is the affine ADE quiver. Distinct from the Gabriel-quiver case (ade-quivers) which uses finite ADE quivers; the McKay quiver is the affine version.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

all_edges.description: Derivable from the chain (ade-su2-subgroups → ade-lie-algebras via McKay; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ). The composite relation is real but not primitive; for visual purposes it can be omitted in favor of the two primary edges that compose to it.
Derivable from the chain (ade-du-val → ade-lie-algebras via resolution/Brieskorn; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).
Derivable from the chain (ade-quivers → ade-lie-algebras via Gabriel; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).

The simple components of the finite-dimensional spacetime symmetry groups are real forms of complex simple Lie algebras of ADE type. Lorentz so(3,1) ≃ sl(2,ℂ)_ℝ is a real form of A_1 × A_1; conformal so(2,4) is a real form of A_3 = D_3 = sl(4)/so(6); de Sitter so(4,1) and anti-de Sitter so(3,2) are real forms of D-type complex algebras. The relation does NOT apply to the Galilean cell (different algebraic structure), the 2D-conformal sub-case (Virasoro is infinite-dimensional, not ADE), or the Diff cell (also infinite-dimensional). cell_refs pins the source-side cells where the relation applies; this convention (cell_refs as source-side pins on cross-classification edges) is documented in v15.1 findings as an open spec clarification for v15.2.
citations: Helgason 2001, Differential Geometry, Lie Groups, and Symmetric Spaces (AMS) — real forms of complex Lie algebras
Hsiang 1975, Cohomology Theory of Topological Transformation Groups (Springer) — real-form structure for spacetime groups

all_edges.description: The ten-fold way is the free-fermion specialization of the Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification. Each ten-fold-way symmetry class corresponds to a specific tangential structure plus internal symmetry combination in the Freed-Hopkins framework (e.g., class AII ↔ Pin+; class BDI ↔ Pin-; class A ↔ Spin^c; etc.), and the ten-fold-way's per-(class, dim) topological invariant (0, Z, Z_2, 2Z) is the FREE part or the Z_2-torsion part of the corresponding cobordism group Ω^G_{d+1}. The full cobordism group is in general LARGER (Z_8 vs Z for BDI in 1d; Z_16 vs Z_2 for AII in 4d) because the cobordism classification includes interaction-induced refinements that the free-fermion ten-fold way cannot see. cell_refs pins all 30 ten-fold-way cells (the entire ten-fold-way classification is what specializes). First use of the `specializes` cross-classification subtype in the project; the relation is canonically the example used in v14.1 findings §10.1 to motivate v15's typed cross-classification edges.
citations: Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (ten-fold way; K-theory framing)
Freed, Hopkins 2016 (cobordism framing; recovers ten-fold way as free-fermion specialization)
Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010 (canonical example of where ten-fold way and Freed-Hopkins differ: the Z → Z_8 collapse demarcates the specialization)

all_edges.description: If SU(5) is the correct GUT framework, the SM rep content specializes the SU(5) rep content: each SM multiplet (the cells of sm-rep-content) is contained in a specific SU(5) representation (the cells of su5-gut-rep-content) under the conjectured SU(5) → SM breaking. The 18 cell_refs pin the SM cells whose embedding into SU(5) reps is part of the conjectured connection (15 matter cells + 3 gauge boson cells; the Higgs cell-H-gNA is not pinned because the Higgs embedding in SU(5) requires non-minimal Higgs content (24 + 5 or similar) which is not part of minimal SU(5)'s structural content). Status: conjectured — the connection has specific shape (the 5̄ + 10 embedding) but is not empirically established (proton decay non-observation has falsified minimal non-SUSY SU(5); other variants remain viable but unconfirmed).
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974
Buras, Ellis, Gaillard, Nanopoulos 1978
Super-Kamiokande Collaboration 2017 (current proton decay limits constraining the connection)

all_edges.description: The ADE SU(2)_k modular invariants are a specialization of the d=2 bootstrap classification: they are the bootstrap solutions for rational d=2 conformal field theories with su(2)_k current algebra (modular-invariant partition functions on the torus, equivalently consistent crossing-invariant OPE structures with finitely many primaries). The CIZ classification (Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber 1987) is the complete bootstrap-style classification for this sub-domain. The relation is specializes (ade-modular-invariants is the sub-domain; cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions is the more general framework). Status: partial — the relation is established for the SU(2)_k rational sub-case, but the d=2 minimal-models-Virasoro cell (cell-bs-d2-virasoro) is more general than ADE: it includes the diagonal minimal-model series at all (p,q), of which the SU(2)_k WZW models with ADE modular invariants are a specific extended-chiral-algebra extension. Status `partial` reflects that ADE pins one sub-region of the d=2 bootstrap exclusion regions, not the entire d=2 column. cell_refs pins all 5 ADE-modular-invariant cells, which are the source-side cells specializing into the broader d=2 bootstrap.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 241, 333 (the minimal-model bootstrap that subsumes ADE-rational specifically)
Gannon 2000, Rev. Math. Phys. 12, 739 (review of modular-invariant classification)

all_edges.description: The conformal-bootstrap exclusion regions derive from the spacetime-symmetry-groups classification: the input axioms of the bootstrap are precisely conformal invariance (the conformal group structure recorded in the conformal cells of spacetime-symmetry-groups) plus unitarity plus OPE associativity. The bootstrap's machinery (conformal blocks, crossing equations, unitarity bounds) is constructed from the irreducible-representation theory of the conformal group. In d=2 the relevant group is the infinite-dimensional Virasoro × Virasoro (the d=2 entry of spacetime-symmetry-groups); in d ≥ 3 it is the finite-dimensional so(d,2) (the higher-d conformal cells). Without the symmetry-group input, the bootstrap has no equations to solve. Subtype `derives-from` per the v15 spec §3.2 reading: the bootstrap is constructed from the conformal-symmetry-group structure. cell_refs pins the source-side cells (bootstrap cells); per v15.1 convention, cell_refs pins source-side cells (cross-classification edges read from source to target via the relation, here 'derives-from'). Every bootstrap cell derives from the conformal-group structure, hence the comprehensive cell_refs list.
citations: Mack 1977, Commun. Math. Phys. 53, 155 (conformal-algebra unitarity bounds in d ≥ 3)
Dolan, Osborn 2000, Nucl. Phys. B 599, 459 (conformal blocks as eigenfunctions of conformal Casimirs)
Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984 (d=2 Virasoro case)

all_edges.description: AdS/CFT aspires to demonstrate that the bootstrap exclusion regions for holographic CFTs (large-N, large-gap CFTs with sparse low-twist spectrum) are determined by the cobordism classification of the bulk SymTFT / invertible field theory data.
