The bulk theory is a cobordism-classified invertible (anomaly-inflow) theory; the boundary CFT data, in particular the central charges and stress-tensor 3-point function ratios subject to Hofman-Maldacena bounds, should derive from the bulk's cobordism data via holographic renormalization. Currently the bulk-boundary correspondence is established at the level of anomaly matching (Witten 1998; Henningson-Skenderis 1998) but the precise derivation of bootstrap exclusion boundaries from bulk cobordism data is aspired-not-shown. The four cell_refs pin the canonical holographic CFT cells where this aspired derivation would land first: 4D N=4 SYM (dual to AdS_5 × S^5), 6D (2,0) (dual to AdS_7 × S^4), 3D N=8 ABJM (dual to AdS_4 × S^7), and the universal stress-tensor sector (where bulk Einstein gravity directly fixes the boundary central charges). Discharge: established once a specific bootstrap exclusion bound (e.g., the Hofman-Maldacena bound on a/c ratios, or central charge minimization) is derived from bulk cobordism data without holographic-renormalization shortcuts.
citations: Heemskerk, Penedones, Polchinski, Sully 2009, JHEP 10, 079
Maldacena, Shenker, Stanford 2015, JHEP 08, 106 (chaos bound from holography matching bootstrap)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (topological symmetry in QFT)
Apruzzi et al. 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895 (SymTFT from string theory)
Witten 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 253 (anti-de Sitter space and holography)

all_edges.description: NCG aspires to derive the SM rep content from the K-theoretic / cobordism classification of anomaly-free fermion contents in 4D. Both programs land in K-theory: Connes-Chamseddine spectral triples are K-theoretic objects (Connes 1995); Freed-Hopkins cobordism for invertible field theories is K-theory-flavored (Freed-Hopkins 2021); García-Etxebarria-Montero have shown the SM is anomaly-free in the cobordism-precise sense (no Dai-Freed anomalies). The aspired connection: the cobordism-K-theory classification of admissible anomaly-free 4D fermion families should coincide with the spectral-triple classification at the level of SM-like solutions, providing a K-theoretic explanation for why the SM has exactly its observed rep content (rather than this being an arbitrary input). The 15 cell_refs pin the 15 matter cells (3 generations × 5 left/right-handed multiplets) that constitute the rep content being explained. Discharge: established once one demonstrates that the cobordism-K-theory classification (with appropriate symmetry data) and the spectral-triple classification (with appropriate finite-dimensional algebra input) bijectively identify the SM matter content as their joint solution. Currently partial successes exist on both sides separately but not as a derivation.
citations: Connes 1995, J. Math. Phys. 36, 6194 (NCG and reality)
Chamseddine, Connes, Marcolli 2007, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 11, 991 (gravity and SM with neutrino mixing)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases)
García-Etxebarria, Montero 2019, JHEP 08, 003 (Dai-Freed anomalies in particle physics)
Davighi, Lohitsiri 2020, JHEP 05, 098 (anomaly interplay for SM)

all_edges.description: Top-down holography (AdS/CFT applied to string compactifications) aspires to embed the SM rep content as the low-energy spectrum of a specific holographic CFT compactified on an internal geometry, making SM rep content categorically equivalent to a subset of bootstrap-allowed CFT data with the appropriate symmetry-breaking pattern. Concrete realizations: F-theory GUTs (Beasley-Heckman-Vafa 2009), where SM-like spectra arise from D-brane configurations whose dual CFTs sit in the bootstrap-allowed region. The aspiration is that some bootstrap-allowed SCFT, when compactified on a specific geometric background, gives exactly the SM at low energies — making the SM a 'special point' in the moduli space of bootstrap-allowed SCFTs. The four cell_refs pin the canonical SCFTs from which string compactifications might descend to SM-like physics: 4D N=4 SYM, 4D N=2 SCFTs (most relevant for SM-like phenomenology), 3D N=8 (M-theory side), and 6D (2,0) (F-theory parent). Discharge: established once a specific bootstrap-allowed SCFT plus a specific compactification geometry is shown to give exactly the SM gauge group, fermion content, and Yukawa structure (or equivalently, the SM is shown to sit at a specific point in the moduli space of bootstrap-allowed holographic CFTs).
citations: Maldacena 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (the large N limit of superconformal field theories)
Beasley, Heckman, Vafa 2009, JHEP 01, 058 (GUTs and exceptional branes in F-theory I)
Heckman 2010, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 60, 237 (particle physics implications of F-theory)
Weigand 2018, PoS TASI2017, 016 (TASI lectures on F-theory)

all_edges.description: The ten-fold-way classification of topological phases (in dimensions 1, 2, 3 with the ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes) aspires to derive from boundary CFT bootstrap data via the bulk-boundary correspondence. A (d+1)-dimensional topological phase has a d-dimensional boundary CFT or gapless boundary mode whose spectrum is subject to bootstrap exclusion bounds; the topological invariants in the bulk (Z, Z_2, 2Z entries of the table) classify the inequivalent gapless boundaries, which in turn correspond to bootstrap-allowed CFT spectra. AdS/CFT pursues this via the AdS_3/CFT_2 dictionary applied to Chern-Simons-style bulk theories; the tensor-network program (Vidal MERA; Evenbly-White) pursues it via explicit lattice constructions of holographic-like correspondences. Aspired discharge: established once every non-trivial entry of the ten-fold-way table is matched to a specific bootstrap-allowed boundary CFT solution. The 9 cell_refs pin the canonical non-trivial entries (the topological phases with anomalous boundary modes most likely to admit a CFT bootstrap dual).
citations: Vidal 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 220405 (entanglement renormalization, MERA)
Ryu, Schnyder, Furusaki, Ludwig 2010, New J. Phys. 12, 065010 (topological insulators and superconductors)
Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (periodic table of topological insulators)
Witten 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 035001 (three lectures on topological phases of matter)

all_edges.description: NCG aspires to derive the SM rep content jointly with spacetime symmetry data from a single underlying spectral triple, where the spectral triple consists of a continuum spin manifold (encoding spacetime symmetries — Poincaré in the continuum limit) tensored with a finite-dimensional algebra A_F (encoding internal gauge structure and matter content). In this aspirational picture, both the SM rep content and the spacetime symmetries are jointly determined: changing the spectral triple changes both at once, and the SM's specific structure is the unique (or nearly-unique) solution consistent with a small set of axioms (order-one condition, finiteness, KO-dimension 6 mod 8, etc.). Partial successes: gauge group U(1) × SU(2) × SU(3) is recovered; three fermion generations recovered (with right input); spectral action contains the Einstein-Hilbert term as the leading term (deriving continuum spacetime structure). Falsified partial: Higgs mass prediction ~170 GeV inconsistent with measured 125 GeV; modifications (Devastato-Lizzi-Martinetti 2014; Chamseddine-Connes-Mukhanov 2014) have been proposed. The 18 cell_refs pin all 15 matter cells plus the 3 gauge cells of sm-rep-content, since the NCG derivation aims to recover both simultaneously. Discharge: established once a single spectral triple is shown to derive SM gauge group, fermion content, Yukawa structure, AND continuum spacetime symmetries consistent with all measurements (including Higgs mass).
citations: Connes, Lott 1991, Nucl. Phys. Proc. Suppl. 18B, 29 (particle models and NCG)
Chamseddine, Connes 1997, Commun. Math. Phys. 186, 731 (spectral action principle)
Chamseddine, Connes, Marcolli 2007, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 11, 991 (gravity and SM with neutrino mixing)
Devastato, Lizzi, Martinetti 2014, JHEP 01, 042 (grand symmetry, spectral action, Higgs mass)
Chamseddine, Connes, Mukhanov 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 114, 091302 (quanta of geometry)

all_edges.description: Causal sets aspires to demonstrate that the classification of admissible continuum spacetime symmetries (Poincaré, diffeomorphism) and the cobordism-K-theory classification of admissible spacetime topologies are categorically equivalent at the discrete causal-set level: the same combinatorial data of an underlying ensemble of causal sets jointly determines both the continuum-limit Lorentz/Poincaré symmetry and the cobordism class of the recovered manifold. The Bombelli-Lee-Meyer-Sorkin program for discrete-to-continuum recovery aims to show that a 'manifoldlike' causal set has a unique continuum limit (up to small fluctuations) whose Lorentz invariance is emergent and whose topology is determined by the causal-set ensemble. The aspired connection makes precise what is currently a hope: that the symmetry-group classification and the cobordism classification are not independent inputs to spacetime physics but joint outputs of a single discrete classificatory framework. The 2 cell_refs pin the Poincaré and diffeomorphism cells (the canonical continuum-limit symmetries that causal sets aspires to recover, and which would be matched to specific cobordism classes). Discharge: established once a specific class of causal sets is shown to admit a continuum limit whose Lorentz symmetry group and cobordism class are jointly fixed by the ensemble structure (e.g., from the Bombelli-Henson-Sorkin manifoldlike criterion). Note: causal sets has the weakest aspired-edge defense among the four candidate-foundational programs because most of its aspirations land on open-frontiers (qg-frontier, cc-frontier) rather than on connections between existing formal-classifications — this edge captures the one substantive cross-classification aspiration but the case is more speculative than the AdS/CFT or NCG entries.
citations: Bombelli, Lee, Meyer, Sorkin 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 59, 521 (space-time as a causal set)
Henson 2009, in Approaches to Quantum Gravity, ed. Oriti (the causal set approach)
Surya 2019, Living Rev. Relativ. 22, 5 (the causal set approach to quantum gravity)
Dowker, Surya 2020, Class. Quantum Grav. 37, 053001 (manifoldlike causal sets)

all_edges.description: Top-down holography (F-theory / string compactifications) aspires to demonstrate that SM rep content and ten-fold-way topological-phase data are categorically-equivalent extractions of a single underlying string compactification: same geometry, different data extractor, structurally-paired content. F-theory GUTs (Beasley-Heckman-Vafa 2009) produce SM-like fermion spectra from intersecting D-brane configurations on Calabi-Yau geometries; the same geometries carry discrete higher-form / generalized symmetry data (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2019; García-Etxebarria-Heidenreich-Regalado 2022) classifying topological-phase content. AdS/CFT aspires to make the equivalence categorical: the moduli of admissible string compactifications would bijectively map to (SM-like rep content, ten-fold-way class) pairs that are jointly realizable. This is the one program-co-targeted shadow edge surfaced by the v15.6 pattern-finding pass: both ends were previously targeted by ads-cft-instance via separate aspired edges to bootstrap (cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions ↔ sm-rep-content and tenfold-way → cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions), but the direct sm-rep-content ↔ ten-fold-way connection was unauthored despite literature support. cell_refs pin the 15 SM matter cells (the source-side data that the categorical equivalence would jointly determine with topological-phase data). Discharge: established once a specific string compactification gives exactly the SM rep content and a specific ten-fold-way class as joint outputs, with the moduli labelling the pair forming a bijection or at least an explicit functor.
citations: Beasley, Heckman, Vafa 2009, JHEP 01, 058 (GUTs and exceptional branes in F-theory I)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (2-group symmetries)
García-Etxebarria, Heidenreich, Regalado 2022, JHEP 09, 130 (IIB flux non-commutativity and higher-form symmetries)
Heckman 2010, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 60, 237 (particle physics implications of F-theory)
Apruzzi et al. 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895 (SymTFT from string theory — geometric origin of higher-form symmetries)

all_edges.description: RG-info aspires that the cobordism classification of invertible (gapped) phases derives from the bootstrap classification of CFT fixed points via RG-flow continuation. In the Kapustin-Seiberg / Freed-Hopkins picture, every cobordism-classified invertible field theory is the RG-flow IR endpoint of some massive deformation of a UV bootstrap-allowed CFT; the cobordism classification is then the structure of trivial-flow endpoints emerging from bootstrap-allowed UV theories. The C-theorem (Zamolodchikov 1986) and a-theorem (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) establish information-monotonicity along RG flows in 2D and 4D respectively; the rg-info aspiration extends this monotonicity to a derivation: cobordism classes are RG-trivial fibres over the bootstrap UV moduli space. NOTE: this edge points opposite to the v15.5 edge edge-xc-bootstrap-derives-cobordism-aspired (which is targeted by ads-cft-instance and reads bootstrap-derives-from-cobordism via the bulk-boundary / SymTFT direction). The two aspirations are non-conflicting: AdS/CFT works in the bulk-boundary direction; RG-info works in the UV→IR direction. Authoring both as separate edges exercises the multi-edge-between-same-pair pattern flagged in v15.5 as previously unused. cell_refs pin 5 source-side cells: Ω^{Spin}_2 = ℤ₂ (2D fermionic invertible phases ↔ minimal-model bootstrap IR endpoints); Ω^{Spin}_4 = ℤ (4D Pontryagin-class invertible phase ↔ 4D bootstrap IR); Ω^{Spin^c}_2 = ℤ (IQHE-class ↔ 2D bootstrap chiral central charge); Ω^{Spin^c}_4 = ℤ² (4D spin^c invertible ↔ 4D bootstrap); Ω^{Pin+}_4 = ℤ₁₆ (Wang-Senthil anomaly ↔ 4D bootstrap with time-reversal). Discharge: established once a specific cobordism class is shown to be the RG-flow IR endpoint of a specific bootstrap-allowed UV CFT, with the RG flow respecting the c/a-theorem ordering.
citations: Zamolodchikov 1986, JETP Lett. 43, 730 (c-theorem)
Komargodski, Schwimmer 2011, JHEP 12, 099 (a-theorem in 4D)
Kapustin, Seiberg 2014, JHEP 04, 001 (higher-form symmetries and RG)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases as RG endpoints)
Apenko 2012, Physica A 391, 62 (information theory and RG)
Beny, Osborne 2015, New J. Phys. 17, 083005 (RG as quantum-information renormalization)

all_edges.description: Modular tensor categories specialize the bootstrap exclusion regions: MTCs are the algebraic data of rational 2D CFTs, which are the sub-domain of bootstrap-allowed CFTs satisfying rationality (finitely many primaries, modular-invariant partition function). Moore-Seiberg 1989 established the categorical equivalence between rational 2D CFTs and MTCs, making this specializes-relation rigorous: every MTC produces a rational CFT in the bootstrap-allowed region, and every rational CFT corresponds to an MTC. The 7 cell_refs pin representative MTCs (Fibonacci, Lee-Yang, Ising, SU(2)_2, tricritical-Ising, toric code, SU(2)_3) that occupy specific points within the d=2 bootstrap exclusion region. Note: a finer subtype `constrains` would more precisely capture the structural-causal relation (MTC modular S-matrix structurally constrains the partition function of any bulk-boundary partner CFT). Such an edge is distinct from this `specializes` edge: this one is about the rational sub-case as a SET; the constrains edge would be about how MTC structure NARROWS the form of any boundary CFT realized as MTC bulk-boundary partner. The `constrains` subtype does not exist in v15.3 schema; this distinct edge is documented in findings as a v16-pending authoring item rather than authored here with a misfit subtype.
citations: Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177
Bakalov, Kirillov 2001, AMS (textbook proof of categorical equivalence)
Cardy 1986, Nucl. Phys. B 270, 186 (operator content of two-dimensional conformally invariant theories)

all_edges.description: The ADE SU(2)_k modular invariants specialize the modular tensor categories: ADE-modular-invariants are exactly the SU(2)_k WZW MTCs (the A-series at each rank k+1, plus the D and exceptional modular extensions). Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber 1987 classified the modular-invariant partition functions for SU(2)_k WZW into ADE families; in MTC language, these are the MTC structures supported on the SU(2)_k chiral algebra (A-series) plus the orbifold-and-extension constructions (D, E_6, E_7, E_8 series). This edge complements the existing v15.4 partial edge `ade-modular-invariants -> cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions [partial/specializes]`: that edge says ADE-modular is part of the bootstrap region; this new edge says ADE-modular is part of the MTC sub-region within bootstrap. Together they document the chain: ade-modular-invariants ⊂ modular-tensor-categories ⊂ rational-CFT ⊂ cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions. cell_refs pin the 5 ADE-modular cells (all of them); each corresponds to an SU(2)_k MTC at the appropriate k.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Gannon 2000, Rev. Math. Phys. 12, 739 (review of modular-invariant classification)
Frohlich, Fuchs, Runkel, Schweigert 2006, Nucl. Phys. B 763, 354 (algebraic approach via Frobenius algebras in MTCs)

all_edges.description: Modular tensor categories specialize the cobordism classification of (2+1)D invertible / non-chiral topological phases. Status: partial — the specialization holds for the invertible (rank-1) and non-chiral (c=0) sub-case of MTC, where MTC data reduces to cobordism-classified anomaly inflow. Specifically: a non-chiral MTC has an associated 3D TQFT that is (in the trivial cobordism class) reducible to a finite group gauge theory, which has a cobordism-classified anomaly. The chiral central charge c (mod 8 for bosonic MTCs, mod 16 for fermionic) is itself cobordism data: Ω_4^{Pin+} = Z_16 captures the gravitational anomaly of fermionic chiral MTCs (Kitaev's 16-fold way). General chiral MTCs (Fibonacci, Ising, SU(2)_k at k ≥ 1) are NOT cobordism-classified — they carry non-invertible anyon content. So the specialization is partial: invertible / non-chiral MTC ⊂ cobordism; general MTC properly extends cobordism by adding non-invertible anyonic data. The 3 cell_refs pin the structurally-degenerate cases: rank-1 vacuum (literally cobordism-class 0), toric code (Z_2 gauge theory, cobordism-classified by group-cohomology), and doubled-Ising (non-chiral Drinfeld center, partially cobordism-overlapping).
citations: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (16-fold way, Pin+ cobordism)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases)
Kong, Wen 2014, arXiv:1405.5858 (braided fusion categories of topological order)

all_edges.description: Tensor-network aspires to demonstrate that the full classification of (2+1)D topological phases factorizes as the composition of ten-fold-way symmetry-protected data (the SPT factor) with modular-tensor-category intrinsic-topological-order data (the MTC factor): full-topological-phase = ten-fold-way × MTC. For (2+1)D this gives the Kitaev-Kong-Wen classification scheme (Lan-Kong-Wen 2017 elaborated it). The two classifications are complementary: ten-fold-way handles invertible / SPT phases (no anyons in the bulk; symmetry-protected gapless or topological boundary modes); MTC handles non-invertible / intrinsic topological order (anyons in the bulk; topological boundary modes are the boundary anyons). The aspiration is that every (2+1)D topological phase is precisely characterized by one ten-fold-way class plus one MTC, with the composition operation explicit. Tensor-network methods (Levin-Wen string-net constructions, PEPS) provide the most concrete framework where this factorization is being made precise. The 3 cell_refs pin canonical (2+1)D ten-fold-way cells whose composition with specific MTCs is the active research target.
citations: Lan, Kong, Wen 2017, Phys. Rev. B 95, 235140 (classification of (2+1)D topological orders)
Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 (string-net condensation)
Senthil 2015, Annu. Rev. Condens. Matter Phys. 6, 299 (review of SPT phases)
Chen, Gu, Liu, Wen 2013, Phys. Rev. B 87, 155114 (SPT phases with on-site symmetries)

all_edges.description: Modular tensor categories structurally constrain the form of any boundary CFT realized as MTC bulk-boundary partner — even before identifying the boundary as rational. The constraints are: (1) the partition function on the torus must transform under SL(2,Z) according to the MTC's modular S and T matrices; (2) the OPE coefficients must factor through the MTC's F-symbols (pentagon-axiom-consistent) and R-symbols (hexagon-axiom-consistent); (3) the chiral central charge c (mod 8 for bosonic MTCs, mod 16 for fermionic) must match the MTC's encoded chiral anomaly. These three constraints together fix the form (not just the set-membership) of any boundary CFT realized as a bulk-boundary partner of a given MTC. This is structurally distinct from the v15.8 specializes edge between the same pair (edge-xc-mtc-specializes-bootstrap): the specializes edge identifies WHICH boundary CFTs are MTC-realized (the rational sub-case as a subset); this constrains edge identifies HOW the MTC structure narrows the form of any candidate boundary CFT realized as MTC partner. The two edges target the same FC pair but capture different content — set-membership vs structural-causal restriction. Status: established. Moore-Seiberg axioms make the constraint rigorous; Cardy's modular-invariance constraint analysis (1986) gives the constructive form; the categorical-equivalence theorem (Moore-Seiberg 1989, refined Bakalov-Kirillov 2001) closes the structural-causal direction. The 7 cell_refs pin the same MTCs as the specializes edge — these are the canonical cases where both the subset and the structural-causal readings apply.
citations: Cardy 1986, Nucl. Phys. B 270, 186 (modular invariance constraints on the operator content)
Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177 (the rational CFT axioms — the structural-causal direction)
Bakalov, Kirillov 2001, AMS (textbook proof of MTC ↔ rational CFT categorical equivalence)
Fuchs, Runkel, Schweigert 2002, Nucl. Phys. B 624, 452 (TFT construction of RCFT correlators — explicit constructive form)

all_edges.description: Generalized symmetries specialize the cobordism classification: the SymTFT bulk associated to any symmetry S in d-dim is a (d+1)-dim invertible TFT, which is cobordism-classified (Freed-Hopkins 2021). Every cell of generalized-symmetries (a specific (d, p, structure-type) triple with realized examples) corresponds to a specific cobordism class in Ω_{d+1}^{SO × BG} (for invertible cases) or to a fusion-categorical extension thereof (for non-invertible cases). The specializes-relation: generalized-symmetries provides MORE structure than cobordism alone (it also tracks form-degree, structure-type, and the boundary QFT's symmetry data), so it specializes the cobordism classification to the symmetry-data context. cell_refs pin 4 cells whose SymTFT bulks are canonically cobordism-classified: 2D Z_2 (3D DW), 3D Z_2 (4D DW), 4D YM center (5D), 6D (2,0) (7D CS-like).
citations: Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases — the cobordism classification used in SymTFT bulk)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471
Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467 (cobordism approach to SPT phases — early SymTFT-like analysis)

all_edges.description: Generalized-symmetry anomalies structurally constrain the bootstrap exclusion regions. A CFT with generalized-symmetry data S must satisfy: (i) the OPE coefficients factor through S-equivariant tensor products; (ii) the partition function transforms under S's modular / cobordism class; (iii) the operator spectrum respects S-graded multiplet structure. These constraints narrow the bootstrap-allowed region within the sector with fixed S. Komargodski-Sulejmanpasic-Unsal 2017 made the constraint precise for 4D pure YM: the Z_N center 1-form anomaly rules out trivially-gapped phases. Subsequent work (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator; Brennan-Cordova; Su et al.) extended to other dimensions and symmetry types. Status established. Subtype `constrains` (v16). This is the SECOND authored use of `constrains` after MTC→bootstrap (v16). The constrains-relation here is genuinely distinct from any specializes / derives-from reading: the symmetry data doesn't IDENTIFY a sub-case of bootstrap CFTs; it RESTRICTS the form of any CFT realized with that symmetry data.
citations: Komargodski, Sulejmanpasic, Unsal 2017, Phys. Rev. B 97, 054418
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184
Brennan, Cordova 2020, JHEP 02, 132 (anomalies and constraints)
Su, Wang, You 2021, JHEP 02, 002 (anomaly matching constraints for bootstrap)

all_edges.description: Modular tensor categories specialize generalized symmetries to the 2D rational case. The Moore-Seiberg axiom data of an MTC IS the data of a 3D SymTFT bulk for a 2D rational CFT. Equivalently: in 2D, the categorical-symmetry framework (cell-gs-d2-p0-categorical) reduces to MTC structure when the boundary is a rational CFT. The hierarchy: generalized-symmetries ⊃ categorical-symmetries-in-2D ⊃ MTC (rational case). cell_refs pin 4 representative MTCs (Fibonacci, Ising, toric code, SU(2)_3) whose SymTFT bulks are RT-TQFTs with these MTCs as input.
citations: Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022 (the connection between MTCs and SymTFT explicitly)
Kapustin, Saulina 2010, arXiv:1012.0911 (surface operators ↔ MTC)
Petkova, Zuber 2001, Phys. Lett. B 504, 157 (Verlinde lines as 2D categorical sym)

all_edges.description: The ten-fold way specializes generalized symmetries: ten-fold-way SPT phases are classified by their generalized-symmetry data including anti-unitary (time-reversal, particle-hole) symmetries treated as generalized symmetries with cobordism-class anomalies. Each ten-fold-way class corresponds to a specific Pin or Spin or Spin^c cobordism class in the bulk SymTFT (Kapustin 2014; Freed-Hopkins 2021). The specializes-relation: the ten-fold-way is the sub-case of generalized-symmetries focused on free fermionic SPT phases with the ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes. cell_refs pin 5 representative ten-fold-way cells whose generalized-symmetry data is canonically derivable.
citations: Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467 (cobordism-theoretic derivation of ten-fold way)
Kapustin, Thorngren, Turzillo, Wang 2015, JHEP 12, 052 (fermionic SPT and generalized cohomology)
Wang, Senthil 2014, Phys. Rev. B 89, 195124 (generalized symmetries in topological insulators)

all_edges.description: Top-down holographic / F-theory compactifications aspire to derive SM rep content from generalized-symmetry data of the bulk SymTFT. The compactification carries discrete higher-form symmetry data (Apruzzi et al. 2023; Garcia-Etxebarria-Heidenreich-Regalado 2022); the boundary 4D QFT inherits both gauge content (sm-rep-content) and higher-form symmetry structure (generalized-symmetries) jointly from the bulk. AdS/CFT aspires this derivation: SM rep content as a boundary-projection of bulk SymTFT data. cell_refs pin all 15 SM matter cells (the source-side data the aspired derivation would produce). Distinct from the v15.7 sm-equiv-tenfold aspired edge (which targets only tenfold-way topological data, not the full generalized-symmetry framework).
citations: Apruzzi, Bonetti, Garcia, Saxena, Heckman, Schafer-Nameki 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895
Garcia-Etxebarria, Heidenreich, Regalado 2022, JHEP 09, 130 (higher-form symmetries from string theory)
Heckman, Hubner, Torres 2024, JHEP 04, 113 (generalized symmetries from F-theory)

all_edges.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches specialize the bootstrap exclusion regions: 4D N=2 SCFTs are a specialization of bootstrap-allowed 4D CFTs, specifically those with N=2 superconformal symmetry. The bootstrap exclusion bounds for 4D N=2 SCFTs (e.g., the bounds on central charges and OPE coefficients in the chiral algebra protected sector) form a subset of the broader 4D bootstrap region. The Higgs/Coulomb branch structure is the moduli-space classification of THESE specific bootstrap-allowed theories. cell_refs pin 5 representative Coulomb branch cells whose corresponding SCFTs sit in the bootstrap-allowed region: SU(2) N_f=4, A_1 Argyres-Douglas, E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky, T_3 class-S, N=4 SU(2). The bootstrap entry's cell-bs-d4-N2-SCFT is the canonical bootstrap-side anchor.
citations: Beem, Rastelli, van Rees 2014, JHEP 05, 020 (4D N=2 bootstrap)
Lemos, van Rees 2016, JHEP 12, 040 (chiral algebra bootstrap)
Beem 2024 review (bootstrap-SCFT-VOA connections)

all_edges.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches are categorically-equivalent to modular tensor categories via the SCFT/VOA correspondence (Beem-Lemos-Liendo-Peelaers-Rastelli 2015): the Schur sector of any 4D N=2 SCFT is a 2D vertex operator algebra, and for rational SCFTs whose VOA is rational, this VOA is an MTC. The equivalence is partial because the VOAs arising from 4D N=2 SCFTs are not always rational — Argyres-Douglas theories give non-unitary or non-rational VOAs that lie outside the standard MTC framework. cell_refs pin 4 representative cells where the SCFT/VOA correspondence is well-studied: A_1 AD (Lee-Yang-like VOA), E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky (VOA on a specific exceptional Lie algebra root system), T_3 (specific known VOA), N=4 SU(2) (rational VOA). Status: partial — established for the rational-VOA sub-case; aspired/under-development for non-rational cases.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli 2015, Commun. Math. Phys. 336, 1359
Bonetti, Meneghelli, Rastelli 2018, JHEP 12, 152 (rational VOAs from AD theories)
Beem 2024 review

all_edges.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches of class-S and Minahan-Nemeschansky theories DERIVE FROM ADE Lie algebra data: the class-S construction (Gaiotto 2009) takes an ADE Lie algebra g and a punctured Riemann surface Σ as input, and outputs a 4D N=2 SCFT T[g, Σ] whose Higgs/Coulomb branches are determined by (g, Σ, puncture-types). The Minahan-Nemeschansky E_{6,7,8} theories have Higgs branches equal to the minimal nilpotent orbits of e_{6,7,8}. Status: partial — the derivation works rigorously for class-S theories of types ADE and for the M-N exceptional theories, but not all 4D N=2 SCFTs admit a class-S construction (e.g., generic non-class-S Lagrangian theories don't fit). cell_refs pin 7 representative cells whose branch data is class-S- or M-N-derived from ADE input data.
citations: Gaiotto 2009, JHEP 08, 034
Tachikawa 2014, Lecture Notes (class-S review)
Cremonesi, Hanany, Mekareeya, Zaffaroni 2014, JHEP 09, 178 (Coulomb branches of class-S)
Moore, Tachikawa 2012, Surv. Diff. Geom. 18, 129 (Higgs branches as affine Grassmannian slices)

all_edges.description: Generalized symmetries of 4D N=2 SCFTs structurally constrain the Higgs/Coulomb branch structure. The constraints: (i) 0-form flavor symmetries act on Higgs branches as isometries of the hyperkähler structure, narrowing the allowed flavor groups for each branch; (ii) 1-form center symmetries (Bhardwaj-Tachikawa 2017) constrain which gauge structures admit conformal-fixed-point UV completions; (iii) 2-group structures (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2019) impose mixed-anomaly conditions between flavor 0-form and gauge 1-form sectors that restrict allowable branch decompositions. The result: generalized-symmetry anomaly matching is one of the most powerful tools for ruling out candidate Higgs/Coulomb branch structures. Status: partial — established for many specific cases (4D N=2 SCFTs with classifiable flavor symmetries; Bhardwaj-Tachikawa 1-form constraints; Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2-group constraints); general framework still developing. Subtype `constrains` (the v16 subtype) — this is the THIRD authored use of `constrains` and the FIRST that does not point at bootstrap. cell_refs pin 3 source-side generalized-symmetry cells representing the 0-form flavor, 1-form center, and 2-group structures that act as constraints on HCB.
citations: Bhardwaj, Tachikawa 2017, JHEP 03, 189 (1-form center symmetries)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (2-group symmetries in N=2)
Argyres, Martone 2022, JHEP 05, 030 (generalized-symmetry classification of rank-2 N=2 SCFTs)

all_edges.description: Tensor-network program aspires that SymTFT (generalized-symmetry) bulk structures are computationally realizable as compositional outputs of MTC data combined with explicit tensor-network construction machinery (matrix product operators, PEPS, string-nets). Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] is compositional output of MTC [input] combined with tensor-network construction-data'. Literature-strength: STRONG. Sub-cases established: matrix product operator (MPO) categorical symmetries from MTC input have been explicitly constructed for the Z_2 toric code, doubled Ising, and several rational MTCs (Bridgeman-Williamson 2018; Lootens-Fuchs-Haegeman-Schweigert-Verstraete 2021). The Levin-Wen string-net model directly realizes the SymTFT bulk for any rational MTC input. Aspired for: full classification of SymTFT structures across all MTCs at higher rank, continuous-symmetry generalizations, non-Lagrangian SymTFT realizations. Status `partial` because specific cases work; full program is aspired. `targeted_by: tensor-network` records the program actively extending. cell_refs pin 4 representative MTCs whose tensor-network SymTFT realizations are either established (toric code, Ising) or actively pursued (doubled-Ising for Levin-Wen, Fibonacci for universal anyon TQC). Multi-edge with v17 edge `modular-tensor-categories specializes generalized-symmetries [established]`: the established specializes-edge captures the categorical-inclusion (MTC is a sub-case); this composition-edge captures the operational construction (tensor-networks realize SymTFT from MTC input).
citations: Bridgeman, Williamson 2018, Phys. Rev. B 97, 144439 (matrix product operator symmetries)
Lootens, Fuchs, Haegeman, Schweigert, Verstraete 2021, SciPost Phys. 10, 053 (MPO symmetries and intertwiners)
Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 (string-net condensation — SymTFT bulk for rational MTC)
Aasen, Mong, Fendley 2016, J. Phys. A 49, 354001 (Ising categorical symmetry on lattice)

all_edges.description: rg-info program aspires that SymTFT (generalized-symmetry) data derives from bootstrap CFT data via RG-flow continuation: tracking a UV bootstrap-allowed CFT down to the IR fixed point, the symmetry data (anomalies, higher-form charges, categorical structures) is preserved or transforms in controlled ways, ultimately determining the IR phase's SymTFT classification. Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] derives from bootstrap [UV input] via RG flow'. Same UV-IR direction as the v15.7 rg-info edge `cobordism → bootstrap [derives-from, aspired]`. Literature-strength: MEDIUM. Sub-cases established: the C-theorem and a-theorem (Zamolodchikov 1986; Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) establish RG-monotonicity of central charges, which constrains the flow of bootstrap-allowed CFTs. Cordova-Dumitrescu 2-group analyses show that 2-group anomalies flow through RG with specific matching conditions, partially deriving SymTFT data from UV bootstrap data. Aspired for: general derivation of SymTFT classification (especially non-invertible / categorical cases) from bootstrap UV data alone. Status `partial` reflects this: the C/A-theorem case works rigorously, but the generalization to full categorical SymTFT classification is aspired. `targeted_by: rg-info` records the program. cell_refs pin 4 SymTFT cells whose UV-IR derivation from bootstrap is actively studied. Multi-edge with v17 edge `generalized-symmetries constrains cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions [established]`: the constrains-edge says SymTFT anomaly DATA narrows bootstrap-allowed CFTs (structural constraint); this derives-from-edge says SymTFT FRAMEWORK (the classification itself) is OPERATIONALLY obtained from RG-flow analysis of bootstrap CFTs. Distinct content; same FC-pair.
citations: Zamolodchikov 1986, JETP Lett. 43, 730 (c-theorem)
Komargodski, Schwimmer 2011, JHEP 12, 099 (a-theorem)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (RG flows of 2-group anomalies)
Apenko 2012, Physica A 391, 62 (information-theoretic RG)
Beny, Osborne 2015, New J. Phys. 17, 083005 (RG as quantum-information renormalization)

all_edges.description: ncg-instance program aspires that SymTFT structures arise as compositional outputs of cobordism-K-theory data combined with noncommutative-geometric spectral-triple machinery. Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] is compositional output of cobordism [K-theoretic input] combined with NCG spectral-triple construction-data'. The NCG framework treats K-theoretic data of a spectral triple as encoding anomaly-inflow content; SymTFT treats cobordism-classified bulk theories as encoding anomaly-inflow content for boundary symmetries. NCG aspires that these two K-theoretic anomaly-inflow pictures are compositionally related — that a spectral triple's K-theoretic data combines with NCG-specific quantization data to produce the corresponding SymTFT bulk. Literature-strength: WEAK. The structural overlap (both frameworks land in K-theory of anomaly inflow) is REAL but no concrete spectral-triple ↔ SymTFT realization has been worked out in the literature I am aware of as of 2026. The Connes-Marcolli 2007 spectral-action analysis and the Freed-Moore-Teleman 2022 SymTFT framework have not been bridged in either direction. Authoring this edge as aspired with weak literature backing is a faithful record of the NCG program's structural position: NCG reaches the K-theoretic anomaly-inflow territory (which IS SymTFT-adjacent) but has not yet developed concrete SymTFT-mediated constructions. Status `aspired` (no sub-cases established, unlike the tensor-network and rg-info edges which are partial). `targeted_by: ncg-instance`. cell_refs pin 3 cobordism cells with structural significance for the aspired connection: Ω_2^{Spin^c} = ℤ (IQHE-class anomaly), Ω_4^{Spin} = ℤ (4D bulk anomaly), Ω_4^{Pin+} = ℤ_16 (Wang-Senthil fermionic gauge anomaly). Multi-edge with v17 edge `generalized-symmetries specializes freed-hopkins-cobordism [established]`: the established specializes-edge captures categorical inclusion (SymTFT bulks are cobordism-classified, established); this composition-edge captures the aspired compositional construction (cobordism data + NCG spectral-triple data → explicit SymTFT realizations, not yet developed).
citations: Connes, Marcolli 2007 (noncommutative geometry and SM as spectral triple — K-theoretic foundation)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (SymTFT topological symmetry framework — K-theoretic anomaly inflow)
Larsson 2014, J. Math. Phys. 55, 122102 (K-theory in NCG — partial bridge to anomaly-inflow K-theory)

all_edges.description: Hadronic states specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups: every hadron is an irreducible representation of the Poincaré group (specifying mass and spin J^P) together with internal symmetry quantum numbers. The classification axis J-PC reads directly off the Poincaré-representation little-group decomposition. cell_refs pin 6 representative hadron families whose Poincaré-representation content is canonical: pseudoscalars (0-+ ↔ massive scalar Poincaré rep with intrinsic parity), vectors (1-- ↔ vector Poincaré rep), tensors (2++ ↔ rank-2 symmetric Poincaré rep), spin-1/2 baryons, spin-3/2 baryons, and the J/ψ / Υ vector spectra. This is the cleanest phenomenon-to-structural-FC edge in the map: hadronic-states (phenomenon-FC, the actual particles) hangs on spacetime-symmetry-groups (structural-FC, the representation theory). Status established (the Poincaré-representation organization of hadrons is foundational physics).
citations: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149 (irreducible reps of Poincaré group)
Weinberg 1995, Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. I ch. 2 (Poincaré representations and particle states)

all_edges.description: Hadronic states derive from SM rep content via QCD binding dynamics: each hadron is a color-singlet bound state of quarks (and gluons) drawn from the SM quark content (cell-uR-g1, cell-dR-g1, etc. for light quarks; the charm and bottom cells for heavy hadrons). The DERIVATION is structurally established (color confinement + QCD Lagrangian → hadrons emerge as IR degrees of freedom) but QUANTITATIVELY partial: while quark content fully determines the existence and quantum numbers of light hadrons via SU(3) flavor classification, the precise masses, decay widths, form factors, and exotic-state spectra require non-perturbative QCD calculations (lattice QCD, effective field theories, quark-model approximations). Status `partial` reflects this: structural derivation is established, full quantitative derivation is partial. cell_refs pin 9 cells spanning light, charm, bottom, and exotic regimes — each derives from specific quark-content compositions.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343 (QCD asymptotic freedom)
Politzer 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1346 (independent QCD asymptotic freedom)
Wilson 1974, Phys. Rev. D 10, 2445 (confinement via lattice QCD)
Aoki et al. (FLAG) 2024, Eur. Phys. J. C 84, 897 (lattice flavor QCD review)

all_edges.description: Hadronic states specialize ADE Lie algebras via flavor SU(3) classification: light hadrons are organized into multiplets of A_2 = SU(3) flavor representations. The pseudoscalar nonet (cell-h-pi) is 8+1 of SU(3); the vector nonet (cell-h-rho) is also 8+1; the baryon octet (cell-h-N) is the 8 of SU(3); the baryon decuplet (cell-h-Delta) is the 10 of SU(3). This is the Gell-Mann eightfold-way classification: flavor SU(3) = A_2 Lie algebra representations classify the light hadron multiplets. The Ω- prediction (cell-h-Delta, see predictive_yield) was made by completing the 10-of-SU(3) and predicting the mass of the missing sss member — the most famous Mendeleev-style success in hadronic physics. Status established for light hadrons; the SU(3) classification breaks down once heavier quarks (c, b) are included (those require SU(4), SU(5) extensions or heavy-quark effective theory). cell_refs pin 4 SU(3)-multiplet cells.
citations: Gell-Mann 1962, Phys. Rev. 125, 1067 (eightfold way)
Ne'eman 1961, Nucl. Phys. 26, 222 (independent eightfold way)
Okubo 1962, Prog. Theor. Phys. 27, 949 (SU(3) mass formula)

all_edges.description: ads-cft-instance program aspires that hadronic-spectrum data derives from bootstrap-allowed CFT data: in the large-N limit of QCD (Polchinski, Strassler, Maldacena), the theory is approximately conformal at high energies, and the hadronic spectrum emerges as the spectrum of bound states in a holographic dual 5D AdS theory. The bootstrap-allowed CFT structure constrains which hadrons are realizable; conversely, hadron observations constrain bootstrap-derived holographic-QCD models. Established for: large-N pure-glue holographic QCD glueball-spectrum predictions (Hashimoto-Tahara 1998; Brower-Mathur-Tan 2000) qualitatively match lattice glueball spectrum. Aspired for: full hadronic-spectrum derivation including chiral-symmetry-breaking patterns and exotic-state predictions from holographic QCD. cell_refs pin 4 hadrons whose holographic-QCD derivations are actively pursued. Status aspired; the program is real and active but the full hadronic-spectrum-from-bootstrap derivation remains incomplete.
citations: Polchinski, Strassler 2002, Phys. Rev. Lett. 88, 031601 (string theory and hard scattering)
Hashimoto, Tahara 1998 (early holographic QCD)
Brower, Mathur, Tan 2000, Nucl. Phys. B 587, 249 (glueball spectrum from holography)
Erlich, Katz, Son, Stephanov 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 261602 (QCD and a holographic model of hadrons)

all_edges.description: Each FQH state is categorically-equivalent to a specific modular tensor category — the MTC that describes its anyon content. The correspondence (Kitaev 2006; Wen 2017): the topological order of an FQH state is fully captured by its MTC (containing the anyon types, fusion rules, braiding statistics, and modular S/T matrices). Established mappings: ν=1/3 Laughlin ↔ Z_3 abelian MTC (cell-mtc-r3-z3); ν=1/5 Laughlin ↔ Z_5 MTC; ν=2/5 Jain ↔ specific abelian MTC of rank 5; ν=5/2 Pfaffian-candidate ↔ Ising MTC (cell-mtc-r3-ising); ν=12/5 Read-Rezayi candidate ↔ Z_3 parafermion / Fibonacci MTC (cell-mtc-r2-fibonacci). Status `partial` reflects: (i) for cleanly-identified states (1/3, 2/5, Laughlin family), the MTC identification is established; (ii) for states with disputed identification (5/2 between Pfaffian/anti-Pfaffian/PH-Pfaffian; 12/5 RR identification), the MTC correspondence is partial pending experimental resolution. cell_refs pin 6 representative FQH cells; the target-side MTC cells are referenced in narrative (no schema field currently exists for cell-to-cell mapping; surfaces v20 candidate `paired_cell_refs`). THIS IS THE CENTRAL PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE OF THE MAP — demonstrating that the structural backbone hooks cleanly into phenomenon-level data when the correspondence exists.
citations: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (anyons and FQH)
Wen 2017, Rev. Mod. Phys. 89, 041004 (topological order review)
Bonderson, Slingerland 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 125323 (12/5 MTC identification analysis)

all_edges.description: MTC consistency conditions structurally constrain which FQH states can exist. Specifically: (i) the modularity (non-degeneracy of S-matrix) requires the anyon content of any FQH state to form a complete MTC; (ii) the pentagon-hexagon coherence conditions on fusion / braiding data constrain quasiparticle interactions; (iii) the Vafa theorem constrains the central charge of edge CFTs; (iv) the genus-g ground-state degeneracy is determined by the MTC rank. Together these MTC consistency conditions narrow the allowed space of FQH topological orders — many naively-plausible FQH wavefunctions are ruled out because their would-be anyon content fails to form a consistent MTC. **FOURTH `constrains` EDGE; SECOND CONSECUTIVE NOT POINTING AT BOOTSTRAP** (HCB v18 was the first non-bootstrap target). The v18-refined finding holds: `constrains` targets exclusion-region-style classifications regardless of node identity. FQH-states is an exclusion-region-style phenomenon-FC (allowed topological orders constrained by structural consistency). Status `established`. cell_refs pin 4 MTC cells whose constraints on FQH states are canonical examples. **SIXTH MULTI-EDGE FC PAIR**: paired with the categorically-equivalent/partial edge above. Different content: cat-equiv says each FQH state maps to an MTC; constrains says MTC structure narrows what FQH states are realizable.
citations: Wen 1990, Phys. Rev. B 41, 12838 (early topological-order constraints)
Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (MTC structure of anyons)
Lan, Kong, Wen 2017, Phys. Rev. B 95, 235140 (classification of 2+1D topological orders via MTC)

all_edges.description: FQH states derive from generalized-symmetry data: the 2+1D Chern-Simons effective theory of an FQH state IS a SymTFT bulk realization, with the 1-form symmetries acting on Wilson loops corresponding to the anyon types. Specifically, abelian FQH states (Laughlin, Jain) have abelian 1-form symmetries directly readable off their K-matrix; non-abelian FQH states (Pfaffian, Read-Rezayi) have categorical 1-form symmetries forming non-trivial fusion categories. The derivation is established for the abelian case (Wen's K-matrix formulation IS a SymTFT formulation); partial for non-abelian / categorical cases where the SymTFT-to-FQH map is being developed. Status `partial`. cell_refs pin 4 FQH cells whose SymTFT derivation is canonical. Connects this phenomenon-FC to the structural SymTFT framework via its bulk Chern-Simons description.
citations: Wen 1995, Adv. Phys. 44, 405 (K-matrix formulation; ESSENTIALLY a SymTFT for abelian FQH)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (SymTFT framework)
Bhardwaj, Schäfer-Nameki 2024, Phys. Rep. 1051, 1 (generalized symmetries review covering FQH applications)

all_edges.description: ads-cft-instance program aspires that FQH topological orders derive from bootstrap-allowed CFT data: the (1+1)D edge theories of FQH states ARE conformal field theories, and the bootstrap-allowed-CFT region constrains which edge CFTs (and hence which bulk FQH topological orders) can exist. Specifically: edge CFTs at Laughlin ν=1/m are c=1 chiral bosons; edge CFTs at Pfaffian 5/2 include a Majorana sector; edge CFTs at Read-Rezayi 12/5 are Z_3 parafermion CFTs. Each of these is a specific bootstrap-allowed CFT; bootstrap constraints on these CFTs structurally constrain FQH topological orders. AdS/CFT framing: a 2+1D bulk topological order corresponds to a 1+1D boundary CFT under the bulk-boundary correspondence. Status `aspired` — the bulk-boundary correspondence is established for specific cases but full derivation of FQH classification from bootstrap is aspirational. cell_refs pin 3 FQH cells with the cleanest edge-CFT realizations.
citations: Wen 1990, Phys. Rev. B 41, 12838 (FQH edge as chiral CFT)
Cappelli, Trugenberger, Zemba 1993, Nucl. Phys. B 396, 465 (W_1+infinity algebra of FQH edges)

all_edges.description: Every compact astrophysical object is a (near-)solution to Einstein's field equations and therefore specializes the cell-stsym-diff cell (general-coordinate / diffeomorphism invariance) of spacetime-symmetry-groups. Asymptotically-flat compact objects (which is the standard case for isolated WDs, NSs, stellar-mass BHs, and most galactic SMBHs to good approximation) additionally have asymptotic Poincaré invariance — specializing cell-stsym-poincare at the asymptotic-region level. Within the strong-field interior, the symmetry reduces: Schwarzschild BHs and non-rotating NSs have SO(3) × time-translation; Kerr BHs and rotating NSs have axisymmetry SO(2) × time-translation; merging binaries have no continuous spatial symmetry. cell_refs pin 6 representative compact-object cells whose Poincaré + diff-invariance specialization is canonical. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE ASTROPHYSICS SECTOR. Status `established` — the GR description of compact objects is foundational physics, tested at the percent level by EHT shadow imaging, BBH-ringdown spectroscopy, and pulsar-timing tests. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Schwarzschild 1916, Sitzungsber. Preuss. Akad. Wiss. (vacuum spherical solution)
Kerr 1963, Phys. Rev. Lett. 11, 237 (rotating BH solution)
Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV equation for NSs)
Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests review)
EHT Collaboration 2019, 2022 (BH shadow tests of Kerr metric)

all_edges.description: Neutron-star interiors derive from hadronic-states physics extended to the dense-uniform-matter regime. Specifically: (i) the outer-crust nuclear-matter composition is direct hadronic spectroscopy at low density (nuclei populated from the chart-of-nuclides, a subset of cell-h-N and adjacent baryon cells); (ii) the inner-crust + outer-core composition (npe + μ matter) is determined by hadronic-state thresholds (when does hyperon population become favorable? — depends on the Λ, Σ, Ξ hyperon thresholds from cell-h-Lambdac-style spectra extrapolated to finite chemical potential, the 'hyperon puzzle'); (iii) inner-core composition possibly involves color-superconducting quark matter or a hadron-quark crossover region — connecting hadronic-states to (presumed-future) dense-quark-matter classification. Status `partial` because: NS-interior physics involves hadronic matter at conditions (high density, low T, finite μ_B and μ_Q) that are FAR outside the standard hadronic-states domain (T ≈ 0, μ ≈ 0, bound-state spectroscopy). The connection is real but mediated by EOS-modeling that extrapolates from spectroscopic input. cell_refs pin 3 NS-related cells whose hadronic-derivation is most operationally active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (connection well-developed for low-density crust; speculative for inner-core quark-matter component).
citations: Lattimer, Prakash 2016, Phys. Rep. 621, 127 (NS observations and dense-matter physics)
Glendenning 2000, Compact Stars (hadronic NS interior)
Alford et al. 2005, Nature 445, E7 (color-superconducting quark matter in NS cores)
Baym, Hatsuda, Kojo, Powell, Song, Takatsuka 2018, Rep. Prog. Phys. 81, 056902 (quark-hadron crossover review)

all_edges.description: Compact-object matter content derives from SM rep content via the matter species that populate compact objects: white dwarfs are supported by electron-degeneracy pressure (cell-eR-g1 and corresponding lepton cells of sm-rep-content) embedded in an ionic Coulomb lattice (light nuclei drawn from QCD-bound states of light quarks — cell-uR-g1, cell-dR-g1). Neutron stars are supported by nucleon degeneracy + nuclear-force repulsion; their composition involves neutrons (n = udd), protons (p = uud), electrons (β-equilibrium), and at higher density possibly muons, hyperons, kaons. The 'derives-from' is straightforward but partial in the sense that the dense-matter-physics machinery that maps SM rep content to compact-object pressure-support is itself a layer of physics not represented in sm-rep-content directly (the Chandrasekhar mass for WDs, the TOV maximum mass for NSs both DEPEND on SM matter content but require the EOS-modeling layer to be evaluated). cell_refs pin 3 WD/NS cells whose SM-content basis is most direct. The hadronic-states edge (separate) handles the bound-state-spectrum connection; this edge handles the underlying-SM-particle connection. Literature strength: STRONG for WDs (electron-degeneracy physics is canonical); MEDIUM for NSs (nuclear-force modeling is empirically less well-constrained at NS-interior densities).
citations: Chandrasekhar 1931, Astrophys. J. 74, 81 (Chandrasekhar mass from electron rep content + GR)
Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV from nucleon rep content + GR)
Shapiro, Teukolsky 1983, Black Holes, White Dwarfs, and Neutron Stars chs. 2-3

all_edges.description: The ads-cft-instance program aspires that black-hole thermodynamic and microstate-structure properties derive from bootstrap-allowed CFT data via the AdS/CFT holographic dictionary. Specifically: (i) Strominger-Vafa 1996 derived the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy of specific BPS BHs by counting BPS states in the dual CFT — a partial derivation establishing the program's empirical bite for a special class of BHs (extremal, supersymmetric, mostly AdS); (ii) general astrophysical (Kerr, non-extremal, asymptotically-flat) BHs are not yet derived from CFT in this sense — that's the aspired part; (iii) the Page-curve / island-formula program (Almheiri et al. 2019+) extends the bootstrap-CFT-derivation of BH thermodynamics to evaporating BHs in specific holographic setups, with partial bite for the information paradox. cell_refs pin 3 BH cells where the holographic-bootstrap derivation is most actively pursued (SMBHs because of their thermodynamic importance; merging stellar-mass BHs because of GW horizon-test relevance). Status `aspired` because the bridge from holographic-CFT data to astrophysical BH observations is still under construction. Literature strength: STRONG for the BPS-BH entropy-counting (foundational); WEAK for general astrophysical BH derivation (program is real and active but most claims remain at gestural-to-partial level for astrophysical applicability). This edge mirrors the FQH→bootstrap-aspired and hadrons→bootstrap-aspired edges in the existing data (same ads-cft program, different phenomenon-FC targets).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BPS-BH entropy from CFT counting)
Maldacena 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (AdS/CFT correspondence)
Witten 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 253 (AdS/CFT thermodynamics)
Almheiri, Engelhardt, Marolf, Maxfield 2019, JHEP 12, 063 (island formula); Penington 2020, JHEP 09, 002 (page curve)

all_edges.description: Each material whose symmetry-class axis value lies in the strict tenfold-way (A, AIII, AI, BDI, D, DIII, AII, CII, C, CI) specializes the corresponding tenfold-way cell (cell-{class}-d{dimension} in tenfold-way's coordinate scheme). HgTe and 1T'-WTe₂ specialize cell-AII-d2; Bi₂Se₃-family, Sb₂Te₃, Bi₂Te₃ specialize cell-AII-d3; FeTe/Se specializes cell-DIII-d3; InSb/Al wire specializes cell-D-d1; predicted class-CI 3D specializes cell-CI-d3 (currently empty in tenfold-way's material-realization sense). THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR TOPOLOGICAL-MATTER. The tenfold-way cells defined formally; this FC's cells are specific materials; the edge is `specializes` because each material IS a particular point in a tenfold-way cell's phase space. Status `established` — the tenfold-way classification is a settled mathematical theorem (Kitaev 2009 K-theory derivation), and the material identifications are unambiguous within the noninteracting domain. Literature strength: STRONG. Materials with crystalline / magnetic / higher-order refinement labels are not directly captured by this edge — those go through subsequent edges to crystalline-symmetry-extension classifications or to freed-hopkins-cobordism.
citations: Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125 (tenfold-way derivation)
Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (K-theory / Bott periodicity)
Ryu, Schnyder, Furusaki, Ludwig 2010, New J. Phys. 12, 065010 (extended table)

all_edges.description: The Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification of SPT phases (Freed-Hopkins 2021 final paper; earlier conjectures from Kapustin 2014 and Freed 2014) gives an extended classification of symmetry-protected topological phases that subsumes the tenfold-way for free fermions and extends to interacting systems. Each material's SPT class corresponds to a cobordism-invariant of its symmetry group: time-reversal protected TIs in class AII map to ΩPin⁺_{d+1}(pt) classes; magnetic-symmetry-protected phases (axion insulators) map to magnetic-cobordism classes; higher-order phases involve crystalline-extension cobordism groups. Status `partial` because: (i) the noninteracting tenfold-way → cobordism map is fully understood and provides the framework for noninteracting TIs / TSCs; (ii) the interacting extension via cobordism is well-developed in principle but very few material candidates exist for the interacting-only SPT phases that distinguish cobordism from the tenfold-way (fractional TIs are the prime target, none confirmed); (iii) crystalline extensions of cobordism (Thorngren-Else 2018 etc.) have classification machinery but sparse material realization. cell_refs pin 5 cells of variable SPT-cobordism relevance: HgTe and Bi₂Se₃-family are noninteracting AII → trivially captured by free-fermion cobordism, but listed because the cobordism framework provides their classification at the most general level; Bi hoti and MnBi₂Te₄ have crystalline / magnetic refinements that REQUIRE cobordism beyond tenfold-way; fractional TI is the canonical predicted target of interacting cobordism classification. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework, MEDIUM for direct material applicability beyond noninteracting cases.
citations: Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1404.6659 (cobordism approach to SPTs)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (final cobordism classification of invertible TQFTs)
Thorngren, Else 2018, Phys. Rev. X 8, 011040 (crystalline SPT cobordism)

all_edges.description: The generalized-symmetries / SymTFT framework (Gaiotto-Kapustin-Seiberg-Willett 2015 and subsequent) classifies symmetries by their topological-defect content: 0-form, 1-form, and higher-form symmetries; ordinary group symmetries and categorical symmetries; T-folds and crystalline-symmetry extensions of these. Several topological-matter phases are protected by symmetries that go beyond on-site 0-form group symmetries: (i) HOTIs (cell-hoti-bismuth) and HOTSCs (cell-predicted-higher-order-DIII-TSC) are protected by crystalline rotational / mirror symmetries that act on extended subspaces and have been recast in SymTFT framework; (ii) axion-insulator phases (cell-axion-MnBi2Te4-candidate, cell-predicted-intrinsic-axion-clean) involve combined T × τ (time-reversal × half-translation) symmetry whose SymTFT analysis is recent and partial; (iii) the recently-explored 'higher-symmetry-protected' interpretation of some HOTI phases connects to higher-form-symmetry phenomenology. Status `partial` because the SymTFT framework for crystalline / magnetic topological-matter classification is actively developing — the framework applies in principle but the systematic mapping is still under construction (Thorngren-Else 2018 onwards). cell_refs pin 4 cells where the SymTFT / generalized-symmetry classification matters most operationally. Literature strength: MEDIUM (real framework with growing applicability; not all topological-matter cells need it).
citations: Gaiotto, Kapustin, Seiberg, Willett 2015, JHEP 02, 172 (generalized global symmetries)
Thorngren, Else 2018, Phys. Rev. X 8, 011040 (crystalline gauging)
Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 111601 (non-invertible symmetries and SymTFT)

all_edges.description: The boundary anyon content of 2D topological superconductors is classified by modular tensor categories. Specifically: a 2D class D topological superconductor with a Majorana zero mode at a vortex core realizes the Ising MTC at the boundary defect level — the MZM is the σ anyon of the Ising MTC; vortex-MZM braiding implements non-Abelian Ising braiding rules. Class DIII 2D TSCs with helical Majorana edge modes have chiral central charge c = 1 from a pair of counter-propagating Majorana modes; the edge CFT is a free Majorana × Majorana. Class D 2D TSC chiral edge has c = 1/2. Higher-order DIII TSC hinge modes (cell-predicted-higher-order-DIII-TSC) carry helical Majorana modes whose braiding obeys MTC-classifiable rules at codimension-2 defects. Status `partial` because: (i) the MTC-classification of MZM-defect braiding is theoretically well-developed (Nayak-Simon-Stern-Freedman-Das Sarma 2008 review); (ii) experimental confirmation of MZM-Ising-MTC braiding remains incomplete — FeTe/Se signatures contested, the 2018 InSb/Al claim retracted, no clean braiding experiment confirmed as of 2026. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the MTC-derivation operates: the FeTe/Se candidate (Ising-MTC braiding aspired), the retracted InSb/Al cell (the falsified attempt to access Ising-MTC braiding), and the predicted higher-order DIII TSC. Literature strength: STRONG for the theory, WEAK for empirical realization. This edge is also a structural-data point: phenomenon-FC topological-matter connects to structural-FC MTC, completing a triangle with fractional-quantum-hall-states (which also connects to MTC, by categorically-equivalent — a different edge subtype reflecting that FQH states ARE MTC realizations whereas TSC-MZM-defects DERIVE-FROM MTC at the boundary).
citations: Nayak, Simon, Stern, Freedman, Das Sarma 2008, Rev. Mod. Phys. 80, 1083 (non-Abelian anyons and topological quantum computation)
Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (Honeycomb model — Ising MTC at the boundary of class D 2D TSC)
Read, Green 2000, Phys. Rev. B 61, 10267 (Pfaffian / Majorana edge mode)

all_edges.description: Neutrino-sector phenomenology specializes the leptonic sector of the SM rep content. Specifically: the three active neutrino flavors (ν_e, ν_μ, ν_τ) are the upper components of the SU(2)_L lepton doublets in cell-nuL-g1, cell-nuL-g2, cell-nuL-g3 of sm-rep-content. The PMNS mixing matrix is the lepton-sector analog of the CKM matrix; its parameterization follows directly from the structure of SM rep content + Yukawa coupling diagonalization. Right-handed neutrinos N_R required for type-I seesaw are gauge-singlet extensions of the SM rep content — predicted within the SM extension and naturally accommodated. Status `established` because the framework of SM-rep-content + PMNS mixing is the textbook understanding of neutrino phenomenology. Literature strength: STRONG. cell_refs pin the 7 cells of this FC most directly tied to SM rep content (the 4 measured oscillation parameters, the mass-squared differences, the Dirac-vs-Majorana question, and the seesaw mechanism which extends SM rep content with N_R). THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE NEUTRINO SECTOR.
citations: Pontecorvo 1957, Sov. Phys. JETP 6, 429 (early ν oscillation proposal)
Maki, Nakagawa, Sakata 1962, Prog. Theor. Phys. 28, 870 (PMNS matrix)
Mohapatra, Pal 2004, Massive Neutrinos in Physics and Astrophysics

all_edges.description: Neutrino-oscillation phenomenology specializes Poincaré invariance + CPT theorem of relativistic quantum mechanics. Specifically: (i) the oscillation phase φ = Δm² L / (2E) requires Lorentz-covariant time-evolution of mass eigenstates and is a direct prediction of Poincaré-invariant QM; (ii) CPT invariance constrains ν and antineutrino mixing matrices to be related (PMNS for ν vs PMNS* for antineutrino); (iii) leptonic CP violation (δ_CP non-trivial) is the symmetry-breaking effect — its measurement is a direct test of CP within the lepton sector, analogous to the CKM-sector CP violation already established. SN 1987A neutrino time-of-flight bounds are themselves a test of Lorentz invariance at the eV-mass scale. Status `established` because the Poincaré-invariance framework is settled physics and neutrino phenomenology specializes it directly. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG (depends on how stringently 'specializes' is read: framework-level specialization is strong; specific observational tests like Lorentz-violation bounds are partial).
citations: Bilenky, Pontecorvo 1978, Phys. Rep. 41, 225
Particle Data Group 2024 (CPT tests in the lepton sector)

all_edges.description: Grand-unification programs aspire that neutrino masses and mixings derive from GUT-scale rep content. Specifically: (i) in SO(10) GUTs, the 16-plet of SO(10) contains one right-handed neutrino per generation, naturally implementing seesaw; (ii) in SU(5) embeddings, N_R is an SU(5)-singlet but its Majorana mass scale M_R is naturally tied to GUT-symmetry-breaking scales; (iii) leptogenesis CP asymmetries arise from CP phases in the N_R Majorana mass matrix, which in SO(10) is linked to the same Yukawa matrices that give up-quark masses — connecting neutrino-CP to quark-sector observables. Status `aspired` because while the SO(10) / SU(5)-extension framework consistently accommodates neutrino-sector phenomenology, no specific GUT-scale prediction has been confirmed at terrestrial precision — the connection is at the framework level, not the parameter-by-parameter level. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework is well-established and SO(10) is a common embedding choice; specific predictions involving GUT-scale CP phases / Majorana masses await indirect tests via leptogenesis confirmation or proton-decay measurements that would also fix GUT scales). cell_refs pin the 3 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. This edge mirrors the su5-aspired derivation of proton-decay predictions and other neutrino-adjacent GUT observables.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw)
Buchmüller, Di Bari, Plümacher 2005, Ann. Phys. 315, 305 (leptogenesis review with GUT motivation)
Babu, Mohapatra 1993, Phys. Rev. Lett. 70, 2845 (predictions in SO(10) neutrino phenomenology)

all_edges.description: Each real critical system specializes a specific bootstrap-allowed CFT — the unique CFT at the corresponding Wilson-Fisher fixed point of the universality class. The bootstrap exclusion regions in cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions pin the critical-exponent values to high precision (3D-Ising ν = 0.6299709..., 3D O(2) ν = 0.6719..., 3D O(3) ν = 0.7117..., etc.); the real systems must lie on the bootstrap-allowed boundary curves. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE CRITICAL-PHENOMENA SECTOR, explicit in PROJECT_GOAL_PHENOMENON_LAYER §5. Status `established` for the universality-class identifications: every cell's universality class is settled. The exponent-value match is precision-confirmed for 3D-Ising and 3D-Heisenberg cells, but contains the 3D-O(2) / 4He tension at high precision (sub-edge-status note: the edge as a whole is `established` but contains a sub-tension cell). Literature strength: STRONG. cell_refs pin 8 cells where the bootstrap pairing is most operationally active.
citations: El-Showk, Paulos, Poland, Rychkov, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2014, JSTAT 8, P08015 (3D Ising bootstrap)
Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin 2014, JHEP 11, 109 (3D Ising precision)
Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 084017 (3D O(2))
Chester et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (3D O(3))

all_edges.description: At a second-order critical point, the relevant field theory has emergent scale invariance and (under broad conditions, in particular for unitary critical theories) full conformal invariance — i.e., the spacetime-symmetry-group enhances from the lattice / classical-Galilean symmetry of the bare microscopic theory to the conformal group SO(d+1,1) (or SO(d,2) in Lorentzian signature). This specializes the cell-stsym-conformal cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups. The 'emergent' qualifier matters: it's a long-wavelength enhancement, not a microscopic symmetry. Status `established` because conformal invariance at criticality is foundational (Polyakov 1970) and tested by the fact that bootstrap (which assumes conformal invariance) gives correct critical exponents. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG (framework is well-established; the conformal-invariance specialization is direct).
citations: Polyakov 1970, JETP Lett. 12, 381 (scale → conformal at criticality)
Cardy 1996, Scaling and Renormalization in Statistical Physics (Cambridge)

all_edges.description: Generalized symmetries / SymTFT framework identifies higher-form symmetries and topological defects in critical theories. Specifically: (i) 2D XY / BKT transition is driven by vortex (codimension-2 in 2D) unbinding — a 1-form symmetry breaking in the SymTFT framework; (ii) Ising / O(N) Wilson-Fisher CFTs have categorical-symmetry structure relevant for boundary / interface critical phenomena; (iii) the generalized-symmetry classification of critical theories is being actively developed (Choi-Cordova-Hsin-Lam-Shao 2022 and subsequent), with much of the structure relevant to surface and disorder-induced classes rather than bulk Wilson-Fisher. Status `partial` because the SymTFT framework applies in principle but the systematic re-derivation of all critical universality classes through SymTFT is incomplete. Literature strength: MEDIUM. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the generalized-symmetry structure most directly applies (2D XY / BKT vortex transition; 4He λ as 3D O(2); 2D Ising boundary structure).
citations: Gaiotto, Kapustin, Seiberg, Willett 2015, JHEP 02, 172
Choi, Cordova, Hsin, Lam, Shao 2022, Phys. Rev. Lett. 128, 111601
Komargodski, Sulejmanpasic, Unsal 2018, JHEP 12, 040 (SymTFT for symmetry-protected critical theories)

all_edges.description: 2D conformal field theories at criticality have rational-CFT structure (when applicable) classified by modular tensor categories. The 2D Ising CFT (c = 1/2, Onsager 1944) has the chiral half = Ising MTC, the same MTC that classifies anyons in 2D class D topological superconductors (cf. topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials edge derives-from MTC). The 2D BKT / 4He film transition is described by a c = 1 free-boson CFT with continuous vortex-operator spectrum — connects to MTC structure for compactified-boson radii at rational points. Status `partial` because: (i) the MTC-classification applies cleanly only to the 2D cells; 3D critical theories have different formal organizing structure (the 3D bootstrap, edge edge-xc-crit-specializes-bootstrap); (ii) the rational-CFT vs irrational-CFT distinction matters — only some 2D criticality has clean MTC structure. cell_refs pin the 2 cells where the MTC-derivation operates. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework, MEDIUM for direct application to physical critical systems. This edge mirrors the topological-insulator-and-superconductor-materials → MTC edge (v22) — both phenomenon-FCs derive their 2D-critical-boundary content from MTC.
citations: Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 241, 333 (2D CFT framework)
Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177 (MTC framework)
Read, Green 2000, Phys. Rev. B 61, 10267 (Ising-MTC at boundary of 2D class D TSC)

all_edges.description: Particle DM candidates derive from extensions of SM rep content. Specifically: (i) WIMPs in SUSY scenarios derive from the supersymmetric extension of the SM (neutralino = mixture of bino + wino + higgsinos, all in SM-gauge-group representations extended by superpartners); (ii) QCD axion couples to SM gauge bosons via anomaly, derives from PQ-extension of SM with axion as Goldstone of broken PQ U(1); (iii) sterile neutrinos are gauge-singlet right-handed neutrinos, extending the SM rep content with N_R; (iv) dark photon is a kinetic-mixing extension of SM U(1)_Y with U(1)' (mediated by ε F_μν F'^μν); (v) gravitino-LSP is the spin-3/2 superpartner of graviton in SUSY-extended supergravity. Status `partial` because: (a) some candidates (PBHs, MACHOs, fuzzy-DM-from-stringy-axiverse) do not derive from SM rep content — PBHs are gravitational, MACHOs are baryonic-compact, fuzzy-DM derives from stringy / cosmological structures beyond SM; (b) for the candidates that do derive from SM extensions, the derivation is conjectural at the level of specific BSM-extension predictions. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the SM-rep derivation operates most directly. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR PARTICLE-DM CANDIDATES. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (DM-as-BSM-particle is a major program); MEDIUM for specific candidate predictions.
citations: Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (DM-from-BSM-SM framework)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996, Phys. Rep. 267, 195 (SUSY-LSP DM review)
Holdom 1986, Phys. Lett. B 166, 196 (dark-photon kinetic mixing)

all_edges.description: All DM candidates — particle or compact-object — are gravitating matter satisfying Poincaré invariance + diffeomorphism invariance. For particle candidates, the connection is via standard quantum-field theory on a Poincaré-invariant background. For compact-object candidates (PBHs, MACHOs), the connection is more direct: each PBH or MACHO is a GR solution (Schwarzschild / Kerr for PBHs; ordinary stellar-mass solutions for MACHOs) and therefore specializes the spacetime-symmetry-groups cells in the same sense as compact-astrophysical-objects (v21). Fuzzy DM specializes Poincaré invariance via its bosonic-field action on Minkowski background. Status `established` — GR + Poincaré framework is settled and DM candidates satisfy it as a baseline. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework-level specialization; specific tests of Lorentz-invariance for DM candidates are limited).
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests overview)
Carr, Kühnel 2020 (PBHs as GR solutions)

all_edges.description: Several DM candidates aspire to derive from GUT-extended frameworks: (i) SUSY-LSP WIMPs are naturally embedded in GUT-extended SUSY (where the GUT-symmetry-breaking scale sets the SUSY-breaking-mediation scale); (ii) sterile-ν as N_R derives most naturally from SO(10) 16-plet which contains N_R as a singlet under SU(5) ⊂ SO(10) — gives keV-scale sterile-ν via seesaw at high scales; (iii) gravitino-LSP-DM connects to SUSY-breaking-mediation which is most naturally embedded in GUT frameworks; (iv) string-axiverse ALPs derive from string-theoretic compactifications that include GUT-style gauge structure. Status `aspired` because no specific GUT-derivation has been observationally confirmed; the connections are at the framework level. cell_refs pin 4 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM. This edge mirrors the neutrino-sector-phenomenology v23 edge derives-from su5-gut-rep-content — both phenomenon-FCs target su5/GUT as the most-natural high-scale derivation framework.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw → sterile-ν DM)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996 (SUSY-LSP in GUT frameworks)
Arvanitaki et al. 2010 (string axiverse)

all_edges.description: Cosmological observations specialize the FRW (Friedmann-Robertson-Walker) solutions of general relativity, which specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups. Specifically: (i) FRW cosmology is a specific class of GR solutions with maximally-symmetric spatial slices (specializes cell-stsym-maximal-isotropy or similar in spacetime-symmetry-groups); (ii) the CMB acoustic-peak structure is sensitive to the global FRW expansion + GR perturbation theory; (iii) the BAO sound-horizon is a clean GR-encoded length scale; (iv) spatial curvature Ω_K bounds the deviation from flat FRW; (v) the Hubble constant measures the FRW expansion rate; (vi) primordial gravitational waves are tensor modes of the FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — FRW-cosmology + GR-perturbation-theory is the foundational framework for cosmology. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR COSMOLOGY.
citations: Friedmann 1922; Robertson 1935; Walker 1937 (FRW framework)
Mukhanov 2005, Physical Foundations of Cosmology
Weinberg 2008, Cosmology

all_edges.description: Several cosmological observables derive directly from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BBN-era nuclear physics involves SM-content nucleons (proton, neutron) and SM-content nuclear-physics cross-sections — primordial D/H, ⁴He/H, ⁷Li/H are computed from SM particle physics + BBN framework; (ii) recombination-epoch atomic physics (the optical-depth τ_reio and the acoustic-oscillation damping) involves SM-content electrons + protons; (iii) the cosmological Σm_ν bound depends on SM neutrino content; (iv) CMB-acoustic-peak heights depend on SM neutrino effective number N_eff (Planck constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with 3 SM neutrinos). Status `partial` because: (a) some cosmological observables depend on BSM physics that the SM-rep-content FC doesn't capture (DM candidates, DE candidates); (b) the BBN nuclear cross-sections are partly experimentally-measured rather than derived. cell_refs pin 5 cells where SM-rep derivation is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (BBN, recombination, neutrino-decoupling are all SM-derived).
citations: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN from SM physics)
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (BBN review)
Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020 (N_eff measurement)

all_edges.description: The ads-cft-instance program targets bootstrap-CFT derivation of cosmological observables through dS/CFT correspondence (Strominger 2001) and the cosmological-bootstrap program (Arkani-Hamed-Maldacena 2015; Pajer et al. 2020+).
