Specifically: (i) inflationary correlators (CMB anisotropy, primordial non-Gaussianity, primordial GW spectrum) should derive from a CFT-correlator on the boundary of de Sitter via dS/CFT; (ii) the cosmological-bootstrap program seeks to derive cosmological correlation functions from symmetry + unitarity + analyticity constraints without invoking specific inflationary potentials; (iii) recent work (Pajer-Stefanyszyn-Welling 2020+) has reproduced single-field inflation correlators via boundary-CFT methods. Status `aspired` because: while the framework exists and has produced specific results for inflation correlators, no specific cosmological observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT without additional physical assumptions. cell_refs pin 3 cells (the inflation-era observables) where the bootstrap-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (real research program with growing technical results; observational confirmation pending future precision data). This edge mirrors the ads-cft-instance program targeting of hadronic-states / FQH / compact-astro from prior phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Strominger 2001, JHEP 10, 034 (dS/CFT)
Arkani-Hamed, Maldacena 2015, arXiv:1503.08043 (cosmological bootstrap setup)
Pajer, Stefanyszyn, Welling 2020, JHEP 11, 116 (cosmological bootstrap technical results)

all_edges.description: GW events specialize Poincaré-invariant GR + perturbation theory on background spacetimes. Specifically: (i) BBH/BNS/NSBH mergers are GR solutions emitting gravitational radiation, specializing the cell-stsym-GR or analogous cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) ringdown phase is BH perturbation theory in Kerr geometry, specializing Kerr-metric content; (iii) GW propagation at c specializes the Poincaré-invariance + null-cone-propagation prediction; (iv) the no-hair-theorem tests specialize the Kerr-uniqueness result; (v) PTA nHz background specializes the GW stress-energy tensor + cosmological FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — GR + numerical relativity is the foundational framework and waveform matches confirm at high precision. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE.
citations: Einstein 1916 (GR)
Maggiore 2008, Gravitational Waves Vol. 1 (review)

all_edges.description: FIRST PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET. Each GW merger event specializes a merger of objects classified in compact-astrophysical-objects. Specifically: (i) GW150914 specializes a merger of two stellar-mass BHs (cells cell-compact-stellar-BH-XRB / cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW from compact-astro v21); (ii) GW170817 specializes a merger of two canonical neutron stars (cell-compact-NS-canonical in compact-astro); (iii) GW190521 specializes an intermediate-mass-BBH merger (cell-compact-IMBH-gap in compact-astro); (iv) GW190814 and GW230529 specialize lower-mass-gap mergers, populating the boundary between compact-astro cell-compact-NS-massive and cell-compact-stellar-BH; (v) GWTC-3 BBH population is the empirical realization of the stellar-mass BH population in compact-astro. The relationship is `specializes` because each event is a specific instance (merger of two specific objects) of the abstract object-classes in compact-astro. Status `partial` because: (a) not all GW event categories specialize compact-astro cells (NANOGrav PTA nHz signal is a stochastic background, not an individual merger of compact-astro objects; continuous-wave-pulsar searches are individual NSs not mergers; superradiance-bounded ultralight-boson searches are not even compact-object events); (b) the directional `specializes` relation is reasonable for events-to-object-types but the reverse (compact-astro object inferences derive from GW events as data) also holds — the relationship is genuinely intertwined. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the specialization is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (events ARE mergers of objects); MEDIUM for the directional interpretation. This edge is justified by direct structural relation, not by the desideratum of producing a phen↔phen edge — the relation would be drawn independently.
citations: Abbott et al. 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102 (GW150914 = stellar-mass BBH merger)
Abbott et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 161101 (GW170817 = BNS merger)
Abbott et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 = IMBH-region merger)

all_edges.description: BNS merger events derive matter-side physics from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BNS-merger kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis (Au, Pt, lanthanide production at AT2017gfo) involves SM-content nuclear physics + GR-MHD; (ii) BNS-merger tidal deformability constraints on dense-matter EOS (Λ < 800 from GW170817) connect to SM-content hadronic physics — specializes the cell-compact-NS-EOS-bound content from compact-astro and hadronic-states. Status `partial` because: (a) BBH events do not derive from SM-rep-content (they are vacuum-GR solutions); (b) only the BNS / NSBH subset of GW events has SM-rep relevance; (c) the derivation is at the matter-physics-input level, not the basic GW signal level. cell_refs pin 2 cells (BNS merger and multi-messenger coincidence). Literature strength: MEDIUM. This connection mirrors compact-astrophysical-objects v21 derives-from sm-rep-content edge for NS-EOS cells.
citations: Abbott et al. 2017 (GW170817 tidal deformability)
Kasen, Metzger, Barnes, Quataert, Ramirez-Ruiz 2017, Nature 551, 80 (kilonova r-process)

all_edges.description: BH ringdown spectra (QNMs) and EMRI signals connect to holographic-CFT calculations via the ads-cft-instance program. Specifically: (i) Kerr-BH quasi-normal modes and the Teukolsky equation map onto CFT-spectrum calculations through AdS/CFT (for AdS-Kerr-BH variants) and dS-Kerr quasi-normal mode analyses; (ii) the no-hair theorem itself has CFT counterparts (rigidity of CFT spectrum); (iii) EMRI signals from supermassive BHs would test holographic-CFT predictions at very high precision (10⁵ orbital cycles in LISA-band). Status `aspired` because the holographic-derivation of GW phenomenology is technically active but no specific GW observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT — the framework applies in principle. cell_refs pin 2 cells where holographic-relevance is most active. Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. This edge continues the ads-cft-instance pattern across phenomenon-FCs (hadrons, FQH, compact-astro, cosmology, now gravitational-waves — 5 of 10 phenomenon-FCs target bootstrap aspirationally).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BH-CFT correspondence)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)
Maldacena, Susskind 2013, Fortsch. Phys. 61, 781 (ER=EPR; related holographic-BH framework)

all_edges.description: QCD-phase-diagram derives directly from the SU(3)_c gauge sector of sm-rep-content. Specifically: (i) the quark cells (QL-g1, QL-g2, QL-g3 and right-handed quark cells in sm-rep-content) are the fundamental fermionic matter content; (ii) the gluon cell (cell-gluons-gNA in sm-rep-content) is the gauge field; (iii) asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) is a property of the SU(3)_c rep-content of sm-rep — non-abelian gauge with N_f = 6 quarks gives β_0 > 0; (iv) all phase transitions in QCD-phase-diagram are transitions of this gauge content under varying thermodynamic conditions. Status `established` because QCD-as-SU(3)_c is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. cell_refs pin 5 cells where the sm-rep derivation is most direct.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343
Politzer 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1346
Gell-Mann 1964, Phys. Lett. 8, 214

all_edges.description: QCD-phase-diagram specializes Poincaré-invariant QFT (T = 0 ground state) and the finite-temperature extension via Euclidean spacetime / Matsubara frequencies. Hydrodynamic flow in QGP specializes relativistic-fluid GR-coupled hydrodynamics. The crossover at T_c is a thermodynamic property of finite-T QFT specializing standard Wightman-axiom QFT on Minkowski background. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG.
citations: Kapusta, Gale 2006, Finite-Temperature Field Theory (2nd ed., Cambridge)
Le Bellac 2000, Thermal Field Theory (Cambridge)

all_edges.description: Two derivation pathways. (i) Holographic-QGP: AdS-CFT duality predicts strongly-coupled gauge-theory thermal transport coefficients — η/s ≥ 1/(4π) (KSS bound from Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005). RHIC + LHC QGP measurements extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π), close to KSS bound, validating the holographic-fluid framework. This is one of the cleanest empirical hits of the ads-cft-instance program in real experimental data — promotes the edge status from `aspired` to `partial` because the η/s prediction has been quantitatively validated. (ii) Chiral-transition universality: massless-2-flavor chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984), classified by bootstrap O(4) exclusion regions — though physical-quark-mass realizations are smoothed to crossover. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the bootstrap connection is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for KSS-η/s match (genuine empirical hit); MEDIUM for chiral-transition O(4) (real-crit overlap). This edge is notably NOT purely `aspired` — the η/s prediction is the cleanest existing ads-cft-instance empirical validation across all phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601 (KSS bound)
Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301 (η/s extraction from data)
Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338 (chiral O(4))
Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge — book-length AdS-CFT-to-QCD overview)

all_edges.description: Photons specialize Poincaré-invariant massless gauge bosons (Wigner classification of irreducible Poincaré representations as helicity-±1 massless particles). EM propagation on FRW + curved-spacetime backgrounds (CMB free-streaming through expansion; gravitational lensing in clusters; GR redshift of all EM emission) specializes the GR / Minkowski framework. GW + GR-test cells (GW170817 GW-EM speed = c) bound photon-graviton-equivalence. Hawking radiation derives from GR + QFT on curved background. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149 (Poincaré irreps)
Jackson 1999, Classical Electrodynamics (3rd ed., Wiley)

all_edges.description: The photon IS the U(1)_em gauge boson of sm-rep-content (specifically the (1,1,0) sub-content arising as the unbroken combination of B and W³ in electroweak symmetry breaking). All EM-phenomena cells derive from this gauge structure. Specifically: (i) thermal/blackbody emission derives from QED + thermal-equilibrium; (ii) synchrotron / inverse-Compton derive from QED + relativistic charged-fermion electroweak couplings; (iii) hadronic-pion-decay-gamma derives from QCD pion content + electroweak π⁰ → γγ amplitude (chiral anomaly); (iv) FEL-coherent + laser-coherent derive from QED stimulated-emission processes. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG. This is the primary structural-content derivation.
citations: Glashow 1961, Nucl. Phys. 22, 579 (electroweak unification)
Weinberg 1967, Phys. Rev. Lett. 19, 1264; Salam 1968 (electroweak)
Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

all_edges.description: THIRD PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET (after v21 compact-astro→hadronic-states and v27 gravitational-waves→compact-astro). Many EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-object phenomena: (i) cell-em-crab-multiband specializes compact-astro pulsar-wind-nebula cells; (ii) cell-em-AGN-jet-synchrotron specializes SMBH cells; (iii) cell-em-GRBs-multiband specializes BNS-merger (short-GRB) + collapsar (long-GRB) cells; (iv) cell-em-GW170817-kilonova specializes BNS-merger cells (same physical object that GW catalog v27 also specializes); (v) cell-em-IceCube-TXS-multimessenger specializes blazar SMBH cells; (vi) cell-em-LHAASO-PeV-gamma specializes galactic-PeVatron-source cells (SNR + PWN populations in compact-astro). Status `partial` because not all EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-objects — laboratory-coherent sources (FEL, lasers) and thermal-cosmological-backgrounds (CMB) do not derive from compact objects. cell_refs pin 6 cells with direct compact-astro specialization. The relation is honest physics: many EM cells ARE compact-object EM emission organized by photon-spectrum rather than object-class. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework.
citations: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (PWN/Crab as compact-object EM)
Mészáros 2006, Rep. Prog. Phys. 69, 2259 (GRB compact-object connection)
Begelman, Blandford, Rees 1984, Rev. Mod. Phys. 56, 255 (AGN jets from SMBHs)

all_edges.description: Thermal-QED + relativistic-magnetohydrodynamics admits CFT-like high-energy limits; holographic models of accretion-flow and jet acceleration use AdS-CFT for strongly-coupled plasma dynamics. Status `aspired` — the connections exist at framework level but no specific EM phenomenon has been derived directly from bootstrap-CFT. cell_refs pin 2 cells where the connection is most active (AGN jet plasma + UHE acceleration). Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. Continues the ads-cft-instance program targeting across phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)

all_edges.description: PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR — closes §5 natural-pairing su5-gut-rep-content ↔ proton-decay-searches. Each cell in this FC specializes a specific GUT-variant prediction from the structural classification of GUT rep content. Specifically: (i) p → π⁰ e⁺ specializes minimal-Georgi-Glashow-SU(5) dim-6 operator content; (ii) p → K⁺ ν̄ specializes SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 colored-Higgs-triplet operators; (iii) p → η e⁺, p → K⁰ ℓ⁺, etc., specialize sub-leading SU(5) operator structures; (iv) SO(10) and flipped-SU(5) cells specialize alternative-GUT rep-content predictions. Status `established` — the GUT-experimental-test relationship is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. This is a §5 explicit closure.
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533
Langacker 1981, Phys. Rep. 72, 185

all_edges.description: Proton decay processes involve SM-content particles in initial + final states: proton = uud bound state (SM-rep quark content); final-state mesons (π⁰, π⁺, K⁺, η⁰, K⁰, ρ⁰, ω⁰, ...) are SM-content hadrons; final-state leptons (e⁺, μ⁺, ν̄) are SM-content. Decay mediators (X/Y bosons of SU(5)) are BEYOND-SM but the decay processes themselves are SM-content-to-SM-content transitions modulo baryon-number-violation. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

all_edges.description: Proton-decay searches are QFT-on-Minkowski-background processes specializing Poincaré-invariant local field theory. Standard framework. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995

all_edges.description: Each N=2 SCFT spectrum-data cell is categorically equivalent to a Higgs/Coulomb-branch cell in higgs-coulomb-branches — they organize the same physical theories from data-side vs geometry-side. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-SU2Nf4-Coulomb-Delta and cell-hcb-su2-Nf4-coulomb both describe the SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 Coulomb-branch operator (one as data, one as geometry); (ii) cell-n2scft-AD-H0-Delta and cell-hcb-ad-h0 both describe AD H_0 theory; (iii) cell-n2scft-MN-E6-* and cell-hcb-mn-e6-* both describe Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6. The categorical equivalence reflects that the data and the geometry are two facets of the same N=2 SCFT object. Status `partial` because (a) not all HCB cells have a partner N2-SCFT-data cell (this FC has 17 cells vs HCB's 18, and the matching is not 1:1); (b) the chiral-algebra-bootstrap-data cells here have no direct geometric partner. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 HCB natural-pairing.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015 (chiral algebras unifying data and geometry)

all_edges.description: N=2 SCFT bootstrap-bounded observables derive from cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions specialized to N=2 supersymmetric stress-tensor 4-point functions. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-bootstrap-a-c-bound derives from cft-bootstrap general unitarity + Hofman-Maldacena energy-positivity, specialized to N=2; (ii) AD H_0 Δ_u = 6/5 has been independently verified via N=2 bootstrap to high precision (Beem et al. 2014); (iii) chiral-algebra-2D-4D correspondence cells cross-reference 2D-CFT bootstrap data for the associated chiral algebras. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM. Cross-references the foundational cft-bootstrap structural-FC.

Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012

all_edges.description: Exceptional Minahan-Nemeschansky theories and class-S constructions derive their flavor-symmetry structure from ADE Lie-algebra data. Specifically: (i) MN E_6 / E_7 / E_8 theories have flavor symmetry = corresponding exceptional Lie algebra e_6, e_7, e_8; (ii) Higgs branches are minimal nilpotent orbits of those Lie algebras; (iii) Coulomb-branch operator dimensions Δ_u = 3, 4, 6 for E_6, E_7, E_8 connect to dual Coxeter numbers; (iv) class-S T_N theories have SU(N)³ flavor symmetry from A_{N-1} M5-brane choice. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for the ADE labeling correspondence.

Gaiotto 2009 (class-S A_{N-1} from M5)

all_edges.description: SPT phase classification derives directly from freed-hopkins-cobordism: every SPT phase corresponds to a cobordism class in Ω^G_{d+1}. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-Z8-FidkowskiKitaev's Z_8 classification derives from cobordism of 1+1D Pin⁻ structures; (ii) cell-spt-cobordism-SPT-classification directly invokes Freed-Hopkins 2016 cobordism classification; (iii) anomaly-inflow Callan-Harvey provides the link between bulk topological terms (which cobordism counts) and boundary anomalies. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 freed-hopkins natural-pairing. Status `established` because the cobordism-classification framework is rigorously proven for symmetry-protected fermionic + bosonic SPTs.
citations: Freed, Hopkins 2016, arXiv:1604.06527
Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467

all_edges.description: SM-anomaly-related cells specialize sm-rep-content: they apply anomaly-cancellation and anomaly-matching constraints to the specific SM gauge × matter content. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-SM-anomaly-cancellation constrains the SM Y-charges from gauge anomaly cancellation; (ii) cell-spt-Witten-SU2-anomaly requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; (iii) cell-spt-pi0-gamma-gamma-ABJ derives from SM electromagnetic + QCD content via triangle anomaly. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.

Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324

all_edges.description: Specific SPT-phase observations specialize realized topological materials in topo-mat v22. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-3DTI-Z2-FuKaneMele cross-references topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family; (ii) cell-spt-parity-anomaly-3DTI references magnetically-doped TIs; (iii) cell-spt-MZM-Kitaev-chain partially-overlaps with topo-mat InSb/Al cells; (iv) cell-spt-Haldane-chain references S=1 chain materials. all_edges.description: SPT-phase classifications in fermionic systems derive from the tenfold-way (Altland-Zirnbauer 1997). Specifically: (i) 3D TI Z_2 is the AII class (time-reversal-invariant insulator); (ii) Z_8 1D topological superconductor is BDI class with interactions; (iii) Majorana zero modes occur in D, BDI, DIII classes; (iv) integer QH is A class (broken T-reversal). The tenfold-way provides the non-interacting backbone that anomaly + interaction analyses then refine. Status `partial` — covers fermionic SPTs only. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Altland, Zirnbauer 1997, Phys. Rev. B 55, 1142
Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125

all_edges.description: Modified-gravity alternatives derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups by generalizing or modifying the GR backbone. Specifically: (i) all modified-gravity tests reference the GR baseline established by spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) scalar-tensor and Horndeski variants modify the Einstein-Hilbert action while preserving Poincaré invariance; (iii) Einstein-aether variants break Lorentz invariance — explicit specialization within spacetime-symmetry-groups; (iv) massive gravity introduces graviton mass while preserving general covariance. PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. Status `established` — GR forms the unambiguous baseline.
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (PPN formalism)

all_edges.description: Modified-gravity tests specialize gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 — specifically the GW170817 multi-messenger event cell which provides the key graviton-speed bound. cell_refs list 7 cells where GW catalog observations are the falsifying test. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — fifth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — many modified-gravity variants falsified by GW170817 (TeVeS, Horndeski G_5, bigravity, Galileons, Einstein-aether); others constrained but surviving (dRGT massive gravity, narrow Horndeski subset).
citations: Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817)
Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382

all_edges.description: Specific modified-gravity tests specialize compact-astrophysical-objects v21 cells — PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar tests reference the NS-binary cell; EHT shadow tests reference the SMBH cells (M87, Sgr A*). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — sixth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for the test framework.
citations: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050
Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104

all_edges.description: MOND was originally proposed as a DM alternative — accounts for galaxy rotation curves without invoking particle dark matter. Specializes the broader dark-matter-candidates question by offering modified-gravity rather than particle-physics resolution. Status `partial` — MOND succeeds galactically but Bullet Cluster falsifies pure MOND at cluster scales (cf. dark-matter-candidates v25 evidence cells). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — seventh such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365
Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

all_edges.description: Macroscopic-classical phenomena derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups via Galilean-invariant Navier-Stokes (fluids), nonrelativistic MHD (plasmas), classical-mechanics kinetic-theory (statistical mechanics). Specifically: (i) Navier-Stokes equation invariant under Galilean transformations (subgroup of Poincaré); (ii) classical-mechanics Hamiltonian dynamics is Galilean-invariant; (iii) Jarzynski + Crooks fluctuation theorems derive from time-reversal-symmetric microscopic dynamics. Status `established` — foundational classical-physics structure. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Landau, Lifshitz Vol. 6 (Fluid Mechanics)

all_edges.description: Plasma physics phenomena derive from sm-rep-content via classical EM (Maxwell equations) + classical ion-electron dynamics. Specifically: (i) magnetic-reconnection is a topological rearrangement of magnetic-field lines — classical EM in plasma medium; (ii) tokamak confinement uses classical EM B-fields confining ion-electron plasma; (iii) ICF inertial-confinement uses laser EM driving classical hydrodynamics + nuclear reactions; (iv) solar flares are EM-emitting plasma phenomena. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for EM-driven plasma physics derivation. Cell_refs pin specific plasma-related cells.
citations: Krall, Trivelpiece 1973, Principles of Plasma Physics (McGraw-Hill)

all_edges.description: Specific macroscopic-classical cells overlap with EM-phenomena cells via the electromagnetic emission of plasma processes. Specifically: (i) cell-mc-solar-flare-reconnection produces observable hard X-ray (RHESSI) + EUV (SDO) emissions — characterized in EM-phenomena AGN-and-Galactic-source cells; (ii) Parker Solar Probe measures in-situ plasma but the solar wind emits across the radio-to-UV spectrum. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — eighth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — only specific plasma-EM cells participate.
citations: Shibata, Magara 2011, Living Rev. Solar Phys. 8, 6

ade-su2-subgroups.description: McKay correspondence: each finite subgroup Γ ⊂ SU(2) has a McKay quiver built from tensor products of the defining 2-dim rep with all irreps of Γ; the McKay quiver is the affine ADE Dynkin diagram of the corresponding Lie algebra. The bijection runs symmetrically: given an ADE Lie algebra, the corresponding SU(2) subgroup is recovered as the binary polyhedral group of matching ADE type. Canonical direction (subgroups → Lie algebras) reflects McKay's original construction direction.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

ade-su2-subgroups.description: Quotient construction: each Du Val singularity is realized as ℂ²/Γ where Γ ⊂ SU(2) is the corresponding ADE finite subgroup; the singularity is derived from the group action. Direction: 'ade-du-val derives-from ade-su2-subgroups' since the singularity's structure is read off the group.
citations: Klein 1884
Slodowy 1980, LNM 815

ade-su2-subgroups.description: McKay quiver: each finite SU(2) subgroup Γ has a canonical quiver (the McKay quiver) whose vertices are irreps of Γ and edges record the tensor decomposition of the defining 2-dim rep ⊗ V_i; the McKay quiver of a finite SU(2) subgroup is the affine ADE quiver. Distinct from the Gabriel-quiver case (ade-quivers) which uses finite ADE quivers; the McKay quiver is the affine version.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

ade-su2-subgroups.description: Derivable from the chain (ade-su2-subgroups → ade-lie-algebras via McKay; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ). The composite relation is real but not primitive; for visual purposes it can be omitted in favor of the two primary edges that compose to it.

ade-lie-algebras.description: McKay correspondence: each finite subgroup Γ ⊂ SU(2) has a McKay quiver built from tensor products of the defining 2-dim rep with all irreps of Γ; the McKay quiver is the affine ADE Dynkin diagram of the corresponding Lie algebra. The bijection runs symmetrically: given an ADE Lie algebra, the corresponding SU(2) subgroup is recovered as the binary polyhedral group of matching ADE type. Canonical direction (subgroups → Lie algebras) reflects McKay's original construction direction.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

ade-lie-algebras.description: Brieskorn-Grothendieck: resolution of a Du Val singularity attaches a configuration of (-2)-curves on a non-singular replacement; the intersection graph of these curves is the ADE Dynkin diagram. The Lie algebra structure (Dynkin diagram, root system) is read off the singularity. Per v15 spec convention, 'A derives-from B' means A's structure is read off B; here the Lie algebra (A) is derived from the singularity (B).
citations: Brieskorn 1966, Math. Ann. 166, 76
Brieskorn 1968, Actes Congr. Internat. Math. Nice 2, 279
Slodowy 1980, Simple Singularities and Simple Algebraic Groups (LNM 815)

ade-lie-algebras.description: Gabriel's theorem: connected representation-finite quivers are exactly the ADE quivers (those with ADE underlying graph). Moreover, indecomposable representations of an ADE quiver are in bijection with positive roots of the corresponding ADE root system. The bijection is symmetric: given a Lie algebra, the corresponding quiver is the underlying Dynkin diagram with any orientation; given a representation-finite quiver, its underlying graph is the Dynkin diagram.
citations: Gabriel 1972, Manuscripta Mathematica 6, 71
Bernstein, Gelfand, Ponomarev 1973, Russian Math. Surveys 28, 17

ade-lie-algebras.description: Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber classification: each ADE Cartan type corresponds to a modular invariant of the SU(2)_k WZW model (with appropriate level matching). A-series at all levels; D-series at even levels; E_6/E_7/E_8 at sporadic levels 10, 16, 28. The bijection is between Cartan types and modular-invariant structural patterns; the level axis adds a parametric refinement.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Commun. Math. Phys. 113, 1

ade-lie-algebras.description: The simple components of the finite-dimensional spacetime symmetry groups are real forms of complex simple Lie algebras of ADE type. Lorentz so(3,1) ≃ sl(2,ℂ)_ℝ is a real form of A_1 × A_1; conformal so(2,4) is a real form of A_3 = D_3 = sl(4)/so(6); de Sitter so(4,1) and anti-de Sitter so(3,2) are real forms of D-type complex algebras. The relation does NOT apply to the Galilean cell (different algebraic structure), the 2D-conformal sub-case (Virasoro is infinite-dimensional, not ADE), or the Diff cell (also infinite-dimensional). cell_refs pins the source-side cells where the relation applies; this convention (cell_refs as source-side pins on cross-classification edges) is documented in v15.1 findings as an open spec clarification for v15.2.
citations: Helgason 2001, Differential Geometry, Lie Groups, and Symmetric Spaces (AMS) — real forms of complex Lie algebras
Hsiang 1975, Cohomology Theory of Topological Transformation Groups (Springer) — real-form structure for spacetime groups

ade-lie-algebras.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches of class-S and Minahan-Nemeschansky theories DERIVE FROM ADE Lie algebra data: the class-S construction (Gaiotto 2009) takes an ADE Lie algebra g and a punctured Riemann surface Σ as input, and outputs a 4D N=2 SCFT T[g, Σ] whose Higgs/Coulomb branches are determined by (g, Σ, puncture-types). The Minahan-Nemeschansky E_{6,7,8} theories have Higgs branches equal to the minimal nilpotent orbits of e_{6,7,8}. Status: partial — the derivation works rigorously for class-S theories of types ADE and for the M-N exceptional theories, but not all 4D N=2 SCFTs admit a class-S construction (e.g., generic non-class-S Lagrangian theories don't fit). cell_refs pin 7 representative cells whose branch data is class-S- or M-N-derived from ADE input data.
citations: Gaiotto 2009, JHEP 08, 034
Tachikawa 2014, Lecture Notes (class-S review)
Cremonesi, Hanany, Mekareeya, Zaffaroni 2014, JHEP 09, 178 (Coulomb branches of class-S)
Moore, Tachikawa 2012, Surv. Diff. Geom. 18, 129 (Higgs branches as affine Grassmannian slices)

ade-lie-algebras.description: Hadronic states specialize ADE Lie algebras via flavor SU(3) classification: light hadrons are organized into multiplets of A_2 = SU(3) flavor representations. The pseudoscalar nonet (cell-h-pi) is 8+1 of SU(3); the vector nonet (cell-h-rho) is also 8+1; the baryon octet (cell-h-N) is the 8 of SU(3); the baryon decuplet (cell-h-Delta) is the 10 of SU(3). This is the Gell-Mann eightfold-way classification: flavor SU(3) = A_2 Lie algebra representations classify the light hadron multiplets. The Ω- prediction (cell-h-Delta, see predictive_yield) was made by completing the 10-of-SU(3) and predicting the mass of the missing sss member — the most famous Mendeleev-style success in hadronic physics. Status established for light hadrons; the SU(3) classification breaks down once heavier quarks (c, b) are included (those require SU(4), SU(5) extensions or heavy-quark effective theory). cell_refs pin 4 SU(3)-multiplet cells.
citations: Gell-Mann 1962, Phys. Rev. 125, 1067 (eightfold way)
Ne'eman 1961, Nucl. Phys. 26, 222 (independent eightfold way)
Okubo 1962, Prog. Theor. Phys. 27, 949 (SU(3) mass formula)

ade-lie-algebras.description: Exceptional Minahan-Nemeschansky theories and class-S constructions derive their flavor-symmetry structure from ADE Lie-algebra data. Specifically: (i) MN E_6 / E_7 / E_8 theories have flavor symmetry = corresponding exceptional Lie algebra e_6, e_7, e_8; (ii) Higgs branches are minimal nilpotent orbits of those Lie algebras; (iii) Coulomb-branch operator dimensions Δ_u = 3, 4, 6 for E_6, E_7, E_8 connect to dual Coxeter numbers; (iv) class-S T_N theories have SU(N)³ flavor symmetry from A_{N-1} M5-brane choice. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for the ADE labeling correspondence.
citations: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, 1997 (MN E_n theories)
Gaiotto 2009 (class-S A_{N-1} from M5)

ade-du-val.description: Brieskorn-Grothendieck: resolution of a Du Val singularity attaches a configuration of (-2)-curves on a non-singular replacement; the intersection graph of these curves is the ADE Dynkin diagram. The Lie algebra structure (Dynkin diagram, root system) is read off the singularity. Per v15 spec convention, 'A derives-from B' means A's structure is read off B; here the Lie algebra (A) is derived from the singularity (B).
citations: Brieskorn 1966, Math. Ann. 166, 76
Brieskorn 1968, Actes Congr. Internat. Math. Nice 2, 279
Slodowy 1980, Simple Singularities and Simple Algebraic Groups (LNM 815)

ade-du-val.description: Quotient construction: each Du Val singularity is realized as ℂ²/Γ where Γ ⊂ SU(2) is the corresponding ADE finite subgroup; the singularity is derived from the group action. Direction: 'ade-du-val derives-from ade-su2-subgroups' since the singularity's structure is read off the group.
citations: Klein 1884
Slodowy 1980, LNM 815

ade-du-val.description: Bridgeland-King-Reid: derived equivalence between coherent sheaves on the minimal resolution of a Du Val singularity and representations of the McKay quiver of the corresponding finite SU(2) subgroup. The equivalence is functorial and runs symmetrically; canonical direction reflects the original 2001 result's statement.
citations: Bridgeland, King, Reid 2001, J. Amer. Math. Soc. 14, 535

ade-du-val.description: Derivable from the chain (ade-du-val → ade-lie-algebras via resolution/Brieskorn; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).

ade-quivers.description: Gabriel's theorem: connected representation-finite quivers are exactly the ADE quivers (those with ADE underlying graph). Moreover, indecomposable representations of an ADE quiver are in bijection with positive roots of the corresponding ADE root system. The bijection is symmetric: given a Lie algebra, the corresponding quiver is the underlying Dynkin diagram with any orientation; given a representation-finite quiver, its underlying graph is the Dynkin diagram.
citations: Gabriel 1972, Manuscripta Mathematica 6, 71
Bernstein, Gelfand, Ponomarev 1973, Russian Math. Surveys 28, 17

ade-quivers.description: Bridgeland-King-Reid: derived equivalence between coherent sheaves on the minimal resolution of a Du Val singularity and representations of the McKay quiver of the corresponding finite SU(2) subgroup. The equivalence is functorial and runs symmetrically; canonical direction reflects the original 2001 result's statement.
citations: Bridgeland, King, Reid 2001, J. Amer. Math. Soc. 14, 535

ade-quivers.description: McKay quiver: each finite SU(2) subgroup Γ has a canonical quiver (the McKay quiver) whose vertices are irreps of Γ and edges record the tensor decomposition of the defining 2-dim rep ⊗ V_i; the McKay quiver of a finite SU(2) subgroup is the affine ADE quiver. Distinct from the Gabriel-quiver case (ade-quivers) which uses finite ADE quivers; the McKay quiver is the affine version.
citations: McKay 1980, in Proc. Sympos. Pure Math. 37, 183

ade-quivers.description: Derivable from the chain (ade-quivers → ade-lie-algebras via Gabriel; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).

ade-modular-invariants.description: Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber classification: each ADE Cartan type corresponds to a modular invariant of the SU(2)_k WZW model (with appropriate level matching). A-series at all levels; D-series at even levels; E_6/E_7/E_8 at sporadic levels 10, 16, 28. The bijection is between Cartan types and modular-invariant structural patterns; the level axis adds a parametric refinement.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Commun. Math. Phys. 113, 1

ade-modular-invariants.description: Derivable from the chain (ade-su2-subgroups → ade-lie-algebras via McKay; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ). The composite relation is real but not primitive; for visual purposes it can be omitted in favor of the two primary edges that compose to it.
Derivable from the chain (ade-du-val → ade-lie-algebras via resolution/Brieskorn; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).
Derivable from the chain (ade-quivers → ade-lie-algebras via Gabriel; ade-lie-algebras → ade-modular-invariants via CIZ).

The ADE SU(2)_k modular invariants are a specialization of the d=2 bootstrap classification: they are the bootstrap solutions for rational d=2 conformal field theories with su(2)_k current algebra (modular-invariant partition functions on the torus, equivalently consistent crossing-invariant OPE structures with finitely many primaries). The CIZ classification (Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber 1987) is the complete bootstrap-style classification for this sub-domain. The relation is specializes (ade-modular-invariants is the sub-domain; cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions is the more general framework). Status: partial — the relation is established for the SU(2)_k rational sub-case, but the d=2 minimal-models-Virasoro cell (cell-bs-d2-virasoro) is more general than ADE: it includes the diagonal minimal-model series at all (p,q), of which the SU(2)_k WZW models with ADE modular invariants are a specific extended-chiral-algebra extension. Status `partial` reflects that ADE pins one sub-region of the d=2 bootstrap exclusion regions, not the entire d=2 column. cell_refs pins all 5 ADE-modular-invariant cells, which are the source-side cells specializing into the broader d=2 bootstrap.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 241, 333 (the minimal-model bootstrap that subsumes ADE-rational specifically)
Gannon 2000, Rev. Math. Phys. 12, 739 (review of modular-invariant classification)

ade-modular-invariants.description: The ADE SU(2)_k modular invariants specialize the modular tensor categories: ADE-modular-invariants are exactly the SU(2)_k WZW MTCs (the A-series at each rank k+1, plus the D and exceptional modular extensions). Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber 1987 classified the modular-invariant partition functions for SU(2)_k WZW into ADE families; in MTC language, these are the MTC structures supported on the SU(2)_k chiral algebra (A-series) plus the orbifold-and-extension constructions (D, E_6, E_7, E_8 series). This edge complements the existing v15.4 partial edge `ade-modular-invariants -> cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions [partial/specializes]`: that edge says ADE-modular is part of the bootstrap region; this new edge says ADE-modular is part of the MTC sub-region within bootstrap. Together they document the chain: ade-modular-invariants ⊂ modular-tensor-categories ⊂ rational-CFT ⊂ cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions. cell_refs pin the 5 ADE-modular cells (all of them); each corresponds to an SU(2)_k MTC at the appropriate k.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Gannon 2000, Rev. Math. Phys. 12, 739 (review of modular-invariant classification)
Frohlich, Fuchs, Runkel, Schweigert 2006, Nucl. Phys. B 763, 354 (algebraic approach via Frobenius algebras in MTCs)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: The simple components of the finite-dimensional spacetime symmetry groups are real forms of complex simple Lie algebras of ADE type. Lorentz so(3,1) ≃ sl(2,ℂ)_ℝ is a real form of A_1 × A_1; conformal so(2,4) is a real form of A_3 = D_3 = sl(4)/so(6); de Sitter so(4,1) and anti-de Sitter so(3,2) are real forms of D-type complex algebras. The relation does NOT apply to the Galilean cell (different algebraic structure), the 2D-conformal sub-case (Virasoro is infinite-dimensional, not ADE), or the Diff cell (also infinite-dimensional). cell_refs pins the source-side cells where the relation applies; this convention (cell_refs as source-side pins on cross-classification edges) is documented in v15.1 findings as an open spec clarification for v15.2.
citations: Helgason 2001, Differential Geometry, Lie Groups, and Symmetric Spaces (AMS) — real forms of complex Lie algebras
Hsiang 1975, Cohomology Theory of Topological Transformation Groups (Springer) — real-form structure for spacetime groups

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: The conformal-bootstrap exclusion regions derive from the spacetime-symmetry-groups classification: the input axioms of the bootstrap are precisely conformal invariance (the conformal group structure recorded in the conformal cells of spacetime-symmetry-groups) plus unitarity plus OPE associativity. The bootstrap's machinery (conformal blocks, crossing equations, unitarity bounds) is constructed from the irreducible-representation theory of the conformal group. In d=2 the relevant group is the infinite-dimensional Virasoro × Virasoro (the d=2 entry of spacetime-symmetry-groups); in d ≥ 3 it is the finite-dimensional so(d,2) (the higher-d conformal cells). Without the symmetry-group input, the bootstrap has no equations to solve. Subtype `derives-from` per the v15 spec §3.2 reading: the bootstrap is constructed from the conformal-symmetry-group structure. cell_refs pins the source-side cells (bootstrap cells); per v15.1 convention, cell_refs pins source-side cells (cross-classification edges read from source to target via the relation, here 'derives-from'). Every bootstrap cell derives from the conformal-group structure, hence the comprehensive cell_refs list.
citations: Mack 1977, Commun. Math. Phys. 53, 155 (conformal-algebra unitarity bounds in d ≥ 3)
Dolan, Osborn 2000, Nucl. Phys. B 599, 459 (conformal blocks as eigenfunctions of conformal Casimirs)
Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984 (d=2 Virasoro case)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: NCG aspires to derive the SM rep content jointly with spacetime symmetry data from a single underlying spectral triple, where the spectral triple consists of a continuum spin manifold (encoding spacetime symmetries — Poincaré in the continuum limit) tensored with a finite-dimensional algebra A_F (encoding internal gauge structure and matter content). In this aspirational picture, both the SM rep content and the spacetime symmetries are jointly determined: changing the spectral triple changes both at once, and the SM's specific structure is the unique (or nearly-unique) solution consistent with a small set of axioms (order-one condition, finiteness, KO-dimension 6 mod 8, etc.). Partial successes: gauge group U(1) × SU(2) × SU(3) is recovered; three fermion generations recovered (with right input); spectral action contains the Einstein-Hilbert term as the leading term (deriving continuum spacetime structure). Falsified partial: Higgs mass prediction ~170 GeV inconsistent with measured 125 GeV; modifications (Devastato-Lizzi-Martinetti 2014; Chamseddine-Connes-Mukhanov 2014) have been proposed. The 18 cell_refs pin all 15 matter cells plus the 3 gauge cells of sm-rep-content, since the NCG derivation aims to recover both simultaneously. Discharge: established once a single spectral triple is shown to derive SM gauge group, fermion content, Yukawa structure, AND continuum spacetime symmetries consistent with all measurements (including Higgs mass).
citations: Connes, Lott 1991, Nucl. Phys. Proc. Suppl. 18B, 29 (particle models and NCG)
Chamseddine, Connes 1997, Commun. Math. Phys. 186, 731 (spectral action principle)
Chamseddine, Connes, Marcolli 2007, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 11, 991 (gravity and SM with neutrino mixing)
Devastato, Lizzi, Martinetti 2014, JHEP 01, 042 (grand symmetry, spectral action, Higgs mass)
Chamseddine, Connes, Mukhanov 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 114, 091302 (quanta of geometry)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Causal sets aspires to demonstrate that the classification of admissible continuum spacetime symmetries (Poincaré, diffeomorphism) and the cobordism-K-theory classification of admissible spacetime topologies are categorically equivalent at the discrete causal-set level: the same combinatorial data of an underlying ensemble of causal sets jointly determines both the continuum-limit Lorentz/Poincaré symmetry and the cobordism class of the recovered manifold. The Bombelli-Lee-Meyer-Sorkin program for discrete-to-continuum recovery aims to show that a 'manifoldlike' causal set has a unique continuum limit (up to small fluctuations) whose Lorentz invariance is emergent and whose topology is determined by the causal-set ensemble. The aspired connection makes precise what is currently a hope: that the symmetry-group classification and the cobordism classification are not independent inputs to spacetime physics but joint outputs of a single discrete classificatory framework. The 2 cell_refs pin the Poincaré and diffeomorphism cells (the canonical continuum-limit symmetries that causal sets aspires to recover, and which would be matched to specific cobordism classes). Discharge: established once a specific class of causal sets is shown to admit a continuum limit whose Lorentz symmetry group and cobordism class are jointly fixed by the ensemble structure (e.g., from the Bombelli-Henson-Sorkin manifoldlike criterion). Note: causal sets has the weakest aspired-edge defense among the four candidate-foundational programs because most of its aspirations land on open-frontiers (qg-frontier, cc-frontier) rather than on connections between existing formal-classifications — this edge captures the one substantive cross-classification aspiration but the case is more speculative than the AdS/CFT or NCG entries.
citations: Bombelli, Lee, Meyer, Sorkin 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 59, 521 (space-time as a causal set)
Henson 2009, in Approaches to Quantum Gravity, ed. Oriti (the causal set approach)
Surya 2019, Living Rev. Relativ. 22, 5 (the causal set approach to quantum gravity)
Dowker, Surya 2020, Class. Quantum Grav. 37, 053001 (manifoldlike causal sets)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Hadronic states specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups: every hadron is an irreducible representation of the Poincaré group (specifying mass and spin J^P) together with internal symmetry quantum numbers. The classification axis J-PC reads directly off the Poincaré-representation little-group decomposition. cell_refs pin 6 representative hadron families whose Poincaré-representation content is canonical: pseudoscalars (0-+ ↔ massive scalar Poincaré rep with intrinsic parity), vectors (1-- ↔ vector Poincaré rep), tensors (2++ ↔ rank-2 symmetric Poincaré rep), spin-1/2 baryons, spin-3/2 baryons, and the J/ψ / Υ vector spectra. This is the cleanest phenomenon-to-structural-FC edge in the map: hadronic-states (phenomenon-FC, the actual particles) hangs on spacetime-symmetry-groups (structural-FC, the representation theory). Status established (the Poincaré-representation organization of hadrons is foundational physics).
citations: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149 (irreducible reps of Poincaré group)
Weinberg 1995, Quantum Theory of Fields Vol. I ch. 2 (Poincaré representations and particle states)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Every compact astrophysical object is a (near-)solution to Einstein's field equations and therefore specializes the cell-stsym-diff cell (general-coordinate / diffeomorphism invariance) of spacetime-symmetry-groups. Asymptotically-flat compact objects (which is the standard case for isolated WDs, NSs, stellar-mass BHs, and most galactic SMBHs to good approximation) additionally have asymptotic Poincaré invariance — specializing cell-stsym-poincare at the asymptotic-region level. Within the strong-field interior, the symmetry reduces: Schwarzschild BHs and non-rotating NSs have SO(3) × time-translation; Kerr BHs and rotating NSs have axisymmetry SO(2) × time-translation; merging binaries have no continuous spatial symmetry. cell_refs pin 6 representative compact-object cells whose Poincaré + diff-invariance specialization is canonical. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE ASTROPHYSICS SECTOR. Status `established` — the GR description of compact objects is foundational physics, tested at the percent level by EHT shadow imaging, BBH-ringdown spectroscopy, and pulsar-timing tests. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Schwarzschild 1916, Sitzungsber. Preuss. Akad. Wiss. (vacuum spherical solution)
Kerr 1963, Phys. Rev. Lett. 11, 237 (rotating BH solution)
Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV equation for NSs)
Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests review)
EHT Collaboration 2019, 2022 (BH shadow tests of Kerr metric)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Neutrino-oscillation phenomenology specializes Poincaré invariance + CPT theorem of relativistic quantum mechanics. Specifically: (i) the oscillation phase φ = Δm² L / (2E) requires Lorentz-covariant time-evolution of mass eigenstates and is a direct prediction of Poincaré-invariant QM; (ii) CPT invariance constrains ν and antineutrino mixing matrices to be related (PMNS for ν vs PMNS* for antineutrino); (iii) leptonic CP violation (δ_CP non-trivial) is the symmetry-breaking effect — its measurement is a direct test of CP within the lepton sector, analogous to the CKM-sector CP violation already established. SN 1987A neutrino time-of-flight bounds are themselves a test of Lorentz invariance at the eV-mass scale. Status `established` because the Poincaré-invariance framework is settled physics and neutrino phenomenology specializes it directly. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG (depends on how stringently 'specializes' is read: framework-level specialization is strong; specific observational tests like Lorentz-violation bounds are partial).
citations: Bilenky, Pontecorvo 1978, Phys. Rep. 41, 225
Particle Data Group 2024 (CPT tests in the lepton sector)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: At a second-order critical point, the relevant field theory has emergent scale invariance and (under broad conditions, in particular for unitary critical theories) full conformal invariance — i.e., the spacetime-symmetry-group enhances from the lattice / classical-Galilean symmetry of the bare microscopic theory to the conformal group SO(d+1,1) (or SO(d,2) in Lorentzian signature). This specializes the cell-stsym-conformal cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups. The 'emergent' qualifier matters: it's a long-wavelength enhancement, not a microscopic symmetry. Status `established` because conformal invariance at criticality is foundational (Polyakov 1970) and tested by the fact that bootstrap (which assumes conformal invariance) gives correct critical exponents. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG (framework is well-established; the conformal-invariance specialization is direct).
citations: Polyakov 1970, JETP Lett. 12, 381 (scale → conformal at criticality)
Cardy 1996, Scaling and Renormalization in Statistical Physics (Cambridge)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: All DM candidates — particle or compact-object — are gravitating matter satisfying Poincaré invariance + diffeomorphism invariance. For particle candidates, the connection is via standard quantum-field theory on a Poincaré-invariant background. For compact-object candidates (PBHs, MACHOs), the connection is more direct: each PBH or MACHO is a GR solution (Schwarzschild / Kerr for PBHs; ordinary stellar-mass solutions for MACHOs) and therefore specializes the spacetime-symmetry-groups cells in the same sense as compact-astrophysical-objects (v21). Fuzzy DM specializes Poincaré invariance via its bosonic-field action on Minkowski background. Status `established` — GR + Poincaré framework is settled and DM candidates satisfy it as a baseline. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework-level specialization; specific tests of Lorentz-invariance for DM candidates are limited).
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests overview)
Carr, Kühnel 2020 (PBHs as GR solutions)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Cosmological observations specialize the FRW (Friedmann-Robertson-Walker) solutions of general relativity, which specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups. Specifically: (i) FRW cosmology is a specific class of GR solutions with maximally-symmetric spatial slices (specializes cell-stsym-maximal-isotropy or similar in spacetime-symmetry-groups); (ii) the CMB acoustic-peak structure is sensitive to the global FRW expansion + GR perturbation theory; (iii) the BAO sound-horizon is a clean GR-encoded length scale; (iv) spatial curvature Ω_K bounds the deviation from flat FRW; (v) the Hubble constant measures the FRW expansion rate; (vi) primordial gravitational waves are tensor modes of the FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — FRW-cosmology + GR-perturbation-theory is the foundational framework for cosmology. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR COSMOLOGY.
citations: Friedmann 1922; Robertson 1935; Walker 1937 (FRW framework)
Mukhanov 2005, Physical Foundations of Cosmology
Weinberg 2008, Cosmology

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: GW events specialize Poincaré-invariant GR + perturbation theory on background spacetimes. Specifically: (i) BBH/BNS/NSBH mergers are GR solutions emitting gravitational radiation, specializing the cell-stsym-GR or analogous cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) ringdown phase is BH perturbation theory in Kerr geometry, specializing Kerr-metric content; (iii) GW propagation at c specializes the Poincaré-invariance + null-cone-propagation prediction; (iv) the no-hair-theorem tests specialize the Kerr-uniqueness result; (v) PTA nHz background specializes the GW stress-energy tensor + cosmological FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — GR + numerical relativity is the foundational framework and waveform matches confirm at high precision. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE.
citations: Einstein 1916 (GR)
Maggiore 2008, Gravitational Waves Vol. 1 (review)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: QCD-phase-diagram specializes Poincaré-invariant QFT (T = 0 ground state) and the finite-temperature extension via Euclidean spacetime / Matsubara frequencies. Hydrodynamic flow in QGP specializes relativistic-fluid GR-coupled hydrodynamics. The crossover at T_c is a thermodynamic property of finite-T QFT specializing standard Wightman-axiom QFT on Minkowski background. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG.
citations: Kapusta, Gale 2006, Finite-Temperature Field Theory (2nd ed., Cambridge)
Le Bellac 2000, Thermal Field Theory (Cambridge)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Photons specialize Poincaré-invariant massless gauge bosons (Wigner classification of irreducible Poincaré representations as helicity-±1 massless particles). EM propagation on FRW + curved-spacetime backgrounds (CMB free-streaming through expansion; gravitational lensing in clusters; GR redshift of all EM emission) specializes the GR / Minkowski framework. GW + GR-test cells (GW170817 GW-EM speed = c) bound photon-graviton-equivalence. Hawking radiation derives from GR + QFT on curved background. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149 (Poincaré irreps)
Jackson 1999, Classical Electrodynamics (3rd ed., Wiley)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Proton-decay searches are QFT-on-Minkowski-background processes specializing Poincaré-invariant local field theory. Standard framework. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Modified-gravity alternatives derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups by generalizing or modifying the GR backbone. Specifically: (i) all modified-gravity tests reference the GR baseline established by spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) scalar-tensor and Horndeski variants modify the Einstein-Hilbert action while preserving Poincaré invariance; (iii) Einstein-aether variants break Lorentz invariance — explicit specialization within spacetime-symmetry-groups; (iv) massive gravity introduces graviton mass while preserving general covariance. PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. Status `established` — GR forms the unambiguous baseline.
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (PPN formalism)

spacetime-symmetry-groups.description: Macroscopic-classical phenomena derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups via Galilean-invariant Navier-Stokes (fluids), nonrelativistic MHD (plasmas), classical-mechanics kinetic-theory (statistical mechanics). Specifically: (i) Navier-Stokes equation invariant under Galilean transformations (subgroup of Poincaré); (ii) classical-mechanics Hamiltonian dynamics is Galilean-invariant; (iii) Jarzynski + Crooks fluctuation theorems derive from time-reversal-symmetric microscopic dynamics. Status `established` — foundational classical-physics structure. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Landau, Lifshitz Vol. 6 (Fluid Mechanics)

tenfold-way.description: The ten-fold way is the free-fermion specialization of the Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification. Each ten-fold-way symmetry class corresponds to a specific tangential structure plus internal symmetry combination in the Freed-Hopkins framework (e.g., class AII ↔ Pin+; class BDI ↔ Pin-; class A ↔ Spin^c; etc.), and the ten-fold-way's per-(class, dim) topological invariant (0, Z, Z_2, 2Z) is the FREE part or the Z_2-torsion part of the corresponding cobordism group Ω^G_{d+1}. The full cobordism group is in general LARGER (Z_8 vs Z for BDI in 1d; Z_16 vs Z_2 for AII in 4d) because the cobordism classification includes interaction-induced refinements that the free-fermion ten-fold way cannot see. cell_refs pins all 30 ten-fold-way cells (the entire ten-fold-way classification is what specializes). First use of the `specializes` cross-classification subtype in the project; the relation is canonically the example used in v14.1 findings §10.1 to motivate v15's typed cross-classification edges.
citations: Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (ten-fold way; K-theory framing)
Freed, Hopkins 2016 (cobordism framing; recovers ten-fold way as free-fermion specialization)
Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010 (canonical example of where ten-fold way and Freed-Hopkins differ: the Z → Z_8 collapse demarcates the specialization)

tenfold-way.description: The ten-fold-way classification of topological phases (in dimensions 1, 2, 3 with the ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes) aspires to derive from boundary CFT bootstrap data via the bulk-boundary correspondence. A (d+1)-dimensional topological phase has a d-dimensional boundary CFT or gapless boundary mode whose spectrum is subject to bootstrap exclusion bounds; the topological invariants in the bulk (Z, Z_2, 2Z entries of the table) classify the inequivalent gapless boundaries, which in turn correspond to bootstrap-allowed CFT spectra. AdS/CFT pursues this via the AdS_3/CFT_2 dictionary applied to Chern-Simons-style bulk theories; the tensor-network program (Vidal MERA; Evenbly-White) pursues it via explicit lattice constructions of holographic-like correspondences. Aspired discharge: established once every non-trivial entry of the ten-fold-way table is matched to a specific bootstrap-allowed boundary CFT solution. The 9 cell_refs pin the canonical non-trivial entries (the topological phases with anomalous boundary modes most likely to admit a CFT bootstrap dual).
citations: Vidal 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 220405 (entanglement renormalization, MERA)
Ryu, Schnyder, Furusaki, Ludwig 2010, New J. Phys. 12, 065010 (topological insulators and superconductors)
Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (periodic table of topological insulators)
Witten 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 035001 (three lectures on topological phases of matter)

tenfold-way.description: Top-down holography (F-theory / string compactifications) aspires to demonstrate that SM rep content and ten-fold-way topological-phase data are categorically-equivalent extractions of a single underlying string compactification: same geometry, different data extractor, structurally-paired content. F-theory GUTs (Beasley-Heckman-Vafa 2009) produce SM-like fermion spectra from intersecting D-brane configurations on Calabi-Yau geometries; the same geometries carry discrete higher-form / generalized symmetry data (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2019; García-Etxebarria-Heidenreich-Regalado 2022) classifying topological-phase content. AdS/CFT aspires to make the equivalence categorical: the moduli of admissible string compactifications would bijectively map to (SM-like rep content, ten-fold-way class) pairs that are jointly realizable. This is the one program-co-targeted shadow edge surfaced by the v15.6 pattern-finding pass: both ends were previously targeted by ads-cft-instance via separate aspired edges to bootstrap (cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions ↔ sm-rep-content and tenfold-way → cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions), but the direct sm-rep-content ↔ ten-fold-way connection was unauthored despite literature support. cell_refs pin the 15 SM matter cells (the source-side data that the categorical equivalence would jointly determine with topological-phase data). Discharge: established once a specific string compactification gives exactly the SM rep content and a specific ten-fold-way class as joint outputs, with the moduli labelling the pair forming a bijection or at least an explicit functor.
citations: Beasley, Heckman, Vafa 2009, JHEP 01, 058 (GUTs and exceptional branes in F-theory I)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (2-group symmetries)
García-Etxebarria, Heidenreich, Regalado 2022, JHEP 09, 130 (IIB flux non-commutativity and higher-form symmetries)
Heckman 2010, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 60, 237 (particle physics implications of F-theory)
Apruzzi et al. 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895 (SymTFT from string theory — geometric origin of higher-form symmetries)

tenfold-way.description: Tensor-network aspires to demonstrate that the full classification of (2+1)D topological phases factorizes as the composition of ten-fold-way symmetry-protected data (the SPT factor) with modular-tensor-category intrinsic-topological-order data (the MTC factor): full-topological-phase = ten-fold-way × MTC. For (2+1)D this gives the Kitaev-Kong-Wen classification scheme (Lan-Kong-Wen 2017 elaborated it). The two classifications are complementary: ten-fold-way handles invertible / SPT phases (no anyons in the bulk; symmetry-protected gapless or topological boundary modes); MTC handles non-invertible / intrinsic topological order (anyons in the bulk; topological boundary modes are the boundary anyons). The aspiration is that every (2+1)D topological phase is precisely characterized by one ten-fold-way class plus one MTC, with the composition operation explicit. Tensor-network methods (Levin-Wen string-net constructions, PEPS) provide the most concrete framework where this factorization is being made precise. The 3 cell_refs pin canonical (2+1)D ten-fold-way cells whose composition with specific MTCs is the active research target.
citations: Lan, Kong, Wen 2017, Phys. Rev. B 95, 235140 (classification of (2+1)D topological orders)
Levin, Wen 2005, Phys. Rev. B 71, 045110 (string-net condensation)
Senthil 2015, Annu. Rev. Condens. Matter Phys. 6, 299 (review of SPT phases)
Chen, Gu, Liu, Wen 2013, Phys. Rev. B 87, 155114 (SPT phases with on-site symmetries)

tenfold-way.description: The ten-fold way specializes generalized symmetries: ten-fold-way SPT phases are classified by their generalized-symmetry data including anti-unitary (time-reversal, particle-hole) symmetries treated as generalized symmetries with cobordism-class anomalies. Each ten-fold-way class corresponds to a specific Pin or Spin or Spin^c cobordism class in the bulk SymTFT (Kapustin 2014; Freed-Hopkins 2021). The specializes-relation: the ten-fold-way is the sub-case of generalized-symmetries focused on free fermionic SPT phases with the ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes. cell_refs pin 5 representative ten-fold-way cells whose generalized-symmetry data is canonically derivable.
citations: Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467 (cobordism-theoretic derivation of ten-fold way)
Kapustin, Thorngren, Turzillo, Wang 2015, JHEP 12, 052 (fermionic SPT and generalized cohomology)
Wang, Senthil 2014, Phys. Rev. B 89, 195124 (generalized symmetries in topological insulators)

tenfold-way.description: Each material whose symmetry-class axis value lies in the strict tenfold-way (A, AIII, AI, BDI, D, DIII, AII, CII, C, CI) specializes the corresponding tenfold-way cell (cell-{class}-d{dimension} in tenfold-way's coordinate scheme). HgTe and 1T'-WTe₂ specialize cell-AII-d2; Bi₂Se₃-family, Sb₂Te₃, Bi₂Te₃ specialize cell-AII-d3; FeTe/Se specializes cell-DIII-d3; InSb/Al wire specializes cell-D-d1; predicted class-CI 3D specializes cell-CI-d3 (currently empty in tenfold-way's material-realization sense). THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR TOPOLOGICAL-MATTER. The tenfold-way cells defined formally; this FC's cells are specific materials; the edge is `specializes` because each material IS a particular point in a tenfold-way cell's phase space. Status `established` — the tenfold-way classification is a settled mathematical theorem (Kitaev 2009 K-theory derivation), and the material identifications are unambiguous within the noninteracting domain. Literature strength: STRONG. Materials with crystalline / magnetic / higher-order refinement labels are not directly captured by this edge — those go through subsequent edges to crystalline-symmetry-extension classifications or to freed-hopkins-cobordism.
citations: Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125 (tenfold-way derivation)
Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (K-theory / Bott periodicity)
Ryu, Schnyder, Furusaki, Ludwig 2010, New J. Phys. 12, 065010 (extended table)

tenfold-way.description: SPT-phase classifications in fermionic systems derive from the tenfold-way (Altland-Zirnbauer 1997). Specifically: (i) 3D TI Z_2 is the AII class (time-reversal-invariant insulator); (ii) Z_8 1D topological superconductor is BDI class with interactions; (iii) Majorana zero modes occur in D, BDI, DIII classes; (iv) integer QH is A class (broken T-reversal). The tenfold-way provides the non-interacting backbone that anomaly + interaction analyses then refine. Status `partial` — covers fermionic SPTs only. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Altland, Zirnbauer 1997, Phys. Rev. B 55, 1142
Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: The ten-fold way is the free-fermion specialization of the Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification. Each ten-fold-way symmetry class corresponds to a specific tangential structure plus internal symmetry combination in the Freed-Hopkins framework (e.g., class AII ↔ Pin+; class BDI ↔ Pin-; class A ↔ Spin^c; etc.), and the ten-fold-way's per-(class, dim) topological invariant (0, Z, Z_2, 2Z) is the FREE part or the Z_2-torsion part of the corresponding cobordism group Ω^G_{d+1}. The full cobordism group is in general LARGER (Z_8 vs Z for BDI in 1d; Z_16 vs Z_2 for AII in 4d) because the cobordism classification includes interaction-induced refinements that the free-fermion ten-fold way cannot see. cell_refs pins all 30 ten-fold-way cells (the entire ten-fold-way classification is what specializes). First use of the `specializes` cross-classification subtype in the project; the relation is canonically the example used in v14.1 findings §10.1 to motivate v15's typed cross-classification edges.
citations: Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (ten-fold way; K-theory framing)
Freed, Hopkins 2016 (cobordism framing; recovers ten-fold way as free-fermion specialization)
Fidkowski, Kitaev 2010 (canonical example of where ten-fold way and Freed-Hopkins differ: the Z → Z_8 collapse demarcates the specialization)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: AdS/CFT aspires to demonstrate that the bootstrap exclusion regions for holographic CFTs (large-N, large-gap CFTs with sparse low-twist spectrum) are determined by the cobordism classification of the bulk SymTFT / invertible field theory data. The bulk theory is a cobordism-classified invertible (anomaly-inflow) theory; the boundary CFT data, in particular the central charges and stress-tensor 3-point function ratios subject to Hofman-Maldacena bounds, should derive from the bulk's cobordism data via holographic renormalization. Currently the bulk-boundary correspondence is established at the level of anomaly matching (Witten 1998; Henningson-Skenderis 1998) but the precise derivation of bootstrap exclusion boundaries from bulk cobordism data is aspired-not-shown. The four cell_refs pin the canonical holographic CFT cells where this aspired derivation would land first: 4D N=4 SYM (dual to AdS_5 × S^5), 6D (2,0) (dual to AdS_7 × S^4), 3D N=8 ABJM (dual to AdS_4 × S^7), and the universal stress-tensor sector (where bulk Einstein gravity directly fixes the boundary central charges). Discharge: established once a specific bootstrap exclusion bound (e.g., the Hofman-Maldacena bound on a/c ratios, or central charge minimization) is derived from bulk cobordism data without holographic-renormalization shortcuts.
citations: Heemskerk, Penedones, Polchinski, Sully 2009, JHEP 10, 079
Maldacena, Shenker, Stanford 2015, JHEP 08, 106 (chaos bound from holography matching bootstrap)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (topological symmetry in QFT)
Apruzzi et al. 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895 (SymTFT from string theory)
Witten 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 253 (anti-de Sitter space and holography)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: NCG aspires to derive the SM rep content from the K-theoretic / cobordism classification of anomaly-free fermion contents in 4D. Both programs land in K-theory: Connes-Chamseddine spectral triples are K-theoretic objects (Connes 1995); Freed-Hopkins cobordism for invertible field theories is K-theory-flavored (Freed-Hopkins 2021); García-Etxebarria-Montero have shown the SM is anomaly-free in the cobordism-precise sense (no Dai-Freed anomalies). The aspired connection: the cobordism-K-theory classification of admissible anomaly-free 4D fermion families should coincide with the spectral-triple classification at the level of SM-like solutions, providing a K-theoretic explanation for why the SM has exactly its observed rep content (rather than this being an arbitrary input). The 15 cell_refs pin the 15 matter cells (3 generations × 5 left/right-handed multiplets) that constitute the rep content being explained. Discharge: established once one demonstrates that the cobordism-K-theory classification (with appropriate symmetry data) and the spectral-triple classification (with appropriate finite-dimensional algebra input) bijectively identify the SM matter content as their joint solution. Currently partial successes exist on both sides separately but not as a derivation.
citations: Connes 1995, J. Math. Phys. 36, 6194 (NCG and reality)
Chamseddine, Connes, Marcolli 2007, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 11, 991 (gravity and SM with neutrino mixing)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases)
García-Etxebarria, Montero 2019, JHEP 08, 003 (Dai-Freed anomalies in particle physics)
Davighi, Lohitsiri 2020, JHEP 05, 098 (anomaly interplay for SM)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: Causal sets aspires to demonstrate that the classification of admissible continuum spacetime symmetries (Poincaré, diffeomorphism) and the cobordism-K-theory classification of admissible spacetime topologies are categorically equivalent at the discrete causal-set level: the same combinatorial data of an underlying ensemble of causal sets jointly determines both the continuum-limit Lorentz/Poincaré symmetry and the cobordism class of the recovered manifold. The Bombelli-Lee-Meyer-Sorkin program for discrete-to-continuum recovery aims to show that a 'manifoldlike' causal set has a unique continuum limit (up to small fluctuations) whose Lorentz invariance is emergent and whose topology is determined by the causal-set ensemble. The aspired connection makes precise what is currently a hope: that the symmetry-group classification and the cobordism classification are not independent inputs to spacetime physics but joint outputs of a single discrete classificatory framework. The 2 cell_refs pin the Poincaré and diffeomorphism cells (the canonical continuum-limit symmetries that causal sets aspires to recover, and which would be matched to specific cobordism classes). Discharge: established once a specific class of causal sets is shown to admit a continuum limit whose Lorentz symmetry group and cobordism class are jointly fixed by the ensemble structure (e.g., from the Bombelli-Henson-Sorkin manifoldlike criterion). Note: causal sets has the weakest aspired-edge defense among the four candidate-foundational programs because most of its aspirations land on open-frontiers (qg-frontier, cc-frontier) rather than on connections between existing formal-classifications — this edge captures the one substantive cross-classification aspiration but the case is more speculative than the AdS/CFT or NCG entries.
citations: Bombelli, Lee, Meyer, Sorkin 1987, Phys. Rev. Lett. 59, 521 (space-time as a causal set)
Henson 2009, in Approaches to Quantum Gravity, ed. Oriti (the causal set approach)
Surya 2019, Living Rev. Relativ. 22, 5 (the causal set approach to quantum gravity)
Dowker, Surya 2020, Class. Quantum Grav. 37, 053001 (manifoldlike causal sets)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: RG-info aspires that the cobordism classification of invertible (gapped) phases derives from the bootstrap classification of CFT fixed points via RG-flow continuation. In the Kapustin-Seiberg / Freed-Hopkins picture, every cobordism-classified invertible field theory is the RG-flow IR endpoint of some massive deformation of a UV bootstrap-allowed CFT; the cobordism classification is then the structure of trivial-flow endpoints emerging from bootstrap-allowed UV theories. The C-theorem (Zamolodchikov 1986) and a-theorem (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) establish information-monotonicity along RG flows in 2D and 4D respectively; the rg-info aspiration extends this monotonicity to a derivation: cobordism classes are RG-trivial fibres over the bootstrap UV moduli space. NOTE: this edge points opposite to the v15.5 edge edge-xc-bootstrap-derives-cobordism-aspired (which is targeted by ads-cft-instance and reads bootstrap-derives-from-cobordism via the bulk-boundary / SymTFT direction). The two aspirations are non-conflicting: AdS/CFT works in the bulk-boundary direction; RG-info works in the UV→IR direction. Authoring both as separate edges exercises the multi-edge-between-same-pair pattern flagged in v15.5 as previously unused. cell_refs pin 5 source-side cells: Ω^{Spin}_2 = ℤ₂ (2D fermionic invertible phases ↔ minimal-model bootstrap IR endpoints); Ω^{Spin}_4 = ℤ (4D Pontryagin-class invertible phase ↔ 4D bootstrap IR); Ω^{Spin^c}_2 = ℤ (IQHE-class ↔ 2D bootstrap chiral central charge); Ω^{Spin^c}_4 = ℤ² (4D spin^c invertible ↔ 4D bootstrap); Ω^{Pin+}_4 = ℤ₁₆ (Wang-Senthil anomaly ↔ 4D bootstrap with time-reversal). Discharge: established once a specific cobordism class is shown to be the RG-flow IR endpoint of a specific bootstrap-allowed UV CFT, with the RG flow respecting the c/a-theorem ordering.
citations: Zamolodchikov 1986, JETP Lett. 43, 730 (c-theorem)
Komargodski, Schwimmer 2011, JHEP 12, 099 (a-theorem in 4D)
Kapustin, Seiberg 2014, JHEP 04, 001 (higher-form symmetries and RG)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases as RG endpoints)
Apenko 2012, Physica A 391, 62 (information theory and RG)
Beny, Osborne 2015, New J. Phys. 17, 083005 (RG as quantum-information renormalization)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: Modular tensor categories specialize the cobordism classification of (2+1)D invertible / non-chiral topological phases. Status: partial — the specialization holds for the invertible (rank-1) and non-chiral (c=0) sub-case of MTC, where MTC data reduces to cobordism-classified anomaly inflow. Specifically: a non-chiral MTC has an associated 3D TQFT that is (in the trivial cobordism class) reducible to a finite group gauge theory, which has a cobordism-classified anomaly. The chiral central charge c (mod 8 for bosonic MTCs, mod 16 for fermionic) is itself cobordism data: Ω_4^{Pin+} = Z_16 captures the gravitational anomaly of fermionic chiral MTCs (Kitaev's 16-fold way). General chiral MTCs (Fibonacci, Ising, SU(2)_k at k ≥ 1) are NOT cobordism-classified — they carry non-invertible anyon content. So the specialization is partial: invertible / non-chiral MTC ⊂ cobordism; general MTC properly extends cobordism by adding non-invertible anyonic data. The 3 cell_refs pin the structurally-degenerate cases: rank-1 vacuum (literally cobordism-class 0), toric code (Z_2 gauge theory, cobordism-classified by group-cohomology), and doubled-Ising (non-chiral Drinfeld center, partially cobordism-overlapping).
citations: Kitaev 2006, Ann. Phys. 321, 2 (16-fold way, Pin+ cobordism)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases)
Kong, Wen 2014, arXiv:1405.5858 (braided fusion categories of topological order)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: Generalized symmetries specialize the cobordism classification: the SymTFT bulk associated to any symmetry S in d-dim is a (d+1)-dim invertible TFT, which is cobordism-classified (Freed-Hopkins 2021). Every cell of generalized-symmetries (a specific (d, p, structure-type) triple with realized examples) corresponds to a specific cobordism class in Ω_{d+1}^{SO × BG} (for invertible cases) or to a fusion-categorical extension thereof (for non-invertible cases). The specializes-relation: generalized-symmetries provides MORE structure than cobordism alone (it also tracks form-degree, structure-type, and the boundary QFT's symmetry data), so it specializes the cobordism classification to the symmetry-data context. cell_refs pin 4 cells whose SymTFT bulks are canonically cobordism-classified: 2D Z_2 (3D DW), 3D Z_2 (4D DW), 4D YM center (5D), 6D (2,0) (7D CS-like).
citations: Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases — the cobordism classification used in SymTFT bulk)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471
Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467 (cobordism approach to SPT phases — early SymTFT-like analysis)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: ncg-instance program aspires that SymTFT structures arise as compositional outputs of cobordism-K-theory data combined with noncommutative-geometric spectral-triple machinery. Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] is compositional output of cobordism [K-theoretic input] combined with NCG spectral-triple construction-data'. The NCG framework treats K-theoretic data of a spectral triple as encoding anomaly-inflow content; SymTFT treats cobordism-classified bulk theories as encoding anomaly-inflow content for boundary symmetries.
