NCG aspires that these two K-theoretic anomaly-inflow pictures are compositionally related — that a spectral triple's K-theoretic data combines with NCG-specific quantization data to produce the corresponding SymTFT bulk. Literature-strength: WEAK. The structural overlap (both frameworks land in K-theory of anomaly inflow) is REAL but no concrete spectral-triple ↔ SymTFT realization has been worked out in the literature I am aware of as of 2026. The Connes-Marcolli 2007 spectral-action analysis and the Freed-Moore-Teleman 2022 SymTFT framework have not been bridged in either direction. Authoring this edge as aspired with weak literature backing is a faithful record of the NCG program's structural position: NCG reaches the K-theoretic anomaly-inflow territory (which IS SymTFT-adjacent) but has not yet developed concrete SymTFT-mediated constructions. Status `aspired` (no sub-cases established, unlike the tensor-network and rg-info edges which are partial). `targeted_by: ncg-instance`. cell_refs pin 3 cobordism cells with structural significance for the aspired connection: Ω_2^{Spin^c} = ℤ (IQHE-class anomaly), Ω_4^{Spin} = ℤ (4D bulk anomaly), Ω_4^{Pin+} = ℤ_16 (Wang-Senthil fermionic gauge anomaly). Multi-edge with v17 edge `generalized-symmetries specializes freed-hopkins-cobordism [established]`: the established specializes-edge captures categorical inclusion (SymTFT bulks are cobordism-classified, established); this composition-edge captures the aspired compositional construction (cobordism data + NCG spectral-triple data → explicit SymTFT realizations, not yet developed).
citations: Connes, Marcolli 2007 (noncommutative geometry and SM as spectral triple — K-theoretic foundation)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (SymTFT topological symmetry framework — K-theoretic anomaly inflow)
Larsson 2014, J. Math. Phys. 55, 122102 (K-theory in NCG — partial bridge to anomaly-inflow K-theory)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: The Freed-Hopkins cobordism classification of SPT phases (Freed-Hopkins 2021 final paper; earlier conjectures from Kapustin 2014 and Freed 2014) gives an extended classification of symmetry-protected topological phases that subsumes the tenfold-way for free fermions and extends to interacting systems. Each material's SPT class corresponds to a cobordism-invariant of its symmetry group: time-reversal protected TIs in class AII map to ΩPin⁺_{d+1}(pt) classes; magnetic-symmetry-protected phases (axion insulators) map to magnetic-cobordism classes; higher-order phases involve crystalline-extension cobordism groups. Status `partial` because: (i) the noninteracting tenfold-way → cobordism map is fully understood and provides the framework for noninteracting TIs / TSCs; (ii) the interacting extension via cobordism is well-developed in principle but very few material candidates exist for the interacting-only SPT phases that distinguish cobordism from the tenfold-way (fractional TIs are the prime target, none confirmed); (iii) crystalline extensions of cobordism (Thorngren-Else 2018 etc.) have classification machinery but sparse material realization. cell_refs pin 5 cells of variable SPT-cobordism relevance: HgTe and Bi₂Se₃-family are noninteracting AII → trivially captured by free-fermion cobordism, but listed because the cobordism framework provides their classification at the most general level; Bi hoti and MnBi₂Te₄ have crystalline / magnetic refinements that REQUIRE cobordism beyond tenfold-way; fractional TI is the canonical predicted target of interacting cobordism classification. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework, MEDIUM for direct material applicability beyond noninteracting cases.
citations: Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1404.6659 (cobordism approach to SPTs)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (final cobordism classification of invertible TQFTs)
Thorngren, Else 2018, Phys. Rev. X 8, 011040 (crystalline SPT cobordism)

freed-hopkins-cobordism.description: SPT phase classification derives directly from freed-hopkins-cobordism: every SPT phase corresponds to a cobordism class in Ω^G_{d+1}. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-Z8-FidkowskiKitaev's Z_8 classification derives from cobordism of 1+1D Pin⁻ structures; (ii) cell-spt-cobordism-SPT-classification directly invokes Freed-Hopkins 2016 cobordism classification; (iii) anomaly-inflow Callan-Harvey provides the link between bulk topological terms (which cobordism counts) and boundary anomalies. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 freed-hopkins natural-pairing. Status `established` because the cobordism-classification framework is rigorously proven for symmetry-protected fermionic + bosonic SPTs.
citations: Freed, Hopkins 2016, arXiv:1604.06527
Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467

sm-rep-content.description: If SU(5) is the correct GUT framework, the SM rep content specializes the SU(5) rep content: each SM multiplet (the cells of sm-rep-content) is contained in a specific SU(5) representation (the cells of su5-gut-rep-content) under the conjectured SU(5) → SM breaking. The 18 cell_refs pin the SM cells whose embedding into SU(5) reps is part of the conjectured connection (15 matter cells + 3 gauge boson cells; the Higgs cell-H-gNA is not pinned because the Higgs embedding in SU(5) requires non-minimal Higgs content (24 + 5 or similar) which is not part of minimal SU(5)'s structural content). Status: conjectured — the connection has specific shape (the 5̄ + 10 embedding) but is not empirically established (proton decay non-observation has falsified minimal non-SUSY SU(5); other variants remain viable but unconfirmed).
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974
Buras, Ellis, Gaillard, Nanopoulos 1978
Super-Kamiokande Collaboration 2017 (current proton decay limits constraining the connection)

sm-rep-content.description: NCG aspires to derive the SM rep content from the K-theoretic / cobordism classification of anomaly-free fermion contents in 4D. Both programs land in K-theory: Connes-Chamseddine spectral triples are K-theoretic objects (Connes 1995); Freed-Hopkins cobordism for invertible field theories is K-theory-flavored (Freed-Hopkins 2021); García-Etxebarria-Montero have shown the SM is anomaly-free in the cobordism-precise sense (no Dai-Freed anomalies). The aspired connection: the cobordism-K-theory classification of admissible anomaly-free 4D fermion families should coincide with the spectral-triple classification at the level of SM-like solutions, providing a K-theoretic explanation for why the SM has exactly its observed rep content (rather than this being an arbitrary input). The 15 cell_refs pin the 15 matter cells (3 generations × 5 left/right-handed multiplets) that constitute the rep content being explained. Discharge: established once one demonstrates that the cobordism-K-theory classification (with appropriate symmetry data) and the spectral-triple classification (with appropriate finite-dimensional algebra input) bijectively identify the SM matter content as their joint solution. Currently partial successes exist on both sides separately but not as a derivation.
citations: Connes 1995, J. Math. Phys. 36, 6194 (NCG and reality)
Chamseddine, Connes, Marcolli 2007, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 11, 991 (gravity and SM with neutrino mixing)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases)
García-Etxebarria, Montero 2019, JHEP 08, 003 (Dai-Freed anomalies in particle physics)
Davighi, Lohitsiri 2020, JHEP 05, 098 (anomaly interplay for SM)

sm-rep-content.description: Top-down holography (AdS/CFT applied to string compactifications) aspires to embed the SM rep content as the low-energy spectrum of a specific holographic CFT compactified on an internal geometry, making SM rep content categorically equivalent to a subset of bootstrap-allowed CFT data with the appropriate symmetry-breaking pattern. Concrete realizations: F-theory GUTs (Beasley-Heckman-Vafa 2009), where SM-like spectra arise from D-brane configurations whose dual CFTs sit in the bootstrap-allowed region. The aspiration is that some bootstrap-allowed SCFT, when compactified on a specific geometric background, gives exactly the SM at low energies — making the SM a 'special point' in the moduli space of bootstrap-allowed SCFTs. The four cell_refs pin the canonical SCFTs from which string compactifications might descend to SM-like physics: 4D N=4 SYM, 4D N=2 SCFTs (most relevant for SM-like phenomenology), 3D N=8 (M-theory side), and 6D (2,0) (F-theory parent). Discharge: established once a specific bootstrap-allowed SCFT plus a specific compactification geometry is shown to give exactly the SM gauge group, fermion content, and Yukawa structure (or equivalently, the SM is shown to sit at a specific point in the moduli space of bootstrap-allowed holographic CFTs).
citations: Maldacena 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (the large N limit of superconformal field theories)
Beasley, Heckman, Vafa 2009, JHEP 01, 058 (GUTs and exceptional branes in F-theory I)
Heckman 2010, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 60, 237 (particle physics implications of F-theory)
Weigand 2018, PoS TASI2017, 016 (TASI lectures on F-theory)

sm-rep-content.description: NCG aspires to derive the SM rep content jointly with spacetime symmetry data from a single underlying spectral triple, where the spectral triple consists of a continuum spin manifold (encoding spacetime symmetries — Poincaré in the continuum limit) tensored with a finite-dimensional algebra A_F (encoding internal gauge structure and matter content). In this aspirational picture, both the SM rep content and the spacetime symmetries are jointly determined: changing the spectral triple changes both at once, and the SM's specific structure is the unique (or nearly-unique) solution consistent with a small set of axioms (order-one condition, finiteness, KO-dimension 6 mod 8, etc.). Partial successes: gauge group U(1) × SU(2) × SU(3) is recovered; three fermion generations recovered (with right input); spectral action contains the Einstein-Hilbert term as the leading term (deriving continuum spacetime structure). Falsified partial: Higgs mass prediction ~170 GeV inconsistent with measured 125 GeV; modifications (Devastato-Lizzi-Martinetti 2014; Chamseddine-Connes-Mukhanov 2014) have been proposed. The 18 cell_refs pin all 15 matter cells plus the 3 gauge cells of sm-rep-content, since the NCG derivation aims to recover both simultaneously. Discharge: established once a single spectral triple is shown to derive SM gauge group, fermion content, Yukawa structure, AND continuum spacetime symmetries consistent with all measurements (including Higgs mass).
citations: Connes, Lott 1991, Nucl. Phys. Proc. Suppl. 18B, 29 (particle models and NCG)
Chamseddine, Connes 1997, Commun. Math. Phys. 186, 731 (spectral action principle)
Chamseddine, Connes, Marcolli 2007, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 11, 991 (gravity and SM with neutrino mixing)
Devastato, Lizzi, Martinetti 2014, JHEP 01, 042 (grand symmetry, spectral action, Higgs mass)
Chamseddine, Connes, Mukhanov 2014, Phys. Rev. Lett. 114, 091302 (quanta of geometry)

sm-rep-content.description: Top-down holography (F-theory / string compactifications) aspires to demonstrate that SM rep content and ten-fold-way topological-phase data are categorically-equivalent extractions of a single underlying string compactification: same geometry, different data extractor, structurally-paired content. F-theory GUTs (Beasley-Heckman-Vafa 2009) produce SM-like fermion spectra from intersecting D-brane configurations on Calabi-Yau geometries; the same geometries carry discrete higher-form / generalized symmetry data (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator 2019; García-Etxebarria-Heidenreich-Regalado 2022) classifying topological-phase content. AdS/CFT aspires to make the equivalence categorical: the moduli of admissible string compactifications would bijectively map to (SM-like rep content, ten-fold-way class) pairs that are jointly realizable. This is the one program-co-targeted shadow edge surfaced by the v15.6 pattern-finding pass: both ends were previously targeted by ads-cft-instance via separate aspired edges to bootstrap (cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions ↔ sm-rep-content and tenfold-way → cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions), but the direct sm-rep-content ↔ ten-fold-way connection was unauthored despite literature support. cell_refs pin the 15 SM matter cells (the source-side data that the categorical equivalence would jointly determine with topological-phase data). Discharge: established once a specific string compactification gives exactly the SM rep content and a specific ten-fold-way class as joint outputs, with the moduli labelling the pair forming a bijection or at least an explicit functor.
citations: Beasley, Heckman, Vafa 2009, JHEP 01, 058 (GUTs and exceptional branes in F-theory I)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (2-group symmetries)
García-Etxebarria, Heidenreich, Regalado 2022, JHEP 09, 130 (IIB flux non-commutativity and higher-form symmetries)
Heckman 2010, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 60, 237 (particle physics implications of F-theory)
Apruzzi et al. 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895 (SymTFT from string theory — geometric origin of higher-form symmetries)

sm-rep-content.description: Top-down holographic / F-theory compactifications aspire to derive SM rep content from generalized-symmetry data of the bulk SymTFT. The compactification carries discrete higher-form symmetry data (Apruzzi et al. 2023; Garcia-Etxebarria-Heidenreich-Regalado 2022); the boundary 4D QFT inherits both gauge content (sm-rep-content) and higher-form symmetry structure (generalized-symmetries) jointly from the bulk. AdS/CFT aspires this derivation: SM rep content as a boundary-projection of bulk SymTFT data. cell_refs pin all 15 SM matter cells (the source-side data the aspired derivation would produce). Distinct from the v15.7 sm-equiv-tenfold aspired edge (which targets only tenfold-way topological data, not the full generalized-symmetry framework).
citations: Apruzzi, Bonetti, Garcia, Saxena, Heckman, Schafer-Nameki 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895
Garcia-Etxebarria, Heidenreich, Regalado 2022, JHEP 09, 130 (higher-form symmetries from string theory)
Heckman, Hubner, Torres 2024, JHEP 04, 113 (generalized symmetries from F-theory)

sm-rep-content.description: Hadronic states derive from SM rep content via QCD binding dynamics: each hadron is a color-singlet bound state of quarks (and gluons) drawn from the SM quark content (cell-uR-g1, cell-dR-g1, etc. for light quarks; the charm and bottom cells for heavy hadrons). The DERIVATION is structurally established (color confinement + QCD Lagrangian → hadrons emerge as IR degrees of freedom) but QUANTITATIVELY partial: while quark content fully determines the existence and quantum numbers of light hadrons via SU(3) flavor classification, the precise masses, decay widths, form factors, and exotic-state spectra require non-perturbative QCD calculations (lattice QCD, effective field theories, quark-model approximations). Status `partial` reflects this: structural derivation is established, full quantitative derivation is partial. cell_refs pin 9 cells spanning light, charm, bottom, and exotic regimes — each derives from specific quark-content compositions.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343 (QCD asymptotic freedom)
Politzer 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1346 (independent QCD asymptotic freedom)
Wilson 1974, Phys. Rev. D 10, 2445 (confinement via lattice QCD)
Aoki et al. (FLAG) 2024, Eur. Phys. J. C 84, 897 (lattice flavor QCD review)

sm-rep-content.description: Compact-object matter content derives from SM rep content via the matter species that populate compact objects: white dwarfs are supported by electron-degeneracy pressure (cell-eR-g1 and corresponding lepton cells of sm-rep-content) embedded in an ionic Coulomb lattice (light nuclei drawn from QCD-bound states of light quarks — cell-uR-g1, cell-dR-g1). Neutron stars are supported by nucleon degeneracy + nuclear-force repulsion; their composition involves neutrons (n = udd), protons (p = uud), electrons (β-equilibrium), and at higher density possibly muons, hyperons, kaons. The 'derives-from' is straightforward but partial in the sense that the dense-matter-physics machinery that maps SM rep content to compact-object pressure-support is itself a layer of physics not represented in sm-rep-content directly (the Chandrasekhar mass for WDs, the TOV maximum mass for NSs both DEPEND on SM matter content but require the EOS-modeling layer to be evaluated). cell_refs pin 3 WD/NS cells whose SM-content basis is most direct. The hadronic-states edge (separate) handles the bound-state-spectrum connection; this edge handles the underlying-SM-particle connection. Literature strength: STRONG for WDs (electron-degeneracy physics is canonical); MEDIUM for NSs (nuclear-force modeling is empirically less well-constrained at NS-interior densities).
citations: Chandrasekhar 1931, Astrophys. J. 74, 81 (Chandrasekhar mass from electron rep content + GR)
Oppenheimer, Volkoff 1939, Phys. Rev. 55, 374 (TOV from nucleon rep content + GR)
Shapiro, Teukolsky 1983, Black Holes, White Dwarfs, and Neutron Stars chs. 2-3

sm-rep-content.description: Neutrino-sector phenomenology specializes the leptonic sector of the SM rep content. Specifically: the three active neutrino flavors (ν_e, ν_μ, ν_τ) are the upper components of the SU(2)_L lepton doublets in cell-nuL-g1, cell-nuL-g2, cell-nuL-g3 of sm-rep-content. The PMNS mixing matrix is the lepton-sector analog of the CKM matrix; its parameterization follows directly from the structure of SM rep content + Yukawa coupling diagonalization. Right-handed neutrinos N_R required for type-I seesaw are gauge-singlet extensions of the SM rep content — predicted within the SM extension and naturally accommodated. Status `established` because the framework of SM-rep-content + PMNS mixing is the textbook understanding of neutrino phenomenology. Literature strength: STRONG. cell_refs pin the 7 cells of this FC most directly tied to SM rep content (the 4 measured oscillation parameters, the mass-squared differences, the Dirac-vs-Majorana question, and the seesaw mechanism which extends SM rep content with N_R). THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE NEUTRINO SECTOR.
citations: Pontecorvo 1957, Sov. Phys. JETP 6, 429 (early ν oscillation proposal)
Maki, Nakagawa, Sakata 1962, Prog. Theor. Phys. 28, 870 (PMNS matrix)
Mohapatra, Pal 2004, Massive Neutrinos in Physics and Astrophysics

sm-rep-content.description: Particle DM candidates derive from extensions of SM rep content. Specifically: (i) WIMPs in SUSY scenarios derive from the supersymmetric extension of the SM (neutralino = mixture of bino + wino + higgsinos, all in SM-gauge-group representations extended by superpartners); (ii) QCD axion couples to SM gauge bosons via anomaly, derives from PQ-extension of SM with axion as Goldstone of broken PQ U(1); (iii) sterile neutrinos are gauge-singlet right-handed neutrinos, extending the SM rep content with N_R; (iv) dark photon is a kinetic-mixing extension of SM U(1)_Y with U(1)' (mediated by ε F_μν F'^μν); (v) gravitino-LSP is the spin-3/2 superpartner of graviton in SUSY-extended supergravity. Status `partial` because: (a) some candidates (PBHs, MACHOs, fuzzy-DM-from-stringy-axiverse) do not derive from SM rep content — PBHs are gravitational, MACHOs are baryonic-compact, fuzzy-DM derives from stringy / cosmological structures beyond SM; (b) for the candidates that do derive from SM extensions, the derivation is conjectural at the level of specific BSM-extension predictions. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the SM-rep derivation operates most directly. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR PARTICLE-DM CANDIDATES. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (DM-as-BSM-particle is a major program); MEDIUM for specific candidate predictions.
citations: Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (DM-from-BSM-SM framework)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996, Phys. Rep. 267, 195 (SUSY-LSP DM review)
Holdom 1986, Phys. Lett. B 166, 196 (dark-photon kinetic mixing)

sm-rep-content.description: Several cosmological observables derive directly from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BBN-era nuclear physics involves SM-content nucleons (proton, neutron) and SM-content nuclear-physics cross-sections — primordial D/H, ⁴He/H, ⁷Li/H are computed from SM particle physics + BBN framework; (ii) recombination-epoch atomic physics (the optical-depth τ_reio and the acoustic-oscillation damping) involves SM-content electrons + protons; (iii) the cosmological Σm_ν bound depends on SM neutrino content; (iv) CMB-acoustic-peak heights depend on SM neutrino effective number N_eff (Planck constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with 3 SM neutrinos). Status `partial` because: (a) some cosmological observables depend on BSM physics that the SM-rep-content FC doesn't capture (DM candidates, DE candidates); (b) the BBN nuclear cross-sections are partly experimentally-measured rather than derived. cell_refs pin 5 cells where SM-rep derivation is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (BBN, recombination, neutrino-decoupling are all SM-derived).
citations: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN from SM physics)
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (BBN review)
Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020 (N_eff measurement)

sm-rep-content.description: BNS merger events derive matter-side physics from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BNS-merger kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis (Au, Pt, lanthanide production at AT2017gfo) involves SM-content nuclear physics + GR-MHD; (ii) BNS-merger tidal deformability constraints on dense-matter EOS (Λ < 800 from GW170817) connect to SM-content hadronic physics — specializes the cell-compact-NS-EOS-bound content from compact-astro and hadronic-states. Status `partial` because: (a) BBH events do not derive from SM-rep-content (they are vacuum-GR solutions); (b) only the BNS / NSBH subset of GW events has SM-rep relevance; (c) the derivation is at the matter-physics-input level, not the basic GW signal level. cell_refs pin 2 cells (BNS merger and multi-messenger coincidence). Literature strength: MEDIUM. This connection mirrors compact-astrophysical-objects v21 derives-from sm-rep-content edge for NS-EOS cells.
citations: Abbott et al. 2017 (GW170817 tidal deformability)
Kasen, Metzger, Barnes, Quataert, Ramirez-Ruiz 2017, Nature 551, 80 (kilonova r-process)

sm-rep-content.description: QCD-phase-diagram derives directly from the SU(3)_c gauge sector of sm-rep-content. Specifically: (i) the quark cells (QL-g1, QL-g2, QL-g3 and right-handed quark cells in sm-rep-content) are the fundamental fermionic matter content; (ii) the gluon cell (cell-gluons-gNA in sm-rep-content) is the gauge field; (iii) asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) is a property of the SU(3)_c rep-content of sm-rep — non-abelian gauge with N_f = 6 quarks gives β_0 > 0; (iv) all phase transitions in QCD-phase-diagram are transitions of this gauge content under varying thermodynamic conditions. Status `established` because QCD-as-SU(3)_c is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. cell_refs pin 5 cells where the sm-rep derivation is most direct.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343
Politzer 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1346
Gell-Mann 1964, Phys. Lett. 8, 214

sm-rep-content.description: The photon IS the U(1)_em gauge boson of sm-rep-content (specifically the (1,1,0) sub-content arising as the unbroken combination of B and W³ in electroweak symmetry breaking). All EM-phenomena cells derive from this gauge structure. Specifically: (i) thermal/blackbody emission derives from QED + thermal-equilibrium; (ii) synchrotron / inverse-Compton derive from QED + relativistic charged-fermion electroweak couplings; (iii) hadronic-pion-decay-gamma derives from QCD pion content + electroweak π⁰ → γγ amplitude (chiral anomaly); (iv) FEL-coherent + laser-coherent derive from QED stimulated-emission processes. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG. This is the primary structural-content derivation.
citations: Glashow 1961, Nucl. Phys. 22, 579 (electroweak unification)
Weinberg 1967, Phys. Rev. Lett. 19, 1264; Salam 1968 (electroweak)
Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

sm-rep-content.description: Proton decay processes involve SM-content particles in initial + final states: proton = uud bound state (SM-rep quark content); final-state mesons (π⁰, π⁺, K⁺, η⁰, K⁰, ρ⁰, ω⁰, ...) are SM-content hadrons; final-state leptons (e⁺, μ⁺, ν̄) are SM-content. Decay mediators (X/Y bosons of SU(5)) are BEYOND-SM but the decay processes themselves are SM-content-to-SM-content transitions modulo baryon-number-violation. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

sm-rep-content.description: SM-anomaly-related cells specialize sm-rep-content: they apply anomaly-cancellation and anomaly-matching constraints to the specific SM gauge × matter content. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-SM-anomaly-cancellation constrains the SM Y-charges from gauge anomaly cancellation; (ii) cell-spt-Witten-SU2-anomaly requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; (iii) cell-spt-pi0-gamma-gamma-ABJ derives from SM electromagnetic + QCD content via triangle anomaly. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519
Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324

sm-rep-content.description: Plasma physics phenomena derive from sm-rep-content via classical EM (Maxwell equations) + classical ion-electron dynamics. Specifically: (i) magnetic-reconnection is a topological rearrangement of magnetic-field lines — classical EM in plasma medium; (ii) tokamak confinement uses classical EM B-fields confining ion-electron plasma; (iii) ICF inertial-confinement uses laser EM driving classical hydrodynamics + nuclear reactions; (iv) solar flares are EM-emitting plasma phenomena. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for EM-driven plasma physics derivation. Cell_refs pin specific plasma-related cells.
citations: Krall, Trivelpiece 1973, Principles of Plasma Physics (McGraw-Hill)

su5-gut-rep-content.description: If SU(5) is the correct GUT framework, the SM rep content specializes the SU(5) rep content: each SM multiplet (the cells of sm-rep-content) is contained in a specific SU(5) representation (the cells of su5-gut-rep-content) under the conjectured SU(5) → SM breaking. The 18 cell_refs pin the SM cells whose embedding into SU(5) reps is part of the conjectured connection (15 matter cells + 3 gauge boson cells; the Higgs cell-H-gNA is not pinned because the Higgs embedding in SU(5) requires non-minimal Higgs content (24 + 5 or similar) which is not part of minimal SU(5)'s structural content). Status: conjectured — the connection has specific shape (the 5̄ + 10 embedding) but is not empirically established (proton decay non-observation has falsified minimal non-SUSY SU(5); other variants remain viable but unconfirmed).
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974
Buras, Ellis, Gaillard, Nanopoulos 1978
Super-Kamiokande Collaboration 2017 (current proton decay limits constraining the connection)

su5-gut-rep-content.description: Grand-unification programs aspire that neutrino masses and mixings derive from GUT-scale rep content. Specifically: (i) in SO(10) GUTs, the 16-plet of SO(10) contains one right-handed neutrino per generation, naturally implementing seesaw; (ii) in SU(5) embeddings, N_R is an SU(5)-singlet but its Majorana mass scale M_R is naturally tied to GUT-symmetry-breaking scales; (iii) leptogenesis CP asymmetries arise from CP phases in the N_R Majorana mass matrix, which in SO(10) is linked to the same Yukawa matrices that give up-quark masses — connecting neutrino-CP to quark-sector observables. Status `aspired` because while the SO(10) / SU(5)-extension framework consistently accommodates neutrino-sector phenomenology, no specific GUT-scale prediction has been confirmed at terrestrial precision — the connection is at the framework level, not the parameter-by-parameter level. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework is well-established and SO(10) is a common embedding choice; specific predictions involving GUT-scale CP phases / Majorana masses await indirect tests via leptogenesis confirmation or proton-decay measurements that would also fix GUT scales). cell_refs pin the 3 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. This edge mirrors the su5-aspired derivation of proton-decay predictions and other neutrino-adjacent GUT observables.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw)
Buchmüller, Di Bari, Plümacher 2005, Ann. Phys. 315, 305 (leptogenesis review with GUT motivation)
Babu, Mohapatra 1993, Phys. Rev. Lett. 70, 2845 (predictions in SO(10) neutrino phenomenology)

su5-gut-rep-content.description: Several DM candidates aspire to derive from GUT-extended frameworks: (i) SUSY-LSP WIMPs are naturally embedded in GUT-extended SUSY (where the GUT-symmetry-breaking scale sets the SUSY-breaking-mediation scale); (ii) sterile-ν as N_R derives most naturally from SO(10) 16-plet which contains N_R as a singlet under SU(5) ⊂ SO(10) — gives keV-scale sterile-ν via seesaw at high scales; (iii) gravitino-LSP-DM connects to SUSY-breaking-mediation which is most naturally embedded in GUT frameworks; (iv) string-axiverse ALPs derive from string-theoretic compactifications that include GUT-style gauge structure. Status `aspired` because no specific GUT-derivation has been observationally confirmed; the connections are at the framework level. cell_refs pin 4 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM. This edge mirrors the neutrino-sector-phenomenology v23 edge derives-from su5-gut-rep-content — both phenomenon-FCs target su5/GUT as the most-natural high-scale derivation framework.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw → sterile-ν DM)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996 (SUSY-LSP in GUT frameworks)
Arvanitaki et al. 2010 (string axiverse)

su5-gut-rep-content.description: PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR — closes §5 natural-pairing su5-gut-rep-content ↔ proton-decay-searches. Each cell in this FC specializes a specific GUT-variant prediction from the structural classification of GUT rep content. Specifically: (i) p → π⁰ e⁺ specializes minimal-Georgi-Glashow-SU(5) dim-6 operator content; (ii) p → K⁺ ν̄ specializes SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 colored-Higgs-triplet operators; (iii) p → η e⁺, p → K⁰ ℓ⁺, etc., specialize sub-leading SU(5) operator structures; (iv) SO(10) and flipped-SU(5) cells specialize alternative-GUT rep-content predictions. Status `established` — the GUT-experimental-test relationship is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. This is a §5 explicit closure.
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533
Langacker 1981, Phys. Rep. 72, 185

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: The ADE SU(2)_k modular invariants are a specialization of the d=2 bootstrap classification: they are the bootstrap solutions for rational d=2 conformal field theories with su(2)_k current algebra (modular-invariant partition functions on the torus, equivalently consistent crossing-invariant OPE structures with finitely many primaries). The CIZ classification (Cappelli-Itzykson-Zuber 1987) is the complete bootstrap-style classification for this sub-domain. The relation is specializes (ade-modular-invariants is the sub-domain; cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions is the more general framework). Status: partial — the relation is established for the SU(2)_k rational sub-case, but the d=2 minimal-models-Virasoro cell (cell-bs-d2-virasoro) is more general than ADE: it includes the diagonal minimal-model series at all (p,q), of which the SU(2)_k WZW models with ADE modular invariants are a specific extended-chiral-algebra extension. Status `partial` reflects that ADE pins one sub-region of the d=2 bootstrap exclusion regions, not the entire d=2 column. cell_refs pins all 5 ADE-modular-invariant cells, which are the source-side cells specializing into the broader d=2 bootstrap.
citations: Cappelli, Itzykson, Zuber 1987, Nucl. Phys. B 280, 445
Belavin, Polyakov, Zamolodchikov 1984, Nucl. Phys. B 241, 333 (the minimal-model bootstrap that subsumes ADE-rational specifically)
Gannon 2000, Rev. Math. Phys. 12, 739 (review of modular-invariant classification)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: The conformal-bootstrap exclusion regions derive from the spacetime-symmetry-groups classification: the input axioms of the bootstrap are precisely conformal invariance (the conformal group structure recorded in the conformal cells of spacetime-symmetry-groups) plus unitarity plus OPE associativity. The bootstrap's machinery (conformal blocks, crossing equations, unitarity bounds) is constructed from the irreducible-representation theory of the conformal group. In d=2 the relevant group is the infinite-dimensional Virasoro × Virasoro (the d=2 entry of spacetime-symmetry-groups); in d ≥ 3 it is the finite-dimensional so(d,2) (the higher-d conformal cells). Without the symmetry-group input, the bootstrap has no equations to solve. Subtype `derives-from` per the v15 spec §3.2 reading: the bootstrap is constructed from the conformal-symmetry-group structure. cell_refs pins the source-side cells (bootstrap cells); per v15.1 convention, cell_refs pins source-side cells (cross-classification edges read from source to target via the relation, here 'derives-from'). Every bootstrap cell derives from the conformal-group structure, hence the comprehensive cell_refs list.
citations: Mack 1977, Commun. Math. Phys. 53, 155 (conformal-algebra unitarity bounds in d ≥ 3)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: AdS/CFT aspires to demonstrate that the bootstrap exclusion regions for holographic CFTs (large-N, large-gap CFTs with sparse low-twist spectrum) are determined by the cobordism classification of the bulk SymTFT / invertible field theory data. The bulk theory is a cobordism-classified invertible (anomaly-inflow) theory; the boundary CFT data, in particular the central charges and stress-tensor 3-point function ratios subject to Hofman-Maldacena bounds, should derive from the bulk's cobordism data via holographic renormalization. Currently the bulk-boundary correspondence is established at the level of anomaly matching (Witten 1998; Henningson-Skenderis 1998) but the precise derivation of bootstrap exclusion boundaries from bulk cobordism data is aspired-not-shown. The four cell_refs pin the canonical holographic CFT cells where this aspired derivation would land first: 4D N=4 SYM (dual to AdS_5 × S^5), 6D (2,0) (dual to AdS_7 × S^4), 3D N=8 ABJM (dual to AdS_4 × S^7), and the universal stress-tensor sector (where bulk Einstein gravity directly fixes the boundary central charges). Discharge: established once a specific bootstrap exclusion bound (e.g., the Hofman-Maldacena bound on a/c ratios, or central charge minimization) is derived from bulk cobordism data without holographic-renormalization shortcuts.
citations: Heemskerk, Penedones, Polchinski, Sully 2009, JHEP 10, 079
Maldacena, Shenker, Stanford 2015, JHEP 08, 106 (chaos bound from holography matching bootstrap)
Freed, Moore, Teleman 2022, arXiv:2209.07471 (topological symmetry in QFT)
Apruzzi et al. 2023, Commun. Math. Phys. 402, 895 (SymTFT from string theory)
Witten 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 253 (anti-de Sitter space and holography)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Top-down holography (AdS/CFT applied to string compactifications) aspires to embed the SM rep content as the low-energy spectrum of a specific holographic CFT compactified on an internal geometry, making SM rep content categorically equivalent to a subset of bootstrap-allowed CFT data with the appropriate symmetry-breaking pattern. Concrete realizations: F-theory GUTs (Beasley-Heckman-Vafa 2009), where SM-like spectra arise from D-brane configurations whose dual CFTs sit in the bootstrap-allowed region. The aspiration is that some bootstrap-allowed SCFT, when compactified on a specific geometric background, gives exactly the SM at low energies — making the SM a 'special point' in the moduli space of bootstrap-allowed SCFTs. The four cell_refs pin the canonical SCFTs from which string compactifications might descend to SM-like physics: 4D N=4 SYM, 4D N=2 SCFTs (most relevant for SM-like phenomenology), 3D N=8 (M-theory side), and 6D (2,0) (F-theory parent). Discharge: established once a specific bootstrap-allowed SCFT plus a specific compactification geometry is shown to give exactly the SM gauge group, fermion content, and Yukawa structure (or equivalently, the SM is shown to sit at a specific point in the moduli space of bootstrap-allowed holographic CFTs).
citations: Maldacena 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (the large N limit of superconformal field theories)
Beasley, Heckman, Vafa 2009, JHEP 01, 058 (GUTs and exceptional branes in F-theory I)
Heckman 2010, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 60, 237 (particle physics implications of F-theory)
Weigand 2018, PoS TASI2017, 016 (TASI lectures on F-theory)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: The ten-fold-way classification of topological phases (in dimensions 1, 2, 3 with the ten Altland-Zirnbauer symmetry classes) aspires to derive from boundary CFT bootstrap data via the bulk-boundary correspondence. A (d+1)-dimensional topological phase has a d-dimensional boundary CFT or gapless boundary mode whose spectrum is subject to bootstrap exclusion bounds; the topological invariants in the bulk (Z, Z_2, 2Z entries of the table) classify the inequivalent gapless boundaries, which in turn correspond to bootstrap-allowed CFT spectra. AdS/CFT pursues this via the AdS_3/CFT_2 dictionary applied to Chern-Simons-style bulk theories; the tensor-network program (Vidal MERA; Evenbly-White) pursues it via explicit lattice constructions of holographic-like correspondences. Aspired discharge: established once every non-trivial entry of the ten-fold-way table is matched to a specific bootstrap-allowed boundary CFT solution. The 9 cell_refs pin the canonical non-trivial entries (the topological phases with anomalous boundary modes most likely to admit a CFT bootstrap dual).
citations: Vidal 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 220405 (entanglement renormalization, MERA)
Ryu, Schnyder, Furusaki, Ludwig 2010, New J. Phys. 12, 065010 (topological insulators and superconductors)
Kitaev 2009, AIP Conf. Proc. 1134, 22 (periodic table of topological insulators)
Witten 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 035001 (three lectures on topological phases of matter)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: RG-info aspires that the cobordism classification of invertible (gapped) phases derives from the bootstrap classification of CFT fixed points via RG-flow continuation. In the Kapustin-Seiberg / Freed-Hopkins picture, every cobordism-classified invertible field theory is the RG-flow IR endpoint of some massive deformation of a UV bootstrap-allowed CFT; the cobordism classification is then the structure of trivial-flow endpoints emerging from bootstrap-allowed UV theories. The C-theorem (Zamolodchikov 1986) and a-theorem (Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) establish information-monotonicity along RG flows in 2D and 4D respectively; the rg-info aspiration extends this monotonicity to a derivation: cobordism classes are RG-trivial fibres over the bootstrap UV moduli space. NOTE: this edge points opposite to the v15.5 edge edge-xc-bootstrap-derives-cobordism-aspired (which is targeted by ads-cft-instance and reads bootstrap-derives-from-cobordism via the bulk-boundary / SymTFT direction). The two aspirations are non-conflicting: AdS/CFT works in the bulk-boundary direction; RG-info works in the UV→IR direction. Authoring both as separate edges exercises the multi-edge-between-same-pair pattern flagged in v15.5 as previously unused. cell_refs pin 5 source-side cells: Ω^{Spin}_2 = ℤ₂ (2D fermionic invertible phases ↔ minimal-model bootstrap IR endpoints); Ω^{Spin}_4 = ℤ (4D Pontryagin-class invertible phase ↔ 4D bootstrap IR); Ω^{Spin^c}_2 = ℤ (IQHE-class ↔ 2D bootstrap chiral central charge); Ω^{Spin^c}_4 = ℤ² (4D spin^c invertible ↔ 4D bootstrap); Ω^{Pin+}_4 = ℤ₁₆ (Wang-Senthil anomaly ↔ 4D bootstrap with time-reversal). Discharge: established once a specific cobordism class is shown to be the RG-flow IR endpoint of a specific bootstrap-allowed UV CFT, with the RG flow respecting the c/a-theorem ordering.
citations: Zamolodchikov 1986, JETP Lett. 43, 730 (c-theorem)
Komargodski, Schwimmer 2011, JHEP 12, 099 (a-theorem in 4D)
Kapustin, Seiberg 2014, JHEP 04, 001 (higher-form symmetries and RG)
Freed, Hopkins 2021, Geom. Topol. 25, 1165 (reflection positivity and invertible phases as RG endpoints)
Apenko 2012, Physica A 391, 62 (information theory and RG)
Beny, Osborne 2015, New J. Phys. 17, 083005 (RG as quantum-information renormalization)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Modular tensor categories specialize the bootstrap exclusion regions: MTCs are the algebraic data of rational 2D CFTs, which are the sub-domain of bootstrap-allowed CFTs satisfying rationality (finitely many primaries, modular-invariant partition function). Moore-Seiberg 1989 established the categorical equivalence between rational 2D CFTs and MTCs, making this specializes-relation rigorous: every MTC produces a rational CFT in the bootstrap-allowed region, and every rational CFT corresponds to an MTC. The 7 cell_refs pin representative MTCs (Fibonacci, Lee-Yang, Ising, SU(2)_2, tricritical-Ising, toric code, SU(2)_3) that occupy specific points within the d=2 bootstrap exclusion region. Note: a finer subtype `constrains` would more precisely capture the structural-causal relation (MTC modular S-matrix structurally constrains the partition function of any bulk-boundary partner CFT). Such an edge is distinct from this `specializes` edge: this one is about the rational sub-case as a SET; the constrains edge would be about how MTC structure NARROWS the form of any boundary CFT realized as MTC bulk-boundary partner. The `constrains` subtype does not exist in v15.3 schema; this distinct edge is documented in findings as a v16-pending authoring item rather than authored here with a misfit subtype.
citations: Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177
Bakalov, Kirillov 2001, AMS (textbook proof of categorical equivalence)
Cardy 1986, Nucl. Phys. B 270, 186 (operator content of two-dimensional conformally invariant theories)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Modular tensor categories structurally constrain the form of any boundary CFT realized as MTC bulk-boundary partner — even before identifying the boundary as rational. The constraints are: (1) the partition function on the torus must transform under SL(2,Z) according to the MTC's modular S and T matrices; (2) the OPE coefficients must factor through the MTC's F-symbols (pentagon-axiom-consistent) and R-symbols (hexagon-axiom-consistent); (3) the chiral central charge c (mod 8 for bosonic MTCs, mod 16 for fermionic) must match the MTC's encoded chiral anomaly. These three constraints together fix the form (not just the set-membership) of any boundary CFT realized as a bulk-boundary partner of a given MTC. This is structurally distinct from the v15.8 specializes edge between the same pair (edge-xc-mtc-specializes-bootstrap): the specializes edge identifies WHICH boundary CFTs are MTC-realized (the rational sub-case as a subset); this constrains edge identifies HOW the MTC structure narrows the form of any candidate boundary CFT realized as MTC partner. The two edges target the same FC pair but capture different content — set-membership vs structural-causal restriction. Status: established. Moore-Seiberg axioms make the constraint rigorous; Cardy's modular-invariance constraint analysis (1986) gives the constructive form; the categorical-equivalence theorem (Moore-Seiberg 1989, refined Bakalov-Kirillov 2001) closes the structural-causal direction. The 7 cell_refs pin the same MTCs as the specializes edge — these are the canonical cases where both the subset and the structural-causal readings apply.
citations: Cardy 1986, Nucl. Phys. B 270, 186 (modular invariance constraints on the operator content)
Moore, Seiberg 1989, Commun. Math. Phys. 123, 177 (the rational CFT axioms — the structural-causal direction)
Bakalov, Kirillov 2001, AMS (textbook proof of MTC ↔ rational CFT categorical equivalence)
Fuchs, Runkel, Schweigert 2002, Nucl. Phys. B 624, 452 (TFT construction of RCFT correlators — explicit constructive form)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Generalized-symmetry anomalies structurally constrain the bootstrap exclusion regions. A CFT with generalized-symmetry data S must satisfy: (i) the OPE coefficients factor through S-equivariant tensor products; (ii) the partition function transforms under S's modular / cobordism class; (iii) the operator spectrum respects S-graded multiplet structure. These constraints narrow the bootstrap-allowed region within the sector with fixed S. Komargodski-Sulejmanpasic-Unsal 2017 made the constraint precise for 4D pure YM: the Z_N center 1-form anomaly rules out trivially-gapped phases. Subsequent work (Cordova-Dumitrescu-Intriligator; Brennan-Cordova; Su et al.) extended to other dimensions and symmetry types. Status established. Subtype `constrains` (v16). This is the SECOND authored use of `constrains` after MTC→bootstrap (v16). The constrains-relation here is genuinely distinct from any specializes / derives-from reading: the symmetry data doesn't IDENTIFY a sub-case of bootstrap CFTs; it RESTRICTS the form of any CFT realized with that symmetry data.
citations: Komargodski, Sulejmanpasic, Unsal 2017, Phys. Rev. B 97, 054418
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184
Brennan, Cordova 2020, JHEP 02, 132 (anomalies and constraints)
Su, Wang, You 2021, JHEP 02, 002 (anomaly matching constraints for bootstrap)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Higgs/Coulomb branches specialize the bootstrap exclusion regions: 4D N=2 SCFTs are a specialization of bootstrap-allowed 4D CFTs, specifically those with N=2 superconformal symmetry. The bootstrap exclusion bounds for 4D N=2 SCFTs (e.g., the bounds on central charges and OPE coefficients in the chiral algebra protected sector) form a subset of the broader 4D bootstrap region. The Higgs/Coulomb branch structure is the moduli-space classification of THESE specific bootstrap-allowed theories. cell_refs pin 5 representative Coulomb branch cells whose corresponding SCFTs sit in the bootstrap-allowed region: SU(2) N_f=4, A_1 Argyres-Douglas, E_6 Minahan-Nemeschansky, T_3 class-S, N=4 SU(2). The bootstrap entry's cell-bs-d4-N2-SCFT is the canonical bootstrap-side anchor.
citations: Beem, Rastelli, van Rees 2014, JHEP 05, 020 (4D N=2 bootstrap)
Lemos, van Rees 2016, JHEP 12, 040 (chiral algebra bootstrap)
Beem 2024 review (bootstrap-SCFT-VOA connections)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: rg-info program aspires that SymTFT (generalized-symmetry) data derives from bootstrap CFT data via RG-flow continuation: tracking a UV bootstrap-allowed CFT down to the IR fixed point, the symmetry data (anomalies, higher-form charges, categorical structures) is preserved or transforms in controlled ways, ultimately determining the IR phase's SymTFT classification. Reading: 'generalized-symmetries [output] derives from bootstrap [UV input] via RG flow'. Same UV-IR direction as the v15.7 rg-info edge `cobordism → bootstrap [derives-from, aspired]`. Literature-strength: MEDIUM. Sub-cases established: the C-theorem and a-theorem (Zamolodchikov 1986; Komargodski-Schwimmer 2011) establish RG-monotonicity of central charges, which constrains the flow of bootstrap-allowed CFTs. Cordova-Dumitrescu 2-group analyses show that 2-group anomalies flow through RG with specific matching conditions, partially deriving SymTFT data from UV bootstrap data. Aspired for: general derivation of SymTFT classification (especially non-invertible / categorical cases) from bootstrap UV data alone. Status `partial` reflects this: the C/A-theorem case works rigorously, but the generalization to full categorical SymTFT classification is aspired. `targeted_by: rg-info` records the program. cell_refs pin 4 SymTFT cells whose UV-IR derivation from bootstrap is actively studied. Multi-edge with v17 edge `generalized-symmetries constrains cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions [established]`: the constrains-edge says SymTFT anomaly DATA narrows bootstrap-allowed CFTs (structural constraint); this derives-from-edge says SymTFT FRAMEWORK (the classification itself) is OPERATIONALLY obtained from RG-flow analysis of bootstrap CFTs. Distinct content; same FC-pair.
citations: Zamolodchikov 1986, JETP Lett. 43, 730 (c-theorem)
Komargodski, Schwimmer 2011, JHEP 12, 099 (a-theorem)
Cordova, Dumitrescu, Intriligator 2019, JHEP 02, 184 (RG flows of 2-group anomalies)
Apenko 2012, Physica A 391, 62 (information-theoretic RG)
Beny, Osborne 2015, New J. Phys. 17, 083005 (RG as quantum-information renormalization)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: ads-cft-instance program aspires that hadronic-spectrum data derives from bootstrap-allowed CFT data: in the large-N limit of QCD (Polchinski, Strassler, Maldacena), the theory is approximately conformal at high energies, and the hadronic spectrum emerges as the spectrum of bound states in a holographic dual 5D AdS theory. The bootstrap-allowed CFT structure constrains which hadrons are realizable; conversely, hadron observations constrain bootstrap-derived holographic-QCD models. Established for: large-N pure-glue holographic QCD glueball-spectrum predictions (Hashimoto-Tahara 1998; Brower-Mathur-Tan 2000) qualitatively match lattice glueball spectrum. Aspired for: full hadronic-spectrum derivation including chiral-symmetry-breaking patterns and exotic-state predictions from holographic QCD. cell_refs pin 4 hadrons whose holographic-QCD derivations are actively pursued. Status aspired; the program is real and active but the full hadronic-spectrum-from-bootstrap derivation remains incomplete.
citations: Polchinski, Strassler 2002, Phys. Rev. Lett. 88, 031601 (string theory and hard scattering)
Hashimoto, Tahara 1998 (early holographic QCD)
Brower, Mathur, Tan 2000, Nucl. Phys. B 587, 249 (glueball spectrum from holography)
Erlich, Katz, Son, Stephanov 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 95, 261602 (QCD and a holographic model of hadrons)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: ads-cft-instance program aspires that FQH topological orders derive from bootstrap-allowed CFT data: the (1+1)D edge theories of FQH states ARE conformal field theories, and the bootstrap-allowed-CFT region constrains which edge CFTs (and hence which bulk FQH topological orders) can exist. Specifically: edge CFTs at Laughlin ν=1/m are c=1 chiral bosons; edge CFTs at Pfaffian 5/2 include a Majorana sector; edge CFTs at Read-Rezayi 12/5 are Z_3 parafermion CFTs. Each of these is a specific bootstrap-allowed CFT; bootstrap constraints on these CFTs structurally constrain FQH topological orders. AdS/CFT framing: a 2+1D bulk topological order corresponds to a 1+1D boundary CFT under the bulk-boundary correspondence. Status `aspired` — the bulk-boundary correspondence is established for specific cases but full derivation of FQH classification from bootstrap is aspirational. cell_refs pin 3 FQH cells with the cleanest edge-CFT realizations.
citations: Wen 1990, Phys. Rev. B 41, 12838 (FQH edge as chiral CFT)
Cappelli, Trugenberger, Zemba 1993, Nucl. Phys. B 396, 465 (W_1+infinity algebra of FQH edges)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: The ads-cft-instance program aspires that black-hole thermodynamic and microstate-structure properties derive from bootstrap-allowed CFT data via the AdS/CFT holographic dictionary. Specifically: (i) Strominger-Vafa 1996 derived the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy of specific BPS BHs by counting BPS states in the dual CFT — a partial derivation establishing the program's empirical bite for a special class of BHs (extremal, supersymmetric, mostly AdS); (ii) general astrophysical (Kerr, non-extremal, asymptotically-flat) BHs are not yet derived from CFT in this sense — that's the aspired part; (iii) the Page-curve / island-formula program (Almheiri et al. 2019+) extends the bootstrap-CFT-derivation of BH thermodynamics to evaporating BHs in specific holographic setups, with partial bite for the information paradox. cell_refs pin 3 BH cells where the holographic-bootstrap derivation is most actively pursued (SMBHs because of their thermodynamic importance; merging stellar-mass BHs because of GW horizon-test relevance). Status `aspired` because the bridge from holographic-CFT data to astrophysical BH observations is still under construction. Literature strength: STRONG for the BPS-BH entropy-counting (foundational); WEAK for general astrophysical BH derivation (program is real and active but most claims remain at gestural-to-partial level for astrophysical applicability). This edge mirrors the FQH→bootstrap-aspired and hadrons→bootstrap-aspired edges in the existing data (same ads-cft program, different phenomenon-FC targets).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BPS-BH entropy from CFT counting)
Maldacena 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 231 (AdS/CFT correspondence)
Witten 1998, Adv. Theor. Math. Phys. 2, 253 (AdS/CFT thermodynamics)
Almheiri, Engelhardt, Marolf, Maxfield 2019, JHEP 12, 063 (island formula); Penington 2020, JHEP 09, 002 (page curve)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Each real critical system specializes a specific bootstrap-allowed CFT — the unique CFT at the corresponding Wilson-Fisher fixed point of the universality class. The bootstrap exclusion regions in cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions pin the critical-exponent values to high precision (3D-Ising ν = 0.6299709..., 3D O(2) ν = 0.6719..., 3D O(3) ν = 0.7117..., etc.); the real systems must lie on the bootstrap-allowed boundary curves. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR THE CRITICAL-PHENOMENA SECTOR, explicit in PROJECT_GOAL_PHENOMENON_LAYER §5. Status `established` for the universality-class identifications: every cell's universality class is settled. The exponent-value match is precision-confirmed for 3D-Ising and 3D-Heisenberg cells, but contains the 3D-O(2) / 4He tension at high precision (sub-edge-status note: the edge as a whole is `established` but contains a sub-tension cell). Literature strength: STRONG. cell_refs pin 8 cells where the bootstrap pairing is most operationally active.
citations: El-Showk, Paulos, Poland, Rychkov, Simmons-Duffin, Vichi 2014, JSTAT 8, P08015 (3D Ising bootstrap)
Kos, Poland, Simmons-Duffin 2014, JHEP 11, 109 (3D Ising precision)
Chester, Landry, Liu, Poland, Simmons-Duffin, Su, Vichi 2020, Phys. Rev. D 101, 084017 (3D O(2))
Chester et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. D 104, 105013 (3D O(3))

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: The ads-cft-instance program targets bootstrap-CFT derivation of cosmological observables through dS/CFT correspondence (Strominger 2001) and the cosmological-bootstrap program (Arkani-Hamed-Maldacena 2015; Pajer et al. 2020+). Specifically: (i) inflationary correlators (CMB anisotropy, primordial non-Gaussianity, primordial GW spectrum) should derive from a CFT-correlator on the boundary of de Sitter via dS/CFT; (ii) the cosmological-bootstrap program seeks to derive cosmological correlation functions from symmetry + unitarity + analyticity constraints without invoking specific inflationary potentials; (iii) recent work (Pajer-Stefanyszyn-Welling 2020+) has reproduced single-field inflation correlators via boundary-CFT methods. Status `aspired` because: while the framework exists and has produced specific results for inflation correlators, no specific cosmological observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT without additional physical assumptions. cell_refs pin 3 cells (the inflation-era observables) where the bootstrap-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (real research program with growing technical results; observational confirmation pending future precision data). This edge mirrors the ads-cft-instance program targeting of hadronic-states / FQH / compact-astro from prior phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Strominger 2001, JHEP 10, 034 (dS/CFT)
Arkani-Hamed, Maldacena 2015, arXiv:1503.08043 (cosmological bootstrap setup)
Pajer, Stefanyszyn, Welling 2020, JHEP 11, 116 (cosmological bootstrap technical results)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: BH ringdown spectra (QNMs) and EMRI signals connect to holographic-CFT calculations via the ads-cft-instance program. Specifically: (i) Kerr-BH quasi-normal modes and the Teukolsky equation map onto CFT-spectrum calculations through AdS/CFT (for AdS-Kerr-BH variants) and dS-Kerr quasi-normal mode analyses; (ii) the no-hair theorem itself has CFT counterparts (rigidity of CFT spectrum); (iii) EMRI signals from supermassive BHs would test holographic-CFT predictions at very high precision (10⁵ orbital cycles in LISA-band). Status `aspired` because the holographic-derivation of GW phenomenology is technically active but no specific GW observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT — the framework applies in principle. cell_refs pin 2 cells where holographic-relevance is most active. Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. This edge continues the ads-cft-instance pattern across phenomenon-FCs (hadrons, FQH, compact-astro, cosmology, now gravitational-waves — 5 of 10 phenomenon-FCs target bootstrap aspirationally).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BH-CFT correspondence)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)
Maldacena, Susskind 2013, Fortsch. Phys. 61, 781 (ER=EPR; related holographic-BH framework)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Two derivation pathways. (i) Holographic-QGP: AdS-CFT duality predicts strongly-coupled gauge-theory thermal transport coefficients — η/s ≥ 1/(4π) (KSS bound from Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005). RHIC + LHC QGP measurements extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π), close to KSS bound, validating the holographic-fluid framework. This is one of the cleanest empirical hits of the ads-cft-instance program in real experimental data — promotes the edge status from `aspired` to `partial` because the η/s prediction has been quantitatively validated. (ii) Chiral-transition universality: massless-2-flavor chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984), classified by bootstrap O(4) exclusion regions — though physical-quark-mass realizations are smoothed to crossover. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the bootstrap connection is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for KSS-η/s match (genuine empirical hit); MEDIUM for chiral-transition O(4) (real-crit overlap). This edge is notably NOT purely `aspired` — the η/s prediction is the cleanest existing ads-cft-instance empirical validation across all phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601 (KSS bound)
Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301 (η/s extraction from data)

Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge — book-length AdS-CFT-to-QCD overview)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: Thermal-QED + relativistic-magnetohydrodynamics admits CFT-like high-energy limits; holographic models of accretion-flow and jet acceleration use AdS-CFT for strongly-coupled plasma dynamics. Status `aspired` — the connections exist at framework level but no specific EM phenomenon has been derived directly from bootstrap-CFT. cell_refs pin 2 cells where the connection is most active (AGN jet plasma + UHE acceleration). Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. Continues the ads-cft-instance program targeting across phenomenon-FCs.

Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)

cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions.description: N=2 SCFT bootstrap-bounded observables derive from cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions specialized to N=2 supersymmetric stress-tensor 4-point functions. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-bootstrap-a-c-bound derives from cft-bootstrap general unitarity + Hofman-Maldacena energy-positivity, specialized to N=2; (ii) AD H_0 Δ_u = 6/5 has been independently verified via N=2 bootstrap to high precision (Beem et al. 2014); (iii) chiral-algebra-2D-4D correspondence cells cross-reference 2D-CFT bootstrap data for the associated chiral algebras. Moore-Seiberg 1989 established the categorical equivalence between rational 2D CFTs and MTCs, making this specializes-relation rigorous: every MTC produces a rational CFT in the bootstrap-allowed region, and every rational CFT corresponds to an MTC.
