Specifically: (i) WIMPs in SUSY scenarios derive from the supersymmetric extension of the SM (neutralino = mixture of bino + wino + higgsinos, all in SM-gauge-group representations extended by superpartners); (ii) QCD axion couples to SM gauge bosons via anomaly, derives from PQ-extension of SM with axion as Goldstone of broken PQ U(1); (iii) sterile neutrinos are gauge-singlet right-handed neutrinos, extending the SM rep content with N_R; (iv) dark photon is a kinetic-mixing extension of SM U(1)_Y with U(1)' (mediated by ε F_μν F'^μν); (v) gravitino-LSP is the spin-3/2 superpartner of graviton in SUSY-extended supergravity. Status `partial` because: (a) some candidates (PBHs, MACHOs, fuzzy-DM-from-stringy-axiverse) do not derive from SM rep content — PBHs are gravitational, MACHOs are baryonic-compact, fuzzy-DM derives from stringy / cosmological structures beyond SM; (b) for the candidates that do derive from SM extensions, the derivation is conjectural at the level of specific BSM-extension predictions. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the SM-rep derivation operates most directly. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR PARTICLE-DM CANDIDATES. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (DM-as-BSM-particle is a major program); MEDIUM for specific candidate predictions.
citations: Bertone, Hooper, Silk 2005, Phys. Rep. 405, 279 (DM-from-BSM-SM framework)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996, Phys. Rep. 267, 195 (SUSY-LSP DM review)
Holdom 1986, Phys. Lett. B 166, 196 (dark-photon kinetic mixing)

dark-matter-candidates.description: All DM candidates — particle or compact-object — are gravitating matter satisfying Poincaré invariance + diffeomorphism invariance. For particle candidates, the connection is via standard quantum-field theory on a Poincaré-invariant background. For compact-object candidates (PBHs, MACHOs), the connection is more direct: each PBH or MACHO is a GR solution (Schwarzschild / Kerr for PBHs; ordinary stellar-mass solutions for MACHOs) and therefore specializes the spacetime-symmetry-groups cells in the same sense as compact-astrophysical-objects (v21). Fuzzy DM specializes Poincaré invariance via its bosonic-field action on Minkowski background. Status `established` — GR + Poincaré framework is settled and DM candidates satisfy it as a baseline. Literature strength: MEDIUM (framework-level specialization; specific tests of Lorentz-invariance for DM candidates are limited).
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Relativ. 17, 4 (GR tests overview)
Carr, Kühnel 2020 (PBHs as GR solutions)

dark-matter-candidates.description: Several DM candidates aspire to derive from GUT-extended frameworks: (i) SUSY-LSP WIMPs are naturally embedded in GUT-extended SUSY (where the GUT-symmetry-breaking scale sets the SUSY-breaking-mediation scale); (ii) sterile-ν as N_R derives most naturally from SO(10) 16-plet which contains N_R as a singlet under SU(5) ⊂ SO(10) — gives keV-scale sterile-ν via seesaw at high scales; (iii) gravitino-LSP-DM connects to SUSY-breaking-mediation which is most naturally embedded in GUT frameworks; (iv) string-axiverse ALPs derive from string-theoretic compactifications that include GUT-style gauge structure. Status `aspired` because no specific GUT-derivation has been observationally confirmed; the connections are at the framework level. cell_refs pin 4 cells where the GUT-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM. This edge mirrors the neutrino-sector-phenomenology v23 edge derives-from su5-gut-rep-content — both phenomenon-FCs target su5/GUT as the most-natural high-scale derivation framework.
citations: Mohapatra, Senjanović 1980, Phys. Rev. Lett. 44, 912 (SO(10) seesaw → sterile-ν DM)
Jungman, Kamionkowski, Griest 1996 (SUSY-LSP in GUT frameworks)
Arvanitaki et al. 2010 (string axiverse)

dark-matter-candidates.description: MOND was originally proposed as a DM alternative — accounts for galaxy rotation curves without invoking particle dark matter. Specializes the broader dark-matter-candidates question by offering modified-gravity rather than particle-physics resolution. Status `partial` — MOND succeeds galactically but Bullet Cluster falsifies pure MOND at cluster scales (cf. dark-matter-candidates v25 evidence cells). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — seventh such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365
Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

cosmological-observations.description: Cosmological observations specialize the FRW (Friedmann-Robertson-Walker) solutions of general relativity, which specialize spacetime-symmetry-groups. Specifically: (i) FRW cosmology is a specific class of GR solutions with maximally-symmetric spatial slices (specializes cell-stsym-maximal-isotropy or similar in spacetime-symmetry-groups); (ii) the CMB acoustic-peak structure is sensitive to the global FRW expansion + GR perturbation theory; (iii) the BAO sound-horizon is a clean GR-encoded length scale; (iv) spatial curvature Ω_K bounds the deviation from flat FRW; (v) the Hubble constant measures the FRW expansion rate; (vi) primordial gravitational waves are tensor modes of the FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — FRW-cosmology + GR-perturbation-theory is the foundational framework for cosmology. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE FOR COSMOLOGY.
citations: Friedmann 1922; Robertson 1935; Walker 1937 (FRW framework)
Mukhanov 2005, Physical Foundations of Cosmology
Weinberg 2008, Cosmology

cosmological-observations.description: Several cosmological observables derive directly from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BBN-era nuclear physics involves SM-content nucleons (proton, neutron) and SM-content nuclear-physics cross-sections — primordial D/H, ⁴He/H, ⁷Li/H are computed from SM particle physics + BBN framework; (ii) recombination-epoch atomic physics (the optical-depth τ_reio and the acoustic-oscillation damping) involves SM-content electrons + protons; (iii) the cosmological Σm_ν bound depends on SM neutrino content; (iv) CMB-acoustic-peak heights depend on SM neutrino effective number N_eff (Planck constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with 3 SM neutrinos). Status `partial` because: (a) some cosmological observables depend on BSM physics that the SM-rep-content FC doesn't capture (DM candidates, DE candidates); (b) the BBN nuclear cross-sections are partly experimentally-measured rather than derived. cell_refs pin 5 cells where SM-rep derivation is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (BBN, recombination, neutrino-decoupling are all SM-derived).
citations: Wagoner, Fowler, Hoyle 1967, Astrophys. J. 148, 3 (BBN from SM physics)
Cyburt, Fields, Olive, Yeh 2016, Rev. Mod. Phys. 88, 015004 (BBN review)
Aghanim et al. (Planck) 2020 (N_eff measurement)

cosmological-observations.description: The ads-cft-instance program targets bootstrap-CFT derivation of cosmological observables through dS/CFT correspondence (Strominger 2001) and the cosmological-bootstrap program (Arkani-Hamed-Maldacena 2015; Pajer et al. 2020+). Specifically: (i) inflationary correlators (CMB anisotropy, primordial non-Gaussianity, primordial GW spectrum) should derive from a CFT-correlator on the boundary of de Sitter via dS/CFT; (ii) the cosmological-bootstrap program seeks to derive cosmological correlation functions from symmetry + unitarity + analyticity constraints without invoking specific inflationary potentials; (iii) recent work (Pajer-Stefanyszyn-Welling 2020+) has reproduced single-field inflation correlators via boundary-CFT methods. Status `aspired` because: while the framework exists and has produced specific results for inflation correlators, no specific cosmological observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT without additional physical assumptions. cell_refs pin 3 cells (the inflation-era observables) where the bootstrap-derivation is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (real research program with growing technical results; observational confirmation pending future precision data). This edge mirrors the ads-cft-instance program targeting of hadronic-states / FQH / compact-astro from prior phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Strominger 2001, JHEP 10, 034 (dS/CFT)
Arkani-Hamed, Maldacena 2015, arXiv:1503.08043 (cosmological bootstrap setup)
Pajer, Stefanyszyn, Welling 2020, JHEP 11, 116 (cosmological bootstrap technical results)

gravitational-wave-event-catalog.description: GW events specialize Poincaré-invariant GR + perturbation theory on background spacetimes. Specifically: (i) BBH/BNS/NSBH mergers are GR solutions emitting gravitational radiation, specializing the cell-stsym-GR or analogous cell of spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) ringdown phase is BH perturbation theory in Kerr geometry, specializing Kerr-metric content; (iii) GW propagation at c specializes the Poincaré-invariance + null-cone-propagation prediction; (iv) the no-hair-theorem tests specialize the Kerr-uniqueness result; (v) PTA nHz background specializes the GW stress-energy tensor + cosmological FRW perturbation theory. Status `established` — GR + numerical relativity is the foundational framework and waveform matches confirm at high precision. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY PHENOMENON-FC ↔ STRUCTURAL-FC EDGE.
citations: Einstein 1916 (GR)
Maggiore 2008, Gravitational Waves Vol. 1 (review)

gravitational-wave-event-catalog.description: FIRST PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET. Each GW merger event specializes a merger of objects classified in compact-astrophysical-objects. Specifically: (i) GW150914 specializes a merger of two stellar-mass BHs (cells cell-compact-stellar-BH-XRB / cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW from compact-astro v21); (ii) GW170817 specializes a merger of two canonical neutron stars (cell-compact-NS-canonical in compact-astro); (iii) GW190521 specializes an intermediate-mass-BBH merger (cell-compact-IMBH-gap in compact-astro); (iv) GW190814 and GW230529 specialize lower-mass-gap mergers, populating the boundary between compact-astro cell-compact-NS-massive and cell-compact-stellar-BH; (v) GWTC-3 BBH population is the empirical realization of the stellar-mass BH population in compact-astro. The relationship is `specializes` because each event is a specific instance (merger of two specific objects) of the abstract object-classes in compact-astro. Status `partial` because: (a) not all GW event categories specialize compact-astro cells (NANOGrav PTA nHz signal is a stochastic background, not an individual merger of compact-astro objects; continuous-wave-pulsar searches are individual NSs not mergers; superradiance-bounded ultralight-boson searches are not even compact-object events); (b) the directional `specializes` relation is reasonable for events-to-object-types but the reverse (compact-astro object inferences derive from GW events as data) also holds — the relationship is genuinely intertwined. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the specialization is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (events ARE mergers of objects); MEDIUM for the directional interpretation. This edge is justified by direct structural relation, not by the desideratum of producing a phen↔phen edge — the relation would be drawn independently.
citations: Abbott et al. 2016, Phys. Rev. Lett. 116, 061102 (GW150914 = stellar-mass BBH merger)
Abbott et al. 2017, Phys. Rev. Lett. 119, 161101 (GW170817 = BNS merger)
Abbott et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 101102 (GW190521 = IMBH-region merger)

gravitational-wave-event-catalog.description: BNS merger events derive matter-side physics from SM rep content. Specifically: (i) BNS-merger kilonova r-process nucleosynthesis (Au, Pt, lanthanide production at AT2017gfo) involves SM-content nuclear physics + GR-MHD; (ii) BNS-merger tidal deformability constraints on dense-matter EOS (Λ < 800 from GW170817) connect to SM-content hadronic physics — specializes the cell-compact-NS-EOS-bound content from compact-astro and hadronic-states. Status `partial` because: (a) BBH events do not derive from SM-rep-content (they are vacuum-GR solutions); (b) only the BNS / NSBH subset of GW events has SM-rep relevance; (c) the derivation is at the matter-physics-input level, not the basic GW signal level. cell_refs pin 2 cells (BNS merger and multi-messenger coincidence). Literature strength: MEDIUM. This connection mirrors compact-astrophysical-objects v21 derives-from sm-rep-content edge for NS-EOS cells.
citations: Abbott et al. 2017 (GW170817 tidal deformability)
Kasen, Metzger, Barnes, Quataert, Ramirez-Ruiz 2017, Nature 551, 80 (kilonova r-process)

gravitational-wave-event-catalog.description: BH ringdown spectra (QNMs) and EMRI signals connect to holographic-CFT calculations via the ads-cft-instance program. Specifically: (i) Kerr-BH quasi-normal modes and the Teukolsky equation map onto CFT-spectrum calculations through AdS/CFT (for AdS-Kerr-BH variants) and dS-Kerr quasi-normal mode analyses; (ii) the no-hair theorem itself has CFT counterparts (rigidity of CFT spectrum); (iii) EMRI signals from supermassive BHs would test holographic-CFT predictions at very high precision (10⁵ orbital cycles in LISA-band). Status `aspired` because the holographic-derivation of GW phenomenology is technically active but no specific GW observation has been definitively derived from bootstrap-CFT — the framework applies in principle. cell_refs pin 2 cells where holographic-relevance is most active. Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. This edge continues the ads-cft-instance pattern across phenomenon-FCs (hadrons, FQH, compact-astro, cosmology, now gravitational-waves — 5 of 10 phenomenon-FCs target bootstrap aspirationally).
citations: Strominger, Vafa 1996, Phys. Lett. B 379, 99 (BH-CFT correspondence)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)
Maldacena, Susskind 2013, Fortsch. Phys. 61, 781 (ER=EPR; related holographic-BH framework)

gravitational-wave-event-catalog.description: Modified-gravity tests specialize gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 — specifically the GW170817 multi-messenger event cell which provides the key graviton-speed bound. cell_refs list 7 cells where GW catalog observations are the falsifying test. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — fifth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — many modified-gravity variants falsified by GW170817 (TeVeS, Horndeski G_5, bigravity, Galileons, Einstein-aether); others constrained but surviving (dRGT massive gravity, narrow Horndeski subset).
citations: Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817)
Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382

qcd-phase-diagram.description: QCD-phase-diagram derives directly from the SU(3)_c gauge sector of sm-rep-content. Specifically: (i) the quark cells (QL-g1, QL-g2, QL-g3 and right-handed quark cells in sm-rep-content) are the fundamental fermionic matter content; (ii) the gluon cell (cell-gluons-gNA in sm-rep-content) is the gauge field; (iii) asymptotic freedom (Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) is a property of the SU(3)_c rep-content of sm-rep — non-abelian gauge with N_f = 6 quarks gives β_0 > 0; (iv) all phase transitions in QCD-phase-diagram are transitions of this gauge content under varying thermodynamic conditions. Status `established` because QCD-as-SU(3)_c is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. THIS IS THE PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. cell_refs pin 5 cells where the sm-rep derivation is most direct.
citations: Gross, Wilczek 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1343
Politzer 1973, Phys. Rev. Lett. 30, 1346
Gell-Mann 1964, Phys. Lett. 8, 214

qcd-phase-diagram.description: QCD-phase-diagram specializes Poincaré-invariant QFT (T = 0 ground state) and the finite-temperature extension via Euclidean spacetime / Matsubara frequencies. Hydrodynamic flow in QGP specializes relativistic-fluid GR-coupled hydrodynamics. The crossover at T_c is a thermodynamic property of finite-T QFT specializing standard Wightman-axiom QFT on Minkowski background. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG.
citations: Kapusta, Gale 2006, Finite-Temperature Field Theory (2nd ed., Cambridge)
Le Bellac 2000, Thermal Field Theory (Cambridge)

qcd-phase-diagram.description: Two derivation pathways. (i) Holographic-QGP: AdS-CFT duality predicts strongly-coupled gauge-theory thermal transport coefficients — η/s ≥ 1/(4π) (KSS bound from Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005). RHIC + LHC QGP measurements extract η/s ≈ (1-3) × 1/(4π), close to KSS bound, validating the holographic-fluid framework. This is one of the cleanest empirical hits of the ads-cft-instance program in real experimental data — promotes the edge status from `aspired` to `partial` because the η/s prediction has been quantitatively validated. (ii) Chiral-transition universality: massless-2-flavor chiral transition is in 3D O(4) universality class (Pisarski-Wilczek 1984), classified by bootstrap O(4) exclusion regions — though physical-quark-mass realizations are smoothed to crossover. cell_refs pin 3 cells where the bootstrap connection is most active. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for KSS-η/s match (genuine empirical hit); MEDIUM for chiral-transition O(4) (real-crit overlap). This edge is notably NOT purely `aspired` — the η/s prediction is the cleanest existing ads-cft-instance empirical validation across all phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Kovtun, Son, Starinets 2005, Phys. Rev. Lett. 94, 111601 (KSS bound)
Romatschke, Romatschke 2007, Phys. Rev. Lett. 99, 172301 (η/s extraction from data)
Pisarski, Wilczek 1984, Phys. Rev. D 29, 338 (chiral O(4))
Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge — book-length AdS-CFT-to-QCD overview)

electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-.description: Photons specialize Poincaré-invariant massless gauge bosons (Wigner classification of irreducible Poincaré representations as helicity-±1 massless particles). EM propagation on FRW + curved-spacetime backgrounds (CMB free-streaming through expansion; gravitational lensing in clusters; GR redshift of all EM emission) specializes the GR / Minkowski framework. GW + GR-test cells (GW170817 GW-EM speed = c) bound photon-graviton-equivalence. Hawking radiation derives from GR + QFT on curved background. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Wigner 1939, Ann. Math. 40, 149 (Poincaré irreps)
Jackson 1999, Classical Electrodynamics (3rd ed., Wiley)

electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-.description: The photon IS the U(1)_em gauge boson of sm-rep-content (specifically the (1,1,0) sub-content arising as the unbroken combination of B and W³ in electroweak symmetry breaking). All EM-phenomena cells derive from this gauge structure. Specifically: (i) thermal/blackbody emission derives from QED + thermal-equilibrium; (ii) synchrotron / inverse-Compton derive from QED + relativistic charged-fermion electroweak couplings; (iii) hadronic-pion-decay-gamma derives from QCD pion content + electroweak π⁰ → γγ amplitude (chiral anomaly); (iv) FEL-coherent + laser-coherent derive from QED stimulated-emission processes. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG. This is the primary structural-content derivation.
citations: Glashow 1961, Nucl. Phys. 22, 579 (electroweak unification)
Weinberg 1967, Phys. Rev. Lett. 19, 1264; Salam 1968 (electroweak)
Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-.description: THIRD PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET (after v21 compact-astro→hadronic-states and v27 gravitational-waves→compact-astro). Many EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-object phenomena: (i) cell-em-crab-multiband specializes compact-astro pulsar-wind-nebula cells; (ii) cell-em-AGN-jet-synchrotron specializes SMBH cells; (iii) cell-em-GRBs-multiband specializes BNS-merger (short-GRB) + collapsar (long-GRB) cells; (iv) cell-em-GW170817-kilonova specializes BNS-merger cells (same physical object that GW catalog v27 also specializes); (v) cell-em-IceCube-TXS-multimessenger specializes blazar SMBH cells; (vi) cell-em-LHAASO-PeV-gamma specializes galactic-PeVatron-source cells (SNR + PWN populations in compact-astro). Status `partial` because not all EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-objects — laboratory-coherent sources (FEL, lasers) and thermal-cosmological-backgrounds (CMB) do not derive from compact objects. cell_refs pin 6 cells with direct compact-astro specialization. The relation is honest physics: many EM cells ARE compact-object EM emission organized by photon-spectrum rather than object-class. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework.
citations: Hester 2008, Annu. Rev. Astron. Astrophys. 46, 127 (PWN/Crab as compact-object EM)
Mészáros 2006, Rep. Prog. Phys. 69, 2259 (GRB compact-object connection)
Begelman, Blandford, Rees 1984, Rev. Mod. Phys. 56, 255 (AGN jets from SMBHs)

electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-.description: Thermal-QED + relativistic-magnetohydrodynamics admits CFT-like high-energy limits; holographic models of accretion-flow and jet acceleration use AdS-CFT for strongly-coupled plasma dynamics. Status `aspired` — the connections exist at framework level but no specific EM phenomenon has been derived directly from bootstrap-CFT. cell_refs pin 2 cells where the connection is most active (AGN jet plasma + UHE acceleration). Literature strength: WEAK to MEDIUM. Continues the ads-cft-instance program targeting across phenomenon-FCs.
citations: Casalderrey-Solana, Liu, Mateos, Rajagopal, Wiedemann 2014, Gauge/String Duality, Hot QCD and Heavy Ion Collisions (Cambridge)
Hartnoll, Lucas, Sachdev 2018, Holographic Quantum Matter (MIT)

electromagnetic-phenomena-across-energy-.description: Specific macroscopic-classical cells overlap with EM-phenomena cells via the electromagnetic emission of plasma processes. Specifically: (i) cell-mc-solar-flare-reconnection produces observable hard X-ray (RHESSI) + EUV (SDO) emissions — characterized in EM-phenomena AGN-and-Galactic-source cells; (ii) Parker Solar Probe measures in-situ plasma but the solar wind emits across the radio-to-UV spectrum. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — eighth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — only specific plasma-EM cells participate.
citations: Shibata, Magara 2011, Living Rev. Solar Phys. 8, 6

proton-decay-searches.description: PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR — closes §5 natural-pairing su5-gut-rep-content ↔ proton-decay-searches. Each cell in this FC specializes a specific GUT-variant prediction from the structural classification of GUT rep content. Specifically: (i) p → π⁰ e⁺ specializes minimal-Georgi-Glashow-SU(5) dim-6 operator content; (ii) p → K⁺ ν̄ specializes SUSY-SU(5) dim-5 colored-Higgs-triplet operators; (iii) p → η e⁺, p → K⁰ ℓ⁺, etc., specialize sub-leading SU(5) operator structures; (iv) SO(10) and flipped-SU(5) cells specialize alternative-GUT rep-content predictions. Status `established` — the GUT-experimental-test relationship is the textbook framework. Literature strength: STRONG. This is a §5 explicit closure.
citations: Georgi, Glashow 1974, Phys. Rev. Lett. 32, 438
Sakai, Yanagida 1982, Nucl. Phys. B 197, 533
Langacker 1981, Phys. Rep. 72, 185

proton-decay-searches.description: Proton decay processes involve SM-content particles in initial + final states: proton = uud bound state (SM-rep quark content); final-state mesons (π⁰, π⁺, K⁺, η⁰, K⁰, ρ⁰, ω⁰, ...) are SM-content hadrons; final-state leptons (e⁺, μ⁺, ν̄) are SM-content. Decay mediators (X/Y bosons of SU(5)) are BEYOND-SM but the decay processes themselves are SM-content-to-SM-content transitions modulo baryon-number-violation. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995, Introduction to QFT (Westview)

proton-decay-searches.description: Proton-decay searches are QFT-on-Minkowski-background processes specializing Poincaré-invariant local field theory. Standard framework. Status `established`. Literature strength: MEDIUM.
citations: Peskin, Schroeder 1995

N2-SCFT-spectrum-data.description: Each N=2 SCFT spectrum-data cell is categorically equivalent to a Higgs/Coulomb-branch cell in higgs-coulomb-branches — they organize the same physical theories from data-side vs geometry-side. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-SU2Nf4-Coulomb-Delta and cell-hcb-su2-Nf4-coulomb both describe the SU(2) N=2 SQCD with N_f=4 Coulomb-branch operator (one as data, one as geometry); (ii) cell-n2scft-AD-H0-Delta and cell-hcb-ad-h0 both describe AD H_0 theory; (iii) cell-n2scft-MN-E6-* and cell-hcb-mn-e6-* both describe Minahan-Nemeschansky E_6. The categorical equivalence reflects that the data and the geometry are two facets of the same N=2 SCFT object. Status `partial` because (a) not all HCB cells have a partner N2-SCFT-data cell (this FC has 17 cells vs HCB's 18, and the matching is not 1:1); (b) the chiral-algebra-bootstrap-data cells here have no direct geometric partner. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 HCB natural-pairing.
citations: Beem, Lemos, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2015 (chiral algebras unifying data and geometry)

N2-SCFT-spectrum-data.description: N=2 SCFT bootstrap-bounded observables derive from cft-bootstrap-exclusion-regions specialized to N=2 supersymmetric stress-tensor 4-point functions. Specifically: (i) cell-n2scft-bootstrap-a-c-bound derives from cft-bootstrap general unitarity + Hofman-Maldacena energy-positivity, specialized to N=2; (ii) AD H_0 Δ_u = 6/5 has been independently verified via N=2 bootstrap to high precision (Beem et al. 2014); (iii) chiral-algebra-2D-4D correspondence cells cross-reference 2D-CFT bootstrap data for the associated chiral algebras. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM. Cross-references the foundational cft-bootstrap structural-FC.
citations: Beem, Liendo, Peelaers, Rastelli, van Rees 2014, JHEP 03, 100 (N=2 bootstrap)
Hofman, Maldacena 2008, JHEP 05, 012

N2-SCFT-spectrum-data.description: Exceptional Minahan-Nemeschansky theories and class-S constructions derive their flavor-symmetry structure from ADE Lie-algebra data. Specifically: (i) MN E_6 / E_7 / E_8 theories have flavor symmetry = corresponding exceptional Lie algebra e_6, e_7, e_8; (ii) Higgs branches are minimal nilpotent orbits of those Lie algebras; (iii) Coulomb-branch operator dimensions Δ_u = 3, 4, 6 for E_6, E_7, E_8 connect to dual Coxeter numbers; (iv) class-S T_N theories have SU(N)³ flavor symmetry from A_{N-1} M5-brane choice. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for the ADE labeling correspondence.
citations: Minahan, Nemeschansky 1996, 1997 (MN E_n theories)
Gaiotto 2009 (class-S A_{N-1} from M5)

SPT-phase-and-anomaly-matching-observati.description: SPT phase classification derives directly from freed-hopkins-cobordism: every SPT phase corresponds to a cobordism class in Ω^G_{d+1}. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-Z8-FidkowskiKitaev's Z_8 classification derives from cobordism of 1+1D Pin⁻ structures; (ii) cell-spt-cobordism-SPT-classification directly invokes Freed-Hopkins 2016 cobordism classification; (iii) anomaly-inflow Callan-Harvey provides the link between bulk topological terms (which cobordism counts) and boundary anomalies. PRIMARY PHENOMENON↔STRUCTURAL EDGE — closes §5 freed-hopkins natural-pairing. Status `established` because the cobordism-classification framework is rigorously proven for symmetry-protected fermionic + bosonic SPTs.
citations: Freed, Hopkins 2016, arXiv:1604.06527
Kapustin 2014, arXiv:1403.1467

SPT-phase-and-anomaly-matching-observati.description: SM-anomaly-related cells specialize sm-rep-content: they apply anomaly-cancellation and anomaly-matching constraints to the specific SM gauge × matter content. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-SM-anomaly-cancellation constrains the SM Y-charges from gauge anomaly cancellation; (ii) cell-spt-Witten-SU2-anomaly requires even number of SM left-handed doublets; (iii) cell-spt-pi0-gamma-gamma-ABJ derives from SM electromagnetic + QCD content via triangle anomaly. Status `established`. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Bouchiat, Iliopoulos, Meyer 1972, Phys. Lett. B 38, 519
Witten 1982, Phys. Lett. B 117, 324

SPT-phase-and-anomaly-matching-observati.description: Specific SPT-phase observations specialize realized topological materials in topo-mat v22. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-3DTI-Z2-FuKaneMele cross-references topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family; (ii) cell-spt-parity-anomaly-3DTI references magnetically-doped TIs; (iii) cell-spt-MZM-Kitaev-chain partially-overlaps with topo-mat InSb/Al cells; (iv) cell-spt-Haldane-chain references S=1 chain materials. Status `partial` because (a) not all SPT cells specialize topo-mat (SM anomaly cells, cobordism formal cells don't); (b) phen↔phen edge: FOURTH such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Cross-references topo-mat v22 specific material cells

SPT-phase-and-anomaly-matching-observati.description: SPT-phase classifications in fermionic systems derive from the tenfold-way (Altland-Zirnbauer 1997). Specifically: (i) 3D TI Z_2 is the AII class (time-reversal-invariant insulator); (ii) Z_8 1D topological superconductor is BDI class with interactions; (iii) Majorana zero modes occur in D, BDI, DIII classes; (iv) integer QH is A class (broken T-reversal). The tenfold-way provides the non-interacting backbone that anomaly + interaction analyses then refine. Status `partial` — covers fermionic SPTs only. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Altland, Zirnbauer 1997, Phys. Rev. B 55, 1142
Schnyder, Ryu, Furusaki, Ludwig 2008, Phys. Rev. B 78, 195125

modified-gravity-alternatives.description: Modified-gravity alternatives derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups by generalizing or modifying the GR backbone. Specifically: (i) all modified-gravity tests reference the GR baseline established by spacetime-symmetry-groups; (ii) scalar-tensor and Horndeski variants modify the Einstein-Hilbert action while preserving Poincaré invariance; (iii) Einstein-aether variants break Lorentz invariance — explicit specialization within spacetime-symmetry-groups; (iv) massive gravity introduces graviton mass while preserving general covariance. PRIMARY STRUCTURAL ANCHOR. Status `established` — GR forms the unambiguous baseline.
citations: Will 2014, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (PPN formalism)

modified-gravity-alternatives.description: Modified-gravity tests specialize gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 — specifically the GW170817 multi-messenger event cell which provides the key graviton-speed bound. cell_refs list 7 cells where GW catalog observations are the falsifying test. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — fifth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — many modified-gravity variants falsified by GW170817 (TeVeS, Horndeski G_5, bigravity, Galileons, Einstein-aether); others constrained but surviving (dRGT massive gravity, narrow Horndeski subset).
citations: Abbott et al. 2017, Astrophys. J. Lett. 848, L13 (GW170817)
Lombriser, Lima 2017, Phys. Lett. B 765, 382

modified-gravity-alternatives.description: Specific modified-gravity tests specialize compact-astrophysical-objects v21 cells — PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar tests reference the NS-binary cell; EHT shadow tests reference the SMBH cells (M87, Sgr A*). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — sixth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for the test framework.
citations: Kramer et al. 2021, Phys. Rev. X 11, 041050
Psaltis et al. 2020, Phys. Rev. Lett. 125, 141104

modified-gravity-alternatives.description: MOND was originally proposed as a DM alternative — accounts for galaxy rotation curves without invoking particle dark matter. Specializes the broader dark-matter-candidates question by offering modified-gravity rather than particle-physics resolution. Status `partial` — MOND succeeds galactically but Bullet Cluster falsifies pure MOND at cluster scales (cf. dark-matter-candidates v25 evidence cells). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — seventh such FC-level edge in the dataset.
citations: Milgrom 1983, Astrophys. J. 270, 365
Clowe et al. 2006, Astrophys. J. Lett. 648, L109

macroscopic-classical-phenomena.description: Macroscopic-classical phenomena derive from spacetime-symmetry-groups via Galilean-invariant Navier-Stokes (fluids), nonrelativistic MHD (plasmas), classical-mechanics kinetic-theory (statistical mechanics). Specifically: (i) Navier-Stokes equation invariant under Galilean transformations (subgroup of Poincaré); (ii) classical-mechanics Hamiltonian dynamics is Galilean-invariant; (iii) Jarzynski + Crooks fluctuation theorems derive from time-reversal-symmetric microscopic dynamics. Status `established` — foundational classical-physics structure. Literature strength: STRONG.
citations: Landau, Lifshitz Vol. 6 (Fluid Mechanics)

macroscopic-classical-phenomena.description: Plasma physics phenomena derive from sm-rep-content via classical EM (Maxwell equations) + classical ion-electron dynamics. Specifically: (i) magnetic-reconnection is a topological rearrangement of magnetic-field lines — classical EM in plasma medium; (ii) tokamak confinement uses classical EM B-fields confining ion-electron plasma; (iii) ICF inertial-confinement uses laser EM driving classical hydrodynamics + nuclear reactions; (iv) solar flares are EM-emitting plasma phenomena. Status `partial`. Literature strength: STRONG for EM-driven plasma physics derivation. Cell_refs pin specific plasma-related cells.
citations: Krall, Trivelpiece 1973, Principles of Plasma Physics (McGraw-Hill)

macroscopic-classical-phenomena.description: Specific macroscopic-classical cells overlap with EM-phenomena cells via the electromagnetic emission of plasma processes. Specifically: (i) cell-mc-solar-flare-reconnection produces observable hard X-ray (RHESSI) + EUV (SDO) emissions — characterized in EM-phenomena AGN-and-Galactic-source cells; (ii) Parker Solar Probe measures in-situ plasma but the solar wind emits across the radio-to-UV spectrum. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — eighth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — only specific plasma-EM cells participate.
citations: Shibata, Magara 2011, Living Rev. Solar Phys. 8, 6

phen_phen_edges.description: Neutron-star interiors derive from hadronic-states physics extended to the dense-uniform-matter regime. Specifically: (i) the outer-crust nuclear-matter composition is direct hadronic spectroscopy at low density (nuclei populated from the chart-of-nuclides, a subset of cell-h-N and adjacent baryon cells); (ii) the inner-crust + outer-core composition (npe + μ matter) is determined by hadronic-state thresholds (when does hyperon population become favorable? — depends on the Λ, Σ, Ξ hyperon thresholds from cell-h-Lambdac-style spectra extrapolated to finite chemical potential, the 'hyperon puzzle'); (iii) inner-core composition possibly involves color-superconducting quark matter or a hadron-quark crossover region — connecting hadronic-states to (presumed-future) dense-quark-matter classification. Status `partial` because: NS-interior physics involves hadronic matter at conditions (high density, low T, finite μ_B and μ_Q) that are FAR outside the standard hadronic-states domain (T ≈ 0, μ ≈ 0, bound-state spectroscopy). The connection is real but mediated by EOS-modeling that extrapolates from spectroscopic input. cell_refs pin 3 NS-related cells whose hadronic-derivation is most operationally active. Literature strength: MEDIUM (connection well-developed for low-density crust; speculative for inner-core quark-matter component).

FIRST PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET. Each GW merger event specializes a merger of objects classified in compact-astrophysical-objects. Specifically: (i) GW150914 specializes a merger of two stellar-mass BHs (cells cell-compact-stellar-BH-XRB / cell-compact-stellar-BH-GW from compact-astro v21); (ii) GW170817 specializes a merger of two canonical neutron stars (cell-compact-NS-canonical in compact-astro); (iii) GW190521 specializes an intermediate-mass-BBH merger (cell-compact-IMBH-gap in compact-astro); (iv) GW190814 and GW230529 specialize lower-mass-gap mergers, populating the boundary between compact-astro cell-compact-NS-massive and cell-compact-stellar-BH; (v) GWTC-3 BBH population is the empirical realization of the stellar-mass BH population in compact-astro. The relationship is `specializes` because each event is a specific instance (merger of two specific objects) of the abstract object-classes in compact-astro. Status `partial` because: (a) not all GW event categories specialize compact-astro cells (NANOGrav PTA nHz signal is a stochastic background, not an individual merger of compact-astro objects; continuous-wave-pulsar searches are individual NSs not mergers; superradiance-bounded ultralight-boson searches are not even compact-object events); (b) the directional `specializes` relation is reasonable for events-to-object-types but the reverse (compact-astro object inferences derive from GW events as data) also holds — the relationship is genuinely intertwined. cell_refs pin 7 cells where the specialization is most direct. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework (events ARE mergers of objects); MEDIUM for the directional interpretation. This edge is justified by direct structural relation, not by the desideratum of producing a phen↔phen edge — the relation would be drawn independently.

THIRD PHENOMENON-FC ↔ PHENOMENON-FC EDGE IN THE DATASET (after v21 compact-astro→hadronic-states and v27 gravitational-waves→compact-astro). Many EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-object phenomena: (i) cell-em-crab-multiband specializes compact-astro pulsar-wind-nebula cells; (ii) cell-em-AGN-jet-synchrotron specializes SMBH cells; (iii) cell-em-GRBs-multiband specializes BNS-merger (short-GRB) + collapsar (long-GRB) cells; (iv) cell-em-GW170817-kilonova specializes BNS-merger cells (same physical object that GW catalog v27 also specializes); (v) cell-em-IceCube-TXS-multimessenger specializes blazar SMBH cells; (vi) cell-em-LHAASO-PeV-gamma specializes galactic-PeVatron-source cells (SNR + PWN populations in compact-astro). Status `partial` because not all EM-phenomena cells specialize compact-objects — laboratory-coherent sources (FEL, lasers) and thermal-cosmological-backgrounds (CMB) do not derive from compact objects. cell_refs pin 6 cells with direct compact-astro specialization. The relation is honest physics: many EM cells ARE compact-object EM emission organized by photon-spectrum rather than object-class. Literature strength: STRONG for the framework.

Specific SPT-phase observations specialize realized topological materials in topo-mat v22. Specifically: (i) cell-spt-3DTI-Z2-FuKaneMele cross-references topo-mat cell-ti-Bi2Se3-family; (ii) cell-spt-parity-anomaly-3DTI references magnetically-doped TIs; (iii) cell-spt-MZM-Kitaev-chain partially-overlaps with topo-mat InSb/Al cells; (iv) cell-spt-Haldane-chain references S=1 chain materials. Status `partial` because (a) not all SPT cells specialize topo-mat (SM anomaly cells, cobordism formal cells don't); (b) phen↔phen edge: FOURTH such FC-level edge in the dataset.

Modified-gravity tests specialize gravitational-wave-event-catalog v27 — specifically the GW170817 multi-messenger event cell which provides the key graviton-speed bound. cell_refs list 7 cells where GW catalog observations are the falsifying test. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — fifth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — many modified-gravity variants falsified by GW170817 (TeVeS, Horndeski G_5, bigravity, Galileons, Einstein-aether); others constrained but surviving (dRGT massive gravity, narrow Horndeski subset).

Specific modified-gravity tests specialize compact-astrophysical-objects v21 cells — PSR J0737-3039 binary-pulsar tests reference the NS-binary cell; EHT shadow tests reference the SMBH cells (M87, Sgr A*). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — sixth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial`. Literature strength: MEDIUM-to-STRONG for the test framework.

MOND was originally proposed as a DM alternative — accounts for galaxy rotation curves without invoking particle dark matter. Specializes the broader dark-matter-candidates question by offering modified-gravity rather than particle-physics resolution. Status `partial` — MOND succeeds galactically but Bullet Cluster falsifies pure MOND at cluster scales (cf. dark-matter-candidates v25 evidence cells). PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — seventh such FC-level edge in the dataset.

Specific macroscopic-classical cells overlap with EM-phenomena cells via the electromagnetic emission of plasma processes. Specifically: (i) cell-mc-solar-flare-reconnection produces observable hard X-ray (RHESSI) + EUV (SDO) emissions — characterized in EM-phenomena AGN-and-Galactic-source cells; (ii) Parker Solar Probe measures in-situ plasma but the solar wind emits across the radio-to-UV spectrum. PHENOMENON↔PHENOMENON EDGE — eighth such FC-level edge in the dataset. Status `partial` — only specific plasma-EM cells participate.
